BUT, the leftovers were so good and tasted like bacon. Not kidding. BACON!
My grated tofu looked like grated cheese. So the leftovers, (just wrapped in the original parchment paper and stuck in the fridge) were all hard, like the texture of leftover bacon. Deliciously chewy! Perfect for topping a pizza. (Although I just ate it up with a spoon.)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
I combined this recipe with Jocelyn Ramirez' crispy mushroom taco recipe. The result was the best vegetarian "carnitas" I've had. The two textures work very well together. 
If the tomato paste and oil step seems cumbersome, barbecue sauce also works.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: So good! Don’t be worried about cooking the tofu too much.  I crumbled it by hand and spread it on one large sheet pan. Cooked long enough for dark crunchy bits. My new go to for adding tofu to salads and noodles. Leftovers were just as good the next day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Used salsa instead of tomato paste and thought the tofu came out well. I also fried the tortillas to make a hard shelled taco. It was pretty easy and takes only 15 -30 seconds per shell. I ditched the mayo too. I like the taste of ripe avocados and lime.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Do NOT line your pans with foil to cook the tofu! Line them with parchment. The tofu sticks to the foil and you will lose a ton of it trying to scrape it off. Use parchment paper!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Joann I completely agree - the foil reduced the total amount.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @JoannI sprayed a light coating of olive oil and had no sticking at all

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: if you live a similar life to me and find yourself with gochujang but no tomato paste in your fridge, give it a try - really delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I just have to say that this recipe has been life changing. We make a handful of tofu recipes in our house on repeat but never have we ended up craving tofu the way we do with this recipe. The 5 year old even thinks it's fantastic. Give Alexa all the bonuses this year.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was excellent but I would double the amount of tofu next time and omit the tomato paste/oil mix. The spiced crispy bits were far more appealing before the paste. Also, I topped it with corn salsa that contrasted nicely.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Where do we use the other 1T soy sauce?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: 1 T. Soy sauce per pan. Two pans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I’m obsessed! I NEVER write reviews on anything but this one is so good I had to say something. Kept everything in the recipe exactly as is and loved it so so much.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Would not add mayo to the avocado sauce again. That was an odd flavor. Personally, I would probably go with some kind of dairy. Sour cream or yogurt. Otherwise, delicious! I must have pressed my tofu *too* well because it did start to burn a little.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Any suggested substitutions for tomato paste, for a guest allergic to tomatoes?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Francine, I am also unable to eat tomatoes. We admitted the tomato paste and added 2 tablespoons of chili crisp instead.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!  Subbed Mexican sour cream (crema agria) for the mayo - adds a bit more tanginess.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: It was a hot summer day, so I ended up cooking this on a tray on a charcoal grill. I think it added some nice smokiness.  I added a little fresh minced garlic (3–4 cloves) and ancho chile powder (.25 or .5 tsp) to the tofu. At the very end I drizzled some olive oil and added a little extra salt (for my taste).  Since I had the grill going, I also grilled onions, red pepper, and jalapeños on skewers (briefly marinated with a mix of equal parts olive oil, soy sauce, and vegan Worcestershire sauce, and some minced garlic).  Otherwise, I did everything as suggested. Family loved it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I reduced the time to cook because I popped this into an air fryer, turned out soooooo good! Even my picky kids and less than enthusiastic about tofu husband enjoyed this!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Bee were you able to cut some of the oil? This recipe uses a ton of oil

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
Made a big batch, reheated for 30-40 seconds in microwave, to eat through week. Worked well. 
Hand tore the tofu, which resulted in some larger pieces. Nice textural diversity. 
Mixed tofu and all spices/oil in bowl before putting on sheet pan - no foil or parchment - 25 mins in oven. Put back in bowl and mixed with tomato paste and oil, another 20 mins in oven. 
Ate with TJs corn tortillas, greek yogurt guacamole, salsa, sour cream and a loose interpretation of the NYT Cowboy Caviar recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: My wife and I have been plant-based for over two decades, so we know our way around bean curd.  This has, after only one try, jumped up into the top tier of tofu recipes for us.  Super simple to make, and the crispy texture and "slightly burnt" flavor of it is just magnificent.  We had it over brown rice and steamed broccoli..."chef's kiss"!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I made this recipe twice:  first as stated and then with some tweaks. I would recommend leaving out the tomato paste step entirely -- just add another couple of Tbsp of oil at the outset. Otherwise the tomato paste overwhelms the flavor of the tofu and spices. There is also no need for a food processor -- just mash the avocado and stir in the lime and mayo! This is a very good recipe when simplified. I also made some lime-pickled red onions (rather than just diced raw onions).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Made these exactly as written (2X now), and added cilantro, quick-pickled radishes, and fresh jalapeño rings. ‘These are the best tacos EVER’ declared my husband, who often says, on Meatless Mondays, ‘this would be really good if it had [bacon/chicken/beef] in it. SO good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was seriously amazing. I did mix the oil and soy sauce first and toss the crumbled tofu in it, then mixed the spices and tossed the whole bowl with that. The tomato paste/oil addition later adds incredible flavor. I served the tacos with pickled onions, which really complemented the tofu and the avocado crema. This will be a special go-to for my vegetarian family from now on!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I finally made these, and WOW, these are so great! Different & makes a wholesome meatless meal. I half-shredded & coarsely chopped the tofu, used a bowl to mix the tofu w/whisked soy sauce & oil, then sprinkled the spice mix, & later mixed in the whisked tomato paste & oil & used parchment. I served with pico de gallo, shredded lettuce, guacamole, charred corn salad, refried beans with green chiles, rice, sour cream & shredded cheddar. I will make these again & again! Delicious recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: These are amazing. Added some diced zucchini for the last 10 mins of roasting. No other changes, really - these are soooooooo good

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I’ll definitely make this again, but I’ll omit the tomato paste/oil step — this was unnecessary and didn’t taste great. Also, be careful not to make the crumbles too small! I had a lot of extra crispy bits, so I’ll probably chop the tofu next time to better control the size of the crumbles. Excited to make this again because I know it’ll be even better!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is ace!! So simple but so yum. Followed the recipe exactly.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
Wow this is probably my fav NYT cooking recipe I’ve done so far. I was skeptical at first but the tomato paste really gives it that umami flavor and crispness. It’s gorgeous.

I also took the same spice mix and added it to some chopped up Zucchini and baked it in the over alongside the Tofu. Would highly recommend as it was a nice topping on the tacos.

I also despise mayo lol, so swapped it with plain Greek yogurt - turned out great!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I was a bit surprised by this one, I expected more. I grated the tofu, and found after 20 minutes it was not just crisping up but burning. Perhaps too thin? I stirred, and watched carefully, it crackled as indicated. We found it bland over all, chewy in some spots, rubbery in others. For seconds we added sauteed onions & peppers, cheese and salsa to improve the taste, but this did not help the texture. Would not make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
Made this for my meat-loving husband, vegetarian daughter, and her vegetarian partner. We all loved it! Easy, yet final product is complex in its flavor. 

Only change: I baked tofu on a single baking sheet. There was plenty of space, and finished product was crispy and yummy—with one less thing to clean afterward!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I cooked this at my bf’s place, and he does not have a large grater, only a very small one that looks like one of the ones you use on your feet (sorry for the imagery). I went with it because I was tickled by the result, and wanted to see how it came out. On the extra firm tofu, the grater created a texture that went into the oven looking like spaghetti, and came out looking like diner hash browns. The end result was delicious. I upped many of the seasoning ingredients to compensate for the increased surface area and the two 16oz blocks of tofu.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I made this as written but instead of avocado mayo I just sliced up an avocado squeezed lime juice on it and it was delicious. Trying to keep the fat content and calories down. Not to mention it was much easier.  Love this recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This is also great tucked into romaine lettuce shells, or you could you could use iceberg shells.   The baby heads of romaine are perfect if you can find them, I use Organic Girl brand.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: any chance someone's figured out how to prepare the tofu in an air fryer and be willing to share the instructions?  thanks!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: If you want to be irritated and disappointed on a busy weeknight, make this dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
I love this recipe overall — it’s become one of our favourites.

Quick pickled red onions work really well with the crispy tofu and avocado mayo, and take no extra time as they can be prepared while the tofu is in the oven.

1 red onion, very thinly sliced
120 ml apple cider vinegar (white vinegar works as well)
120 ml water
1 tbsp sugar
1 tsp salt

Warm the vinegar, water, sugar and salt just until dissolved, then pour over the sliced onions in a jar. Let sit for 20–30 minutes before serving.

The vinegar gives a nice sharp, slightly sweet contrast to the creamy avocado mayo and crispy tofu.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Prepared as directed, this was dry and overcooked, but the seasoning was good. I will prepare it again, but i will not grate the tofu. I will crumble it into larger “curds,” bean-size, and probably cook for less time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
@Kathi

Agreed. It seems to me you can just squeeze the tofu into “curds” when removing excess liquid in the beginning. One less thing to wash!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was truly delicious and super-easy. I made the same technique modifications as others to simplify and use less dishes - used the microwave to dry the tofu, shredded with my hands, mixed the oil, soy and spices in a bowl and tossed the tofu, then roasted on one pan, not two. Also made the avocado cream in a bowl with a spoon; no need to dirty a food processor for that. Cilantro and onion were key. Simple and quick enough that my 12 year old, who is learning to cook, asked if he would be able to make them and I didn't hesitate to say he definitely can.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Sounds painful ("shredded my hands")!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Giulia Michelini. That isn’t what they said….. reread it

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This is amazing.  You will not miss the lack of meat.  Such an innovative preparation of tofu - deep, rich, umami flavor.  Never grated tofu before but will do again. Only change I made was to use 2 percent greek yoghurt instead of mayo and to pickle the onions in lime and salt.  Will make again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Love this and have made it twice. Every scrap was gobbled up! Cut down the lime juice the second time, as I found it overpowering.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Super tasty!  I found grating the tofu yielded bits that were too small -- will tear by hand next time, or even cube.  Also mixed ingredients in a bowl first and tossed the tofu.  And I also used just one 11x17 cookie sheet, which fit all the two block's worth of tofu and cooked very nicely.  The avocado crema was *delicious* and kept nicely overnight.  Will use the leftovers for a Mexican inspired dinner.  Love this recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: CAVEAT: DO NOT trust the temperature and timing in this recipe the first time you made it. I did and am left with burnt crumbles that are utterly dried out. I began with Trader Joe's excellent regular organic tofu, frozen because the Use By Date was nearing. After 2 days of thawing, I squeezed it so dry that I should have stopped roasting 1 tray after 15 minutes and continued with tomato paste for 5 minutes more.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Awesome recipe!! I did most as instructed, although I did press the tofu & mix everything together in a bowl before splitting onto baking sheets. Could be a great addition to add chopped pineapple to the mix to give it an al pastor vibe! Head to your local Mexican grocer for fresh tortillas, radishes, and salsas!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
Am I crazy? Where is the second tablespoon of soy sauce? And the olive oil doesn’t add up either. 1/4 cup plus 3 tablespoons is 7 total, but I only see 5. 
Delicious recipe, no complaints. Just confusion :)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: You drizzle 2 tablespoons oil and 1 tablespoon soy sauce over each of the two pans, so 4 tablespoons oil and 2 tablespoons soy sauce total. Then the remaining 3 tablespoons oil are added to the tomato paste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Made this tonite, but crumbled tofu by hand after resting a carton of juice( horizontally) on it for 3 hours in the fridge. After crumbling it, I added sesame oil, soy sauce, and the spices, mixed it all up with a spatula, placed it on parchment paper on the toaster oven tray, and baked at 400 for 30 minutes. Came out great. Saved a step and extra baking time. I made the avocado cream by mashing with a fork. No need to clean up a blender.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Note for those cutting the recipe in half: the scaled instructions mis-calculate the initial oil and soy sauce. It should be 2 Tbsp oil and 1 Tbsp soy sauce. The un-scaled original lists ingredients *per baking sheet.*

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Omg I love NYtimes recipes so much but this one frustrated me to death.  Half the tofu ended up in the sink as I tried to squeeze the water out.  Did not realize I should mix the oil and seasoning etc in the bowl and had some with too much soy sauce and others not enough.  What was the point in grating  the tofu, it already fell apart so just crumbled it with my hands. Could not get tomato paste to thin out to “drizzle” so not sure how that was supposed to work.  Not sure I’ll try again?? Argh

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Katie to squeeze water out of tofu, wrap it in a tea towel and then place a heave object on top

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I was very excited about this recipe and wanted so badly to love this dish but it really wasn't that great, in my opinion.  Clearly other folks like it.  We followed the recipe exactly and yes, the tofu was crispy, but it felt more like eating tofu jerky than a taco fixing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Awesome recipe!  My husband is from Mexico and was very reluctant to even try tofu tacos.  Both he and my daughter loved them. I tried to mix together the tomato paste and oil for but found they wouldn't incorporate well so I eliminated that step and just tossed the tofu with all the oil, tomato paste, and spices at one time (I mixed the spices in the oil too).  My tofu said it was firm but when I squeezed the water out it was really crumbly so I just crumbled it instead of shredding.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Not a home run.  You CANNOT "drizzle" tomato paste and oil on anything.  I did what one poster suggested.  All the tofu crumbles in a bowl, drizzle with oil and tamari (soy sauce) and mix gently.  Add the spices and mix again.  Save the bowl.  Put the oil and the tomato paste in the bowl. When the 30 minutes is done, put the tofu in the bowl and mix with a spatula.  I have another point to make about the written recipe, but you limit us too much here!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Is there any reason to use a sheet pan covered in foil other than ease of cleanup. I found a lot of the tofu stuck. Anyone tried parchment paper instead of foil or knows a reason to avoid it.  Thanks

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Wow!  My I don’t love tofu but I guess I’ll eat it if you make it  husband said he liked these tacos better than regular tacos.  The comments first, and I would’ve tossed the tofu with all the seasonings in the tomato paste and oil and soy sauce in the beginning. For the three of us, this was too much, but Im going to use the leftover tofu in enchiladas later this week.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Such a great recipe. Followed tips given by others and mixing everything in a  bowl. Tossed tofu after first roast in mixing bowl with tomato paste as well. Instead of using big oven, used countertop toaster oven. I could eat the entire tray. So delicious. Let’s see if it makes it to dinner time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: We're in camp 'this didn't work', and we cook with tofu a fair bit.  My spouse asked me what they were being punished for...  It's crispy for sure, but the flavour is just kind of...toasted.  Works better as a topping maybe, rather than the star of the show.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Made as written but halved the quantity and loved it! Crunchiest tofu I’ve ever achieved. Better than most “tofu chorizo” episodes out there. Wish I had made the full recipe so I had more leftovers!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Just a heads up that when I followed the recipe as written, my tofu was way overdone (dry, not good). I'll try again and experiment with less oven time for the first go-round. Remember: all ovens are different!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I used the coarse side of my box grater and got shreds, not crumbles.  They got way too crisp and dry.  Next time I will revert to my usual way of making tofu "meat" and freeze the block, thaw it, and then crumble it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: These were pretty dang good! I added some plain yogurt to the avocado cream and sauteed some mushrooms with the same seasoning as the tofu to add some veg and some bulk. Devoured by adults and kids alike!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I just came here to say that this recipe was so good, my only-slightly-clueless husband thought he was eating chicken. 10/10

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Awesome. Followed the recipe with a slight reduction in baking time (my oven runs hot).  A+

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious. I followed commenter’s advice and tossed my tofu with the seasonings in a bowl before baking, and then again with the tomato paste near the end. I also doubled the seasoning as I feel many recipes I try from NYT are underseasoned. Came out amazing, will definitely be cooking this again. Also, freeze your tofu before cooking for meatier texture!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Not sure how this dish is supposed to taste, but I I didn't get a deep umami flavor from the tomato paste and olive oil drizzle. The dish was crispy but tasteless. The avocado cream was tasty, though. I am not experienced with preparing tofu, and found this a lot of effort for not much gratification.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: No need to use mayo. Cashew crema is divine in this. Raw cashews, water and a squeeze of lime juice in a Bullet or high-speed blender. Unlike mayo, cashews are "good" fat and the result is dairy-free and even more delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Halved the recipe. Flavor was good but did not like the texture. Next time I will skip all that oven baking just do it in the skillet.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Tip: put the tofu in the freezer, thaw and squeeze. It turns into "ground beef" texture.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I've made tofu tacos for years. Another way is sauteing onion and garlic in olive oil adding hte tofu and spices and then squeezing lime on top. I'd add that you should use the firmest tofu you can find. I love using the super firm tofu. It cooks faster by not having to remove as much water from the tofu.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I’ve used a recipe that recommends freezing the firm or extra firm tofu first. Then thawing, crumbling, and toasting in the oven with seasonings and nutritional yeast. People used to ground beef in their chili don’t notice the difference. I will try it without freezing for these tacos.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: How did freezing the tofu first work?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I really enjoy mashed avocado with plain yogurt. It's a perfect accompaníment to tacos.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This sounds so good. Is there a way to make this without mayonnaise? I don't love it, not even the vegan one.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Auni M I used Costco guacamole that I had in the fridge and added more lime juice and lime zest and it was delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was very good. Cut recipe in half and it was enough for two.  Like a few others, I had trouble getting the tofu off the foil.  Switched to no foil and it worked just fine.  Very easy to clean pan after.  I used xtra firm tofu which seemed to crisp up sooner than the recipe cook time.  Good texture. Flavor seemed to come more from other elements like the avocado sauce, etc.  But all good.  Next time I want to try this with TVP instead.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Made this last night, and it was yummy! I quick pickled the radishes and red onions, and threw on some thinly sliced cucumbers. The only issue I had was that the tofu didn't get crispy, and I cooked it for quite a while. I used firm, and drained it well (I thought). I have to say, though, even not crispy this was a delicious hit - and this is coming from a tofu-adverse family!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I used Extra Firm tofu, and I think I over-grated and over-roasted.  This resulted in some remarkably crispy tofu.  The "tofu crispies" were quite delicious but they weren't exactly right for the tacos.  However, the leftover crispies have been a brilliant salad topping and omelet enhancement, and would likely be marvelous on potato-leek soup or just about anything where you might consider bacon bits.  I have discovered a recipe within a recipe, and I'm making these again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: We are a fan of tofu, and I use it frequently for dinner. These tofu tacos are indeed delicious. BUT, they are incredibly time consuming in order to get the tofu to achieve the taste and texture of ground meat.  The prep-time is under what it takes in reality, and I am a very experienced cook. So, doubt I will make these again, since I do not think it makes a lot of sense to have to work this hard to get tofu to taste like ground-meat. Unless you are meat-adverse, just use ground meat.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Annie Huh. I found this recipe extremely easy and hands off. The prep seemed straight forward to me, and most of the time is just waiting for it to bake. On the other hand, I never thought the goal was for it to taste like ground beef.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Texture-wise, I always put my tofu in a casserole dish with a plate on top and then put heavy kitchen items (a pot or my canister of utensils etc) on top to help get all the water out.  I usually do this hours before and let it sit.  By the time I need to cook, or in this case grate the tofu, so much water has come out and the texture is much better!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
These were great! We are vegetarian, so these are perfect for us. I did mix the soy sauce, olive oil and spices into the tofu in a bowl prior to putting on the baking sheet. I don’t know why NYT likes to tell you to mix it on the pan - it’s just so messy and hard to coat evenly that way! (I do actually - I know they are trying to reduce the dishes, but in this case I prefer the extra dish). 

I was skeptical about the avocado cream (why not just make guacamole?), but I made it as weekend and it actually was really good. :)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious! 
My only changes: I did a quick pickle of sliced cucumber, radishes, jalapeños and garlic. 
Broiled the tofu at the end to get out crispy. 

I really think the pickled veggies make a difference.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This one’s a mystery to me. I followed the recipe exactly, except for doubling the spices as others had suggested, and the tofu was decent. I wasn’t wowed, but I don’t love tofu and it was a pretty reasonable way to eat it. But the avocado cream? Yeuch. I would much, much rather have just had slices of fresh avocado with a sprinkle of lime juice—the mayo and blending took all the freshness out of 2 perfectly good avocados while adding work and more dishes to wash. My vegetarian husband eats avocado every day and I thought he’d love this; nope. If I try the recipe again, it’ll be just the tofu “meat.”

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I liked this well enough, but there were too many steps and too many dishes to clean (during and afterwards) for what it offered.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was a bit of a fail for me. Despite mixing the tofu with oil, it all adhered to the foil and I lost a lot of it. It tasted fine, and I really enjoyed the avocado sauce, but I don't think this will become part of my rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
Prep hacks:

My kids ask for this weekly so I invested in cutting down the prep. 

1. Use super-firm tofu. No need to drain
2. Pre-measure spices. I do 10 servings at a time and keep it in a mason jar. 40g cumin, 27g paprika, 22g garlic powder, 18g onion powder, 6g cayenne (that’s the 1/4t amount), 37g kosher salt, 22g black pepper. Use 17g per 2 blocks of tofu, which is just under 2T. 

Convection oven: 
20 min at 375, rotating halfway 
10 min after adding the tomato paste. It’s very close to the original recipe. 5 more min would probably d hl make it exact.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Jeremy additional hack: I tried doing the tofu stovetop and it turned out decently similar. This way cuts down the entire prep/cooking to 20 min

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: The directions say to sprinkle half the seasoning mix onto the tofu. But then it never says to do with the other half. Is that an error or am I reading it wrong?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
Runner48, your question prompted me to reread the recipe! Sure enough, it says to use two sheet pans, and sprinkle half the seasonings over the tofu mixture *in each pan*. 

I might make half the recipe, as it makes a LOT of tacos. 😊

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Karen Lee Thank you! Family loved it including two teenage boys. Trying to find ways to eat less meat and this is a great find for us!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I used my air fryer. Made a 1/2 recipe and cooked it in 2 batches, about 6 minutes per batch. Then i put the 2 batches together and cooked again for a couple minutes. Like others, I mixed the oil, soy sauce etc. into the tofu first. Great and simple.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I added thinly sliced cabbage to the onion with some lime juice + salt for a slaw to bulk up the veg in the tacos.  Otherwise, I made the dish as written and loved it.  Easy, flavorful, and unique.  As a long-time vegan, I always love trying a new protein preparation that uses a familiar ingredient (tofu) in a new way.  Will definitely be making this again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: parchment paper instead of foil to prevent sticking. with parchment it peals right off and still bakes the same.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I live in an RV and don’t have an oven. I followed the recipe using the same ingredients in a tabletop, plug-in nonstick skillet with lid. Minor adjustments made: I cooked it all in one batch instead of two pans, removed the lid for the last few minutes after adding the tomato paste & oil,  and just crumbled the tofu with my hands instead of grating. It was excellent and I will be making this weekly!!!!!!!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious but the tofu stuck to the foil.  I’d use parchment paper next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I followed other reviewers’ recommendations and mixed the tofu with spices and olive oil in a bowl. After the first bake, I put it back in the bowl to toss with tomato paste and olive oil. I quick pickled the onion and radishes first, made the rest of the dinner, and they were perfect by the time everything was done! Served with grilled portobellos and peppers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: These are FIRE!!! One of my fav tofu recipes ever as someone trying to swap it out for animal proteins.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I can’t find that Mark Bittman tortilla recipe??

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Any suggestions for a non tomato substitute for the tomato paste or ketchup?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Bonnie Red pepper paste?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I make a similar tofu taco dish, add nutritional yeast to this for another cheesy flavor. It’s delish!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
I think this just wasn’t for us. No one seemed to enjoy eating a tortilla with a mouth full of crunchy tofu. I can see how having crunch would be good mixed with juicy bits. 
We did enjoy the avocado cream- I used it as a dip for the cut up veggies

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I will make a half recipe to try. Has anyone successfully crisped the tofu in an air fryer? I would use a parchment liner.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was fantastic and it reheats well. So far I have made this twice and the first time I baked the tofu too long, so it was extra crispy. We thought it tasted like bacon bits, so no one was disappointed.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: What am I missing? I followed the recipe to a T…yes, the tofu was crispy, but had no taste. I had pickled onions, I even pickled the radishes, avocado had no taste…. Definitely not worth the effort!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I added hot sauce too. But the next day, leftovers are amazing!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Yes to the taco tortillas, but ugh to that mayo-avocado crema gunk. Not worth the nutritional "value", or the hours of labor, for a casual Tuesday nigh meal that only adults might venture to nibble.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Wow, these tacos are delicious! Finally, a recipe where the tofu really does get crispy. Great substitute for ground meat. May become my new go-to taco recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Really yummy vegetarian dinner that had plenty of leftovers. Added grilled onions and bell peppers and topped with pickled red onion. Could see adding cojita cheese being a very yummy addition as well. Didn't have tomato paste, but still tasted great! The better you drain the liquid out of the tofu, the less time you'll need to bake them in the oven.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: We eat tofu frequently but this was my first time grating it-worked better than I thought! The only issue I encountered was that the tomato paste/oil mixture never got to a texture where it could be “drizzled.” I think next time I will play around with the ratio of paste to oil and maybe add some water to thin it out.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Delicious . Love the avocado cream with it . I served with almond flour tortilla from Trader Joe’s and sautéed garlicky kale . So good

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I gotta say, this was a revelation. I'd never grated and roasted tofu, and I loved the texture. I made this recipe exactly as written except I doubled the spices for the tofu. But the crunch of the tofu, the creamy avocado sauce, some red onion and radish slices made very, very good tacos. I also thought the grated tofu would be interesting in other dishes like pasta or used as a crunchy topping on salads.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Sorry to ask a dumb question, but when the recipe says "grate" the tofu, does that actually mean with a box grater or does it mean finely crumble with fingers, which is what I usually do when making taco filling out of tofu?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Not a dumb question! I wish they would describe what they mean. I used the coarsest side of a box grater and it came out way too fine, and I had a hard time scraping it off the aluminum foil when it was done cooking. It seems like crumbling it with your fingers would give a better result.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Erin thank you!!! Making this tonight so glad you responded!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Really good! My toddler basically drank the crema. I only had hard shells so used them but I think it definitely would have been better with soft taco for textural interest. The tofu itself was very surprising-- we're a ground beef taco sort of house fairly often and everyone finished every crumb. In the rotation for sure.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I skipped adding the tomato paste mixture on one tray, so we had “two flavors.” They were both delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious. Made it with a charred corn salad from Catherine Perez RD.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Dee-Lish. Painless Prep, no specialty shop ingredients...fabulous flavor. I dumped the tofu off the baking sheet into a bowl, 'cause a drizzle, it's NOT. But with a quick flick of my spatula in the chocolate-chip-cookie making bowl...and we were all set. I agree with other commenteers...watch that like your Golden Reteriver's tail near a campfire....if you look away she might burn. Def going into the bi weekly rotation. Avo-Crema is 5 stars herself!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I used Follow Your Heart brand veganaise mixed with fresh lime just as a “sour cream” for all the vegan tacos I make. Definitely would recommend that brand to anyone making the avocado crema vegan!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
My partner and I have been trying to get into tofu, but all the other recipes seemed to fall flat. NOT THIS ONE! We *devoured* these tacos. Made tofu as directed but halved the recipe, and topped with the avocado mayo, black beans, diced tomatoes, shredded cheddar, and cilantro. 

I think we’re going to reinstate taco Tuesday as tofu taco Tuesday!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Unless I missed it, if you are skipping carbs why not use this as filling in lettuce leaves.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: FYI - if you drain and freeze the block of tofu, when you thaw it out to cook with it the texture has changed to be more firm and chewy.   It would be perfect that way with this recipe. :)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Ah!  Awful.  The baking times must be way off.  This turned out to be dried out tofu gravel!  Terrible! Ultra-crispy. It was nearly "blackened" and I didn't bake it for the full time after the tomato paste mixture.  Hard to "dribble" tomato paste mixed with oil- It wasn't very fluid.  Oh well.  At least the ingredients were cheap.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I love tofu but I did NOT like this dish. The tofu is way too crispy and doesn’t taste like anything. The crema is good but 3 tablespoons is too much lime juice. Leftovers went in the compost sadly, even my most enthusiastic eater didn’t like it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was a winner. I added a little bit of chipotle in adobo with the tomato paste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I crumbled the tofu by hand directly into my instant pot and used the roast function. Easy and turned out great. I find two blocks to be just enough for two people who want to have sufficient  leftovers for lunch.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I really wanted to like this, but even following directions exactly, the tofu had the consistency of dried chewy kibble without much flavor.  I did grate it on the large side of a box grater, so the pieces were really small.  I wonder if breaking it up by hand into larger pieces and doubling the seasoning would help.  Grateful for any suggestions.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I was looking for vegetarian meals to make on the grill during a summer heatwave and came across this recipe. To adapt for that - I preheated with all burners on high, then I shut off the middle burner and placed a single baking sheet with the tofu on the grill in the middle. Roasted for 15, tossed, did another 10, then added the tomato paste mixture and went back on for a final five minutes. Came out great!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Very good- only needs 25 minutes in air fryer for first cook.  I used the avocado sauce from another recipe bc I hate mayo - it uses cashews instead.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Even my young kids loved this! Be sure to include the onions and cilantro—the combination of flavors is what makes this dish pop. The one thing I would change is to add more lime to the sauce.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Tofu became my friend when banqueting in China (the least weird thing). And now I really like it! This was great will do again. I will spray the foil or scooch around some oil. My tofu stuck quite a bit (maybe I did too well getting water out). Next time I'll make it per the recipe (and the mixing many have suggested below). My chores ran late, so I used some aioli I had in the fridge & other subs, but it was still awesome. I keep picking at it but I think there'll be enough for dinner!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Used flour taco shell like pizza dough. Layered the tofu mix on top of grated cheese and baked to crispy delish for 4-5 minutes at 400. You could also make a lahmajun: after pulling the taco pizza out of the oven, sprinkle on top some antep/aleppo pepper, few slices of red onion, a sprinkle of sumac and a dollop of avocado mayo. Roll it up like a wrap. Devour. Yummm!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Don’t swap out the tinfoil for parchment paper, I noticed a big difference in the texture and moisture level of the tofu.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Maybe I am too good at getting the water out of my tofu, but via the recommended cooking time, this was a crispy, inedible mess. I can’t fathom all the good reviews. And I cook a lot of tofu!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: LOVE this recipe! It’s quickly become a favorite. The tofu is so crisp and delicious and the avocado sauce is the perfect accompaniment. I halve the recipe for my two person household and use the leftovers to make fantastic breakfast tacos with the addition of some scrambled egg.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: really enjoyed this. the tofu I had in the house was frozen, which results in a very dry product after defrosting/squeezing which translated to a final dish where crunch was more texturally present than chewiness. I'll make this again, but if starting from frozen I'll likely try a nonstick skillet instead of the oven so I have a little more control over the crispiness.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Made one pan with the tomato paste, one without since several reviewers didn't care for the tomato paste.  We preferred it with the tomato paste.  Issue we had was that some of the tofu was too crispy (smaller bits).  Didn't bother with foil - cooked directly on pans. Will try to get tofu pieces more of the same size and keep a close eye on it next time to not get so crispy/hard. Served with pickled red onion, avocado sauce (1/2 of recipe was plenty), radish, shredded lettuce and cojita.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This recipe was so easy and delicious! We'll be making this again and again -- everyone loved this one -- vegans and meat-eaters alike.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Love this!! I halved it and won’t the next time!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This is a fantastic recipe—great way to make tacos for everyone. According to one of my granddaughter’s friends, also great on grilled cheese the next day!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Could I make this filling ahead of time to take to my time-crunched son and daughter-in-law? Not sure if it reheats well, and how best to do that. Any thoughts? Thanks.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Four stars because just "so-so" as taco filling (the shreds flew all over the place and were pretty chewy; next time I'll try small cubes), but AMAZING as leftovers as a chorizo sub mixed-in with one scrambled egg . The single egg was (almost) enough to bind the warmed up (with some extra olive oil) tofu, and delish served open face on butter sourdough toast. Just the right amount of heat...I'm a fan!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: made 1/2 recipe this last night w/ extra firm Ota tofu tossed dry and wet ingredients in bowl then. on sheet pan 400 regular back for 20 min then tried to toss with paste and oil  which did not go well. cooked another 15-min to get brown color and "crispy" but that made it dried out and crunchy. I was hoping for more moisture. flavor is ok. I wonder if should just try in skillet would definitely add tomato paste earlier with dry and wet ingredients. and cook for much less time

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! And easy to put together. I did add a sprinkle of Monterey Jack cheese in the tortilla just because. This is definitely a keeper. Jimmy G, thanks for the suggestion of tossing it all in a bowl before spreading out to toast. That worked great!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I used about half the olive oil this recipe calls for, mostly because I was running out. Even after doing that and making this a day early and reheating in the microwave, it was crispy and delicious in texture.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Really good! Made this in the convection oven so after reading some other posts, I ran the first round (sans tomato) at 350 degrees for around 15 minutes (can add a couple more minutes when you check) and then the second round (w tomato) for around 8 min. I feel like this gave me a good balance of crispy and soft bits. I also roasted some zucchini and bell pepper on the second rack which turned out to be a really nice addition!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Good.  Not 5 stars, but a solid 4.  Avocado crema is what makes it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I’ve been making something similar for awhile, but I press the tofu in the fridge overnight and then dust with 2 teaspoons of tomato powder. Variations on a theme I suppose.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Has anyone tried roasting on parchment instead of foil?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: If you use the temperature and times in the recipe you’ll end up with a pile of charcoal so reduce the temperature and keep an eye on it.  Other people have commented that it was burning well before the time was over.  If it hadn’t been burnt I would have given it five stars.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Used one block of tofu, pressed it for about 10-15 minutes and then tore it into small pieces by hand. Cut up the red onion and roasted it with the tofu with a full recipe of seasonings. Roasted it for the full amount of time, but it never got crispy, probably due to the onion. Made a full recipe of avocado crema (but no more than 2 T lime juice), could have used more. But it was all quite tasty.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Made this last night, and it was a hit with the family. I think next time I will crumble the tofu instead of grating it because, even using the wide holes on the grater, it became too fine. The crumbles are also a great topping for a salad the next day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: My version was burnt to a crisp. Oops used extra firm tofu. I’ll try again but cut the baking time in half.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!  I halved the recipe and combined it with the NYT shrimp tacos.  We ate the crisps alone in the taco and mixed them with the shrimp.  Great both ways.  We also did a red cabbage quick pickle and added that.  Yum!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Outstanding recipe that's easy to make. I probably tripled the spices called for and it was nice but certainly not overwhelming. I did add shredded carrot and sliced lettuce and that worked well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: IDK who’s getting their mix of oil and tomato paste into anything like “drizzle”. I put the paste/oil mix (oily paste) into a mixing bowl, stirred in the tofu and put it back on the sheets to finish. Otherwise a tasty taco

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was excellent!  I halted the recipe which gave us four generous tacos with a bit leftover.  I took the advice of the commenters and mixed the tofu with the spicy in a bowl and the same with the tomato paste.  Really a great new way to eat tofu.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Can you use parchment instead of foil or not line the pans?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I’m on a high protein low-carb diet so I made these with ground chicken and they were fantastic. I used egg life wraps as a tortilla.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I made this. It tasted delicious.i would crumble the tofu ne t time as the tofu got too crunchy . I will definitely make it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Big hit! Due to construction, I only have a toaster oven, so I tried to speed up the cooking by using super convection. The first tray got a bit overdone, but I did better on the second one. I mixed the two together and it actually turned out great, some more tender and some really crispy. I used red cabbage instead of radishes because that's what I had. The  avocado cream really made the dish! My husband gave it two thumbs up.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Loved this recipe!! Will go in regular rotation for family of vegetarians. My notes: No need to grate tofu - crumble after squeezing out moisture. Use heavy duty tin foil - tofu crisps and sticks a bit. Don’t forget to stir and rotate pans. Combine all tofu onto one pan after coating with tomato paste mixture so it browns evenly. Half a recipe of avocado créma was plenty. Make this!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Folks, we can do this easier. Instead of aluminum foil & energy consumption of 400 degree oven for almost 1 hour & running air filter full speed to take the smell out of the house - far better solution is to shred/make "pulled" royal trumpet mushrooms, sautee in skillet, add great bbque sauce, touch of mustard, hint of 100% pomegranate syrup, a little shoyu - easy & superb as main dish, sandwich, slider, taco - far better mouth feel/texture & healthier too w mushroom immune boosting benefits.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was easy to prepare! I followed the tips in the other comments - using a bowl to toss the tofu with the oil and seasonings in Step 2 and and with the tomato paste and oil in Step 4. The only minor issue I had was that after Step 3, the tofu had stuck together and came out as a sheet - all the tofu pieces stuck together. Could this be prevented by drizzling the oil and seasonings instead? Also, for Step 4, the tofu fit on one sheet pan, so I wonder if I could use one pan instead of two.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: instructions on how to convert tortillas to tacos please

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Donelle W these are soft tacos.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I made this last night and it was absolutely delicious! I can’t wait to share it with my family and friends! I will definitely be making this again!! Give it a try. You will love it too!💚🥰

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Amazing. Cut recipe in half but otherwise followed exactly other than adding a little umami seasoning. Crisped perfectly before tossing with tomato paste and olive oil mixture. I didn't use it in tacos - I added it to a pot of chili. I usually use homemade veggie chorizo (made with dry soy crumbles) in chili so looking forward to trying it tomorrow, but of course quality controlled the tofu today - awesome!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was a straightforward recipe that was easy to make. I followed the directions for making 1/2 of the original recipe (since I was eating alone). Will keep this recipe to make when I want a meatless night.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I never understand the "drizzle" instructions. Why not put whatever it is that is being drizzled into a bowl and pour the oil or whatever over it and stir and then put the items on the sheet pan? Is there some benefit to drizzling versus another approach?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Flavor was fine but I didn’t care for the chewy texture.  I would shorten the cooking time if I made it again but I don’t think it’s worth the effort. The avocado sauce was quite nice.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I won't make this again. Too much work for not a lot of payoff

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: My meat eating men actually LOVED this.  Even without pretending it wasn't tofu...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This is my new vegan taco recipe.   No other holds a candle to this one.  The texture is outstanding

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
I cook from the New York Times cooking app almost every day. This is where I find my recipes and I find the comments to be quite helpful.

This is the first recipe I’ve made that we literally could not eat, and I had to throw out. It was disgusting on so many levels and hard to execute.
1. The tomato paste and oil was not exactly drizzle-able.
2. There was way too much cumin.
3. The texture of the tofu was very difficult to chew.

unless you’re adventurous, or you’re interested in course, correcting after doing it wrong the first time, steer clear of this recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: liked it.  very good. ovens are different, just sharing for mine set on 400F - 25 mins on initial tofu cook, then add the paste/EVOO mixture until "crackling" worked better.  I try to cook tofu 2-3 mins longer than my "eyes tell me" when I check the sheet to really get the crunchy texture.  for my pallette - season aggressively, maybe increasing the paprika, onion, garlic ratios in the seasoning mix - and the cayenne if one is not heat adverse, to give a bit more kick to the flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Really really good! I shortcut the cooking time by pressing the tofu quite a bit and then microwaving it for a couple of minutes (which I always do when I panfy). This way I only baked it for 20 and then 10 with the tomato oil mixture and it came out extra crispy. Quick pickled radishes and thin sliced shallot as a topper and it was perfect!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I recommend not using foil lined pan.  The tofu just stuck to the foil.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This is so much work for not much flavor.  Great texture but that is it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
I used one block of tofu and kept other measurements the same and it worked out very well. I felt it would have been too bland with twice the tofu to the spices. Agree with others that "drizzling" tomato paste and oil is impossible so it's best to just toss it all together in a bowl and then spread back on the sheet pan.

I used Trader Joe's super firm tofu which grated easily. I want to experiment with different tofus and getting different textures when grated.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: If you toss the tofu wirh the oil and tomato paste in a bowl first you can get away with using less oil.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Super firm tofu is the way to go here. Less water, holds its shape better, and got crispy quicker. We really loved these and will make often for Meatless Monday!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: *I* liked these, alot - but my husband (TBH, the least adventurous eater ever) did not like them at all, wouldn't even finish them. 20yo son (who is slightly more adventurous) ate them but said he prefers ground meat. So--if I make this again, I"ll plan on it being just for me. Instead of putting them in tacos, I put mine in a bowl with rice and beans and the fixins-turned out well. I did use parchment paper on one tray and thought that worked better than foil-although it got crispier, quicker.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Next time I will bake the tofu less than directions called for because it was starting to resemble overcooked bacon with not much flavor. I had tasted along the way and it was better a little earlier. And I will double the avocado crema, yum.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and easy to make! I followed the suggestion of mixing the tofu with tomato in a bowl instead of drizzling. I’m also going to try this tofu on top of nachos,

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I made the mistake of leaving the tofu in the oven after I turned it off to keep it warm.  Don't do it! It actually got too crispy and the tomato paste made some parts burned.  Still, I made black beans with it and enjoyed it.  Next time I will be sure not to overdo it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Excellent.  Used one block of tofu but used most of the seasoning.  Mixed the tofu both times in a bowl to better coat it.  Even good a few days later warmed up in the microwave.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This turned out great and will make again. Used extra firm tofu.  Definitely monitor the baking progress to achieve the crisp level you want.  The stated times were too long for my oven. Tomato paste mixture certainly doesn’t drizzle.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: highly recommend using parchment paper on the sheet pan instead of foil. The tofu stuck to the foil, which made it difficult to "toss" during cooking

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I pulled this together on a weeknight. Such a treat!  I didn’t bother squeezing water out of the tofu. I actually would’ve baked the filling a little less, because some parts were baked too hard. The filling did stick to the foil, so I had to carefully pry it off when it was time to add the tomato paste. By that point, the tofu shrank down enough that I put it all on one sheet pan to finish. I kinda want to try doubling the filling while still only using two sheet pans; one can never have too many tacos. Great flavor overall. I’d love to try adding some diced pineapple sometime or even experimenting with a tofu al pastor using this technique. I appreciate having just enough lime juice leftover for a margarita — very considerate of you, Alexa. Tejal Rao’s Arroz Verde was a great accompaniment for this recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Relatively easy recipe but the tofu itself was meh.  I accidentally put in more lime zest and juice in the avocado cream and that was delicious.  I’ll make this again because I like tofu and I like tacos but it’s not something I’d serve to anyone else.  Maybe I’ll try to pickle the onions and radishes next time and up the cayenne.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Simply did not like this recipe at all. Several adjustments could be made to improve. Avoid small chunks of tofu, bump up the spices, add salt to the avocado cream (or as others have said, don’t bother with the cream - just make guacamole). Even with theses adjustments, I won’t take the time to make it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I don't cook tofu regularly, but thought I would try this as my family tries to eat vegetarian once a week and I need some new dishes in the rotation. With the exception of tossing the tofu in a bowl initially to ensure more even coverage of oil and spices, I followed the recipe.  This did NOT come out well for me. The tofu did not achieve the coloring or texture described.  By the time it browned nicely, the tofu had the texture of what my husband aptly described as Bacos - very bad.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
We made this tonight and were extremely pleased with the way the tofu crisped up. It was very flavorful and very reminiscent of cooked ground meat.
We served it as a "taco" salad rather than tacos, to eliminate the bread. The avocado crema was a great alternative to plain sour cream, and we added tomatoes, lettuce, black olives and peppers to the recipe. Will definitely make this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I was excited to make this after all the rave reviews. I don't know if I did something wrong (I followed the instructions!), but it was really disappointing and a lot of work for little payoff. The tofu was flavorless, some of it was burnt to a crisp, some of it not crispy at all. The creme was okay. I quick pickled red onions and radishes, served with cilantro and roasted cauliflower on the side. Overall, lacked flavor and crunch and I would not make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made these twice now. First time exactly as written and they were more crunchy than crispy. Probably because the grated crumbles were too small and I used the convection setting on my oven- don’t recommend! Second time I crumbled the tofu by hand, intentionally leaving some pieces larger than others. Both times I took the advice of mixing the oil and spices in a bowl however in my haste the second time around I completely forgot to add the spices after the oil and soy sauce before the first bake. I added them in the tomato paste step- just whisked the spices, paste and oil in a large bowl, tossed the  baked tofu in, back on the sheet pans and in the oven for the final step. It was just as flavorful as if added before the initial bake. The varied size of the crumbles and baking without convection resulted in a crispy, chewy, tiny bit crunchy texture that was so satisfying! Used Greek yogurt instead of mayo in the crema and it was delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious! I will definitely make this again. I added about 1/4 cup of potato starch along with the spices. Also just finely crumbled the tofu instead of grating. The result was extra crispy and delicious! 

As a native Texan and lover of good Tex Mex, I was dubious about the avocado sauce and tempted to make plain guacamole instead, but decided to try the sauce. I have to say, I thought it was better for this dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I just crumbled the tofu instead of grating and worked wonderfully.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I used extra firm and just hand crumbled the tofu, no need for a grater! Great recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Soooooo Yummy. I crumbled the tofu rather than grate. It was practical to toss the tofu in a bowl in step 2. Reuse the bowl with the tomato paste and oil in step 4, toss again. Could try broiling for a few mins at the end to crisp up even more?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: this is delicious over bowls and salads as well. depending on the pan and placement in your oven, you can even take a little time off this recipe. my tofu came out a little extra crispy the first time, but since then I've adjusted the time by about 4 min and it comes out perfectly. do NOT overfill your pans or your tofu won't get crispy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Use sour cream instead of mayo. For a vegan version just whip avocado with the lime juice and that’s it. In a small food processor. Delicious and easy and no sub required.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I don’t know what I did wrong here but it was borderline burnt and basically inedible. Will try again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: My kids and I agree that this is a game-changer! I find this recipe very adaptable, too: I press fresh garlic into the tomato paste/oil mixture (instead of garlic powder and onion powder), and use pico de gallo with diced avocado instead of the avocado cream. Scrumptious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: A fantastic recipe!  The crispy tofu was exceptional.  I might make it again for any recipe that can use a meat free crumble, and will certainly make it again for this recipe.  The tomato paste treatment resulted in next-level taco "meat".  Yum!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
This is great and we will make it again.
I was absolutely terrified of the amount of seasoning called for, but it worked.
We drizzled the tomato paste/oil combo directly on the sheet pans as called for, and had no trouble getting it integrated.
However, the serving size is way off. We ate 10 tacos heaped with the tofu, and didn't even get through half of it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I halve the recipe to make 2 ample dinner portions. I’ve made it about 5 times already but am I missing something? The recipe calls for 2 TBS of soy sauce. 1 TBS is used to roast the tofu. But where is the other 1 TBS used? Am I having a senior moment?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
The recipe calls for 2 pans so the olive oil and soy sauce are split evenly between them.
"Coarsely grate the drained tofu onto two large (11-by-17-inch), foil-lined sheet pans. (The tofu will crumble chaotically, and that is fine!) Drizzle the tofu in each pan with 2 tablespoons olive oil and 1 tablespoon soy sauce. "

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @Pookie I had to read the recipe 8 times to get it. Written very strangely. But delicious recipe

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Made this for some experienced vegans who marveled over the crisp tofu texture! Looks and feels just like meat. Followed all directions as provided except we cut the red onion into very thin  slices, for more visual interest and mouthfeel. Also, I did not use a grater for the tofu… after squeezing it, it was easy enough just to hand-crumble it onto the baking pan. All of us loved this recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
This was delicious. My suggestion:

Sub harissa paste for the tomato paste for more heat and umami. Remove the tofu from the pan and mix it with the harissa and olive oil in a bowl before putting it back onto the pan and into the oven for 10-15 min.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: The only changes I made is that I pickled the vegetables (red onion, carrot and jalapeño) and that I used other's suggestions to mix the tofu with the spices and tomato paste/oil mixture in a bowl. Awesome meal!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Fabulous recipe! Pickling the onions and radishes is the way to go. Also topped with a bit of cotija. Yum! Adding to my rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: These sound amazing. Any suggestions on how to swap the tofu for cauliflower? (Cooking time variations, etc)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious-do take the hint to mix the spice and tomato paste mixture in a bag with the tofu.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Maybe a bowl? A lot of plastic in the earth and sea already . . . But a bowl would probably allow mixing evenly and address the issue you're raising . . .

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
The recipe tastes like tacos al pastor (made with pork), That recipe has a hint of achiote and pineapple. I prefer serving with guacamole instead of the purée and caramelized onions.

I get corn street taco tortillas. It taste great if you put a small amount of oil on each tortilla with a small pinch of salt on each side. Then toast the taco on a pan or skillet and place on a baking rack. This way each element of the dish is seasoned.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
Recipe is similar to taco salad Pastor made with pork, a hint of achiote and pineapple. Consider topping with caramelized onions and lime juice.

For the tortillas, I get corn street tortillas. I put oil and salt on each side of the tortilla before placing them on a skillet to warm up.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This is almost identical to a recipe I make, except the liquid from a can of chipotle in adobo is added to the mayo. It's a divine sauce.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Loved these tacos and recipe! I recommend not halving the recipe. After the tofu roasts, there’s not as much as I started with. Still good though! Chopped red onion, radish and some cotija cheese makes it extra special.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Rubbery, more work and longer on a hot oven than necessary. Using same spice mix, would be tastier with tempeh crumbled and pan-fried. Deleted from my recipe box!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I have grated tofu plenty of times and roasted tofu plenty of times but never thought to combine grating and roasting. Duh! Can’t wait to try this technique and this recipe. It sounds delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: My meat eating husband love these!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This was actually too crispy and the tomato paste charred during the instructed cooking time.  I will try this again using a shorter cooking time and skipping the tomato paste, oil toss.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Quick question: what’s the best way to warm the tortillas for this recipe?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious. We did mix everything together in a bowl before putting on baking sheets covered with parchment paper and a veggie spray. As well added toppings to the finished tacos: thinly sliced red cabbage and chopped fresh tomatoes along with a mango salsa. The textures and flavors were perfect!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
Made this last night and the tofu mixture is very yummy. I will be using this method with varying spices  as part of the regular rotation. 

The tofu didn’t really get crispy, either because it wasn’t sufficiently drained or wasn’t cooked long enough. But the texture was fine. I used a shallow baking pan rather than a sheet pan so that might have contributed. 

Overall a keeper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Perfection ! I served on almond flour tortillas from Trader Joe’s with garlicky collard greens . Delicious and easy !

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Big fail, and a waste if time. The tomato paste ruined this dish. You end up with way more crema than needed for the minuscule amount of ground tofu this produces.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I mean they were ok but 5 stars? I followed the recipe exact. The crunch of the tofu was nice but other than acting as a vessel for spices, had nothing else going for it. I’m not a big meat eater but honestly ground beef would have been better.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: what happens with the other half of the dry spice mixture (cumin,paprika etc)?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: gsil, the recipe uses two sheet pans, with half of the spices on each. I had to reread it. 😊

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody:
Yum!
I didn't have avocado, so mixed some of Kroger's Chile Lime adobo seasoning in a little mayo instead of the avocado. I also added a little to the tofu for fun. Delicious. There were also no radishes at the store, so I topped with shredded cabbage, tomato, and red onion. Next time I will use flour tortillas so they don't break. 
I smashed the tofu instead of grating.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: delicious! Thank you.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: This is a delicious recipe. We are trying to eat less meat, so more of these accessible tofu recipes would be appreciated.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I substituted ground pork for the tofu and these were excellent!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Made as described, delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Really delicious! I love tofu but had never tried grating it. The texture and seasoning are perfect for the tacos and the avocado cream is great match. As others have pointed out, it's not easy to mix tomato paste and oil, but it doesn't really matter since you're just going to toss them with the tofu anyway.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: very tasty and fairly easy to pull together.  the avocado spread helps secure all the tasty crunchy bits inside the taco while lending a creamy, bright flavour.  Besides the toppings listed, we also added shredded lettuce, cheddar cheese, and jalapeños.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: My 6 year old loved these! I skipped the tomato paste mixture on one tray, so we had “two fl

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Unbelievable. “A spiritual experience.” - my wife.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Every time a new-to-me summery sounding recipe pops up, and it’s summer, I become filled with rage when it tells me to roast something. Who roasts in summer????? Oh it’s 100 degrees lemme turn my oven on

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: @eliza383 *raises hand*

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Made as directed, other than subbing ground chipotle for the cayenne. Turned out really well and the kids have asked me to keep it in the rotation (highest possible praise)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: I really enjoyed this, although I feel it was a little lack luster. My hubby LOVED it.  I know this may not be the crowd, but if anyone bulk preps protein for the week, this is a great and fairly easy vegetarian option.  Tacos, taco salad, enchiladas, on toast with eggs and cheese.  Lots of possibilities. I will add that I feel you could take some oil out of the recipe.  And I felt the tomato paste mixture was a bit hard to incorporate as well - maybe I’ll try tomato sauce with a tablespoon of paste next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Crispy Tofu Tacos Recipe
commentBody: Our kids really loved this recipe. Would add a saltier component like a grilled cotija cheese next time as well
