recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: The cashew/tofu cream added protein, I guess, but didn't add much flavor.  I served the broccoli with quinoa, and the cream was a nice complement to the quinoa.  I'm glad to have another use for tofu, for which I am struggling to develop a taste... I'll probably make this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Adding lemon to the tofu-cashew cream really made this dish shine. I don’t feel the seasoning on the broccoli as written did a lot, so I’d probably just steam/roast that next time plain and then focus on bringing seasoning via the crisp and cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I made as written and was disappointed.  I found the cashew cream to be very sweet and not complementary with the roasted broccoli and chili crisp.  I did roast the broccoli longer and uncovered to get it crispy.  I guess not every recipe is a star.  My husband liked it more.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
i too used 1.5c cashews pulverized
the cashew cream was very bland - i added some garlic, miso and sesame oil
i like cold and hot so that was good with me
i too roasted rather than steamed the broccoli

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Stellar recipe! Skipped the foil. Made cashew butter per Daniel since we had roasted cashews on hand. Will try the tahini and the peanut butter options in future, for sure. Had no broccoli leftover from dinner for two, but did have extra cashew cream and crisp topping, so we'll roast a head of cauliflower and finish those off for another delicious dinner! Also, thinking that making the tofu/nut cream might be possible using a stick blender if you were starting with a nut butter (fewer dishes!)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Loved it! The broccoli texture was perfect, although I would like a bit more browning. I even tried to cut the broccoli into large flat sections and had those sitting against the pan, and put the tray on the lower rack to get extra heat applied to the bottom. For the cashew sauce, I blended soaked cashews and this worked great. I added 1/4 to 1/2 cup of nutritional yeast into the blender for a cheese-like flavor (fantastic add).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Surprisingly wonderful for what is basically roasted broccoli. Do not skip the cashew butter fluff, which could be repurposed many ways for other vegan dishes. The fluff is a bit bland out of the box, so I added a 1/4 tsp. of maple syrup to make it more cream-like. Will make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
This was amazing. I had to make some substitutions based on what I had in my pantry.
For the cashew cream:
- replaced cashew butter with one cup of soaked cashews, and added  60ml each of tamari & lemon juice plus 15ml tahini
-used regular tofu instead of silken (plus 30ml water)
For the panko topping:
- I used chopped roasted (salted) pistachios (used up all my cashews on the cream!), and skipped the added salt
Regrets:
-roasting the broccoli in a "tin foil tent" leaves them soft, not crisp

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I made this as directed and am baffled by the 5-star reviews. All flavor is from the chili crunch, very little other flavor or complexity (2 tsps of soy for 2 heads of broccoli??) - it felt like it needed some real acidity. All the butter, oil, nuts and breadcrumbs add lots of unnecessary fat to a meal that would taste the same if you just baked tofu and broccoli and put chili crisp on top (a fraction of the work, time, dishes, and calories).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I have a slight sensitivity to cashews so I subbed in some pecans and almond butter. Already had my chili crisp made. It was really good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: The broccoli is delicious, but as others have noted, I would not cover the pan with foil to steam. Leave it open as is typical for veggie roasting! I used a 2 lb. bag of broccoli florets from Costco for this recipe and did not make other adjustments. I made the cashew butter and silken tofu mousse, and it is plenty to coat 1 lb. of pasta. I made 1 lb. of  spaghetti, tossed the mousse with it, and topped it with the broccoli. Amazing!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This is the worst NYT recipe I have tried (and I’ve made dozens). I’m so confused by the positive reviews.  The cashew cream made me quite nauseous.  The broccoli was ok. The combo was just… odd.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Per other reviews, I don’t bother with tenting the broccoli, but otherwise followed the recipe exactly, and this has come out great. Served with pasta. I also made 1/3 the cashew cream the second time I made it, and that was way more proportional (and less expensive).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I did not care for this recipe at all, despite liking the individual components. However, as someone else suggested, I had this as an "avocado toast" style dinner: toasted bread with the tofu spread as a base and the broccoli and chili crisp topping on top. It was really good this way! I recommend serving it on toast - it also makes it feel more like a meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
Highly recommend this recipe!  My husband and I are obsessed with the chili crisp topping and have been adding it to other veggies…roasted zucchini, potatoes…and sometimes just eating it straight from the bowl!
I did not make the cashew cream base, but instead served it with plain Strauss Greek yogurt on the side, which cooled the heat of the chili crisp when needed.
I like Fly by Jing chili crisp which is available on Amazon and in some stores.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Awesome plant power! This was excellent. The cashew tofu cream dead simple to make and such a nice change of pace; light but indulgent feeling. (I simply blended raw cashews with the tofu + water until it had a whipped texture. 1 min max.) And the "gravel" on top, as my kids called it, was a huge hit. (We added the chilli crisp at the table so the spice adverse didn't have to have it.)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I loved this—any other silken tofu recipes folks recommend?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This is one of my favorite NYT recipes. A showstopper. I recently made it with sunflower butter and sunflower seeds instead of cashews/cashew butter because my dinner guest has a nut allergy. It was excellent that way too!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Literally obsessed with this recipe. I made it a couple of weeks ago and I’m craving it again. As others have suggested, I just made my own cashew butter. It’s so easy to do and you already have to have the food processor out so why not.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This is the way vegetables should be. I always thought I didn’t like vegetables, but turns out, it was just how they were cooked for me as a kid (dumped out of a can and microwaved). This recipe deserves its five stars. I’d only recommend heating the cream before serving. I made cashew butter from scratch using Cookie and Kate’s recipe. The key was to just keep letting it blend in the food processor until it finally turned creamy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was crazy good!  Could be a side at a high end restaurant.  Did two subs out of necessity as I couldn’t find silken tofu at the grocery store and decided not to drop ten bucks on a container of cashew butter. Instead I used soft tofu and a cup of cashews soaked in hot water for 15 min before draining and using. It whipped up beautifully.   I love crispy roasted broccoli so I decided not to seal it as others have said. I guess that’s three subs. Lots going on here and roasted cashew/panko/chili crisp belongs on more things!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Grilled the broccoli and served with grilled swordfish.  In the top 10 dishes I've ever made.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
immediately make this! 
I used broccolini and it was a hit. the chili crisp topping will definitely be seeing other dishes in the near future. I had to add much more water than the recipe called for to get the desired consistency, but it was no bother. only made half the quantity and I was still plenty. We loved it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was fantastic!  My two small tweaks were to add a pinch of sugar to the cashew crisp mixture and to take the foil off the broccoli while it’s crisp tender and broil it for a few minutes to get golden brown edges.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I had a little trouble whipping the tofu. At first, I only added 3 tablespoons of cashew butter, which may be why, along with maybe too much liquid. I saw someone’s note about lime juice and that was nice. I added nutritional yeast to thicken it up and then finally put a total of 7 tablespoons of cashew butter in. Put it in the refrigerator & the texture was pretty good after that. Next time I would cut back on the water, add more lime juice (only put in little over a tablespoon), but this was really really good and a big hit!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This made lots of leftover sauce. Did anyone repurpose it in an interesting way? I’m thinking of doing a riff on sesame noodles

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Used walnuts because I had no cashews, used yogurt as I had no cashew butter. Seriously easy and delicious!  And yes! Don’t cover the broccoli. It’s really does roast wonderfully when it’s just sitting on parchment paper. Yum.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: or cover a roasting pan with a cookie sheet. I appreciate your comment @Mel Harte

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: The broccoli was great, but the cashew cream was bland, as others have noted, and my kids hated the texture.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: My husband made this and we loved the taste. The whipped tofu with cashew butter was surprisingly light and the crumble topping deliciously spiced. I think the broccoli needed to cook longer. The recipe used a lot of pans and we had major clean up (not fun). Not sure how to store or heat leftovers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Make own cashew butter with roasted cashews in food processor, add oil (and salt if cashews not salted). Make extra crunch topping.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Very tasty meatless Monday dinner. I used a clean-out-the-fridge combo of cauliflower, Brussels sprouts and broccoli, roasted uncovered. As someone suggested I soaked raw cashews in boiling water. When I blended them with the tofu I added a little soy sauce and chili crisp and only 4 T of water. To do it again I would add more chili crisp and only 2-3 T of water. The crispy topping was delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Don’t have cashews so substituted peanut butter and peanuts.  Also tried air frying the broccoli.  I would definitely recommend the broccoli and chili crisp as a side dish.  I enjoyed the whipped tofu but not sure I like these two as a combo.  All in all good flavors just not sure I would serve them together.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I made this exactly as indicated (using Genevieve Ko's chile crisp recipe on this site) and it is delicious. But, yes, as stated in the blurb above the recipe, the broccoli will be steamed and soft, not crispy. This isn't a problem.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I didn't have enough cashews on hand, but I did have pistachios so I added those. Really nice alternative!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I cut the broccoli into larger sizes than illustrated, crowded the roasting pan slightly, and I did not wrap them in foil. This took a bit longer to cook, but I really liked the results: not steamy and not overly-crisp broccoli! Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Good. Used peanut butter instead of cashew butter. Would probably make it again though it needs more of an umami taste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I tried to make my own cashew butter in the Vitamix but it wasn’t enough volume for the blender to do anything but chop. I decided to add the tofu and water in to the blender and it worked beautifully! This still tasted too much like tofu for my taste so I added in some chili crisp, garlic powder, five spice powder and msg to the cashew cream, it was great! It really came together beautifully paired with broccoli, in fact I’m not sure my additions were necessary. Also this seemed to make way more cashew cream than necessary, I’m excited to put the remaining cream over noodles but I think I could get away with a half recipe of the cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Very good. Agree with other commenters, roasted broccoli better than steamed as written. Also, cashew cream needed dressing up —I used a little garlic, coriander and tamarind. Very filling.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Maybe this is great for vegans but for me it was a flavorless, gloopy mess. I can’t believe this is so highly reviewed!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious and is an impressive presentation when complete. I swapped out peanuts and peanut butter for the cashews due to an allergy and loved it. I brought it to a party and we all agreed, the peanuts probably worked better with the other flavors of the dish. Would recommend!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: It was good but too much cream for the broccoli. I would make about half as much for the amount of broccoli and chili crisp topping.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
I added red onion and roasted with the broccoli- it’
 Soaks up the flavors and caramelizes and tastes absolutely phenomenal!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: We did this over a bed of soba noodles. I added half a lemon to the creamy whipped tofu. Would probably be better and easier with tahini instead of cashews. We felt the dish was bland and didn’t have a very dynamic flavor profile

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
Very good, some recommended modifications:
I roasted broccoli (no foil) and used way more garlic (chopped coarsely) with the broccoli. Recipe needs WAY MORE BROCCOLI- I would say and least double if not 3x. 
I added roasted garlic (one head) and lemon juice to the cashew/tofu mix (doesn’t need much water at all, suggested amount would have been too much).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Yum! I used a bag of frozen broccoli since I had it on hand. Turned out great! We ate it as an afternoon snack and have enough leftovers to make another helping on another day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I left out the panko to make it GF. Added garlic and a whole lemon’s worth of juice to the cashew cream. It was SO delicious and a big hit with omnivores and vegetarians alike.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This is a fantastic recipe! I loved the tofu cashew cream, however, there was a lot of it and now I am not sure what to do with it. Any creative ideas for how to incorporate it into other dishes or snacks?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: A touch of lemon depending on your preference really lifts up the dish! If you are cooking recipe in half, half tsp of fresh lemon (in whipped tofu and cashew mixture) works great. The dip works so well in fall weather when you oven roast delicata or acorn squash and serve it with this on top. Consistency can be adjusted to your taste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Loved this! A new fave! I broiled the broccoli for a few minutes before topping on the tofu cream. I also drizzled with a bit of sesame oil.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I love this recipe, and appreciate the comments. Roasting the broccoli loosely covered with foil has become my favorite for most dishes. The whipped tofu cashew cream is terribly bland, and absolutely blossoms with the addition of garam masala.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
I can't believe NYT limits the lengths of comments.

TLDR - Great potential with some modifications. Lose the top layer of foil, so you actualy roast rather than steam (add garlic towards end). Buy raw cashews (pre-roasted increases burning risk).

Use a normal food processor - I dirtied a mini and a Vitamix before I grabbed the Cuisinart (maybe because my tofu was 16 oz?).

Cashew cream needs some acid (I used lemon) and more salt than you think.  I wish the recipe specified a baseline.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Addition to note--Didn't use the tofu/cashew cream because I didn't have the ingredients.  The broccoli was fine without.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I am allergic to cashew:( Is there an alternative to cashew butter for this recipe?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: cashews are pretty neutral in flavor and thus preferred in some recipes, but any nut or seed butter should work if you are ok with the taste of it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Would it work to use almond butter or peanut butter instead of cashew butter?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Cant do Tofu would love to try this with white beans or cauliflower. Thouhts?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Loved this! Will be making peanut noodles with my extra chile crisp. What should I do with the extra cashew tofu whip?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Seems like a college dorm recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I would change the tofu to cashew ratio on the cream, adding a tablespoon of nut butter or whole nuts at a time. I also added lots of black pepper, coconut aminos as it was a little too sweet and flat. Also think rice vinegar would brighten up the sauce.  Also, if you wanted to further lighten it up, the chili crisp   panko gives a good crisp on its own… no need to double cashew for the topping.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Between the cashew butter (31 grams per cup) and the silken tofu (7grams per 7 ounce package), and the broccoli (17.5 grams per 1 1/2 lbs) and the 1/2c cashews (10 grams), there is around 65 grams of protien in the entire dish. Silken tofu is not protien rich. There is not even half of the protien the recipe states in this dish, per serving.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I’m making this now.  I just checked the nutritional info and it shows 50g of carbs?  From a 1/3 cup of panko?  I’m not sure the data is accurate.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was good, but extremely rich with all the oil and cashew butter. I think I'd prefer this as a side dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: So, so good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
This is a bad recipe and an equally bad dish. 

Roasting broccoli in a foil bag for 15 minutes produced broccoli I didn’t want to eat. Why not simply roast it on a baking tray for 16-18 minutes and enjoy crispy oven roasted broccoli? 

Then dropping this unappetizing broccoli onto a bed of unheated (rather bland) cashew butter diluted with silken tofu (cold from the fridge) instantly rendered it cold and negated whatever enjoyment was left in it. 

The spicy bread crumbs and cashew mixture wasn’t bad and indeed might be delicious sprinkled on tomato soup, but it couldn’t save the slop that was this dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Delicious recipe, but have about two cups of cashew cream remaining.  Are there other recipes that the cream can be used?  Thanks for your ideas.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Sub it for heavy cream in tomato soup! Otherwise there are several vegan recipes on this site that use cashew cream. They don't usually have tofu, but it will be fine with the tofu in it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Sounds great.  Wondering if a base of hummus (with the cashew butter -- love cashews) would work for those of us allergic to soy.  Thinking I could substitute coconut aminos for the soy sauce in the recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Would a stick blender work for the creamy base layer if using nut butter or tahini (not nuts) and tofu?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: @Ginnykate. YES!  I used one in a steep bowl in the sink because of potential splatter but it blended quite well!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Sorry for the strange comment but does anyone have success roasting broccoli without totally stinking up the house? I think I am going to give and cook it another way.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Any suggestions for a substitute for the tofu? I'm not a fan.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: You could put it over hummus instead of making the cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I’m going to be that person and ask a really stupid question. My son won’t touch broccoli (or cauliflower or mushrooms), but will eat almost any other vegetable. Any ideas on a different veggie I could roast in this recipe? Maybe squash?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: @Erin sunchokes, carrots, baby turnips or radishes would all do well with this recipe.  I also think eggplant would be a great alternative

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Any thoughts on a substitution for the tofu? My better half can't do soy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: There is a fava bean tofu so not made from soy, Big Mountain brand. This tofu is pretty sturdy though, so you might want to use half a block and add more water to achieve the spreadable consistency? Otherwise, my first thought was Greek yogurt with less water (or maybe even no water, depending on how thick the mixture would be), if you do not mind the tang. Sour cream might be ok (I would skip the water in this case or add maybe a tiny bit at the time) but it will also have that tang. Good luck!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Am I the only person who doesn't know what chile crisp is? Please explain.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Cooking on aluminum foil is really ill advised for your health.  Just roast the broccoli on a sheet pan.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: What could substitute for cashews for a nut allergy? Would just leaving the nuts out be ok?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: use miso and tahini if not allergic to sesame seeds? it would give a nice flavor

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Broccoli got cold while I finished the other elements…possibly use bigger chunks (some got smaller than 1”), have the other elements ready sooner.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Made this delicious dish for dinner tonight. Liked by all. The box of tofu we routinely have available is 12.3oz whereas the recipe calls for 14 oz. That .88 reduction translates to 7oz of nut butter. I only had almond and tahini butter available so I used a 50/50 mix. Delicious and impressive looking. Even with that reduction we ended up with about a cup of extra tofu nut cream. Next time I’d go with 1/2 a recipe of the cream. To cook the broccoli quickly I zapped it in the microwave before finishing by roasting uncovered in the oven.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Wow! This is so good and so easy. I'll definitely be making it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
My wife is a vegetarian so I am always appreciative of these recipes. We are both busy working so I made with the time I had and these were my hacks: par steamed broccoli as well as 4 teeth of garlic to speed process along and as I did not want tough texture; substituted pecans and added pecans to tofu mixture. We don't generally have panko on hand so that go sacrificed. Salted once served as needed a little "wake up." 

Wonderfully filling and good. Thanks again

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I’ve never understood recipes that call for putting vegetable on a sheet pan, drizzling them with oil, then slinging them all over the kitchen.  I put the broccoli or whatever in a large enough bowl so that I can add the oil and soy sauce, cover with a plate and give it a good shake-shake-shake.  E voíla, ready for the oven.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious but we made a few tweaks. Cooked 1.5 times the broccoli and made 1.5 times the crumbs. I'd just roast the broccoli at 425 uncovered next time. There was a LOT of the cashew cream and it's very rich so I just served it on the side so we could add as much or as little as wanted. Served with pitas and it made a delicious wrap.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Blanch the broccoli then follow directions sans covering the pan with foil. Bangarang.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I served it without the cashew cream as a side to saucy macaroni and cheese and my kids and I were happy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I had tahini on hand so used that instead of cashew butter. The resulting cream was incredibly rich. A dollop on top would suffice for many people. And ditto, don't cover the broccoli in the oven.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This exceeded my expectations, loved it!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I riffed on this recipe to create a fish dish last night. After 15 min I opened up the broccoli packet and spread it out on the sheet pan. Nestled 2 Basa inside the halo of broccoli and returned the pan to the oven. I seasoned the fish with S&P and soy sauce and flipped them half way through an 8 minute bake. Topped with breadcrumb/cashew mixture. Delish!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Would make again.  Followed recipe as is.  Question:  had a lot of left over cashew butter & tofu mixture.  What else can I use it with or in?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Made exactly to recipe and it was amazing. We rarely have a hot vegetarian dinner and I only mention that because if any of you eat similarly, you’ll be surprised at how deeply satiating this meal is!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: The cashew fluff makes this heavenly, with the broccoli and toppings a great compliment.  I plan to try with roasted carrots

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Delicious, but used too many appliances....could have just used a mixer for the tofu cashew butter base. Made too much. I live alone, will eat for three meals.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This is an excellent recipe. It was quick and easy to make with straight forward instructions.  There is so much potential to modify this or use certain components of this recipe in different dishes. The chili crisp bread crumbs have so many other applications and is just delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is great if you have a peanut allergy, otherwise I would sub peanuts and peanut butter for the cashews. Definitely cheaper and in my opinion tastier.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I made only two servings, but had some cream and crunch left over. I thought this would make a wonderful alfredo. The cashew tofu cream and chili crisp topping is delicious on the noodles. I made the cashew cream with roasted unsalted cashews and added nothing to it. I simply processed it until it was a cream. I was impressed by how lovely it was. Such a beautiful color. I also prefer roasted more crispy broccoli.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
Delish! I decided to make my own chili oil because I have an excess of dried hot peppers from my garden this past summer. Great way to use them!
I loved everything about this dish, especially the chili/cashew crunch. Yum.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious and a hit. I was supposed to have leftovers.....
 Family members with high blood pressure means that I don't cook with salt if it's "just us" for dinner. This is one of the few recipes where I didn't taste the lack. Fantastic!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
I took the aluminum off for the last bit of baking to crisp up the broccoli. 

I also didn’t have cashew butter, so I used 1/4 cup of tahini and then the rest of the bag of cashews instead. I also added some nutritional yeast, lemon and pepper all to the whipped tofu.

Truly divine.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Made as follows: half a block of frozen, thawed and squeezed extra firm tofu with approx. 1/2 cup tahini, lemon juice from half a lemon and water to desired consistency. Roasted seasoned broccoli directly on the pan with no aluminum foil at all. Topping was sesame seeds, panko and some chili crisp fried in a bit of olive oil. Smeared the tofu/tahini paste on plates and drizzled with oil from my chili crisp jar. Put broccoli on top of smeared paste and topped with seed/chili crisp/panko topping.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: The dish as I made it was very pleasantly different from most I have eaten in my life, but the paste! It tasted really good with the chili crisp oil, so I added some of that oil to my leftovers, and just inhaled the rest of the paste, literally smearing it on my sandwich with some leftover baked fish. The paste as I made it was so, so good! Now, THAT PASTE is going in my rotation!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Also, I did not add any salt and I made the paste a bit in advance and let it sit on the counter to warm up to room temperature, for a total of about 1 hour before sitting down to dinner. Doing this to take the chill off the tofu paste worked like a charm, but the leftover paste was very tasty straight out of the fridge; the flavors married very nicely!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Thank you so much for posting this alteration! I didn't have cashew butter - and was concered about the outcome using firm tofu!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I see that some commenters may be confused about the silken tofu, and I can't blame them, because as of 3/5/25 it's not specified in the ingredients that this recipe most likely calls for the refrigerated silken tofu that indeed mostly comes in 14 oz. packages. I see that some commenters used 12 oz. silken tofu packages, but these are the shelf stable packs, and they themselves have a few options for consistency, like soft or extra firm shelf stable silken tofu. A different tofu for sure.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Loved this! I did not include the cashews in the topping, but made a lot & am still enjoying it (it was awesome on soft-boiled eggs this morning & leftover silken tofu for lunch). I do think that I will limit the water in the cashew cream - or maybe not include any - next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Made w chunked up romanesco fresh from the farmers market.  It was gorgeous!  I didn’t have cashews or cashew butter do replaced w peanuts & peanut butter. Served w rice noodles which I probably wouldn’t do again.  Loved the veggie part!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!  Per someone's helpful comment to soak the cashews in warm water, it worked like a charm.  I added some Chinese five spice and a dash of white pepper to the cashew-tofu cream and it was a nice compliment to the broccoli.  Definitely worth a try!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I was looking for a side dish and I had some broccoli, so I used this recipe as a starting point. I followed the broccoli part of the recipe, except I didn't have cashews, so I used peanuts. Instead of the cashew cream (which sounds delicious btw), I cooked some soba noodles and after cooking and rinsing tossed them in a small amount of sesame oil, then with the broccoli mixture. I finally topped with the breadcrumbs/chili crisps. Everyone loved it. The noodles were good the next day cold.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
This recipe is incredible but DANG, really buried the lede with this cashew cream! It’s going to become a staple for me. 

I ran raw cashews then added firm tofu in my vitamix and ended up with the most delectable whipped concoction! Could be used for a dessert, or pie filling, or….

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was such a unique dish! We ate it over quinoa with some kale. It was very filling and the chili crisp added really good flavor. I’m interested to make the tofu cashew mixture again and use it as some sort of spread!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Double the broccoli or halve the tofu/cashew bonanza, as y he whip needs the broc to taste complete.  Like someone else, I also added miso and lemon   toum to the tofuwhip to give it more flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I cannot share the enthusiasm of others. I cooked it following the recipe, and the taste was exquisitely bland. It needed quite a bit more chile crisp. The whipped tofu resembled hummus, but was unsalvageably bland. Broccoli were ok, but would come out crispier just roasted uncovered.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was such a great tofu recipe to add to my repertoire. It was very delicious and quick to make. I made cashew butter from cashews and added soy sauce to the cashew butter-tofu mixture for a little more flavor. It seems like it would work just as well to use peanut butter, almond butter or tahini. I agree with the commenter who will next time roast the broccoli without the tent.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Made this according to recipe but used about 2oz more tofu than called for bc that was the whole package and couldn’t see keeping that little bit as extra. Even still I had a really runny tofu “sauce”. I added all the water at one time (rookie mistake I know) where next time I would add half the water test consistency and go from there. I also opted to make my own cashew butter with cashews in the blender and then adding olive oil until it had the paste consistency. Would definitely do that again and would make this again as it was super tasty and a completely filling healthy meal with just a small portion and a few frozen gyoza dumplings on the side to compliment it. The cashew/panko/chili crisp topping was awesome and super easy I wish I had more! Will definitely find ways to incorporate that to other recipes as well as use this easy broccoli tofu recipe to compliment other meals. 5/5 stars except watch the consistency if you want it thicker use less than recommended water

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: It was surprisingly yummy! To the cashew butter I actually added garlic, soy sauce and nut butter, enhanced the flavor much more.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Forget the foil, roast the broccoli naked!  This recipe is just delicious, and so simple.  Our minds were expanded by the possibilities here.  Love, love, love.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Foil pouch totally unnecessary and wasteful! Delicious otherwise

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Great side dish or stand alone for apps.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Good, but that was an excessive amount of cashew cream and topping for the amount of broccoli.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I loved the chili crisp, but found the cashew cream bland. Any suggestions on seasoning it?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
This was really tasty. Followed the recipe, but added the zest of a lemon and a tbsp. of lemon juice to the tofu. 
We're thinking the leftovers will make an interesting pasta sauce.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was fabulous piled onto a fresh "Everything" bagel!  I used the tofu cream like hummus, and topped it with the broccoli and chili crisp panko/cashews.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Looks great but need a gluten free version. Any gg suggestions for panko?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I like to use pork rind "bread" crumbs sometimes. You can buy premade ones like Porq brand pork rind bread crumbs in many grocery stores (our local Wally world carries them, for example), or buy a bag of pork rinds and crush them. The plain ones I guess should be gluten free, but please check your bag if you go that route. The premade Porq brand crumbs say they are gluten free. Hope this helps! Enjoy. :)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: What is chili crisp?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: For those who've had it, could you describe the heat level?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: @Western NY guy For us the heat level is mild.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Followed recipe exactly - it's amazing. Next time I would only make half the cashew cream because I felt like it would have been fine with less and cashew butter is pricey. But wow - it was amazing. My son's friend was over for dinner and he had three helpings (it was a side for us but could most definitely have been a main course).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Maybe it was the brand of cashew butter I used but the cream tasted flat and a bit gross. I would try this again with peanut butter. And I highly suggest roasting on a cookie sheet so the broccoli caramelizes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
Agreed the whipped tofu was nothing to write home about. I made cashew butter with roasted salted cashews and some oil in a food processor. I hoped the salt from the nuts would liven it up, but no, it was very blah. I added a bit of salt and a good amount of za’atar seasoning and my toddler wanted to eat the whole bowl!
And yes, it seemed odd to essentially “steam” the broccoli in the oven, but while it wasn’t crispy it did get some coloring on the bottom. It also had a small amount of crunch from not overcooking it. In the end I felt it was a nice middle-ground to the creamy tofu and crispy breadcrumbs. I was too lazy/busy with a kid to pull out the garlic and chop cashews for the top, but we served it with some simple air-fryer salmon and everyone loved it! 
I did leave the spicy breadcrumbs off my toddler’s plate, but he loved everything else!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was surprisingly delicious.  Following the idea of another commenter, I made my own cashew butter.  I soaked 1.5 cups of cashews in water overnight, drained and added them to the blender with the tofu.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: What other veg could be a good sub for the broccoli?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Cashew intolerance here! I'm wondering if almond butter and roasted (or raw) almonds could do the trick?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: @Ivy I think almond butter would work!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Not wild about tofu unless it’s in my Thai food. But I put this broccoli over herbed whipped ricotta and it was absolutely amazing

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: @Tomrely I do it a lot with raw cashews for cashew cream and cheese. No problemo!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Delish and easy. We all couldn't stop picking at the extra topping. It would go well on lots of things. Agree, my broccoli was soft, not crisp but with the crunchy spicy topping it worked well. I might try to crunch up the broccoli more next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: My cashew cream never really got "fluffy," it was very liquidy. Perhaps this is because my cashew butter was already rather runny. I'd probably start with half the water for that step next time and add more if it is too thick.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Let this be a lesson to me - always check the comments before cooking for the first time! I agree with the majority here - roast broccoli on parchment. Zsush it up (and maybe warm and/or pour over a warm grain or noodle). Cashew/panko chili crisp is delicious and pulls it all together. I will try again with the recommended mods!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
Thank you for the recommendations to roast the broccoli uncovered, which I did (along with a few halved fingerling potatoes), and to make the cashew butter out of cashews. Excellent. 
But now I'm left with a large quantity of leftover cashew cream. What have you all done with it? I ate some with pasta, broccoli, and a little parmesan - very good. I can imagine using it as a substitute for mayo. How have you used any leftover cashew cream?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: @Ann this might be coming a little late But you can use the leftover cream for sauces add chipotle peppers or fresh herbs for a great topping on tacos, salads, and dip for veggies. It can also be used in place of sour cream and a diary substitute for vegans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was very good. Don't make the mistake I made. Tried to beef it up by adding ramen noodles. Tossed the noodles with scallions and the sauce on the heat...turned into a sticky clump. Still was very tasty! I think the sauce needs to stay unwarmed to be silky and not thicken. A very yummy meal; might be good with some toasted, oiled bread to swipe through it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I didn’t have panko and instead used psyllium husks which turned out great and adds fiber.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Why would anyone need soy sauce and salt?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Loved the chili crunch!! Hated the whipped tofu. Happy with the broccoli. There are lots of ways to cook broccoli - this technique is more steaming than roasting, keeping it soft without making it soggy and cooked it at high heat without making it crispy. It's very good as long as you aren't expecting classic roasted broccoli. It's fine to choose your own adventure and roast it if you wanna!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I was not a fan of the cashew cream. Actually thinking swapping peanuts/peanut butter would be better. Otherwise the dish was easy and relatively good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
I double the chili crisp and it tastes more authentic...

I also ran the toasted cashews and panko through the blender a bit and then used the cashews sauce and panko/nuts as a double dip.  .Hard to stop eating it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I also did not cover the broccoli. Otherwise, it was amazing!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I don't think crispy broccoli means roasted or toasted, but rather not overcooked by steaming.  There should be firmness, not browned crunchiness.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
Used this as more of a suggestion than a recipe not because I wanted to rogue, but because I realized I was missing a few ingredients and miscalculated my timing. I didn’t have any cashew butter so I made a different whipped tofu base. I used soft tofu and it came out great. 
Didn’t have time to roast broccoli so I steamed it.
For the breadcrumbs, I just toasted 1/3 c panko with a tablespoon of chili crisp and its oil. Even without cashews, it was an excellent topping and provided some much needed crunch and texture to the dish! Would definitely make again, even though I used the recipe more as inspiration.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: So delicious! I made the cashew cream and the panko topping as described. I tossed the broccoli with the olive oil and soy sauce in a bowl and air fried it at 400 for 12 minutes- did the same thing with an eggplant cut into 1 inch cubes and added that to the broccoli. Definitely a make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Made this as written but added a can of northern beans to the breadcrumb/nut/chile crisp mixture to make it more of a full vegetarian meal. Great fresh and as leftovers!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
Lots of pieces in this recipe, but it comes together nicely. Needs more chili crisp! I tripled the garlic. 
As others have said, I also made my own cashew/sunflower seed butter without adding any oil (sunflower seeds are plenty oily). 
This makes a ton of cashew tofu butter. Hopefully it keeps! 
This is a tasty meal, but missing pizzazz. Despite all star ingredients, it comes across a little bland taste wise, although delightful texture-wise.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Brocoli has good flavor, but I think would have a better consistency (crispiness) if roasted open (not covered); I think I'm not a huge fan of silken tofu.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I'm allergic to tofu, so am looking for a substitute.  The only thing I can think of is feta cheese.  Any other suggestions?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This looks delicious! I have a nut allergy. Any suggestions for a substitute for the cashew butter?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Made this during my lunch break when working from home. Roasted the broccoli in the airfryer and used peanuts (what i had) instead of cashews. I had a little bit of roasted chickpeas which I had on the side, but they tasted yummy with the tofu, so I would consider mixing them with the nuts next time for extra crunch.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
I’m making this admittedly for the first time and my cashew tofu mix just tastes way too sweet so I’m adding lemon (and a fair amount of pepper)

I admit, I wonder about heating the tofu cashew mix before serving just so it’s warm in the winter

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Any suggestions on how this could be served as a dip at a potluck type of holiday event? I was thinking of serving it with pita chips. Any thoughts from people who have made it?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Nisha Vora's whipped tofu is better than this. Guaranteed. . .

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
I had just made Genevieve Ko's Crispy Chile (from NYT cooking) and found this a good spot to use some of it. Used half cauliflower and half broccoli roasted with out a foil cover. Used peanuts and peanut butter and tofu. Wow!
This was really good as well as easy to make.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: What do I do with the tofu if I don’t make the cashew cream?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
What is ‘turkish pepper’ ? What is chile crisp?

Who is this recipe written for?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: @Robert Eisenberg Robert, you adorable creature. Never mind, it’s written for the pretentious that just can’t bring themselves to say chilly. All together now: c-h-i-l-l-y !

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Cashew butter and  tofu add protein? Never mind the low protein content of tofu but the cup of cashew butter adds fat. Not that I mind that but please stop making pseudo claims when the nutritional facts are stacked up against them.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Not sure why you think these are pseudo claims - tofu is actually a high quality protein. I think it would be worthwhile actually looking up the nutritional facts involved here.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Cooked the broccoli in foil as written and liked the texture. I thought the cashew butter/tofu cream was a good addition, but made enough for double the amount of broccoli. Extra chili crisp was desired.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Roasted the broccoli; otherwise made as written. Cashews are such mild nut; all they really bring to the tofu cream is a faint sweetness, which tasted … odd. Would definitely do peanut butter next time. First time I’ve made a cream from silken tofu, so that was cool. Breadcrumbs needed more chili crisp, but that’s on me. Has potential with a lot of tweaking. Really like adding protein in this creamy way.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: @JennGrace key words Makor tweaking.   Terrible recipe if you ask me.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: As  transplant in New Mexico, I add roasted poblano to a mushroom and pepper mix with salt, pepper and Marsala then add grill onions.  Grill the shaved ribeye, add mixture and because no cooper sharp a mixture of grated raclette and sharp cheddar.   Roll is critical.  Here it's a potato roll..  or a sourdough  or a torta roll; it's not the same but it works

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: On one dish of this squeezed lemon.  On other squeezed orange.  Feel that the citrus brightens the crème

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
surprisingly tasty. we were out of chili crisp, so I just used Sir Kensington gogache
saltines as bread crumbs

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: SUPER YUM! the Chile crisp crumble is a delightful savory texture bomb. if you just make that to top any roasted veg you won't be sorry. I

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I added chile crisp to the tofu purée - heaven!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Curious if anyone has subbed chili crisp with another condiment? I’m thinking about Eros Pista Hungarian paprika spread (spicy) with this instead…

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I really liked this. I think the 6T water was a bit much for the tofu/cashew butter because mine was more watery, less fluffy, as described in the recipe. I much prefer roasted broccoli to steamed, so I roasted it with a piece of parchment paper laid loosely on top of the broccoli, and it wasn’t soggy and limp like I usually find steamed broccoli to be. I was solo for dinner tonight but am sure my husband will scarf down the leftovers when he gets home.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: hahaha!  By happy accident, I completely missed the 6T of water part of the recipe and the tofu whip was perfect!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: It was great! Took it to a pot luck dinner and everyone loved it. I used Daniel's suggestion (see comments) and made my own cashew butter. I had never used Silken Tofu before and didn't know what to do with the wet silky tofu. Obviously not going to press it like you do with firm tofu. Drained it in a sieve and then used it. I also did not cover the broccoli when roasting.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: The breadcrumbs and broccoli were good, but the texture of the tofu mush was really gross to me

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Delish!  Subbed peanut butter because that's what I had, but I think cashew butter would be better, or perhaps the tahini someone suggested.  As to the broccoli in a foil pouch--it worked very well in the air fryer.  I'm also not a fan of steamed broccoli, but air-fried at 400 for 12 minutes was perfect--still crunchy with a little bit of char.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I used peanuts & PB since that's what we had. Listened to other advice here and didn't use the foil so the broccoli could be roasted rather than steamed. It turned out great! Next time I will add more chile crisp since we like things spicy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Oh so delicious.  The texture of the sauce is amazing. I made cashew butter with toasted (warm) cashews in my vitamix. I used a macha salsa instead. This is great for  gathering of four but could be an amazing buffet dish to put out at a party. Great flavors and textures. Yum!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Dreamy, fantastic, yum! I couldnt stop nibbling the chili crisp cashew topping and the creamy cashew tofu base while making.  Yet somehow, the finished dish exceeded all expectations, somewhat like eating beef stew over mashed potatoes but elevated in every way, panko bits and cashew crunch throughout.  Based on comments I loosely covered veggies and made my own cashew cream.  Hearty, this could easily serve 4 as a main dish. Alexa, this recipe is inspired!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Spicy chili crisp is a new ingredient for me. Now it’s my favorite. Oh so good as others stated, roast the broccoli but not in foil.  I used tahini instead of cashew cream and it was fabulous. My broccoli to cream ratio was off so I’ll be eating the remainder with carrots and celery. Yum

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Really good and an easy unique crowd pleaser. I ended up making more broccoli for this the second time, and per other comments broiled the last few minutes to crisp them.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was AMAZING and tasted like it was from a fancy restaurant. We for sure plan to do this again in the future soon.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This is sooooo good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I agree with other posters that the broccoli steamed in foil is not as good as roasted and the tofu cream is bland and the chili crisp crumbs are brilliant! So the second time I made it with roasted broccoli and cauliflower, made the tofu cream with the seasonings from Nisha Vora’s whipped tofu ricotta on this site, and doubled the chili crisp crumbs so I would have extra to sprinkle on other stuff all week. Cheers for the inspiration!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
I agree with many of the comments made here:
- I think the broccoli would be better roasted rather than steamed
- The cashew cream as written is way way too bland. Granted I used raw cashews as I didn’t have cashew butter, but it turned out thin and tasteless. Based on recs on here and what I had in my kitchen I added more salt/pepper, garlic powder, coriander, cayenne, rice vinegar, and nooch, and it finally became tolerable. 
Had me wondering why don’t I just plate some roast broccoli over some spicy hummus and call it a day

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I’m not quite sure why this is getting such rave reviews. The cashew cream was incredibly heavy and more than double what was needed. I felt ill after eating this supposedly healthy dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Excellent weeknight light supper! This came together quickly and easily. As others suggested I roasted the Broccoli in the oven. I used 420 degrees which was perfect. Also, I used peanut butter with the tofu which was super delish. The chili crisp with cashews takes this dish over the top. Highly recommend!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. Broccoli was a little too al dente after 15 min, so I added five in the oven.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
This was tasty. Brocccoli took closer to 25min to cook inthe oven. 
Cashew cream needsa little soemthing extra - squeeze of lime or dash of soy sauce maybe? 

the chili crumble takes this dish to the next level! 

Easy to come together and if you do the cream it can be a meal in itself.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This dish is really delicious, but for the life of me I cannot figure out what to serve it with. Any ideas? It's very rich in an odd sort of way - small portion is perfect.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I wish someone had replied to this because I have been racking my brain as well. We are vegan, so it makes a little more difficult to figure out what to serve with it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
Made almost as written, including risking cooking the broccoli covered.  I do think I'll roast next time, as that's my family's favorite way to eat it.  
The cashew cream was a hit with everyone including the big kids.  IT's what turns the dish from a regular veggie side to a main dish, the tofu plus half a jar of Wegmans cashew butter was less than a package of chicken thighs, so it's not particularly cost-prohibitive.  And the cashew-chili crisp breadcrumbs are addictive.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I'm on a constant hunt for more quick, easy and tasty vegetarian recipes. I liked this one but as others have mentioned found the tofu/cashew cream to be bland.  Following the spirit of some suggestions here, I added lime juice, Baharat seasoning and a little cayenne pepper.  That jazzed it up!! The amount of the tofu/cashew sauce is definitely way more than you would need for one or two serving.  I used it -- heated -- over some pork tenderloin slices the next night and that worked quite well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I agree with Gregg, cooking broccoli in foil doesn't make it crisp. I remove the foil after 15 minutes and bake 5 minutes extra. Lovely, made this for the third time now, I like it :)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I thought the tofu and cashew cream mix was boring…. I added 4 oz goat cheese and 2T nutritional yeast and 2T coconut oil.   For the extra topping. I sautéed 1/4 c  crushed peanuts, 1/4 c puffed quinoa and 1T chili crisp in 1T butter .  I also added scallions and mushrooms with the broccoli.  My modifications made this an outstanding dish!!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: @Mary ann f In my vegan opinion, It’s heavy enough with the tofu and cashew butter..adding anything else is unnecessary.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was fantastic! The chile crisp crunch is a must and really took the whole thing to another level. I served it as a side dish with some seared chicken, but it could easily be its own standalone meal. (The whipped tofu was great for dipping pieces of chicken into, though!)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: On frequent repeat.  Crave this.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I made home made roasted cashew butter from another recipe here, so that was labor intensive. The cold whipped tofu with the warm broccoli was not my favorite but the flavor grew on me. The nutritional info seems off, I could not eat that much cashew cream! It seems like the cashew cream would be better with something else.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: The room temp tofu and warm roasted broccoli was off to me. I felt like I was eating broccoli with a side of whipped mayo that just happened to be flavored with cashew butter. Overall, I wouldn’t make this again, nor would I tweak it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Would this be good over hummus?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody:
I don't know that I enjoyed this as much as other commenters, but it was solid!  The topping feels like crumbs from a bag of chips that is too dangerous to exist in this world.

The second time I made it, I substituted the cashew butter for Chinese sesame paste and used silken tofu, and thought the whipped tofu came out much better.  The first time, it was a little bit thick, almost pasty in texture.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and easy to make. I used Kokumaro bricks as that brand was highly rated. I added cayenne for extra spice. I never knew Japanese curry existed, but now I'm obsessed and I have a whole new genre of food to cook. NYT recipes are amazing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I didn't make the cashew butter layer. I used the linked NYT chili crisp recipe as I didn't have any ready-made. That turned out great as did the entire dish. I DID cook it sealed in foil and was happy with the result. I served it as a side dish to a simple salmon filet and it went well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Made this exactly as written, including the roasting method which worked just fine. This is REALLY REALLY GOOD!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Found the tofu cashew cream to be too rich to eat much. The chili crisp crumbs are good. Overall found there were just too many cashews - very rich recipe that might be better served in appetizer portions.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: The chile crisp crunch and the tofu sauce were delicious, but the broccoli was way too mushy. Like others, I recommend roasting without foil pouch. I will use leftover crisp on some other meals!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: imma dissent. although each of the 3 components was good, they didn't play well together. huge fan of panko/cashew/chili crisp and can see using that in lots pf places, but overall this lacked cohesion. and - waaay too much cashew cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: @Pinkwiggy agree 100% - way too much cashew cream and just too heavy a component that didn’t go with the rest of the dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I love this recipe! Instead of chopped cashews I add vinegar soaked raisins at the end (and, as others have mentioned, just roast the broccoli instead of steaming). The chopped cashews would probably be good I just haven't had extras around and I wanted another texture.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: This was great. If I were to make a change I would probably add more broccoli and scaling up the seasoning with it, maybe up to 2.5 pounds of broccoli. We ended up with a ton of cashew cream, wishing we had more broccoli to go with it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I tossed the cashew/tofu sauce with pasta and chickpeas and topped it with broccoli, the breadcrumbs, cashews, and chili crisp. I also tossed some nutritional yeast and garlic powder into the cashew/tofu sauce. Next time I’ll roast the chickpeas with the broccoli for another crunchy element.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I couldn't face turning on the oven for this so I just sauteed the broccoli with garlic & soy after making the crumble. a great recipe, going to try a mix of broccoli and cauliflower next, and maybe roasted zucchini later on.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: As usual, I put in WAY more garlic (at least 10 cloves), a dash more tamari and added heaps of nutritional yeast. For the crisp, I mixed Harissa with cashews to roast and then added breadcrumbs. I think it still could've had more bang. We combined it with another recipe on here (honey-glazed mushrooms with udon) and it mixed and marinated as leftovers very well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I made this tonight with a few substitutions. I can't eat cashews so replaced with peanuts, and for the cream with peanut butter+sesame paste. Realised way too late I was out of breadcrumbs so substituted rolled oats, which worked. Inspired by the intro, I used the cream to coat a bowl of noodles, served topped with the broccoli and crisp. After all these swaps: is it the same dish? idk but it was tasty.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: the recipe makes wayyy too much cream. can half the ingredients for that part and still be completely fine.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: I used peanuts for the chile crisp panko topping. For the cream, like many others, I used tahini: I used a 1/2 cup tahini and 8 ounces of silken tofu (what I had left in the package), 1/3 cup water, the juice of most of a lemon, a tsp sesame oil, and plenty of salt and pepper. I served it over a bed of arugula. It was really delicious.  I used broccolini.  Everyone liked it- even the skeptics. Adaptations were based on what I had at home. I am sure the recipe as it is is good too!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted Broccoli and Whipped Tofu With Chile Crisp Crunch Recipe
commentBody: Love this recipe! Could definitely be made with peanuts and peanut butter, though as someone who is not a peanut butter fan, I appreciate that the base recipe uses cashew butter. As a couple other commenters have said, honey enhances this already great dish. A simple drizzle on top at the end goes a long way.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Smoky Tomato Soup Recipe
commentBody:
1/2 c. buttter?
DId I read that correctly?
Seems excessive. no?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Smoky Tomato Soup Recipe
commentBody: @june Butter enhances the flavor and acts as a melding agent for the other ingredients.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Smoky Tomato Soup Recipe
commentBody: @june feeds 4-6 people

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Smoky Tomato Soup Recipe
commentBody: I was thinking the same.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Smoky Tomato Soup Recipe
commentBody: Cut in half. Very good. We are two people. Are in New Mexico swapped Ancho pepper for 1/8 ancho pepper flakes.  Delicious

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Smoky Tomato Soup Recipe
commentBody: Start with this and then enhance to your taste: with smoky anything plus basil, oregano….to 1 large can of crushed tomatoes add 1 regular can of evaporated milk. Get out of the kitchen and relax with comfort food that doesn’t require over an hour!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Smoky Tomato Soup Recipe
