commentBody: @Priscilla Feral What is the reason for the whole grain mustard?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I soaked 1 cup of cashew in enough just-boiled water to cover for 15 minutes. Then I blended the cashew with enough of the soaking liquid to get a cream-like consistency. Very delicious meal. The creamy spicy beans, tart arugula, and crunchy panko was a flavor/texture bonanza.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
will absolutely make this, using the heavy cream subs noted below!  I will not, however, be 'wiping out the skillet' only to add the same ingredients to it (oil, S & P).   For the Society For the Prevention of Redundancy, I remain, VTY.
;0)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as directed, used Parmesan vs pecorino - SO delicious.  The lemony arugula   beans is an amazing combo. This recipe will be on repeat in our house!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as directed but I'm not sure how people are rating this a 5 star recipe. It's good but not great. It's easy, that's a plus and it's fairly healthy, another plus, but it seems like it should be an appetizer not a main course. It's not a flavor bomb and didn't surprise me in any way. So a solid 4 stars for health and ease of making.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this twice in the last 24hr. Last night, for weekly neighbor dinner, then again in the middle of the afternoon today, since there were no leftovers. A few tweaks: deglazed the pan after browning the tomato paste with a splash of sherry. Used Rancho Gordo beans (black-eyed peas and alubias blancas) with a little of their broth instead of canned beans and water. A tbsp of fish sauce increased the umami. I did the panko as written last night, but I'm using crushed potato chips this afternoon

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We love love love this!  With a vegan in the house, I substituted Barista Oat Milk for the cream (same 1cup amount) and held off on cheese till end. It’s delicious and it’s on our steady meal rotation!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this exactly as written and it was ridiculously rich. Will try it with yogurt and less oil. I found the texture of the sundried tomatoes to be kind of gross. Suggest chopping them or eliminating and using more tomato paste. Also-- be sure to use good quality canned beans. The cheapy ones mush up.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I served this as a side dish with some pork chops for my family.  I used about half of the heavy cream called for, and used veggie stock instead of water, and added 2 pats of butter instead. I used one can navy beans and one can of cannellini beans. My husband and  I thought it was stellar and I will make it again.  My kids 10 and 16, who are very adventurous eaters were "meh" on the beans, and have decided that they do not like cannellini beans now, for some reason.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made it vegan by making cashew cream and accidentally forgetting to add the cheese—didn’t even miss it, it was soooo good

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Did this just as the recipe called for, and it was delicious. Spouse who doesn’t usually like beans devoured it. Might add a little more tomato next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Only had half and half, still turned out thick and creamy. No leftovers, it was devoured!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: YES!!  This is very good!  Thank you, thank you!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I love that all the top comments are people who liked the recipe, but changed everything about it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I so appreciated the idea of another cook to substitute cashew cream for the heavy cream. I’m not vegan, but I always have cashews in the fridge and rarely have heavy cream. I also appreciate that cashew cream adds some protein, fiber, and unsaturated fat (and prefer it over coconut milk for its health and flavor profile in this type of preparation). Because the tomato sauce is already so flavorful, you really cannot tell cashew cream was substituted for the heavy cream (no one eating it believed me when I told them). To make cashew cream blitz 1/2 cup raw cashews and 1/2 cup water in a high speed blender until completely smooth. I have made a version of this both with and without Parmesan and both were delicious. The sauce is like a delicious tomato vodka sauce and the arugula salad sends it over the top.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this according to the recipe and it was wonderful!  Next time I’ll add greens to individual portions so that I can freeze leftovers.  This is a lot for one person.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Substituted the heavy cream for sour cream and added a medium fried egg to serve. 
My British boyfriend declared these the best beans he's ever had. Will make again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious — a recipe I totally swiped past until I saw the cult status on Reddit. I made it on a whim, it was easy and deliciously and wowed my carnivore husband. I subbed half and half for the cream and cut the oil by 1/2 in order to mitigate the calories and didn’t miss either. Make it - you won’t be disappointed.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this exactly as written. Fantastic! Such an interesting and scrumptious combination of flavors. My husband, who HATES arugula is on his second giant helping of the dish raving, "I can't believe that I love this arugula!"

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Tried once as written, delicious, second time reduced cream to 3/4 cup, no added Water, also added can of diced tomatoes.  Pretty great, and more tomatoey than original version.  Arugula and lemon dressing remain essential.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a winner. I’m a private chef and have made this dish for several clients and family members. 100% of the people who have had this, truly rave about it. The ragout is warm and creamy, the greens are fresh and bright with the spicy punch of arugula, and the breadcrumbs top it all off with a satisfying, salty crunch. I serve it will charbroiled asparagus and instead of tossing the breadcrumbs with the greens I sprinkle on top at the end. Make this and watch the joy it will bring 🍽️

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Little dry, would simmer less next time. 
Only had butter beans, worked great. 
Could double the red pepper to get more of a kick. 
Used half and half, worked great
Mozzetta sun dried tomatoes 
Good fresh tomatoes might be a nice addition

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We've enjoyed this meal many times. I don't know what a crushed red pepper is, so we dice. If the panko steps are an obstacle, blast some croutons in a food processor and get at it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @N*they mean crushed red pepper flakes…easily found at your grocery store

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @abgor…in the spices section.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made according to recipe - delicious in flavor but agree with the many other comments that heavy cream can be reduced. It was far too rich for us for an otherwise healthy and complete meal. I will make again, maybe with whole milk instead. Great flavors and texture though.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Someone suggested using Yogurt to replace the cream.  NO! Don't do that! Yogurt curdles when heated.  It ruined the dish for me.  I could see mixing some half and half with whole milk.  But maybe just skip the dairy completely if you're watching saturated fat and need a heart healthy dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I didn't have any of the ingredients on hand, and I've never used the internet, so I had no idea how to get them delivered, which I understand is a thing. Instead, I swapped in a pizza I ordered, some ice cream in the freezer, and a handful of edibles. Would definitely make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: One of the best things I've made from NYT Cooking.  Really easy but tastes special and complex.  I don't typically like lemon but it compliments the beans and parmesan cheese beautifully.  I'm sure there are many ways to alter, change, add to this recipe, but it is lovely as is.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I swap out the heavy cream for a cup of Greek yogurt and it’s great. Extra protein without the saturated fats. Also, adding plant based sausage (or regular) is tasty.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This is an amazing comfort food dish that doesn’t require much effort. I crave this, bc it’s so delicious and so easy. 

A few notes:

If you’re not going to eat it all at once, keep the breadcrumbs and arugula separate

We even cutting in half is more than enough for two people. Be prepared for leftovers and keep the arugula separate 

I guess I’m just saying this is delicious and warrants  date night treatment

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I don't get any of these recipes. 
You had ingredients that were so healthy, then you've got to add all the saturated fat. 
I'm not opposed to generous cheese, as you get protein and calcium. But heavy cream, while admittedly delicious, is heavy cream. 
I regularly make beans with sauteed onions, tomato paste, grated cheese, sun dried tomatoes-and I assure you the heavy cream ain't necessary.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this exactly as written. Delicious. Will make again. Next time might play with adding a very small bit bacon or pancetta for added depth of flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
One of the BEST things I have eaten in the last 6 months. So flavorful, rich and satisfying. 
I used Half & Half and 2% milk combined instead of heavy cream, added a couple of dollops of Harissa and 1 generous spoonful of jarred bruschetta sauce I had open.  Red and green arugula were the greens of choice. I will make this again and again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The toasted panko bread crumbs are a delight!!!  Transformative, along with the flavor of the arugula.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This looks so delicious but I'm trying to cut cholesterol. Since this is such a mediterranean type dish I am planning to sub out the cream with cashew cream  (1/2 cup overnight-soaked raw cashews, dash of cold water, blend until creamy). 

While coconut cream is an option I would like to keep with the mediterranean flavors and cashew cream is nice because it's nice and creamy but without any strong flavor profile of its own.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is so high in sodium according to the nutrition stats - any suggested modifications?  I wonder whether the estimate assumes salt was added to the tomato ingredients (it often is unless you look for the no-salt-added label, unfortunately).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @Mel tomato paste doesn’t usually have much salt, actually. It’s probably the other canned items and parmesan. It’s pretty easy to find no salt added beans in my experience, and you could reduce cheese if needed.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I love arugula but, sadly, my husband despises it.  I wonder if thinly sliced kale might be substituted well in this dish?  (I make a salad that is basically thinly sliced kale mixed with toasted panko, minced garlic and shredded Parmesan or Pecorino cheese.)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I would think kale can hold up nicely to the weight of the beans... might need to sit a little longer with the lemon zest and juice to soften a bit.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this with Greek yogurt instead of cream. The rest of the recipe was followed as written. The lemon provides a perfect juxtaposition of acid to the creamy starch of the beans. My husband loved this!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Deeee-licous. We are eating tons of beans (the Bean Protocol-ish). This recipe is a slam DUNK! This is going in the favorites section.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Instead of the cream we added tomato sauce and used it as a pasta sauce over homemade fettuccine and it was delicious. Looking forward to enjoying the leftovers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Second time, I cut the cream to 1/4 cup and doubled the amount of sundried tomatoes, and it was *fantastic*. My family is always trying to get more legumes in our diet, so this one (with these modifications) will go into regular rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
These are...fine. Like, they taste good, but I was expecting something incredible from all the of the reviews. I think if you've grown up in a household that eat a lot of beans than these are going to be underwhelming--you already know that beans can taste good. I feel like the extremely laudatory reviews must come from folks who don't eat beans much or often.

It comes together quick. It's tasty and cheap. But I just don't get the hype.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: My favorite NYT recipe. Made exactly as written, served with toasted sourdough. It’s an absolutely perfect winter meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: A note of appreciation for readers who post variations and substitutions. There are so many NYT recipes I'd need to bypass due to the ingredient list. Thanks to others sharing their hacks, I've been able to enjoy many of them. The comments section is great for those looking to make the recipe "as written" and the ONLY place I can learn about successful variations. I'll be adapting this recipe today, non-dairy and with broccoli rabe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I was not convinced the lore about this dish was real and I was wrong. Make this it is delicious. I followed the recipe exactly and will do so again next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I am OBSESSED with this recipe. These beans, toasted sourdough, and a glass of wine? Perfection.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I guess someone had to be an outlier. Too many calories, too many steps, not that good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
this is great! did not have time for the seasoned panko so just added breadcrumbs to the arugula salad. Also followed directions to use greek yogurt + water instead of heavy cream (I just never have that on hand).

With those changes this meal came together in 20 minutes which was pretty impressive for the quality!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I usually never comment on recipes, but this one is exceptional. I will say that I usually use an entire can/bottle of tomato paste and less heavy cream than the recipe calls for. This has become a STAPLE in my dinner rotation and an absolute comfort food– you wouldn't think that beans and salad could be comfort food! 10/10.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I don't get the big deal.  I mean it was fine, but nothing fantastic or that I would even make again.  Too heavy, no wow.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Subbed the cream for a lighter vegan option and added two big spoons of red pesto - delicious and simple. Will make again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I don't think I would make this again. 1 cup of cream was way too liquidy, so I cooked it down much longer than the recipe called for, and in the end it just didn't have enough flavor to make it into the rotation. Perhaps I needed better/stronger sun-dried tomatoes and red pepper flakes, though I upped the amount of both based on my mid-cook tasting. We love the spicy cheesy black bean skillet recipe on this site, but this one not so much.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was a big hit! I used kale instead of arugula because that's what we had on hand, and that was also delicious. Next time, I'll double or triple the recipe so we have leftovers!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: it’s fine! it’s cheap but elevated and tastes good. Is it the best nyt recipe I’ve ever made? No, but it’s good. I was expecting it to blow my mind and it’s just not at that level for me. Used milk instead of cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I think that if you want to go vegan that in addition to leaving out the heavy cream that you avoid draining and rinsing the cannellini. The aqufaba will give you the creamy texture you need, if I need more creaminess then I might try the suggestion to whip up a little silken tofu and add that.
 The idea that bean liquid is garbage is a garbage idea. If you're afraid of sodium, then taste the bean liquid, it's not very salty, if you're very sodium avoidant then don't forget to omit the pecorino.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I would simmer the sauce for 20-30 before adding the beans. Made with rancho gordo marcella beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a GREAT recipe. Made it tonight, followed directions to the letter. Might try some suggestions from other readers concerning ways to eliminate a bit of saturated fat (sub Greek yogurt for the heavy cream, etc.).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @RevJudith I used half and half and another time milk. Both worked well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delightful! I made a batch of Rancho Gordo cassoullet beans just for this. I let my sundried tomatoes drain in a little strainer over a bowl. I used this drained oil for the onions and garlic. Instead of cream (didn't have any) I used my bean broth. While the batch was simmering, I put in several parmesan rinds in place of grated cheese (I keep them in my freezer for this type of purpose). Finally, I didn't have arugula, so I massaged some kale and used that for the topping. A keeper!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Oh yes! Forgot to add that I parbaked some Trader Joe's meatless meatballs and let them finish simmering in the pot. *chef's kiss*

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this with a couple changes. Added andouille sausage which I sautéed with the onions. Baby spinach instead of arugula. Combo of heavy cream and Greek yogurt. Pretty tasty. May want to add garlic to the fried panko next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
My vegan/gluten free version of this with slightly more protein:
1. Crispy quinoa instead of panko (cook 1C dry quinoa per usual stove method then let cool and toss with olive oil and more salt if desired and spread evenly on a large sheet pan covered in parchment paper and bake @400 for 30-40 min, checking every 10 min and moving around with spatula to make sure it cooks evenly). At the end you want to be careful it doesn't burn, it's a fine line between crispy and burnt to a crisp. 
2. Cashew cream (raw cashews and water) made quickly in the Vitamix. I did 3/4c raw cashews and kept adding water til it was the desired creamy consistency and dumped it all in even though it was clearly more than the cream recommended in the recipe. I like cashew cream better than coconut in most non-Asian/more savory recipes because the flavor is more neutral.  
3. Nutritional yeast and some extra salt to taste to make up for the lack of Parmesan. I like to use 2:3 ratio of nutritional yeast to Parmesan.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I don’t understand all the five star reviews. It was tasty but nothing epic. I feel like the recipe title caught the attention of people who mostly eat super healthy. And they let themselves indulge by making this recipe that has ALL the things-cream, cheese, oil, bread.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The Ingredient Weight Chart over at King Arthur Flour Company says that 2/3 cup grated Parmesan or Pecorino Romano should weigh about 67g or 2.4 oz.  That weight chart is super helpful, btw.  In other news, I really wish the NYT would routinely list ingredient weights as well as volumes.  Especially for things like grated cheese.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I substituted silken tofu for the heavy cream. You need to blend it with a little olive oil to get the perfect, creamy consistency. It makes the recipe vegan (assuming you use a vegan or no cheese) and low in saturated fats

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This looks delicious, but fifty-two grams of fat per serving isn't healthy.  I'm going to look at trimming that down and see how it turns out.  I'm sure you can use half the olive oil, half and half instead of heavy cream and cut the parmesan a bit.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
for my metric peeps, yes the quantities are slightly off but here's what I used and it turned out delicious, hope it helps:

1/3 cup tomato paste = 79mg -> used 70g can
14oz cannellini beans = 397g x 2 = 794g -> used 800g can
1 cup heavy cream = 237ml -> used 250ml bottle
1/2 cup sun-dried tomatoes = 119g -> used a little less than half a 280g jar
2/3 cup Pecorino/Parmesan = 158g -> used 3x 50g bags Pecorino
3oz arugula = 85g -> used 100g bag

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: i would make passionate love to these beans if possible. my god.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Used vegan cream, subbed in a bit of sun-dried tomato paste. Quick, easy recipe. Had with bread but maybe will have sth else as a “base”.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: What kind of vegan cream?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I followed the suggestion of using Greek yogurt instead of heavy cream with great success!! I added some passata to make up for the "liquid". I had packaged sundried tomatoes, vs. packed in oil, and could not tell the difference. Also added spinach and basil at the end. Amazing dish!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I used whole milk instead of cream and it was wonderful!! We love the bean skillet dishes here, but this one was definitely elevated. Will start to have in the regular rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I made per the recipe. As soon as I poured the cream in the pan I knew it was too much. I’m not a health nut. I suggest adding gradually. The result was good but too gloopy/rich. I like the idea of inverting the proportions here of the cream and tomatoes— using some canned tomatoes in addition to the other called for in the recipe. 

The lemony greens save this recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I was out of sun- dried tomatoes, so I substituted chipotle in adobo. It gave the dish more spice and smoke. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Too rich, too much cream. I may try reducing. I had to pop a lactaid pill. It’s also not as tomato-y as I had hoped even though I followed the recipe as directed.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made the recipe as is. Agreed with a few others that the amount of cream just seemed insulting, next time I will cut that in half. You can make food taste creamy without drowning it in cream. Loved the salad topped on it but again, I think I would cut the bread crumbs in half, it was just too much for the salad. Loved the lemon contrast to the creamy beans. Served with some homemade dark rye bread I had on hand, it was great.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is the first time I see this recipe and I think it is a keeper .  Will try this weekend.  Also, I love the substitutes for a lighter dish.  Thank you all!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: From NYTC Substitutions:  For 1 cup heavy cream, use 3/4 cup milk and 1/4 cup melted butter, or thicken 1 cup milk with 1 to 2 tablespoons cornstarch or flour. (Whisk milk into cornstarch or flour little by little.) Coconut milk, coconut cream (beware of increased sweetness), or cream cheese whisked with a little water also work. Note: Alternatives won’t whip into fluffy whipped cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I threw a chopped anchovy in with the onions and garlic to amp the umami and didn’t have arugula but did have a CSA box that needed eating. So I sautéd a zucchini and beet greens in butter cooled them down and continued as written. It’s a keeper!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This has more fat and almost as much sodium as I eat in a full day. Has anyone slimmed it down and made it healthy? I love good food, but I also like to take care of my body.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this with Rancho Gordo marcella beans. I also used 0 fat greek yogurt and 2% milk in place of heavy cream. turned out great, this will be my lunch all week.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious! I added fresh tomatoes and used fewer sun dried tomatoes. 
I’m not usually a fan of beans but this was sooo good. Loved the arugula with panko. Easily served 5 for dinner with toast. Yum!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I guess I'm in the minority here, but these were mediocre at best. I love beans. I love sundried tomatoes. I love lemony arugula. Something about it just did NOT work for me personally. I was so sad because it sounded AMAZING.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Tween Daughter made these for us for valentines dinner. They were absolutely delicious and the arugula salad was fantastic so bright and fresh.  10/10.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Like many users suggested I reduced (in my case halved, but you could go further) the cream and it was still amazing. 
An unexpected bonus highlight are next day leftovers! After some experimenting, my favorite is to roughly squish the beans with the back of a fork and mix all together to make a truly fantastic bread spread! Great on any type of bread with lots of fresh salad (ideally of course the brilliantly easy lemon arugula one from the original recipe)
A truly great recipe that I’ll cook over and cover again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I was skeptical after just tasting the beans but with the arugula it was totally transformed. So bright and delicious! I might sub half and half or cashew cream next time but will definitely make it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Toss the cheese with the panko and sprinkle it on top of the arugula last. If you add those ingredients to the lemon juice and zest as instructed, the panko is soggy, not crunchy. Otherwise, so delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: one of the BEST things i've made at home from NYTimes recipes...absolutely incredible. spicy, complex, tangy, creamy and the arugula salad on top made it even better. cannot recommend more.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We totally frankensteined this recipe. Only because we had so many things to use up that were close to what the recipe called for. And we abhor food waste. So…some tomato paste, some crushed fire roasted tomatoes, partial container of sun dried tomatoes. Had Mexican crema and some mascarpone- which stood in for the cream. Had Rancho Gordo flageolet beans begging for an occupation - sold! Thinned down with some bean broth. Just lovely with the arugula salad on top. This recipe was so well written it allowed us to be efficient with our fridge ingredients. Thank you for a great supper!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Used 2% milk and was creamy enough. Might add chicken sausage next time and some basil. Will be making this again

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a total flavor bomb. Made with Rancho Gordo Vaquero beans (about 1/2 lb dried) and it was amazing. Add some roasted red peppers because I had them. Don't skimp on the panko or toasting it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Used olive oil from the sun dried tomatoes to saute the onions, & used much less oil overall than called for. I'd cooked 1# of Rancho Gordo Royal Coronas the day before and used the whole pound. Used 1/2 C. cream and 1/2 C. of the nice, thick bean-cooking liquid. Next time I'll add white wine instead of water. My husband doesn't like arugula, so I swapped it out for slivered lacinato kale that I massaged pretty aggressively, then rinsed & spun, to soften; served it alongside. PHENOMENALLY GOOD!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Super delicious! Absolutely loved the crunchiness of the panko. I’ve never made anything like this before and I’m so glad I did. The greens blew me away. I used kale instead of arugula (because I hate arugula), and it was perfect.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Thought this was better the second day. The salad really makes it -- without the salad, for me, its too rich. I'll be trying it out with yogurt and milk as some others suggested, or the coconut cream. Its definitely a keeper

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
First - I used forager coconut and cashew “yogurt” bc I can’t have cream. Worked beautifully. 

Second - I think this recipe changed my life. This is peak deliciousness. I added rotisserie chicken bc I had one sitting in the fridge. Next time I cook this (tomorrow), I will put it over pasta, I think. The entire recipe comes together so beautifully perhaps I will never cook anything else.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I think the recipe is great as-is! I’ve made it many times and it’s a staple in my rotation. 

Here’s what I do sometimes change: 
-like other commenters, I use half and half, and usually some combo of water and half and half. 
-I put the beans and water/cream in a food processor before adding to the pot. I like it better when it’s more mixed together!  
-for sun dried tomatoes, I encourage you to measure with your heart. I use the whole jar. Same with tomato paste—god bless whoever taught me that you should just use it all instead of trying to save 1/4 a can. 

I find this recipe very forgiving. If you have more or less of an ingredient it will probably be ok! And  if you are new to cooking, it’s easy to make but very impressive for guests.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
What can I say except that I love this recipe? Well, actually I have a few tips:
Cook the onions in the oil from the sundried tomatoes. I also wilt spinach down into the beans instead of serving on raw arugula. I sometimes end up using whole milk greek yogurt in lieu of cream, but often just do a combo of both. I also use double concentrated tomato paste, and a lot of it. 
One of my favorite dishes ever!!! So easy and delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Small modification made this a healthy high fiber and protein, low saturated fat and cholesterol meal by subbing cream for chicken stock. Added a small dollop of Greek yogurt and got good consistency. Followed recipe otherwise and it was delish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I am only giving this two stars as written. It needs mods. Like others, my opinion is that it’s way too much cream and oil for the amount of beans/veg. That said, if you do reduce the oil by half and reduce the cream to 1/4 cup, it’s a very tasty and quick version of beans and greens. With a whole cup of cream you may just want to revise the title to “beans n’  cream.”

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This dish is loaded with multiple sources of fat.  I can’t imagine the number of calories per serving.  In the future, please focus on offering healthier and less expensive dishes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: There are always some healthier recipes here.  Some of like to indulge in dishes like this.  Just take the recipes you like and leave the rest.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I substituted whole milk for the heavy cream, and a pint of cherry tomatoes for the sun-dried tomatoes. It was delicious! I plan to make it for a dinner party I will be hosting soon.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a lovely dish! I wouldn't call it healthy though - it's loaded with animal fat. Using half & half instead of heavy cream and cutting the oil didn't diminish the flavor at all. It was delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Followed the recipe, but found the flavor to be lacking. This was a lot of work for low payoff.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: thought it was really good.  I only put in 3/4 cup go th cream and balanced it out with extra water, but in the future, I will go with half a cup a cream and add water or broth.  I didnt do the panko, but will next time as everybody below seems to love it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Just served this to French vegetarians and they said it’s the best thing they’ve ever eaten :)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I’m surprised nobody commented on the similarity to Julia Child’s Hericot Maitre’D. After making this NYT version tonight, I am sticking with the original version.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Entirely forgettable.  When I get rich enough to have a volcano carved like my face, i’ll serve a spicier version of this on hotdogs.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This dish is absolutely delicious! The second time I made it, I skipped the panko and opted for homemade croutons instead.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Huge hit with our kids and toddler, and our kids have lovingly referred to this dish as “pizza beans”.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Just checked the nutritional information for this dish as I’m planning to make it tonight. 840 calories?! How can that be? Is that for the whole dish? Even with some heavy cream (which I’m not planning to use) and tomatoes packed in oil I can’t believe this is accurate.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Excellent dish, far more than the sum of its parts. One of the best things I've eaten in recent memory.

I used a half pound of Rancho Gordo alubia blanca beans, soaked three hours and simmered for 90 minutes. Then followed the recipe fairly closely - used only about a half cup of cream and no cheese in the beans. 

Absolutely will make it again, and I see no reason to add back the omitted cheese or cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I can’t eat cow’s milk, so I used 4 oz of soft goat cheese instead. Good stuff.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @Joan Great idea, thank you!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this according to the directions, except I used yogurt instead of cream. I would add more water next time, or use nutritional yeast instead of Parmesan. There are lots of ways to cut down on the fat in this meal. It’s excellent! I love the idea of the salad served in the middle of the bowl, and the leftovers are wonderful! Highly recommend!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This dish was excellent!  Used 1/3 c. half and half and about 3/4 cup of water instead of 1 c. heavy cream and 1/3 c. water. Almost skipped the arugula salad on top — what a mistake that would have been!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Cooked as written, this recipe is pretty heavy and decadent for a main course. I made it again with 1/3 cup heavy cream and replaced the other 2/3 cup with chicken stock to great success.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious! I used the yogurt/milk sub for the cream but oh!!! It was all fantastic!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this as written. Delicious. Another time I subbed the cream for a big dollop of mascarpone because that’s all I had and I really wanted to make the recipe. Delicious. Another time I added a healthy spoonful of gochujang. Delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @Nayna to add, I’ve made this again and stirred through frozen spinach. Delicious. I used butter beans. So delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: All I can offer to this great recipe is don’t forget the Panko!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a great recipe. I felt the need to reduce the cream, particularly given the stated calorie count. I have done my own math on this recipe and can’t get to a per serving calorie count of 841.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I'm signle and made this for myself and I would marry myself after tasting this

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: AMAZINGLY FLAVORFUL AND SO EASY. Made it vegan using coconut cream. This is so delicious! Favorite NYT recipe so far. I’ll be making this weekly.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: It was okay. I’m not sure what all of the hype was about. I wouldn’t make it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Warning: I'm sharing changes to the recipe, but I think they worked. This is probably wonderful as is, but I was looking to lighten it up as a  dish NOT for a special occasion. I also had a lot of grape tomatoes to use due to a no-one-eats-veggie-platter party. I subbed about a pint of those for the sun-dried. I cut the oil in half—one T for the panko, one for the onions, garlic, etc, and one for the dressing. I used 1/4 cup heavy cream because I had it. Milk or broth would work. Yum!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
ROI for this dishd is 1000%.  The lemon dressed salad is the chef's kiss.  Didn't have arugula and used a salad mix. 
I made it more whole-foods, plant-based by using homemade cashew cream.  Coconut fat is 83% saturated which is worse than lard!  I try to avoid it unless it is necessary for the flavor of the dish.   This will be a staple!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Excellent recipe!! I’ll make it with dried beans next time. I added a fried egg to the top. I think I’ll use less cream next time. A cup is a lot. I’ll start at 3/4 cup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this vegan by using cashew cream instead of heavy cream and skipping the cheese. Very good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Don’t skip the panko step. I would have normally omitted, but after reading the comments I kept the step and it added exceptional flavour and texture. 
Served this as described, with grilled bread. Also had it the next day with leftover arancini.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is so underwhelming. Way too much cream - mutes all flavor. Suggested cooking times are way off - at least for my electric stove and stainless steel cookware. Flavor is completely lackluster.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was fantastic. I doubled the garlic because that’s who I am as a person. Used less olive oil & only 1/2 cup of cream & did 3/4 cup of shredded Parmesan & pecorino blend. This is going on our regular dinner rotation!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Of course its good-the ingredients are yummy. But it was even better than I could have imagined being a fave with the whole family of fussy eaters!  It's made it into the rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Wondering if I made some mistake, because I was not remotely as impressed with this recipe as other readers.  Salad was yummy, but I ended up with A LOT of extra breadcrumbs.  The beans were a bit better the next day, but right off the stove they had not absorbed any flavor from the sauce.  It wasn't especially complicated, but still a bit of work compared to the results, imo.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Added some gochujang (I did around 2 tbsp) in at the end, and it added complexity to the dish and a really nice umami flavour. Highly recommend!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Flavors are great and the lemony crunchy salad is a perfect compliment. The cream is the problem, and I love rich creamy dishes. Makes for an overly glutinous dish with unpleasant mouth feel. I made it exactly as written and will  try again after I think about how to lighten it up.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: so delicious! I didn't change a thing. the balance of the lemony greens with the creamy beans was gorgeous.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I didn’t want to use cream and instead used half coconut and half whole milk. Although the beans were delicious, I think the coconut was too overpowering, maybe Greek yogurt instead. The family enjoyed it and it was cheap.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made it tonight.  EXACTLY per the recipe (though I was SO tempted by suggestions from these comments regarding throwing in an anchovy, lessening the fat, etc.).  Husband (who used to own his own restaurant and rarely compliments my cooking) declared it "very good" (read:  Outstanding).  There you go.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I skipped panko to make gluten free.  I used coconut cream and dairy free shredded Parmesan to make it vegan.  This was delicious, quick and filling.  I will definitely make this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Cashew cream is a great sub for heavy cream in this recipe for those, like me, watching sat fat.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was such an amazing surprise. Really delicious. I used one cup of coconut milk and skipped the cheese.  It was really quick and so good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Surprisingly delicious!  I only had canned tomatoes and used 1/2 can over the sun-dried tomatoes, otherwise I followed the directions completely.  My suggestion when tossing the arugula is to put in the zest and cheese first and toss, then add the lemon juice and oil, otherwise the cheese and zest will clump.  Will definitely be making again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made 1/2 for 2. Ad libbed a bit to use as a base for poached eggs. Followed the directions til we got to adding water, and put a bit more in then suggested. Raised the heat a little & dropped the eggs in with 3-4 min to go in the cooking time. Then put the greenery mixture on top after plating. We loved it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved this recipe! easy to make, comforting, great for a weeknight rotation. I subbed cava’s spicy harissa paste because I didn’t have sun dried tomatoes and loved it. Agree with others that it was overly rich and would cut back on the heavy cream- maybe half broth half heavy cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Gave me terrible gas and heartburn. Flavor was ok but too rich. Not sure what to do with the leftovers. Might blend them up like a hummus and eat in moderation or dump into a chili and try to make it less fatty. As others have said, shouldn’t have to read the comments to know how to modify the recipe to make it usable! Do not recommend!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Seriously. Of course it tastes good. It’s got a cup of cream. I made it and we thought it very unhealthy and we actually have tons of better bean dishes. It is easy I’ll say that.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Outstanding! I just knew I had to try this after reading through the ingredient list, when, to my surprise it did not include whole fresh or canned tomatoes! If you're sensitive to heat reduce pepper flakes to 1/4 tsp. I liked it just fine and also used the full cup of cream per recipe. The finishing touch of toasted panko bread crumbs, arugula, and lemon zest+juice is just inspired and completes the dish beautifully as well as provides lovely presentation. Yum!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Amazing.  Great texture with the arugula.  Per one of the comment, I substituted the sun dried tomatoes in oil for canned diced tomatoes to make it a bit healthier.  Fabulous.  I did use very good olive oil in the greens to enhance flavor.  We all loved them.  A definite make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The second time I made this,to cut the fat a little, I used a cup of soy milk with 1/2 cup of beans puréed into it along with a handful of cashews. I also used Meyer lemon balsamic as a dressing. We enjoyed it just as much as the cream version.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Love this dish. Have made it 3 times. After first making it as indicated - I now swap the cream for 2% evaporated milk and add a big squeeze of lemon in the beans at the end.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We add chopped pepperoni to this and call it "Pizza Beans." Absolutely one of our favorites and in regular rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Added frozen spinach (450gr) before adding the beans to increase the amount of vegetables. still delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe! I used spring mix instead of arugula. I added a little lemon juice into the beans, as my tomato paste didn't give it much acid. I also mashed up about half of the beans to make it even creamier. Next time I make it, which will be soon, I'm going to use coconut milk, I think. And I'm going to take about half of the beans and mix them up in a food processor and then add them back to the bean. I also want to use navy beans next.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this once as written and it was fantastic, but very very rich.  I made it a second time and used half and half as a substitute for the heavy cream and it was just as creamy, but a little lighter, and better, in my opinion.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: It works well without the cream. Just the cheese is enough -- I used Pecorino Romano. I added rosemary stalks with the onions. I eye-balled the tomato paste and sun-dried tomatoes (may have added somewhat more than recipe calls for). The sauce was a rich dark red and there was too much of it so I added in some pasta. The cheese went in at the end. For something that took 15-20 minutes of cooking time, it tasted great! Too bad its calorie heavy even without the cream!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
It’s good, just needs a lot of salt. 

Used milk instead of heavy cream and that was fine.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I am so glad I read the comments before I tried this recipe. I still gave it five stars because it was delicious, and easy. I wouldn't call it healthy. I ended up following the recommendations to reduce the heavy cream to 1/4 cup. I also used less cheese. I did add a little extra water because I thought it needed more liquid since I reduced the cream. I wonder if I could have added a splash of white wine or broth, maybe next time. the lemon arugula is sublime. I will definitely make this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: For those also taken aback by the original calorie and fat count: I halved the olive oil at each stage and swapped the heavy cream for Alpro Light Soya Cream. That cut it from 841 kcal & 52 g fat per portion to roughly 490 kcal & 25.5 g fat — same great taste, but far healthier!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We added Hot Italian Sausage from Trader Joes’ (crumbled up) and it was 10/10

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Ok, we’ve made this about four times and it’s a winner. Two simple tips and one recommendation: Make the panko crumbs and set aside as a garnish. It’s much more fun to have the crunch above the arugula! Second, we double the lemon juice and enjoy the citrus finish. We substitute light cream for heavy and it makes zero difference in taste. Great recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: My husband is vegan and I often try to convert vegetarian or meat based mains into vegan. I can’t recommend this one for vegan application. It’s too heavy on cream and Parmasan and the vegan alternatives, in my opinion, really didn’t make this a tasty meal. I will definitely try it as written when I’m with my vegetarian friends. It sounds yummy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Have made this a few times and it’s delicious, super versatile, and forgiving. Made a few substitutions based on what I had on hand at home:
Whole milk + dollop of Greek yogurt instead of cream
One can white beans + one can chickpeas
Half a can of crushed tomatoes instead of sun dried tomatoes
No need to measure out the oil, just coat the pan and arugula leaves to your usual preference.
Have used pecorino, Parmesan and goats cheese on the beans, all excellent.
Have cracked eggs on top, with a cover to set. Also delicious and filling.

Truly a hero recipe and one of the few vegetarian meals my husband loves.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Constantly searching for things that will make being single and eating (intentionally) for 1 easier. Immediately adding this to the rotation

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was outstanding. I had to substitute roasted cherry tomatoes, because I did not have sun dried tomatoes. We were moaning sounds of delight during the whole meal.  This will definitely become a stable before us.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Used one can fire roasted diced tomatoes, no water, and most of can of Cento tomato paste

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Forget the bread, eat this by the SPOONFUL!  It's a huge winner in our house! Even my "meat with every bite" husband asks for this recipe on repeat.  I reduce the cream a bit and add veggie stock to make it a little bit lighter...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a home run! Quick, easy, high-protein, fiber, AND greens. Subbed cream with a 1/2 cup Greek yogurt and 1/2 cup whole milk. Served with Naan. YUM says the teens!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Fantastic recipe all around, but the sleeper hit here is the arugula salad recipe—don’t skip! I’ve even made it to go with other dishes and it’s always delicious. Now my favorite way to prepare an arugula salad!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I like to make it buttermilk to reduce the fat content. It still has a creamy texture, just not as heavy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I’ve made this before and made some fusion-y changes this time. It was FANTASTIC.

Basically, I: 
- added 1.5x the amount of tomato paste + extra garlic and chili flakes to taste
- deglazed with vodka after browning the tomato paste (would’ve tried sake if I had on hand) 
- used 2 cups of chicken broth instead of 1/3 cup water to make it “brothier”
- added a couple tablespoons of white miso, a dash of soy sauce, and a few generous shakes of MSG.
- used much less cream than the recipe called for, maybe half a cup at most

It was SO good and spicy and umami and I will be making this again and again and again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Can make this healthier without sacrificing flavor. It's already plenty rich from the olive oil. Blend or mash some of the beans to get a creamy consistency. Then needs only a dollop of cream (half and half or whole milk work too). For replacing missing liquid, can use 2% milk. I went lighter on the cheese too. Just kept tasting, adding enough to give it that delicious umami element. You do need to compensate for some of the salt that would have been in the full amount of cheese.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Great! I replaced the cream with 1 cup cashew cream I made with blending cashews and hot water to a cream consistency. Also added a little bit of veggies broth to the beans instead of water

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
One my all time favorite NYT recipes. I make it once or twice a month. My tweak is to keep the arugula separate and add it to individual bowls when serving.  This is a must if you aren't cooking for a crowd and will have leftovers. You want the arugula nice and crisp. 

I also add shrimp. I saute them in some garlic and olive oil and add them right on top of the beans. It's an easy addition to add some extra protein and the shrimp are delicious with the beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @Kathleen I would not have thought of this but I love this idea! Thanks for sharing

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Ann
1 year ago
To make this vegan:
I used a can of coconut cream instead of cow cream & added about a teaspoon of whole grain mustard and a tablespoon of nutritional yeast to the beans.
I added about a teaspoon each of nutritional yeast & paprika to the bread crumbs & mixed the arugula with a good glug of the sundried tomato’s oil.
Devine! Bright! Rich! Will be a staple!

Dee
1 year ago
This is delicious! I used approximately 1/2 cup of Greek yogurt mixed with 1/2 cup of 1%

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I was glad to read Ann's comment about making it vegan. Her suggestion of adding nutritional yeast and a bit of whole grain mustard was great! I used cashew cream as I didn't have coconut cream on hand.  I also added Vegan Parmesan cheese.  I plated the bean mixture and topped it with spinach as I'm not a fan of arugula. Very flavorful!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved it.  The salad was a great counterpoint to the richness of the beans.  This one is a keeper!  Thanks.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was fantastic! Like many others I used about half milk and half cream, and to stretch it out and make it a bit less rich of a meal we served it over pasta. Will definitely be making again!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I doubled this recipe last week to serve six. There was about one serving left over.
Everyone thought that the dish was great. I added a little more than the suggested amount of crushed red pepper. The arugula is a genius addition. It makes the dish fresh and more interesting.
The next time I make this recipe, I am going to substitute 1/2 and 1/2 for the heavy cream. The heavy cream makes the dish quite thick. I added 1 1/4 Cups of water to thin it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I just didn't love this one. Not sure why I seem to be the lone dissenter here. Compared to other bean recipes on NYT, this one lacked flavor and was super high in fat. Definitely not worth the calories. Plus, the panko basically loses its crunch almost immediately after joining the salad and beans, so that was a waste. If you make it, I would save yourself the calories and leave that out.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I’m strictly plant-based so I skipped the heavy cream & puréed about a third of a cup of the bean mixture with an immersion blender. Instead of cheese, I use my homemade salty-cheesy “walnut Parmesan”: 1 cup walnuts, 1/4 cup roasted pepitas, 1/4 cup nutritional yeast, 1 tsp garlic powder, salt to taste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: YUM! Great dinner, we pretty much followed the recipe exactly (shocking!) will definitely make again

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We thought this was very good, but not fantastic.   Made with 1/2 & 1/2 instead of heavy cream as I was pretending to be making healthy choices.  Otherwise followed the recipe.  Beans were still rich, but lemon salad on top was a nice counterpoint that offset the richness.  Wouldn't make for company, but would make again -- just not routinely.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I have been making a version of this for a while with Rancho Gordo beans -- I cook whichever white bean I want with some aromatics and a parmesan rind, and then when the beans are tender, add in some premade high quality sun dried tomato pesto (in a jar) to taste.  I've never added cream (who needs those calories!) and it's still divine.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious!
Only complaint - don’t add the breadcrumbs to the arugula mix. It is a waste of delicious and crunchy breadcrumbs!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This turned out great!  The arugula and bread crumb mixture brought it all together. My only change was adding 1/2 tsp Italian Seasoning blend to the plain Panko crumbs.  Probably could have added more, and a full teaspoon of crushed red pepper would have been better - we like spicy.  My water boiled off rapidly so watch the order you add it otherwise it can be a little drier.  Served it with a beet salad with goat cheese over a bed of spinach along with toasted french bread.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: It’s got beans, greens, no potatoes but tomatoes..

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved this recipe! To make it a bit more kid-friendly we kept the salad on the side and the red pepper flakes on the table - otherwise made as written. The kids were happy with beans and a baguette. For us adults, the beans were tasty but the combination of beans, spice and lemony arugula salad really made the difference, so don't skip on those elements.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a FABULOUS recipe. I prepared my own beans.  The only change I made was in plating. I put the wonderful panko on top of arugula so it would be sure to stay crunchy. My family eats in 2 “seatings” several hours apart…so panko wont be soggy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I substituted half-and-half for cream and reconstituted dehydrated julienned dried tomatoes for oil-packed. I like more heat and added another 1/2 tsp pepper flakes as well as a few generous dashes of cayenne. Instead of panko, I used herb-garlic croutons that I make in the air fryer and always have on hand. Placed arugula with croutons w/EVOO drizzle on the side instead of the top. Leftovers required some water. I will make this again - very satisfying and so easy to put together.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So good. I used half and half and it was plenty rich. The cheesiness is wonderful.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious …followed the recipe mostly, but (1) only had 1/2 onion; added 1 stalk minced celery and (2) had to use only 3/4 cup cream because I ran out, lol; topped off with 2% milk.  Would do that again to cut down on fat, otherwise just perfect.  P.S. if you’re a sun dried tomato hater as I am, DON’T skip them.  You don’t really taste them once added and cooked but they add an enormous amount of unami.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Way too rich. I just made as directed and can’t even finish the small bowl I plated for myself. Might try again using half and half instead of cream and more water. Also think this could be a good dip for a party with toasted bread. Too much for a meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! This is a perfect meal. I could eat this every day. I don't like sundried tomatoes so I swapped out those for some fancy olives.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made a vegan version of this. It's difficult to find vegan cream and  mine had coconut oil in it which partially ruined the taste of this dish. But it's otherwise a fantastic recipe. Loved the panko part which I'll use on many other foods. And I added some parsley before serving.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: 2d time made with Pecorino instead of Parmesan. DID NOT LIKE

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: L amde this with light coconut milk instead of cream - loved it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: i totally missed the rinse part of the canned beans, so mine had a bit more liquid but it was still good! I would also cut on the heavy cream just because 1 cup seemed like a lot and my color was lighter than the one in the photo, but i will still be eating it!! My roommate doesn’t like arugula so we used spinach instead and the salad is what saves the dish 100%. the acidity cuts the creaminess of the beans and makes the dish balanced. will definitely make again!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Fabulous! I had conjured the flavor of the beans but the arugula salad is a perfect foil to the creaminess of the beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used only a tiny bit of mascarpone, maybe 1 Tablespoon, no cheese at all. It was very tasty

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Excellent dish, would also be great for a party to make in advance, served over crostini. We loved it and will definitely make it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Anyone who says this recipe is “way too rich” clearly didn’t serve it with the big pile of delicious arugula salad on top as  directed in the recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made it twice now, once as is and the 2nd time using 1/2 n 1/2 and substituting about 1/4 c of the 1/2 n 1/2 with tomato sauce (as suggested by another poster). Still excellent! I accidentally did 1 can of chickpeas and 1 can of white beans the 2 nd, and I think the version with 2 cans of creamy beans is better.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was incredible. I dislike sundried tomatoes, but I found a container of roasted tomatoes in the deli section of Wegmans that hit the right note (concentrated tomato flavor without being leathery). I used about half the cream and upped the water to keep everything moving. Such a delicious and satisfying dinner!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Just adding another note. I LOVE this dish. I load up the red pepper. Lots of crunchy-ish onion - don’t cook to mush. Half a jar of roasted tomatoes - don’t chop too much. Less cream and oil. Cook the beans a while to absorb the flavor - it turns a gentle pink. The salad is a MUST!! Worth the huge ridiculous cost. Lots of lemon juice and a whole lemon zest. The cheese is really for flavor. This is not “cheesy”.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made it vegan by using the top off coconut milk and leaving out the parmigiana.   I also added a can of crushed tomatoes.  Adding oil cured olives adds a bit of fun.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
The arugula and panko dressing mixture was incredible, every piece of panko was a tiny textured flavor bomb, and a perfect compliment to the beans.
I inadvertently used double strength tomato paste (really good). Love that you can easily cut the recipe in half for two and not waste any beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The second time I have made this and it’s a real hit in our household. I served it on a bed of brown rice. Otherwise, cooked it as per recipe. Perfect for a quick, easy  and comforting Sunday night or week night meal in Winter.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Very satisfying, easy dinner. The ingredients meld together well and has a unique taste for a meal that isn’t the same old, same old. 

I didn’t feel like spending money on fancy cheese, so I just used Parmesan cheese and it added a delicious flavor. 

Will definitely be making this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I've been making this every week for months as written...it is so choice!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this for my family and 5 stars all around. I put tempeh in it for a bit extra protein. I also didn’t have any sun dried tomatoes and it was still delish! Next time I will

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was excellent! We had some leftover focaccia that we toasted in the oven and served on the side. Great blending of flavor and textures!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this as written, aside from adding the salad to individual portions instead of the pan. I’m wishing I had left the breadcrumbs separate to keep from getting mushy by time I eat the leftovers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made it exactly as written. Maybe do that. Prepped the bean aspect prior to assembling and oops ate a good portion out of pan. Can’t wait to have dinner!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Bland. Like, really bland. Finally added lots of chipotle, doubled the garlic, increased the salt and it was better, but still not something I want to make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: A whole cup of heavy cream isn't healthy. And a lot of cheese too. I'm glad readers had suggestions for substitutions. Alexa should re-think her recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is AMAZING. What an easy and tasty and filling dish. 10/10 no notes

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Didn’t have cream so threw burrata on top the second time I made this. It was delicious. Might try it with cottage cheese on top next time. Great for a quick lunch or easy weeknight dinner.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I only had whole milk and realized at the end I was out of sun dried tomatoes. They were still the best beans I’ve ever had. And I eat a lot of beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Incredible delicious. The sauce could be used with anything. I did sub coconut milk. I used watercress because there was no arugula at the store. Make the salad. It all feels restaurant quality. 10/10 and we will make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely worth the hype! Followed the recipe as written aside from deglazing the onion/garlic with some white wine and using half and half in place of cream. I kept the breadcrumbs out of the rucola mixture and let people assemble their own plates

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @Kyra Good idea. The bread crumbs are hard to master

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Warning: if you don’t have panko breadcrumbs yours may become a sandy mess (like mine). Will try again because the rest was great.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The recipe looks a bit odd, almost randomly thrown together, but it’s spectacular! Every ingredient is just where it’s needed- a perfect mix of warm and perky, soft and crunchy , just heaven. Maybe the best thing is the way it deals with the arugula-,a great hack for an awkward green. For the leftover I threw in some little pasta-,pasta fagiole style- and made the salad as a side- perfect as well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So delicious! New favorite way to eat cannellini beans. I want to experiment with less heavy cream or a substitute. I'll be making this one again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Just delicious!  And because I am a typical NYT cook, three tweaks & a boo-boo:  used red onion, because I have 10 of them for some reason. Added just a smidge of fresh rosemary. Was using sundried tomatoes in oil, so used some of the oil to sauté the aromatics. Forgot the water, which was fine. Served with a toasted slice of Tartine Bakery porridge bread, which by far was the most expensive part of this dinner (but so worth it).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is also great if you substitute dino kale for the arugula. If you do, bake the kale with the lemon zest, salt and pepper in a 450°F oven for 5-10 minutes. Complement with a dollop of chili crisp.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. We added some pork sausage. Really great.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Though it was tasty, I thought this was too much work for a few cans of beans.  Lots of different steps.  I did like the tangy greens on top of the beans- something I don't usually try and will probably do again.  Ate it for 2 days after, but it didn't get better in the fridge.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Kind of a soup, kind of not a soup. Great for cold weather. I used half and half as that’s what I had on hand and it was great!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Wow. To the commenter who said these were marry me beans..,you’re right. Unpopular opinion: the panko is not necessary. There’s so much going on here, flavor-wise, that the panko is really only there for texture and the fresh veggies give it that already. Speaking of which, if you want to feel a crunch in every bite, I would use kale over arugula or spinach like I did. The dish needs a stronger veggie than stands up to it, texture-wise and doesn’t wilt so fast. I recommend serving over Parmesan mashed potatoes (it was unreal) or plain rice over bread, so the sauce can soak into it better and it turns into even more of a creamy, luscious dish. I used a can of crushed tomatoes   sundried tomatoes and didn’t miss the tomato paste. I also added a healthy dose of paprika and more cheese. Make this now, you won’t regret it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
