commentBody: My new favorite NYT recipe. It’s that flavorful, and leftovers reheat beautifully. The only change I made was to use half & half instead of heavy cream based on a user comment and what I had on hand, and it came out full flavored and creamy. The greens tossed in parm and panko put the flavor over the top. Quick, simple yet big on flavor. This recipe is a winner!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Pair With Sparkling Rosé

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: used gluten free panko and forgot to put in the dried tomatoes and it was still a hit!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Delish. 
I opted to cook the beans from dried and  used bean broth in lieu of the water, otherwise i followed the recipe as written. Extremely satisfying results. And the beans when plated are lovely—orangey sauce with flecks of rich red sun-dried tomatoes smothering the pale cannellini’s topped by the glistening arugula peppered with panko and cheese.  Irresistibly cozy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I think this recipe was improved by using a pint of cherry tomatoes and 2% milk instead of cream.  When I closely followed the recipe, I thought it lacked texture and tomato flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! My teenage kids loved it. I’d recommend dicing the sundried tomatoes so they are spread out and not in clumps. This is not very spicy, so you could add a bit more red pepper if you prefer. Our new favorite recipe. We did it in a nonstick pan (the photo shows a cast iron skillet but I think that would react with the tomatoes).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Thankful for the many suggestions to use less cream.  I used 2/3 cup, could probably have used 1/3 cup and been OK. I didn't want to buy sundried tomatoes so I used 3 tbsp of Brooklyn Deli tomato aachar I had on hand, and some capers (it needed some punch). It was really fast and easy, I'd make it again. But with less cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I donʻt love sun dried tomatoes so replaced with Calabrian chiles- one of my favorite meals. So yummy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was excellent. Didn’t have arugula or breadcrumbs, so stirred in baby spinach until wilted. Can’t wait to make again as written.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I liked this recipe a lot. Instead of cream, I made cashew cream: half cup cashews, one cup broth, put it in the Vitamix for a minute and voila. I also added the tempeh for more veg protein

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I'm seeing wonderful variations on this thread, but I followed the recipe to the T and it was amazing. Definitely entering the regular rotation (and maybe I’ll even try some of the variations!)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: If you don’t have cream on hand but keep a stocked pantry, or if you just want to make it a bit lighter without compromising texture, try evaporated milk.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: It’s even better with some meat - had it with bacon/pineapple chicken sausage and it was so good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I halved the recipe for a quick lunch. Subbed (sorry Corry - epic comment btw) the cream for 5% greek yogurt and used a scant tb of ghee instead of all the oil. Worked for me!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Was surprised at blandness of it, really. Based on comments was expecting some alchemy that created a depth of flavor but in reality, when you look at the ingredients it tastes pretty much as read and there isn't much there.   Possibly, increasing the onion. garlic and pepper, cooking down the tomato paste even more, less of the cream which dampened the flavors.  In fact, I have leftovers so maybe I will just do the onion garlic pepper tomato paste bit again and add it to them.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Crushed cornflakes worked great as a substitute for panko breadcrumbs.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used coconut milk instead and it was delicious. I also added cayenne and some smoked chilis, which gave it a nice spicy pop.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Take out the arugula mix and you have a variant of British beans on toast (scoop onto your crunchy bread, add a shredded cheese that pairs well with Parmesan or pecorino, and toast until the cheese melts). The flavor profile is excellent.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Amazing meal that will enter the weekly rotation. My daughter made it, based on our recommendation and her family loved it as well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Panko substitute: Crushed cornflakes worked great in this recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I didn’t have red pepper flakes (always double check your pantry!) so I used a 1-2 teaspoons of Indian chili powder. So good. I don’t like cooking so easy, one pan dishes are my go tos. Adding this to my regular rotation!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was a winner! I cut the red paper flakes by half - it still had plenty of kick.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So so good! I'm an amateur trying to get into cooking and I think this is the first thing I have ever made that looks and tastes restaurant quality. I subbed half and half for cream and next time might trying upping the red pepper flakes to a full teaspoon next time, especially since the creamier portions offset the spice. Will also consider another's recommendation to sub a less mild cheese like pecorino romano to increase its presence in the sauce.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I have a different version that I call “crazy chickpeas”.  Obviously, my main ingredient is chickpeas.  Have never seen beans and milk in a recipe before in my life… 
In any case, I do use the chickpeas already  cooked.  In a skillet I sauté red onion, minced garlic, green bell pepper and, once  soft, I add tomato sauce and let that simmer for a few minutes.  Then I add that to the chickpeas, adding salt and white pepper and sun dried tomatoes and simmer a few more minutes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I would appreciate having nutritional notes included with all NYT recipes.  Thank you.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: hmmm... thought you shouldn't use cast iron pan with tomato/acidic liquid?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: For those who don't favor beans but like the general concept of this recipe - try subbing canned hominy. Rinse and use.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I wish I had read these comments earlier: went to the market to buy cream. I shouldn’t have. It was delicious but too rich. (and I used a fraction of the recommended amount)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Nothing like a big shot of heavy cream to turn a potentially healthy dish into a nutritional bomb.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: How are you supposed to eat this? With the greens and the beans all mixed together on a piece of bread? Or dip the bread into the beans, but then what happens with the greens? If bread is optional, then what? Someone's comment about leftovers makes sense to me - why not keep these separate as the arugula would not be good stored with the beans...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this after seeing the obsession on Reddit. I should have chopped the sun-dried tomatoes a little bit finer, but still delicious. I normally am not afraid of fat in dishes, but my jeans are on the verge of not being able to button (I’m currently laid off, which hasn’t helped) and so many commenters had success with reducing the cream - all that to say I just used a scant 3/4 cup of heavy cream instead. The real star of the dish in my opinion, and absolutely necessary, is the panko/arugula/lemony salad on top. It provides much needed texture and acts as almost as a gremolata, cutting through the richness of the dish. I don’t even love beans, but this was great. I flew by the seat of my pants and made this in the most chaotic way, without reading the recipe first or doing mise, but if you’re a more functional person than I am, this is an easy weeknight meal with not a ton of mess!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
First, yes it sounds good. But anyone who's ever cooked on a line knows you don't put any kind of sauce ON something you just got 'crispy'....because it won't be crispy even if you run to the diner's table. 

Sauce on the side, or on the bottom corner.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I've made this twice in 2 weeks. It lives up to the hype. Only thing I modify is I use spinach instead of arugula since my husband hates arugula. The second time I made this I skipped the breadcrumbs, when serving with bread I found the breadcrumbs didn't add much

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Step 1.5
Slice a pound of spicy sausage like keilbasa, habanero pork or chorizo and fry in the skillet until slightly charred.  Remove from the skillet and proceed with Step 2, keeping the sausage oil in the skillet.
Step 4
Add the sausage to the skillet with the beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: When this appeared as the recipe of the day today I knew I had to make it. The only small substitute was I used half and half instead of heavy cream. I’m sure I missed out a little bit but it was absolutely delicious and super easy to make for a weeknight meal. This is definitely going to be in the once a month rotation. It’s beautiful comfort food where we used pieces from a baguette to soak up every little bit of the sauce left on the plate. Bravo!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made it as is, so goods. Perfect quick weeknight meal.  Good bread toast is the key.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: After a day of sleet and freezing rain, I was looking for comfort food. I had everything I needed for these beans except the greens. No problem! Followed the recipe exactly, minus greens, and was delighted with the results. Bonus: these taste just as good cold!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Making this tonight, as it sounds so delicious! A simple comment is that I'd sure like to have the measurements in weights - and on all recipes - Just sayin. Thanks NYT cooking!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This recipe hits. I didn’t have sun dried tomatoes, so I deseeded some freshies I had on the counter and roasted them in the oven with sugar, salt, and olive oil until desiccated. I think I’ll do that again. I’m a big, strong boy, and half this recipe is almost too much for me.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Opted for half cream, half 2% milk, still delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made in two parts: dried great northern beans in an InstaPot, then here. Took Ann's advice, went w/high cream coconut milk, a big knob of stone ground mustard, a fat tsp of nutritional yeast (Bravo, Ann). Brown buttered the bread crumb toast. Folded in pecorino as per recipe; the rest I dusted atop the beans, then the crumbs, then ran under the broiler. Didn't get around to the arugula, so finished with chopped cilantro and lemon zest. My wife, a bean lover, gave two thumbs up solid.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was so delicious. I used RG coco beans and as someone else suggested I used a cup of coconut milk in place of the cream because that’s what I had. I also used TJ’s sun dried tomatoes. This recipe is a keeper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Used half-and-half and added a pound of Portuguese chourico sausage, which gave it more of a white bean chili feel.  Delicious with the lemon zested arugula - was grateful to use what I had from my garden. Win-Win!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: what is the point of eating beans if you are going to drown them in cream and cheese?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The point is eating delicious beans drowned in cream and cheese.  Yum!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: A little bland for my taste—rich but not that flavorful. IF I make again, I might use plain Greek yogurt instead of the cream to add a bit of tang. No reason to follow the salad part of the recipe to the letter. I’d probably just use whatever salad greens I have on hand.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
As with many NYT Cooking recipes, I went a little further with the spices, especially the red pepper flakes, and cheese.  I also added a splash of beef stock in step 4.  Also used spinach in place of the arugula.

I have been informed that it will be a regular part of the rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this with kale instead of arugula and whole milk instead of cream. Very tasty!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: These sound great but at more than 800 calories per serving they are more caloric than many desserts. Has anyone been able to alter the recipe in ways that make it still tasty but a bit less caloric?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Use yogurt and a bit of milk mixed instead of the cream. (Not my idea. Someone else suggested it, and it's still terrific.)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Just exceptionally good. The greens make the dish; don't skimp on them. Unbelievably luscious, moreish, and craveable. Instantly earned a spot in the standard rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this today and can't tolerate cream. I'd read comments on substitutions but before I added anything in its place, I tried it without the cream. It is wonderful! So for those who don't have cream or don't want to eat it with cream for whatever reason, know that it's more than ok without.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The hype is real! This is so delicious and will be going on repeat rotation. Easy and delicious and vegetarian - win, win, win. Don’t skip toasting the panko or lightly dressing the arugula - these components made the dish bright and crunchy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Yummy. Use a large, not medium, skillet. A 10" skillet barely held all the liquid, making it difficult to stir. Use a 10" straight-sided (2.5" deep) sauté pan or braiser.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Next time I make this, I will add more chili flakes - I will also use less cream. Maybe 1/2 and 1/2 or coconut milk. I found it just a little too rich.  Be generous with the lemon juice - it cuts the fat.  With all the cheese you will not have to salt at all.  I puréed my leftovers for a yummy bean dip for lunch the next day

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Like others, my wife and I were kind of shocked at how delicious this is. We did half cream and half 2% milk just to help reduce calories. Also cooked the onions a bit longer and simmered the bean mix a little longer for softer, creamier beans. The yin/yang of the creamy tomato-y-ness and tangy lemon - wow!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Fabulous! I used cranberry beans ..very good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this dish tonight, and it was amazing!!   I read the comments, and many said not to use cream, to use milk... so I used UNSWEETENED Silk Soymilk...and did everything else as the recipe states. I generally shy away from arugula since it is a bit bitter for my taste, but ooh-lala, these beans with the lemony arugula and the toasted bread crumbs... I made jasmine rice on the side... such a special dish.   Love it so much.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Superb! The arugula salad is critical and works synergistically with the beans. I It really melds well with the beans and brings out the flavor of both. My one suggestion would be to add even more sun dried tomatoes - at least 1/2 cup if not more.   Highly recommend!
Highly recommend.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was great! Used 1% lactose free milk mixed with lactose free sour cream instead of heavy cream. Froze the leftovers for another meal; would go well with chicken sausages.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Disappointing and not what I expected after a lengthy preparation time for something based on canned beans and onions. Oh, well, at least the ancient jar of dried tomatoes that's been in my fridge forever is now nearly empty.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Reading the comments I am noticing those who say the arugula to breadcrumbs ratio is off with too many breadcrumbs. Easy solution - use a 5z clam shell container of baby arugula and your ratio will be perfect. This is a divine dish as written that will become a staple for me (and that says a LOT) because my husband complains that I never make the same dish twice. Thank you, NY Times!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This is a wonderful recipe. I kept the spirit and flavor of the original recipe, but I made the following modifications:
- Because there are people who don’t eat dairy in my family, instead of heavy cream, I used cashew cream that I made by blending raw cashews with warm water. Turned out delicious, you can’t even tell that it’s not dairy cream.
- I used roasted red peppers in place of sun-dried tomatoes.
- I sautéed chicory in place of the arugula.
- I put fresh homemade croutons on top of everything instead of breadcrumbs.
Super satisfying, delicious, and simple meal that I will definitely make again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made exactly as written. And…meh. Though the salad topping is very nice, the beans themselves were just ok. Reminiscent of something one would get at a mediocre American-Italian restaurant chain, gloppy & undistinguished. A bit puzzling because we like the individual ingredients & used high-quality versions of them all. Felt like a waste—won’t bother with this one again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: A solid recipe. It was a bit too intensely tomatoey for our taste so next time I'd use a can of tomatoes rather than the sun-dried tomatoes. I used Rancho Gordo Alubia Blanco beans I had in the freezer along with some of the bean broth instead of water. I also chopped the arugula in the food processor because we don't particularly care for the texture of the whole leaves/stems.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made the recipe as written for a Super Bowl party with vegetarian friends.  The taste of the dish varied widely from bite to bite based on presence of sundried tomatoes. I found them very hard to chop into small bits without a food processor. Without the sdt, it was bland. And the heavy cream made it even more bland.  We ended up eating the arugula salad on the side as it was better than the beans. Leftovers in the trash!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is delicious. I have been happily eating leftovers all week. Next time, though, I might try reducing the cream by half and adding some crushed tomatoes instead. It's pretty rich.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I was skeptical but this was amazing!!!! And held up as leftovers.  It is the arugula salad with pistachios that really makes this satisfying and the beans have great flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The intro says this recipe is delightful ‘in every bite’ … but only if you mix it up! The picture, with the cloud of salad atop a substrate of gooey beans, looks great but ignores this final step. A gentle, thorough folding of the salad into the beans does, in fact, yield a delicious experience.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I loved this dish!  The creamy beans, the bitter greens with the bright lemon...it was such a great combo.  Next time, I'll cut the cream to 1/2 cup and increase the water.  Otherwise, it was a perfect and delicious dinner with some grilled bread.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I actually cook a lot from NY Times recipes and I have no idea how this has so many 5 star reviews....it was okay, no one at my table finished it though.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Oh. My. Gawd.

Did not deviate from recipe as written other than half of the red pepper. Served with cornbread. Weeknight fast, dinner party worthy. 

My family (kids, spouse, and boomer meat and three parents) was dubious when I served it. At the end of the meal, they admonished me for not doubling the recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious! We both had seconds.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Actually quite heavy and gross. All the positive comments lulled me into thinking this was an exciting dish. It’s mushy beans with salad on top and takes 40 minutes to prepare

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Adding to rotation! My personal changes were to nix the sundried tomatoes and use a 14oz can of chopped romanos. I also used a scant 1/4 c half and half because the beans bring their own richness. Use the salad on top! It's a match made in heaven!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Double the beans, use 1/2 butter beans, double the tomatoe paste, besides the sun dried tomatoes use some fresh chopped tomato, cut cream down by 70%.  Double lemon zest & juice. Use gluten free panko breadcrumbs for more crunch.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious, even when customized! I didn’t have tomato paste or sun dried tomatoes, but a drained can of diced tomatoes worked great. I also substituted spinach for arugula, and it was delicious. So yummy, I’m trying to keep myself from eating the whole pan.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is amazing! Only changes I made were subbing micro-greens for arugula…also using 1/2 cup of cream not 1 cup. Increased the water a bit to thin out.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I followed the recipe exactly, but the beans came out very pale and not at all like the photo. The only thing I can determine is perhaps I didn't cook the tomato paste long enough, but I was  hesitant- didn't want it to burn. If I make this again, I will add it earlier. The beans were bland, even after adding more red pepper and more salt. The salad/bread crumb portion was excellent.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The creamy, cheese beans were of course delicious, but I can't say I much liked the raw arugula piled on top.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as directed, and the result was very satisfying! Will try half cream, half milk or yoghurt in the future, per suggestions. I didn't ever develop the rich red colour of the photograph, and am not sure why. Would consider adding a few spoonfuls of good marinara to develop more of a sauce. For those remarking on the mess of chopping oily sun dried tomatoes: spoon 'em into a bowl and use kitchen scissors!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I halved the recipe (I live alone)  and added minced shallots and a fistful of diced dried sun-dried tomatoes in addition to the canned ones. Deglazed the pan with a splash of Sherry just before adding the cream. Pair with glass of Cabernet! 5 stars!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made according to the recipe without changes, but was not a fan. We ate it, but will not make it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Has anybody tried this with chickpeas instead?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Arugula is key for me!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: delicious over rice. can safely omit bread crumbs. the arugula pairs nicely but a different green leafy veg should work fine too

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I substituted oat milk for the cream and did not add any water. It was divine.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I am a big of NYT Cooking. But this recipe is just...not to my taste. Why pour heavy cream (a cup!) into the mixture? And tons of non-vegetarian cheese? And then you toss the panko with 2 T of olive oil and add another 4 T of olive oil? I get it if you don't want to go veg and you're looking for a work around...but this is not it. Beans...tomatoes...greens. Stop there.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Is the arugula salad supposed to be eaten separately or mixed in with the beans? Thanks in advance.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Stunningly good.  Made it with baby spinach, as that’s what we had, but otherwise followed the recipe.  Will definitely add to the regular rotation!   May try to cut back the cream a bit next time—but probably not… 🤷‍♂️

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this with Rancho Gordo white beans and it was fantastic.  I followed the recipe as is but used half & half rather than full cream.  It was still rich and flavorful.  A strength to this recipe is that each bite has a different feel - crunchiness of the panko, creaminess of the beans, and spiciness of the peppery arugula.  Definitely a keeper.  As one cook said "marry me beans!"

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Why use heavy cream if you’re just going to dilute it all with 1/3 cup of water? Why not just use normal cream?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I chopped up 1/3 of the arugula, and added it, along with all the lemon zest and lemon juice, a handful of cheese (not more) and a teaspoon of Cajun spice and paprika. Added all
That along with the beans

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @note. Yeah I added all the arugula, chopped,  to the beans, to keep my family from picking it out

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: It's just so good! Why is it so good?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @Aaron it’s the heavy cream. If you poured heavy cream and Parmesan cheese over julienned shoe leather I might eat it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was VERY RICH but was otherwise a big hit with my family…. I added one extra can of beans (kidney) and used just  3/4 cup cream, but otherwise followed the recipe. Next time I will use 1/2 cup cream only. Also: to make sure my kids ate the arugula, I chopped it and stirred it plain and undressed into the beans at the last minute.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This was a hit! 
I'm going to be using Love and Lemon's breadcrumb recipe next time

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We didn't love it. The half that was left made a delicious soup, though, with some other leftover things from the fridge.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Cooked this before exactly as written. Mmm.
I made this again with a change -- didn't have heavy cream but I did have sour cream, so used that instead. Different flavor, still very good. I didn't feel the need to add cheese since sour cream has a tanginess that heavy cream doesn't. 
Don't be afraid to modify this. Most bean recipes are open to tweaking to suit your palate or the ingredients you have on hand!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This satisfies my craving for pasta and simultaneously I get a ton of greens because I really stack on the arugula. I don’t have a “weekly rotation”, I just make what my body wants and now I’ve made this recipe about 10 times. It’s forgiving to substitutions. I almost never do heavy cream but instead do a dash of some sort of milk. I like to add pine nuts on top of the arugula. I don’t always add the panko. Thank you to the author of this recipe! I don’t think I would have tried this on my own.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The fear of fat expressed in the most popular comments is inexplicable. I made it as written, and found it delicious and well-balanced. The cream was the best part!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I swapped cashew/coconut vegan yogurt for the heavy cream but otherwise followed the recipe. It is absolutely delicious, potentially habit-forming.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I liked the tomato flavor with the beans but found the heavy cream way too much. Next time I’ll follow another reviewer’s recommendation below to use Greek yogurt and low fat milk. Added calories and heavy fat are fine occasionally if it’s really essential, but this was not the case here. The heavy cream detracted from the flavor and the healthiness.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is delicious, especially on the second day, I didn't think the arugula added anything, and I like arugula.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this recipe for the first time tonight - my only change was using 1/2 cup cream instead of an entire cup since 1/2 cup "looked to be enough". The end result tasted perfect and maybe a full cup would have been even more perfect, if possible. The dish is delicious. I can't say enough about the wonderful flavors and the ease of pulling it together - I could eat it all the time and just might, thank you very much.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Added chorizo and made it a meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This was absolutely spectacular! I made it tonight and everyone cleaned their plates. 
I was a bit skeptical at first because I didn't think the beans had enough flavor. Pairing them with the arugula mixture is nothing but genius. The two go together and make something altogether new and astonishing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! The crispy panko added a nice crunchy texture to the creaminess of the beans and the lemon in the greens was a zesty complement to the sweetness of the sun-dried tomatoes and cream. Definitely a keeper!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We loved this. I will do it again for sure. I did note that my jar of sundried tomatoes and the container of parmesan from my local supermarket are enough for two batches so next time I may just double the recipe. This was a beautiful meal that felt special for a boring Monday!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I only added about 1/3 the cream (to be fair that was all I had in the house) and then added some greek non-fat yogurt to make up the difference.  And I probably only used half the olive oil.  Other than that stuck to the recipe. And WOW - this is DELICIOUS.  The lemony arugula pairs so well with the beans - I did not see that coming. And that little crunch from the toasted panic - it just takes it next level.  Make these!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Very good. Substituted whole milk for the cream. I will increase the amount of pecorino next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: My husband made this for us last night, with a poached egg on top, and it was insanely delicious.  I want to make it again tonight.  We had a little leftover baby spinach so tossed it in with the arugula.  Would do that again, too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made with oat cream and it tasted great but with a lot fewer calories. Also used way less olive oil in the rucola salad. A half tablespoon is fine.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I used baby spinach instead of arugula and it was quite good. It wilted nicely. 

Leftovers smashed onto crusty toast were even better.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious.  I doubled the recipe generally but didn't double the greens topping, I would double that too next time.  I used whole milk that I had on hand and some lemon olive oil that was a gift to dress the greens.  I have the sense it might taste even better tomorrow.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I love Alexa Weibel's recipes and this was no exception. However, I did make a couple of adjustments. I halved the amount of olive oil with no ill effects. The sun-dried tomatoes I used were dried and I reconstituted them in boiling water, no olive oil needed. And instead of a cup of heavy cream, I pureed about 3/4 cup of the beans after draining/rinsing them and then mixed the puree with a 1/4 cup of heavy cream. The resulting dish was still satisfyingly rich and flavorful.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made as per recipe the first time and it was nice but very rich. Made again tonight - I made some cashew cream instead and used a spoon of cream at the end - tasted nice!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is totally YUM!  The panko breadcrumbs in the greens is a wonderful touch that I'll use for other green salads.  I think about half that quantity would be plenty in this recipe.  If I'm cooking for two next time, I'll half this recipe.  The beans are a wonderful small lunch alone if you have leftovers & your greens disappeared.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: what would one need to do for compensation should i make a routine out of a diet plan or healthy alternative involving simple things frequently enough to have money and save money? is there a night that is going to require more than pasta or the redundancies of white meat chkn or ground beef? I'm willing to subjuct myself to being subdued by reality and then creative more than cooperative.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Trying to make more vegan dishes and this was doesn’t disappoint. Even my meat loving hubby loved it!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I have made it thrice this month only for it to hit the spot every single time.
I did variations, each need driven - one time I didn't have arugula, so left the greens out and treated it as just a bean dish. Another time I had spinach and some corn that I wanted to use, so I added it in. Oh, before I forget, in my first try I followed the recipe with flying colors :)
What I am saying is, even with pantry, taste, and diet driven variations, this dish is a rockstar and a champion!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a PERFECT quick and easy fulfilling recipe! If you have alergies will need to change things but for others go ahead and try it EXACTLY per the recipe and after decide if you need to change it?! Thanks for this treat!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Ignore the purists; all of the adaptations in the comments show just how sturdy this recipe is. You can tweak quite a bit and still get a creamy bowl of beans and tomatoes. I haven't made it with the full cup of cream and I don't ever plan to.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this for the first time a year ago. I have eaten the rocket salad recipe from this a minimum of two times a week ever since. I have served it at Christmas and Easter lunches and dinner parties. It is one of my favourite recipes I have ever tried. I now always have toasted breadcrumbs and a nice block of pecorino or Parmesan and lemons in the fridge so I can make the salad.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolute magic. Don’t change a thing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I wanted more of a tomato flavor. I added a can of diced tomatoes. Next time, I will use the entire 6 oz can of tomato paste and use twice as much garlic. I used coconut milk instead of heavy cream, but did use some of the Pecorino Romano. Yummy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Two modifications I've made: subbing yogurt for the cream because that's what I have on hand usually, and adding 1/2 to a whole jar of capers at the very end, including the brine, to up the acidity and brightness.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I love this best made with butter beans. I like to mash up some of them in the pan to add creaminess. And I prefer doubling the sun-dried tomatoes, using an entire jar. So comforting and even better the next day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Followed the recipe exactly and it turned out great. Drizzled a little chili crisp over my serving because I like it a little spicier.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Does everyone spoon out some beans and top with the arugula, or is it better to put a spoonful of the beans on each plate and then top with some of the arugula

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @kellnels I added the arugula pile to the top as stated, but then it was gross soggy for leftovers. Next time I’ll top scoop of beans individually with arugula mix (made as needed) with breadcrumbs added seperate too to keep their crunch.  Big leftover house here.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this as written but subbed ricotta for the cream as that's what I had in the fridge. Came out hearty and tasty. Agree with all, the toasted panko, lemon, parmesan tossed arugula made this great! I live in a cohousing community where we have meals together twice a week and will definitely make this when it's my turn to cook!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was amazing!  Way better than expected. The beans have excellent flavor and texture, and the lemony crunchy arugula topping is a nice contrast. I used oat milk   a scoop of plain coconut yogurt instead of the cream, and added generous nooch to the beans, to keep it vegan.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: for those looking for a non-dairy option for the cream that truly behaves like a dairy cream, nutpods unsweetened coconut creamer works beautifully. I love regular coconut cream but it can sometimes mess with the texture of things in my experience. I skipped the cheese and used GF panko breadcrumbs for a gluten free, dairy free meal that was very satisfying.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this vegan, based on Ann's suggestions in comments...and perfecto! This will enter our monthly rotation. It hits all the flavor sensors and is budget-friendly. Husband liked it so much, he suggested serving it to meat-eating extended family, sure they would dig it, too. Thanks, Ann (and NYT's Alexa). Wish the NYT's writers would ALWAYS include ways to veganize. It's usually not too tough...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: A little more entailed than it originally appears, but well worth the effort! The flavors blend so well together. The crunch from the bread crumbs made the dish!  I did lessen the fat a bit by adding half and half, some heavy cream, and some Greek yogurt.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
F
flavor flavor was very good

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: hmmm  I don't understand all the praises for this recipe.  I opted not to use cream so blended some of the beans and some soaked cashews to make a non-dairy cream (because I didn't have cream) -- but otherwise followed the recipe.  It was bland.  Edible (as one would expect from beans and tomatoes) but not interesting.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: When you change a major component of a recipe the results aren’t the same.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I will put this recipe on repeat. I cooked my Rancho Gordo buckeye beans in chicken broth and herbs, garlic onions celery and carrot in the insta-pot for 15 minutes. Allow to depressure and cool in the broth. I followed the recipe but used coconut cream. I also substituted preserved lemon in the dressing and a splash of red wine vinegar. It hit all the points. Salty,creamy,crunchy,bitter greens and bit of sweet with the dried tomato. One of the best bean recipes I've ever made!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious!  Made as a side with grilled bone-in center cut chops marinated in Italian salad dressing. 
Seems complicated to read, but very easy

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made it without any modifications.  Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a terrific side dish with great flavor, but found it to be a disappointing main attraction. Left something to be desired… when served with bread, feels like an unsatisfying carb overload.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Restaurant quality delicious!!! This would be the star of a dinner party or weeknight hero!  11/10!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The hype is real! Thanks to other reviewers, I used things I already had in my kitchen with excellent results. Whole milk, kale and cherry tomatoes were great substitutions. Will definitely make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I didn’t have panko, I added mushrooms because I had some in my fridge I wanted to get rid of, and I added gochujang with the tomato paste for extra spice. I know I didn’t really make the proper recipe but it still tasted good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I could serve this once a week, 52/weeks a year and no one in my family would mind.  5 stars.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I admit I was shocked by how much I enjoyed that first bite of salad greens and bean tomato mush combined - it was *really* good. Easy and comforting, a great snowy day meal w some toast. Yum!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Ok this is AMAZING and perfect in almost every way and I hate to complain because obviously this recipe-writer is an absolute genius - BUT ! If I had read the recipe all the way through I would have saved some of the garlic to toss with the arugula salad as it was a bit bland when on top of this delicious and tasty dish! 
I made the beans from dried and breadcrumbs from scratch and it was so worth it (especially the beans).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made several times and we're always squabbling over who gets the leftovers for lunch. A NYT winner!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is so delicious! I had burrata in the fridge this last time and one ball per person just took this to the next level.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is quite tasty! Like most people, I made some modifications—I added chopped fresh tomatoes and Parmesan rind at the simmering phase. The rind gives the umami flavor without the extra fat from the cheese, and the fresh tomatoes was mainly because I had some old ones I needed to use up! More importantly, I simmered it low on the stove for way longer, I’m talking 45-60 minutes, and kept adding water as it concentrated down. The result was sooo silky and flavor-packed, it’s worth the extra time!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I decided to try combining Marry Me Chicken with this recipe. Turns out I like just the beans better

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is criminally good for how easy it is. I would never go to a restaurant and order a bean dish, but if I ate this at a restaurant I would be thinking about how soon I could come back and have it again. I’ve made it twice, both times reduced the cream just a bit (maybe 2/3 c rather than a full), otherwise made exactly as described, and it’s phenomenal. Who knew two cans of beans could be this good?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: this is such a fun shared dish!!! my dinner party guests said it's everything delicious about penne alla vodka, but with the added freshness of the arugula and bite of lemon

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We've tried this recipe twice, and the second time we smashed the beans about halfway. This adds to the creamy texture, I'd highly recommend it

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this as is, but with half & half instead of cream and it was great. I also used gluten-free Panko breadcrumbs because that's all I had.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This dish was a hit! I made it ahead of time; next time, I won't add the beans, sundried tomatoes, and liquids until much closer to the time to serve. The bean mix absorbed the added liquids quickly & almost completely, so I had to add quite a lot of broth before serving (didn't want to add more dairy), and the end result was still less sauce-y than I'd have liked. However, the flavors were amazing & the arugula mix takes this recipe to the next level. I'll make this one again & again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Didn't put in the cheese (didn't have any on hand) and this was delicious. Added a diced potato as filler. Flavors were surprisingly bright. A keeper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So good BUT it tasted better to me BEFORE I added parm (yes the good expensive stuff) so next time I will just skip that step.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So excellent! If you know you will have leftovers, I recommend reserving some of the breadcrumbs and arugula and cheese and mixing them with part of the lemon mixture and oil on an as needed basis. having fresh arugula and crunchy breadcrumbs is part of the delight of this dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: #1 recipe obsessed. I serve with a big scoop of Greek yogurt, fresh herbs, and pumpkin seeds

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved this recipe. For anyone (like my wife) who’s not thrilled about bread being the primary carb, we tried a pasta variation and it was fantastic. We used ricciolini (but I think pearl couscous or any small shell-shaped pasta would work just as well). We loosened the bean mixture with a bit of extra water to make it saucier, cooked the pasta separately, then folded it straight into the beans. The sauce worked its way into all the nooks and crannies and turned the whole thing into an ultra-decadent, elevated mac-and-cheese–adjacent situation. Highly recommend if you want to keep the spirit of the dish but skip the bread.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is so good I dream about it and can’t wait to make it again

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Pretty tasty recipe as is, I’ve made it twice so now feel I can modify to my likening but still honoring the recipe.
6 tbsp of oil seems excessive, I did 1 for the panko, 1 for the onion and garlic etc, and 1 for the arugula salad topping. I used the oil from the jar of sun dried tomatoes for the sautéing part. The amount of fat in heavy cream makes this very indulgent, so I subbed 1/2 and 1/2 and it worked out fine. Tried subbing whole milk once and it curdled, tasted ok but not great texture. I cut back on the lemon juice in the topping as it was way too tart for me. There are lots of recipes similar online, some called marry me beans, but this is unique and delicious with the fresh arugula, lemon, breadcrumbs on top.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this with chickpeas instead of beans and it came out delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: subbed half the cream with goat cream cheese. also added anchovies

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We followed the recipe and used upwards of 1/2 cup of cream and 1/3 cup water.  IT WAS DELICIOUS !!  Do the bread too!  Fed a family of 4 and we were thrilled!  Devine! Just delicious..especially if your daughters execute it all !

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Wow, the flavors in this all came through. Whomever created this recipe is a genius.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This was incredible. We did half the heavy cream and also cooked the onions a bit longer in the beginning - you aren’t going to get translucent onions in three minutes. Bacon or pancetta would be a welcomed addition but not necessary. We also used dehydrated sun-dried tomatoes that we rehydrated in hot water for about an hour beforehand. Helped cut some of the calories and didn’t make too much of a difference in the end product. 

Will be adding this to the rotation for sure.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious. The whole family loved it, even our 5yo who normally doesn't like arugula. I'm certainly not one to shy away from heavy cream, butter, etc, but I will say this was extremely rich and creamy, like a decadent pasta alfredo or alla vodka. Next time I would probably use half the cream and sub in stock to make it a little lighter and brothier.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: A cup of cream? For two cans of beans?  Too much!   I am Italian… We don’t cook that way… Maybe a can of reduced fat coconut milk. And then a drizzle of “real” cream on top of each serving. If I were doing this recipe, I would use at least 6 cups of cooked beans, and I would add some passata or crushed tomatoes in addition to the tomato paste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
@Marilisai
I agree. More beans and less cream.  Used spinach (no arugula available) and more lemon.h

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Finally made this and followed the recipe pretty closely except for substituting milk for heavy cream. It was really delicious. The arugula salad is essential. I followed someone’s suggestion to put the Panko crumbs on top rather than mixed into the salad so that they retain their crunchiness. Will definitely make this again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Didn't enjoy this, so probably will not make again. Not sure where I went wrong, but mine came out out thick, dry, and tasteless. Followed recipe exactly except for one thing - I used half and half instead of heavy cream, as did many others who have commented here. I much prefer Beans and Greens Alla Vodka, and Ras el Hanout Chickpea and Spinach Stew.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I 100% agree!  Mine was the furthest thing from creamy - so much so that I was thinking that perhaps I was supposed to mash or blend some of the beans.  
Tasty but there are better recipes out there in my opinion too. 
I enjoyed the salad part and will suffer through the remaining beans but hardly creamy at all.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is the most satisfying and delicious meal I have in my arsenal. It's so easy, healthy, and filling!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was good. I made some slight adjustments... 1/2 cup ceam, more water to replace cream with 1 tsp better than bouillon vegetable concentrate. 2x 19 oz canned beans cause thats what I had. I also only dressed the greens on our individual bowls, without measuring the lemon juice/olive oil. Sprinkled the parm & bread crumbs on top. Was yummy, would make again for sure!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Easy weeknight meal indeed. It's super packed with flavor but the sauce was very concentrated and thick. I wanted to add liquid to make it "saucier", not brothy, but I was afraid of diluting the flavor at the end of the cooking process. I made a few different substitutions to lighter ingredients than the ones listed (e.g. milk instead of cream) and it still delivered with crusty bread and that zippy, peppery salad.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: A great, easy recipe, which I have now made three times. Agree with others - less cream yields a better result, and add a tin of diced tomatoes. I also recommend mixing additional lemon zest and juice into the bean mixture. And more Parmesan doesn’t hurt, either.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I’ll be honest, I didn’t have high hopes for this dish. How can a can of beans, tomato paste, sun dried tomatoes, and cream be anything other than average, even when you add a load of garlic?

Boy, was I wrong. This was outstanding. As in, I need to make this for my friends and tell everyone about it. 

Make it just as it is, eat with warm sourdough and butter, and remember that joy in the world can be found in a simple meal like this.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: A simple trick is to soak dry beans and then store them in the freezer.  They cook very rapidly and the flavor is far superior to canned ones.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: One of the best recipes from NYT

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: For anyone wondering (like I was), I made this recipe exactly as written and it was super delicious. No need to sub or reduce the cream. Will be making this (unchanged) again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. Reminiscent of Rajma. I used 1/2 the oil and cream - still very rich. The arugula, panko and lemon salad on top makes the dish. I also used pecorino, so no need to add much other salt. A definite new regular on my household menu!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Yummy! Make earlier and add cream and Parmesan when you reheat and serve for easy last minute meal. I was heavy handed with the red pepper flakes and lemon juice and light on the panko. Big hit!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious and very easy to make. It was comfort food for sure, but also felt like a fancy treat. I will also be making that arugula salad on its own for lunch!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Instantly a new household favorite. Eat it on its own, on French bread, or a number of other different ways. It’s creamy, rich, savory, and a great rainy/snowy day comfort food.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Just subbed out matzoh crumbs for the panko.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This dish is sooo good, quick, and easy to make. Made for my toddler who devoured it. Pecorino is essential to stand up to the richness of the cream in my opinion.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: What a fabulous recipe. Made exactly as written and just thoroughly enjoyed it. The creaminess of the beans, the sweetness of the tomatoes, the lemony crunchiness of the arugula and panko combine in one wonderful quick meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this almost exactly according to the recipe. The only thing I did differently was keep the salad separate, and we added it to our individual bowls. Served with a crusty baguette, received much applause. Thanks for another great bean dish!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Substituted one tbsp of Greek yogurt for the heavy cream and turned out fantastic

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I was a little worried that might family might not like this, but they loved it!  The contrast between the creamy beans and the lemony greens is great.  I used 3/4 cup half and half instead of 1 cup of cream as my family likes things a little less creamy and added a few extra sun dreid tomatoes. otherwise followed the recipe.  Will definitely make this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this last night and it was a hit with the whole family, including my teen who doesn't usually like beans and always wants meat. Due to various dietary restrictions among the family, I made it with gluten free panko breadcrumbs and vegan heavy cream, although I did use Pecorino Romano (sheep cheese is easier for us to digest). Worked great.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Is it possible to give this 10 stars? I did make a few modifications because I wanted to use what I already had in the kitchen. I substituted full fat unsweetened coconut milk for the cream. I also added a handful of chopped basil at the end. So delicious and filling! We all loved it - even the teenagers!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Really easy, really lovely. I used butter beans and added in a bit of smoked paprika.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Can Greek yogurt be subbed for the heavy cream?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I wouldn't recommend it. The yogurt will curdle in response to both the acidity (from the tomatoes) and the heat, so the results will be lumpy rather than creamy, and you'll also add a tanginess that may or may not be what you're looking for.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious. I did only add half the cream and a quarter of the cheese. Still delicious! I am sure it would be dramatically more scrumptious with the full complement of cream and cheese.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Panko ruined this for me. Would much rather feast on Ali Slagle’s Cheesy White Bean Tomato Bake which is the quintessential comfort food!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Creamy eggy jammy beans

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made dairy free with a can of coconut milk instead of heavy cream and water and nutritional yeast instead of parm. LOVE

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is perfect and I’m obsessed with it. No notes. Just a comforting, creamy, crunchy delicious vegetarian dinner. Don’t overthink it and don’t skip the bread on the side.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious!
And the arugula, toasted panko, cheese, lemony topping was excellent!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The heavy cream was complete overkill here.  Half and half or even whole milk would be better.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was good! We opted to add fresh cherry tomatoes which added some brightness, but used Parmesan which didn’t give as much flavor as we’d hoped. Next time will do pecorino!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Really good, 5 stars. Fast and easy. I used whole milk instead of cream, a small can of fire roasted tomatoes instead of sun dried and water. Also had a partial can of chipotles in adobo that i used and left out the red peppers. Turned out a bit spicy but really good! If you don’t have panko, just toast some sourdough bread and make crumbs. Keeper recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Yummy, easy to make, and adaptable. I used spinach instead of arugula, because that's what we had on hand. Came out great.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Can I make this vegan without compromising the flaor?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made it using vegan cream and vegan butter and it was delicious. We did use real Romano cheese, but I would imagine you could sub that out for either a vegan parm or something like nutritional yeast? It was super yummy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Use common ashew cream!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: There is a helpful ingredient substitution guide at the bottom of this (and nearly all) NYT recipes. This includes vegan substitutions for dairy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this last night and it was delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Seemed like a lot of work to make a dish with canned beans until I tasted it. Wow! This is delicious. Only change I made was to add fresh basil to the arugula.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was a hit at our house. Used whole milk instead of cream and I don’t think we missed the cream at all. Still really creamy and delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved this and will keep it on my active duty list. The only change I made to the recipe was the addition of the remains of a rotisserie chicken, which I shredded and stirred in at the end.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Has anyone made this ahead of time? How does it keep/reheat?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Eating the arugala salad mixed with the  panko breadrumbs resulted in a consistency that reminded me of what my salads taste like if I forget to wash and spin my greens--gritty and unpleasant. I can't believe anyone would come up with this idea. Actually, putting in the breadcrumbs makes washing and spinning the greens unnecessary as the grit on the greens would be indistinguishable from the breadcrumbs

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Serve leftovers on toasted sourdough with a fried egg on top. Yum!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This is a perfect dish. I substituted cashew butter mixed with water for the heavy cream.
Looks beautiful and tastes fantastic.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!  Only change I would make is to add 1-2 T of sugar to offset the heat of the chiles.  That way salt, sweet, hot, and fat are all balanced.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was a fantastic dish. I even added short fasta to the leftovers the next day and, dare I say, the sauce tasted even better. It's definitely a win for the sundried tomato lovers

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Outrageously delicious! i thought the arugula on top might be weird, but it was perfect, just perfect.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The arugula with lemon and crunchy panko crumbs really makes this dish come together. I wish I knew exactly how many cups of minced yellow onions to use (vs “medium”). I’d make this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Susan, the heavy cream is most likely used because the acid in the tomatoes will curdle milk or half n half or yogurt.  It would be good to mention these chemistry bits in the recipe because people often want to cut the cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved it - look forward to making it again. Completely luscious and straight forward to pull together.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!  Made exactly as written.  The greens complimented the beans perfectly

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Do you add the oil from the tomatoes or drain them first?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used a teaspoon or so of the tomato oil.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: As my teen daughters said, "de-lurrrr-cious".  I concur.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So I blended the mixture before adding in the beans to make a creamier, less chunky sauce and I loved it. Will definitely make again. I also used whole milk instead of heavy cream just because I didn’t have heavy cream on hand and it was still delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is very very very good. Scrumptious and ended up licking the plate. Followed the recipe exactly and loved the results. Would maybe sub Greek yogurt for cream next time to see if the tang imparts an impactful flavor - but it’s fine as is.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Yum, yum, yum! Very tasty. Next time will use less cream (I used creme fraiche thinned with milk). But otherwise as written.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Thoughts on subbing in coconut milk or a non-dairy milk for the cream? What would be best?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: One cup of cream is way too much - titrate it in until you’re satisfied with the texture. I added an extra can of beans and a pound of fresh spinach (was out of kale), and it’s delicious but definitely heavier than it needs to be because of all the cream. Don’t be shy with the red pepper flakes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Excellent. A variation might be to replace panko crumbs with homemade croutons using the optional bread.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This looks good! Not a huge sun dried tomato fan. Is there any suggestions on what I can sub for that? Thanks!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I'm not a fan, either. I think I might put in some fire-roasted diced tomatoes and go smoky instead of sweet ...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Meant to note I added 3 leaves of chard — chopped — to the beans part since I needed to use it and wanted to up the healthy factor. Delicious and nutritious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Truly elite recipe. Very sad when we were done eating

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Fantastic. Used half the red pepper. Would definitely make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: It’s very bland. If you like mushy tomato flavor, this recipe will hit a home run for you. But there isn’t much going on here, flavor-wise, beyond too much tomato flavor and a soft texture from the beans. That’s it. Such a disappointing recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Tasty, but needs more liquid, as the beans were almost pasty in texture by the end. Next time, I’ll sub veggie broth for water and increase volume to 1/2 c - 2/3 cup. Someone asked about reheating—the flavors meld together really well, but the beans are even thicker the next day. Would reheat with liquid. Arugula salad is a must. I also put a fried egg on top to make it a meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Followed the recipe but the breadcrumbs ruined it for me. A mushy gritty consistency.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Gritty mushy mess without much flavor. It was disappointing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this exactly according to recipe EXCEPT I don’t like sun dried tomatoes so I subbed a little bit of chopped roasted red bell pepper from a jar. The beans on their own are good, not great, but when piled with the tangy acidic salad, breadcrumbs, and a dusting of pecorino it was delicious. The second time I ate it I toasted and buttered some sourdough bread on the side. Will definitely make again! So much fiber, so easy to make, wonderful flavor payoff.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious! I used half and half because it is what I had on hand and it was still very thick and decadent. Could easily be slimmed down further - less oil in the panko, onions, and/or dressing, whole milk, or just less panko - all could work great.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Really enjoyed this! Was a little unsure about a cool salad on top of hot beans, but they really did complement each other. The arugula stayed crisp, and the lemon worked well with the creamy spiciness. 

Used less cream just because it seemed like enough, but otherwise followed the recipe as written.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made it with coconut milk to cut the calorie count down a little.  Fabulous.   Only had spinach which I cooked it for about 2 min.  Everyone else same and so good. Better the second day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Subbed out heavy cream for cashew cream as I'm lactose intolerant. We added chili crisp for some extra crunch and spice. Nice and easy dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Used leftover rice   1 can of white beans (instead of 2 cans). Didn’t bother with zest and omitted red pepper. Still super tasty. Will add more Parmesan next time!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Used about half the oil called for in the recipe. 2/3 c panko was way too much. Would do 1/3 c next time. No need for heavy cream. Used whole milk and plenty creamy. Store out of sundried tomatoes in oil so used without instead. Tasty and quick dish. Leftovers also reheated well. Will make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Once I read the recipe I got a little impatient to make it and just used what I had: no sundried tomatoes and added big leaves of basil to the arugula. Still crazy delicious. Very satisfying. Yum.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Changes I made: Reserved the bean liquid and used it instead of water, and ended up adding about double the amount to thin the final dish out some. I will absolutely make this again. 

This is legitimately one of the most delicious things I've ever eaten. Using the bean liquid, with all that starch, really turned the beans into a super glossy and smooth base for the delicious crispy and crunchy salad on top. Be sure to taste for salt and pepper before serving, as should be standard practice.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Wonderful recipe 
Doubled the spice 
Added wilted chard 
Will definitely make again- it’s like a vegetarian Sunday supper!
