author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Next time I’ll follow suggestions to spice it up and try half and half or milk; cream seems like overkill.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I substituted veggie broth for half the cream to cut down on the dairy. It was still rich and packed with tons of flavor. It was a huge hit. Will be adding this to our regular dinner rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Pizza . . . brought to you by beans.  I loved this and will make it again.  I left out the panko, and reduced the water and cream a bit to keep it from being soupy from the lack of panko.  I served it with homemade croutons, instead of the crostinis.  The arugula is the perfect green for this.  So good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This dish surpasses all expectations and would be right at home in any fine-dining establishment. I followed the recipe to a T save for the salad, which I halved to serve two the first night -- it will only take a moment to toss together the other half of the salad ingredients to accompany the leftover beans tonight. I accompanied the dish with some toasted French bread (a more socially acceptable method for mopping the sauce than licking the bowl).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This looks so delicious! I’m wondering if I could make this for a camping trip ahead of time and then just heat up and then make the salad ? Any thoughts?
This is also also similar to another NYT recipe that has mozzarella and cannellini beans that you bake in the oven. Very delicious as well!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used half-and-half instead of cream. Simple and good, but maybe too much work for simple dinner. Make sure you use enough salt and hot pepper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used half the cream and my own homemade beans with some of their liquid. It worked well. It’s all about the contrast with the lemony greens.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Incredible dish, one of my favorites. I substituted spinach for arugula (needed to double the amount) and threw in some cut up chicken breast for more body. I recommend TRIPLING the red pepper count if you like some heat.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this tonight, so fabulous!  I agree the cream is unnecessary - the beans and the cheese are plenty rich and give a good mouthfeel. I used a mix of fresh Italian herbs -basil, marjoram, rosemary, parsley - because I had it to harvest.  Also added chard and sorrel for same reason - in season.  It's very filling, satisfying, and feels to be bursting with polyphenols!  Will add to regular rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Stop what you’re doing and make this right now. I make a LOT of NYT Cooking recipes and this is the first time I feel compelled to comment. This dish is a healthy, umami-rich flavor bomb. For its ease, use of mainly pantry ingredients, and flavor payoff for the cost, this dish is a total winner. I added fresh parsley and basil to the arugula because I had them, upped the chile, and omitted the cream, and I’m already dreaming about having the leftovers for breakfast tomorrow. On rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: If you want it mostly dairy-free, blend another can of beans with some water in place of the heavy cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Quick and delicious dinner. The contrast of the cool, lemony arugula on top of the warm beans is fantastic and the only tweak I'd make for next time is to double the amount of the salad.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The tomato and cream combo is delicious. These beans have so much more flavor than they should, given the simplicity of the seasonings! I added dried Italian herbs to the breadcrumbs, used fresh cherry tomatoes instead of sun-dried, added smoked paprika and frozen spinach to the sauce, and used regular spring mix instead of arugula. I cooked the beans from scratch (1 cup beans, 3 cups water, a dash of salt and baking soda, 30 min in the instant pot) instead of using canned.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I started making beans with this technique (tomato paste and milk for a sauce, some spice, some greens, pecorino and bread crumbs as desired) when Justine Doiron posted a lovely recipe with her version (with hot honey and massaged lemony kale) a few months ago. I’m glad to see the approach is making the rounds, since I’ve been telling all my friends about this magical new way to make beans for dinner! It’s cheap, easy, delicious, healthy, and endlessly adaptable.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I made a few changes (alongside halfing the recipe):
- Used Half & Half instead of cream and tomato compote instead of sundried tomatoes 
- Roasted up 4 small tomatoes I needed to use up and stick-blended them with the half & half before adding it to the skillet.

This was a really great dinner on a Monday night.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I cooked this as written except with beans that I cooked from dried rather than canned. So I needed a bit more water because there was no liquid from a can.

When I first tasted the bean mixture it seemed a bit bland. On a hunch I added a bit more salt, and all of the flavours popped out. Again maybe this happened because I used dried rather than canned beans, so no salt from the can. I ate this in a few meals so mixed the salad up fresh each time. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Glad to see Arugula stated by weight as the "packed cups" always left me wondering just what that really meant. With my simple kitchen scale I can weigh that out with a level of exactness that reassures me I'm on the mark. I use the Gram unit of measure to have better results.  FWIW, most plastic boxes of greens are 5 Ounces / 142 Grams but I've had some contain as much as 200 Grams of greens.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Served the beans in individual bowls and I served that salad in my bowl to taste how that worked, and it was complementary, but I wasn’t as comfortable eating as I worried about the greens getting soggy. My traditional husband was happy with the salad in its own dish, and that is how I would serve it to guests. He was delighted with the amount of left overs and reported the beans tasted even better the second day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: No need for heavy cream.  I used light cream and next time will try Half & half

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So I made dinner based on the idea of this recipe - and it was great. I short cutted by using up the bit of jarred sun dried tomato pesto I had left in the fridge combined with one can of white beans.  Served it with the arugula salad. I skipped the breadcrumbs because (boring) calories. This was a delicious, quick dinner and I'll definitely be back to make it as written!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Solid quick dinner with lots of pantry ingredients. Subbed 4 fresh Roma tomatoes for the dried. Add just after the tomato paste darkens up and reduce water. Reduced the amount of cream. Don’t skimp on the red and black pepper. Love the toasted panko and lemon zest. The arugula holds up well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Simple and delicious meal. The whole family, including kids, loved it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Made once with heavy cream and next time with whole milk, half cup, and not a big difference at all.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Here at the edge of the known universe (Tasmania) I don’t have access to fresh mushrooms other than regular garden variety button mushrooms and their more mature form. So I complemented one and a half cups of chopped button mushrooms with rehydrated porcini and shiitake mushrooms. I also added the left-over mushroom soaking liquid to the stock and used gluten-free pasta. It was simply delicious. Thank you, NYT Cooking!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Absolutely a new favorite! 
Everyone loved it.

You could opt to use 1/3 c of breadcrumbs instead of 2/3 if desired.  Or save the extra to sprinkle on plates.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as written and it’s pretty nice, but i knew going in it was WAY more rich than what we would usually have at home.  Next time I’d cut the cream in half, add one more can of beans.  The salad on top was crazy and good! Had more toasted panko than we needed, so I can trim that next time also.  Fun recipe and a keeper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Made this last night, skipping panko because I didn't have any, and subbing arugula with tuscan kale, because that's what I had in the fridge, and topped with some feta. Great quick, easy, and cheap weeknight meal. 

Agree with everyone here that 1 cup heavy cream is too much -- so what I did instead was just add heavy cream until it looked the shade of red that's in the picture. 1 cup would've been a fairly pale pink; I got to the brick red with at least half that, and it tasted great.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: What did everyone serve with this, besides bread/toast. I’d like a side with it, but can’t decide what would work? Maybe a green veg?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I thought it was enough without the bread.  If you really need something with it, maybe grilled chicken, tofu,  or pork chops?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was really delicious!!! I added some leftover shrimp and that took it even more over the top!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: These beans are extra extra creamy, and delicious with the arugula salad. I made the recipe as written, using pecorino romano. I think next time I’ll cut the cream in half, add a little more water, and simmer longer.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I'm curious how Pep cooked the Marcella beans for this recipe.  I have a few bags of the Marcella’s in the pantry.  Thought I might use one for the dish, too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as described to Step 4, but subbed half&half for cream. I live alone, so combined the toasted panko w/lemon zest & cheese & set aside, Made the dressing w/lemon juice, olive oil, cheese, salt and pepper, & kept separate.  My dinner tonight: 1/4 of the cooked bean mixture, a handful of arugula w/ a spoonful of the lemon dressing, put on top of my beans, and sprinkled  with the breadcrumbs. It was delish! And I can do it again, several more times!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was a hit! Loved the creaminess - really good with homemade tortillas.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as directed, (unusual for us) but we always prefer using dry beans cooked in the Instapot with some salt and a garlic clove. We are book marking this into our “Company Worthy” folder~

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is so wonderful, holy heaven! I used light cream and it was still VERY heavy. Next time, I’ll cut that back by half, and maybe add a little more lemon to the leftovers to brighten them. Make this, it rules!!!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: What a great entree!  Easy, tasty, and filling, and a vegetarian guest loved it and asked for the recipe.  Bypassing editorial comments in search of helpful tips, one reader had asked about making this in advance, which gave me a great idea.  I made another batch of beans (as written) minus the panko and arugula, and froze it in 2 1-qt freezer bags.  Tomorrow night after work all I have to do is defrost in warm water, bake and top with the panko and arugula.  Definitely recommend!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: For the win! This was a great way to use up languishing items in my fridge (1/2 jar of sun-dried tomatoes, leftover diced/crushed tomatoes, veggie stock). I substituted a combination of diced/crushed tomatoes for the tomato paste to get them out of my fridge and used veggie stock instead of water. IMO could use a little less cream (maybe 3/4 cup) and forgo the panko as not absolutely necessary. A great dish and a hit in my house! Adding to the weeknight rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Pretty simple, straight forward cream sauce. Felt like a take on mac ‘n cheese but with beans. I would probably halve the cream (or take out almost entirely) and replace with broth. The full cup seemed to detract from the flavor of the other ingredients. Also, home cooked beans would really elevate it as well, but I understand the idea was to spruce up canned ones. 

The arugula salad was great and having some bread to go with was crucial!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made a dairy free version with coconut milk and nutritional yeast, replacing the heavy cream and parm, and quite enjoyed it. Though next time I will add roasted garlic as a spreadable addition for some toast squares in place of some of the panko. Tasty.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is  truly delicious and easy.  You can save some calories by swapping out half and half for the heavy cream.  Also, if making this for 2 people for two lunches or dinners, divide the "bean part" and make and add the arugula when you eat.  Adding the arugula on top of the entire recipe only leads to soggy arugula if you have leftovers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Got some fresh arugula out of the garden and used up some stuff from the pantry. A great meatless meal. I subbed yogurt (goat) for the cream due to a cow dairy allergy and it worked.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I added a 3rd can of beans to stretch this recipe. I was generous with the garlic, red pepper flakes, sun dried tomatoes, and lemon zest, but I cut the amount of cream and parmesan in half.  This was unique and surprisingly good; it is definitely a great meatless recipe. The arugula greens mixture on top was perfect .  I will definitely make this again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I skipped the arugula, as I didn’t have any to hand, grated some lemon zest over the finished beans and topped with the panko - brilliantly simple comfort dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Excellent. Made it as stated, with the exception of doubling amount of sun dried tomatoes, only bc I like them so much. Had no desire to change anything. Will definitely make it again. Very tasty.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: It’s the lemon salad that makes this.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This has potential but mine ended up very dry.  I have leftovers and will try adding milk or bean broth plus tomato paste.  If I can’t solve the dryness, not worth making again.  I’m disappointed since this seemed like it should be good.  Not sure what I did wrong.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: 5 stars - but I modified to make it more nutritious and less heavy. Did half cannellini and half garbanzo beans, used 3 ripe tomatoes instead of paste and sun dried tomatoes, and instead of cream I used aquafaba from the canned garbanzo beans to thicken the sauce. Instead of red pepper flakes I used garlic salt and turmeric, and instead of Parmesan I used Feta. Instead of panko, I used torn crispy focaccia bread. Topped with arugula and lemon juice and pepper. *chef’s kiss*

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Heavy cream was a little much, next time would start with less (1/3 cup?) and add more as needed. Didn't have sundried tomatoes so I used tomato powder which added a little bit of the tang in but without them it was a little tomato-soupy. I used Mantequilla beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Fabulous. I found this so so satisfying. Wouldn’t change a thing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Using what I had on hand, I subbed milk for the cream and Italian bread crumbs for the panko. Delicious. I loved the arugula crunch with beany creaminess! I had a lot of bread crumbs left over, though, so next time I will use more arugula.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this for myself and 3 teens and we all loved it.  The lemony, crunchy salad is a perfect foil to the creamy, tomatoey beans.  Thanks NYT Cooking!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Good.  I made a half recipe - one can beans.  I used the liquid from the can in place of cream or milk.  Very easy and fast.  Good for making for a quick dinner.  Loved the panko with arugula!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Thought we had sun-dried tomatoes on hand, but they were not the kind in oil. We soaked them for 15 minutes in just enough oil to cover, and it still turned out perfectly. This one was a huge winner, and it paired nicely with the non-alcoholic dirty lemon tonic.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I started making this and realized it’s essentially vodka sauce for beans (minus the vodka and lots more cream). I added a bit of oregano. Also a dash of balsamic. Instead of the salad on top, I mixed Parmesan and lemon zest with the panko and sprinkled it on each bowls.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
After following the excellent recipes and taking great advice from commenters on this website for 4 years, I’ve finally developed the confidence and some knowledge to be able to riff on a recipe to make it my own. In this case, I made the following substitutions:
— leeks for onions 
— cherry tomatoes for sun-dried tomatoes 
— whole milk for cream
— dry vermouth and bean liquid for water 
I also added a chipotle pepper in adobo and some fresh spinach.
 A great meal (much thanks to the NYtimes)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Just kind of eyeballed the cream (used half and half) and oil so probably used less than called for. Great concept and while I don’t always love sun dried tomatoes they added depth

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Amazing and a hit with the hubs too. I made it as directed until the end, then I stirred the arugula mixture into the beans right before serving. We will be adding this to our regular rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Stirred in ricotta instead of cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious but way too much cream! Only ended up adding half the cheese and had to add some extra tomato paste to bring back out the tomato flavor. Next time will probably cut the cream in half but keep all of the cheese for the umami.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We looovvved this! A little more generous with the cheese and garlic, and gave a good bit of arugula salad on top to make the texture more interesting. Super lovely paired with some homemade sourdough. Will make again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
An easy and quick meat free meal.  I was leery of the salad on top of beans, but it is delish.
I will make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is perfection. That it's made mostly from ingredients you'll likely already have in your pantry or fridge only makes it more impressive. I have no notes, because, as I said, it's PERFECT.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Very good. Easy. Filling. I used half n half. Loved the arugula salad; a very necessary compliment.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is delicious, I used only a touch of half and half and a couple tablespoons of cheese. Added extra water and smashed some of the beans in and it was perfectly creamy. The arugula is the star, though, I would make that as a salad all on its own, and could probably do without the breadcrumbs, too. It was the lemon zest and the lemon juice, and oh, I added some basil which was a good idea. Simple and bright.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. Served with garlic naan. I topped leftovers with a fried egg for breakfast - highly recommend!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: 1 cup of heavy cream is completely overpowering. Use 1/2 cup at most or leave out completely. This mistake would never be tolerated in my homeland of Italia. Rather, we would find the biggest cow in town, walk her into the kitchen, then squeeze her ripe utters of the directly into the pan. 4-5 squirts should be sufficient.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: What a fresh and hearty comfort food! The crunchy and zesty arugula mix on top really take this dish to the next level. I didn't have red pepper flakes so I substituted in a few dashes of cayenne pepper. Taking others' advice, I opted for whole milk over heavy cream. I ended up with a pile of extra panko breadcrumbs. 1/2 cup should be the sweet spot!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I don't care for sun dried tomatoes so I subbed some blackened bell pepper and that worked perfectly. I also added the greens directly to the mixture at the end. Overall shockingly delicious for the simplicity!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The arugula salad on top is a revelation… been making a version of this for awhile, but the breadcrumb dusted greens are make so good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I tried this because I love beans, it's got all these Italian flavors, and isn't pasta. I followed the recipe, even bought the cheese since I only had regular parm in the fridge. I thought was meh at first; sauce was great and loved the greens. Figured next time to skip the beans and put this with pasta. But on the 3 subsequent days I ate this to finish it off, I changed my mind. It was better the next day. Still would make the sauce for pasta, I'd forgotten how good sundried tomatoes are.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Liked, didn't love this one.  I'll admit that I used Great Northern beans rather than Cannellini and that may have something to do with it. That said, the arugula salad was terrific and I will make that again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I added some fresh basil and because I forgot arugula I used steamed broccoli in its place and I loved it. It was plenty rich and creamy without being too heavy but I think next time I might go a little easier on both oil and heavy cream and see how that goes

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I found the bean mixture pretty bland. Made it according to the recipe except used halfnhalf in place of cream. The arugula salad was good but I probably  won’t make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So, so good. Added some pancetta and used the bean liquid instead of water. Gonna be hard not to make this at least once a week

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: To get a more savory, less sweet (and less pink) dish I halved the cream and added some tomato puree. If the arugula is fresh and crunchy the panko isn't necessary but the toasted bread is a nice touch.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made it tonight. Only substitution was silk soy coffee creamer in lieu of the cream. It was fabulous. Going to make the bean part without the arugula on toast squares as an appetizer for an upcoming bbq with friends.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: It is yummy even without the arugula

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: A weeknight saver that doesn’t skimp on flavor. Used almond milk because that was all I had and it came out perfect. The creaminess of the beans makes up for whatever the milk lacks and I will definitely it make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!  Big flavor for little effort. I agree with other commenters that it wouldn't hurt to cut down on the cream.  Next time I will try half and half instead.  The salad is essential both for the freshness and for its contribution to the overall flavor.  A big hit at my house for dinner tonight.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: It seems like the nutrition facts are wrong

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a delicious recipe.  I made it as written and next time will reduce the cream. I am not convinced by the crumbs either. I love toasted panko but I think it would be just as good without them. The dressing is great.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Yum.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Super good and seems forgiving! I added harissa and used coconut cream in place of heavy cream and it came out great, I agree less cream could be used, or sub a milk with some fat, perhaps in a lower volume.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I thought about this a long time before commenting. I just didn't like this. I think it's the cream with the beans and then the salad. Odd aftertaste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delish! I made it as recommended using Romano cheese. The only change I made was halving the olive oil for the salad. The bite of the arugula is the perfect complement to the creaminess of the beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Really good dish & excellent comfort food. I halved the heavy cream and replaced the other half with chicken stock. Also subbed white wine for the water. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used bean juice instead of water and filled the remaining 2/3 of the measuring cup with half and half. Served the arugula salad on the side for my very picky husband, though I had it on top. Yummy recipe! I buy arugula all the time, so it’ll be nice to have something I can always throw together with pantry items. I bet the salad would be good with kale too!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is really yummy and very rich. The lemony arugula salad is mandatory to cut the richness. I think the balance was good, but it was a bit too heavy for a summer day. Next time I will probably use half and half instead of cream. This would work with lots of different types of beans. I used runner beans that I had on hand. I didn’t have breadcrumbs and didn’t feel like anything was missing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I halved the cream but still found this too rich.  At the same time, I found this dish stodgy and old fashioned.  There are many yummier, more flavourful and also healthier bean dishes here.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I only included a quarter of the cream and cheese. It was still incredible delicious. (Other than decreasing these ingredients, I followed the recipe as written.)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was so good!  Creamy and fantastic flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So gratifyingly good with minimum effort! I used 2 cans of butter beans, and added some fresh thyme to the beans as they simmered. The contrast between the creamy, cheesy beans and the lemony, bitter arugula is excellent. A great, easy, summer stovetop recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was nice on its own, though a bit too tomato-ey dare I say? I enjoyed it much more mixed with some pasta when I ate it as leftovers the next day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was DELICIOUS! Didn't have heavy cream and replaced with coconut milk and it was super yummy (and not very coconut-y in flavor)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Make half. Perfect!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Try adding lots celery

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Incorporate all together
If using hearty leaf wilt with sauce/noodles

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Quick, easy, and delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I added some red lentils and added water as needed to cook. I also used pancetta oil with olive oil as the base for the beans, but still good with just olive oil!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So rich so creamy so delicious

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made for friends for dinner. Served with  fresh loaf of sourdough bread. Delicious as written (started with dried cannelloni beans - instapot with garlic, oregano, touch of red chili and rosemary). Kids asked for seconds. Much better with arugula mixed in when you eat it but served it on top of beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Yes, I was skeptical, and I like a good white bean. I'm not the biggest Pecorino fan, but this was delicious as written, and even better the next day.  Don't forgo the arugula and a full load of lemon juice and panko.  I can't stop thinking about when I can eat it again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was really good. Made as written. Delicious as a vegetarian main, but would also make a really good side for grilled steak.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Perfect food for meat less nights.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is excellent! the bitterness of the greens, combined with the tart lemon and richness of the beans is a perfect combination. Wil make again - likely with half and half cream and maybe omit the panko but I wanted to follow as written first before I make changes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Could this work with garbanzo beans?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Of course it can.  You might need to cook them a bit longer to get them to break down, but the flavors would still work.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Used half and half and cojita cheese, great northern beans.  This is fantastic!   So flavorful, it is not missing 
Meat at all!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Used half n half instead of cream and it tasted fabulous! Scooped it all up with toasted and buttered ciabatta (yum).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Substituting yogurt for cream adds a nice sour note. The liquid is absorbed by the end of cooking so I added a bit of 1/2 and half just before serving to make it slightly creamy. Also added the grated lemon peel to the toasted panko after it cooled for a fresh pop of flavor. Used chopped kale wilted with lemon juice. This dish is the most delicious comfort food!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Can anyone come up with a good dairy-free version of this amazing-looking dish?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Solid, tasty, takes spice well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as is, except substituted a lime. The beans are incredibly rich but the arugula cuts the richness beautifully! Husband said it tastes like a pizza.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was tasty!  I used homemade pinto beans and half and half, adding some chipotle in adobo at the end.  I also significantly decreased the amount of oil used at every stage.  I think that you could omit the Parmesan from the beans, and use 1/4 c. cream + 3/4 c. vegetable broth/water/tomato puree, and the recipe would not suffer.  Pesto might also be a nice addition.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Ridiculously delicious! Made as written except subbed cream for half coconut cream & half water. Will make over & over again……..

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Very good, but I thought it would be spicier (it's in the title).  I will definitely double the hot pepper flakes the next time I make it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this dish in advance to take camping.  It reheated wonderfully.  Flavors are outstanding.l

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as written and this was so delicious with a high reward to effort ratio. Cooking for myself, I ate this for 3 days in a row keeping the bean component separate and crisping panko/combining with arugula and lemon fresh for each meal. I used prewashed small arugula because I’m lazy like that. Sometimes I feel compelled to eat bean dishes with rice or bread but the panko adds that carby crunch so no need here. This dish feels fancy but is easy and very delicious. Definitely a new favorite.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is super simple and delicious. I didn’t have any sun dried tomatoes but I did have some SDT pesto so I chucked some of that in there.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was quick to prepare with lots of great flavor. Served with Asiago chicken sausage, and a good glass of Italian white wine. Perfect meal!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: More nicely spicy with some of the tomato paste replaced with Gochujang and using Gochugaru instead of crushed red pepper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Quite good! I had an open box of cream that probably had 2/3 c. left in it, so I just used that and we both found it plenty creamy. Only other alteration was using extra garlic, because I cannot resist adding a few cloves more than any recipe calls for. Fantastic with some good crusty sourdough.

I recommend not piling the salad onto the beans. It looks nice but makes for a very messy dinner. Let people pile their own salad onto their beans, or their beans-on-toast, or however you’re eating this.

Since I made this for two, I made just half the salad but made the full amount of the breadcrumbs/zest/cheese mix, to have it ready to go for serving with the leftovers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made it as designed and found it way too rich. It was such a disappointment. I should have known by reading the recipe. I might try it again with substitutions, but it is unlikely. There are so many other great recipes to try.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I had to work to get this where I wanted it to be.  So, I added jarred red peppers (not a fan of sun-dried toms).  I also added some red wine to deglaze and added a bit to the sauce prior to adding the beans as it seemed to need more depth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. Made no substitutions because it’s beans ands greens

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Enjoyable to make and eat.  Used coconut cream and added kale to dish with lemon zest and juice.  Covered with panko crumbs.  Only used a total of 4 tbsp of oil and only added a little salt and pepper once.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Very good, but too much cream and it is odd to use tomato paste on its own without actual tomatoes. I also don't understand why you would use Japanese-style breadcrumbs on a panna-style Italian dish. Next time will half the cream, no panko, and use a good can of chopped tomatoes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Wonderful! This is a keeper. We like to add in a can of diced tomatoes as well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The dish started out very promisingly. The aroma of the onions and tomato paste on the pan was intoxicating. However, the final dish was a bit disappointing. Too rich. I didn't like the fried panko. It made for a strange texture. If I were to do this again, I would leave out the arugula and panko bit and just serve it as a white beans in tomato sauce (using milk instead of cream or 1/2 cup of cream and 1 cup of bean broth).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Meh. It was too thick and too creamy. I ended up adding vegetable stock think it out. Just an okay recipe put the gate, IMO. To make it great you will need to tap into your cooking experience.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: delicious! added a tsp chicken bouillon and doubled the chili flakes. Added extra garlic and seasonings, too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I increased this recipe for a large group of vegetarians.  They clapped for me when I walked into the dining room. I will make  a creamy arugula salad next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. As usual, takes a lot longer to prepare, although easy. Used sour cream. No need to wipe out pan. I followed the recipe otherwise, but I don't see why plain sun-dried tomatoes (rather than oil-packed) wouldn't be fine. The salad part is simple and very good. Add it to individual serving, not all at once.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Pair With Sparkling Rosé

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I was a bit skeptical making this but it turned out really great! Thought it was weird to use sundried tomatoes instead of regular or canned, but made according to directions. The only changed Iʻd make it to cut the tomatoes up smaller than I did, lol, I had a lot of breadcrumbs left over which are good to use on other things-soups & salads. Very good for those meatless nights.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Substituted crema mexicana. Added tanginess.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: No substitutions needed. Wonderful as is!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Holy moly. I made this on a snowy and cold day and it was perfect. I had left over cream from Thanksgiving, and so I didn’t substitute it - it was creamy heaven. 🤤

Very easy and filling. The arugula blend is a delight - I didn’t have Panko so I used regular bread crumbs and it was so good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this EXACTLY as instructed. Friggen excellent. I wouldn’t change a single thing. This is going in regular rotation. Definitely serve it with the speedy no-kneed bread also found in the NYT app.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this as written and agree it is delicious.  It is also very rich, so next time I'll go with milk, or perhaps evaporated milk as suggested in the comments.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: My goodness these are delicious! The recipe is very forgiving- I have made them a handful of different ways, and loved them every time. Lately, have been using 1/3 c half and half, 1/3 c whole milk, and water for the rest. Amazing. Left out the pecorino by mistake once- still delicious. Forgot the breadcrumbs- totally fine! Stirred in microgreens or spinach instead of arugula- yum. Really, you can't go wrong here.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this dish with great northern beans and kale (massaged with the dressing), and it was delicious! I went with some suggestions to use less heavy cream, so I used about 1/2 cup and increased the water a bit. Will be putting this into my winter cooking rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious-made 2x and the first I did not have sun dried tomatoes. I think i prefer it without them.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I found it a little too spicy so next time I will halve the pepper flakes.  Also might try with less cream, or use half and half or whole milk.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Excellent. Everyone ate it, from 9 to 49. I might go easier on the cream next time, and maybe would add a little broth to make it less thick. The flavor was EXCELLENT. I followed another commenter's idea to add smoky paprika to the bread crumbs. Good idea!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Two variations that worked for me because it's what I had on hand last night were first, to use crème fraiche and oat milk instead of heavy cream and second, to use Fire Hook Rosemary Sea Salt crackers instead of bread crumbs.  The crackers added flavor and crunchiness to the whole dish and the substitutes for heavy cream worked beautifully.  Definitely a keeper!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I found it a little heavy and too tomatoey for my taste. Was good for a few bites! But I couldn’t eat a lot of it in one sitting.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was fabulous. Even my 11 year old liked it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: My daughter said this was the best thing she ever made. I tried it, but my husband and I didn’t like it. I guess tastes differ.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is easy and delicious on a cold night! I followed as directed, but used 3/4 cup cream. I think you could easily substitute half and half or even milk but maybe use less than a cup and let it simmer more slowly and on lower heat.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Instead of bread crumbs I made a breaded chicken breast, got rave reviews

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved this! My most favorite NYT recipe yet. Flavorful, textural and nutritious. Made as written except reduced heavy cream to 1/2 cup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Thought the beans were tasty. Not the most impressive dish I’ve had, but the recipe was easy to follow and the end result satisfying. Definitely include the arugula salad — otherwise I think it would be too heavy. The only change I made to the recipe was I only used lemon juice and no zest because I didn’t have any zest.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely fantastic! I used about a cup of tomato sauce-had no paste. I let it cook down longer and did not add the water at the end. I added 1/4 cup whole milk and 1/4 cup light sour cream to lighten things up, and only used about half the cheese and used my own dried tomato (not in oil). Made my own small croutons cause didn’t have panko. Delicious!!! Hubby liked too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Would consider some diced tomatoes next time but more decadent this way. Also breadcrumbs were unsung hero.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Not worth the hype. Was expecting something great because of the comment section, now I’m stuck eating a huge bowl of beans that are “meh”. Kind of disappointed. For the record, I followed the recipe exactly…

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We made this thinking this would be life changing beans. We give it a solid 3.5. It's good but for us, not. 5/5. However that being said, we aren't really bean people. We had it with chicken drumsticks and it was a good meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: can this be frozen for a few weeks or months?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @Amag Baz yes without the greens or cheese. Can add those when reheating.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
To make vegan:
* a can of coconut cream instead of cream -- or similar amount of whipped silken tofu
* add about a teaspoon of whole grain mustard and a tablespoon of nutritional yeast to the beans.
* add about a teaspoon each of nutritional yeast & paprika to the bread crumbs 
* mixed the arugula with some sundried tomato’s oil.
Devine! Bright! Rich! Will be a staple!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I have celiac disease--no panko bread crumbs for me and I find that packaged gluten-free bread crumbs taste awful.  Would the dish work without bread crumbs?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @Patricia I think without would be just as tasty!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I bet this is great. But it's hard to imagine it being better that the Cheesy, Spicy Black Bean Bake (https://cooking.nytimes.com/recipes/1020705-cheesy-spicy-black-bean-bake) that is both less fussy and less fatty.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @Hila We LOVE that black bean bake. We add chipotle, but otherwise cook as-written. Glorious in a burrito with Spanish rice and roasted wittle taters.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Can any of you faithful readers and cooks tell me how to print recipes WITH the picture from this site?

Sorry for posting off topic, sort of.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Could you sub edamame for the cannellini beans? Thereby curring carbs by almost 2/3....

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Fantastic! My only mods were oat milk instead of cream, and broasted cherry tomatoes with lots of olive oil. Will definitely add this to my regular rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I'd use a can of good san marzanos and a little white wine

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Skipped the bread crumbs but otherwise followed it exactly with a loaf of crusty bread and it was ridiculously good. Saved half for another night. We served the arugula in the side. Easy and filling. Will try with other bean varieties!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: What is the crushed red pepper? Is that the same as red chili flakes?  Or cayenne pepper?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The jar I have (McCormick brand) says "Red Pepper Crushed". I think this would be comparable to red chili flakes. Not cayenne, which is granulated.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious but next time will serve as a side to a bit of lightly cooked fish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this as written except for the heavy cream (I was out so I subbed half and half). It was plenty rich even without the cream, but I’d be curious to try it with it anyway. It was delicious and flavorful! Loved the arugula & panko topping.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This recipe was amazing and quick and easy!  I saw this was the recipe of the day yesterday and made it for dinner, so had to sub some ingredients based on what I had. I used 1/2 cup creme fraiche with some veggie broth instead of the heavy cream. I also only had smoked dried tomatoes so I had to cook it a little bit longer to soften them up. Even my carnivore husband said it was delicious and satisfying. This will definitely go into the rotation and will be so adaptable - Chickpeas next time!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Super mid-week meal without a lot of work. Didn't have sun-dried tomatoes, so used a half-can of chopped (drained well) and the tomato flavor was still solid. Loved the lemons & greens to balance the creaminess! Go easy on the [salt &] pepper, as the crushed red pepper added enough/just the right heat for us (and we like spicy foods!) As suggested, we served with whole grain, rustic loaf...a winner!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: wonderful

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Lacking cream or parmesan cheese, I decided to go dairy-free on this, and instead used a lot of canned anchovies, chopped, then added to the onions and garlic.  I like the suggestion of adding prepared mustard!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The flavor profile of this reminds me slightly of a vodka sauce. Absolutely incredible, easy... so happy I have leftovers!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Would give this 10 stars it was SO good! Used coconut milk instead of heavy cream. Delightful and will make again

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I thought about coconut milk (which I had) after I went to the store for .5/.5. I'm using CM next time...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I forgot to buy panko so I made my own breadcrumbs with rosemary sourdough bread slices. After that I followed the recipe as written. It was delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This is delicious! I don’t like beans generally, but this I loved and will continue to make over and over.
I used milk instead of cream and it thickened up beautifully. Added more arugula salad, cause that’s what I like and it was perfect! 
Thank you!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Yes they are that good! As suggested, I grated the lemon zest directly into the toasted panko. I doubled the sundried tomatoes to 1 cup, doubled the water to 2/3 cup, and halved the heavy cream to 1/2 cup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious!! Made exactly as written.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Was doubtful about this till it was topped with the arugula, then pretty darn good. I had to make three changes to feed the lactose intolerant used lactose free Hal and half. Also we don’t tolerate the red pepper flakes found in practically every NYT recipe these days. Didn’t find sun dried tomatoes in oil so used a sun dried bruschetta.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I don’t make anything with cream (can’t eat dairy), so like others I subbed some coconut milk and oat milk, and the results were excellent. I do think the directions are overly complicated. No need to mix the arugula and toasted bread crumbs in a separate bowl. Just ladle the beans into bowls, put the arugula and bread crumbs on top. The whole thing gets mixed together anyway.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I had no Panko on hand and so I crushed store-bought garlic croutons to substitute. It is a great recipe that I will make again. Next time I will reduce /substitute the heavy cream to omit fat.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was fantastic! Perfection on a plate! Even my arugula-skeptic husband asked for seconds, and that’s saying something…

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I substituted fresh chopped tomatoes for sun dried and half a cup of Greek yogurt for the cream. My girlfriend didn’t care for it :(

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: As this went viral I decided to make it. I didn't really like it the first night but as it aged in the fridge it got better. I made fresh greens each time. Day 3 was the best meal. I did use a mix of baby arugula and spinach as that was what was available

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Anyone tried making it from a dried bean instead of canned?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this last night, following recipe as written. Awesome flavor and texture.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was great!   I followed the recipe, although added some Aleppo pepper to the mix.  The lemony greens are a wonderful contrast to the beans.  This will be a great vegetarian entree for guests, too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: amazing dish. made it with coconut milk vs. creamand forgot the red pepper, which I think could have been an wonderful add. Def recommend throwing that arugula salad on top and letting it wilt a bit.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I tried Yogurt but the end result was a bit thick.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Use more tomato paste
Don’t forget the salt!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved this recipe! I made a few changes, like nixing the breadcrumbs (not a fan), and just mixing the arugula and lemon juice in at the end instead of serving on top. Also, if this is your main course, this is really only 3 servings. I might double the recipe in the future.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We are just finishing up this dinner. I truly have no idea why this is such a standout. I carefully followed the recipe. The beans are bland. The salad, at least has some crunch, is bright & reasonably nutritious. We’ll try it once more with a lot less cream, more cheese, a lot more sun-dried tomatoes, & perhaps extra hot pepper. Was hoping for a mid-winter lift. Looks like that only comes on the chairlift.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as written - radical, I know. It is crazy delicious for relatively little effort. Next time I’ll use a lighter type of cream, or maybe use half heavy cream and half whole milk. You need to make this!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
My gosh, this is good. Or as some of my students would say: this recipe SLAPS!
i made it for Watching Football w a side of mini corn dogs :)
I halved the recipe, subbed reg sun dried tomatoes, added a drizzle of garlic olive oil, and used half and half. 
I love when a recipe teaches me a new idea or technique. 
Those bread crumbs were easy and elegant and i will dream of them on any arugula salad i make again. 
If like me, you get distracted and the beans sit for a few minutes, just add a little boiling water so they are nice and loose and soupy again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. Pretty. Easy. But make sure you have all the ingredients. I used full on red pepper flakes. If someone isn't good with spicy, go easy but do add some as this needs a bit of a kick. And the kick is a surprising (and good) contrast with the lemon. I also went full on with the zest.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Super easy and so versatile if you need to adapt. Currently canned beans are very hard to find, but subbed with chickpeas, added harissa because i didn’t have sun dried tomatoes, used a whole lot of cherry tomatoes that were about to go bad and vegan cream instead of regular cream because my friend doesn’t do dairy. Added a lot of lemon zest throughout the dish and was delicious. Put the salad on the side so that I could easily heat it up. put spinach inside as well because I had it  :) Ate with pitas

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Does anyone have a good — tried and true — recipe for making your own sun dried tomatoes at home?  Thanks for posting it if you do.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is indeed a delicious recipe and I would definitely make it again. I do agree with many of the other reviews that the cup of cream was too much. Next time I make it I would make it lighter, maybe a small amount of cream and the rest with skim milk or replace the cup of cream with whole milk.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Ok dish, not something that I would make again.  Found the texture and flavor boring.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is delightful! I did tweak it a bit to modify what I had on hand but I am really happy with the results. Switched out one can of beans for a can of artichoke hearts. Also added a jar of roasted red peppers, chopped. Further, I never have heavy cream on hand so I just used a half cup of yogurt. Truly, a delectable dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this into soup instead with dried white beans, homemade vegetable stock, canned tomatoes in addition to the sun drieds, 1/3 c cream and sauteed onion with yellow pepper, fresh parsley at the end. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Love this!
I augmented garlic 2 extra, bigger onion, and extra cheese. Everything else was great
The flavours all complement…..oh I also added some dry cured olives since I had them.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious but it took me almost an hour and 20 minutes with prep and cooking. Won't deter me from cooking it again though.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Regina - not sure why this would take you over an hour. I found this an incredibly fast recipe. I’m going to make this for vegan friends in the next month which will require some ingredient changes but I don’t anticipate it taking any more prep or cooking time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: After trying this recipe, the NYT recipe from Jack Monroe, Cannellini-bean pasta with beurre blanc is much preferred.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I cut this in half to serve just myself. Subbed cream for half and half and did cautious drizzles of oil rather than full tablespoons. The salad is divine, next time I'll make more than the recipe calls for. Paired with fresh baguette, it's a wonderfully cozy dinner for harsh winter evenings.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Really good. Cook your own beans - it is a simple process and makes for an even more flavorful (and economical) dish.
I think it serves 6 rather than 4.
And if your household isn't used to consuming beans, you may want to wait for warmer weather before serving this for dinner. So you can open the windows the next day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious comfort food recipe! Only had one can of cannellini beans so subbed in garbanzos. Used mostly 1% milk and dribble of what was left of the cream. Otherwise followed recipe exactly. Love the bright lemon zest and juice with the panko and arugula. Ate with a nutty toast and a poached egg. Family loved it. Will definitely make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Subbed unrinsed black beans, chicken broth for water, organic Bloody Mary mix (no sugar) for t-paste, spinach with cooking sake, and snuck in half a Poblano. Put all in a slow-cooker, which is bubbling as I type. ... We shall see

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Cooked dry cannellini beans and used half and half instead of cream, otherwise followed the recipe. I would've liked more greens, and would probably double the arugula next time. There were plenty of panko breadcrumbs to accomodate more greens as well.
Will make again, perhaps using a combo of milk and half and half, as it was still a bit rich. Very tasty and a hit for my carnivorous partner.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Excellent, A keeper!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Fantastic.  One of the best things we've made in a long time!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved the creaminess of the spicy beans with the counterpoint of lemony greens and the texture of panko. I used less cream and didn’t miss any creaminess, which is the point of the recipe. I think one could be creative and use these beans in other ways. I haven’t tried it, but I love the idea of breakfast over toast with fried egg.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: A new favorite, like most everyone else a few tweaks: Half and half instead of heavy cream, added 1/2 pound lean bacon chopped and rendered, a little less crushed red pepper. No added salt needed. The arugula salad on top brings really makes all the flavors pop!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Can't say I get why this is so popular. I made it exactly as directed except that I started with dry beans, that I soaked and cooked, and used arugula from our garden. Just sort of gloppy and weird.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this as written with the exception of using half the cream and using beautiful yellow-eyed beans I cooked in advance. I should say I cook a pot of beans each week and so maybe we just were not prepared for how rich these were? I don't know, but the sun-dried tomatoes added nothing. Maybe we just don't like red pesto?  Can't see ever making this again, but so glad that a plant based meal has gotten so much love and attention. Go beans, no matter how they are prepared.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Followed instructions to the letter. This was much saltier than we would have preferred.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was outstanding! Don't change a thing - make it exactly as written, and enjoy the oohs and aahs as people eat this amazing meal. The rich savory depth of the beans paired so well with the sharp bright greens on top.  Now I just have to figure out if I can make this for our main dish again next week - too soon?! I think not!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Skipped the panko. Used a full 6oz can if tomato puree because I didn't want half a can left over. Delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was absolutely delicious. My one misstep is that it really tightened up for me. My finished product did not look nearly so loose and creamy, so I'll have to try it again and maybe add more water.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is food for your soul.  Rich deep flavors in 30 minutes! What???  Yes.  And the arugula salad is so amazing all by itself!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious with toasted rye bread!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this a couple of days ago. I followed the recipe exactly and it was excellent, creamy and flavorful. The arugula is such a nice complement to the beans. This has now become my favorite vegetarian meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I tend to view recipes as guidelines or suggestions. That said, this recipe lends itself to all sorts of modifications which is wonderful. So if you follow or free-spirit this, it gonna fill your hungry mouths with a rich meat-less meal that is hearty.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I meant the toasted panko with arugula - not pistachios (a different recipe).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Really delicious. Made as directed, but in half. Might add more liquid next time…used the liquid from the beans rather than water for a little heft.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Terrible. I will never make this recipe again. I don’t particularly see the appeal. There are so many other great things to make that this just doesn’t hold up.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Used homemade roasted tomatoes instead of sun dried and it was a perfect substitution.
I agree it was a little heavy, but I’ll just use less cream next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Love, love, love Da Beeeeeeans! This is one of the most convincing recipes I’ve cooked in the past year.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So so good.  No need to fiddle around with this recipe at all!  Great as a stand alone or as a side dish for my big multigenerational family!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Alexa Weibel, you are a genius. The idea of  placing fresh greens on top of these gorgeous beans is exactly right. I used micro greens and mounded them high on the plate. Truly a treat!  Like most things of substance, these beans get better the next day for lunch. I look forward to making these again and again. Thank you!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used 1/3 cup of gluten free breadcrumbs but I think I could have used about half as much. I also subbed whole milk for the heavy cream and it was plenty rich enough. Overall it was enjoyed by 3/4 of my family!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I cooked 1/2 lb Rancho Gordo cranberry beans instead of using canned beans. I substituted 2% milk for the heavy cream. The warm arugula salad on the beans worked very well and the toasted panko added a nice little crunch.  My husband, a pinto beans and cornbread guy, liked it too and was surprised how good the arugula was on it. Winner.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: These are magic beans, truly.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made it with pasta instead of beans and with shrimp.  It worked!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious! Ignore others' complaints about modifications; cook this with your heart and taste/readjust often. 

I eyeballed all of the measurements, used a large shallot instead of onion, used less tomato paste, added cherry tomatoes   herbs, subbed half & half (using less than the cream called for) and used extra vegetable stock instead of water— simmering and reducing for much longer than called for to develop the flavors. Don't omit the arugula and toasted panko! Served w/ rosemary sourdough.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: MEH. I regarded the ingredients list with skepticism but the reviews led me to try this (and now question my sanity), hoping for some alchemical miracle medley of flavors. Did I say MEH?  I cook NYT recipes every week with great delight. This tasted -- to me and my partner -- like you'd guess, a mushy pile of beans in tomato sauce.  None of the ingredients stand out. A waste of parmesan, panko, cream and my time. I actually threw it out when I couldn't finish the first serving.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious and even better the next day. My husband isn't big on meatless, so I browned some ground turkey separately and incorporated it into the beans. A great, hearty winter meal!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I followed the recipe and it’s an amazing dish with the lemony arugula salad offsetting the earthy, spicy, creaminess of the beans. I think next time, fewer breadcrumbs and some sort of alternative to a cup of heavy cream. Gotta bring the fat down. Still this is a keeper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as written except used milk plus heavy cream 50:50. Not crazy about this recipe. Too much panko, not enough lemon. Overly rich. My husband liked it and went back for seconds.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We made this dish as a side. We omitted the greens and the crumbs. We folded in some cooked chicken which was delicious. It tasted like a vodka sauce with beans. Super tasty but very rich.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Oh gosh. 

If you have an instant pot/pressure cooker and you’re not making your beans ahead of time for this… why not? It’ll make about 4 cans worth for a buck. 

I had just made marinara so I cooked the dry beans in  a tiny bit of marinara and I had some duck stock. Really, any stock will do. 

Elevated this dish! Everyone raved over it. This can be dressed up however you want but I suggest trying it the original way first. Then maybe add your bacon, toasted prosciutto, Calabrian chilis and fried garlic.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this for our family dinner last week. My grocery store had only a arugula/baby spinach mixture, so that's what I used, but everything else was exactly as written. Delicious! I had no idea beans and greens could taste like this! So many layers of flavor. I'm adding it to the meal rotation for the family.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Really delicious! Any bitter greens will work- my husband and I loved the salad dressing and will use it often.. So simple and easy and light.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made the recipe as written, except I added meatballs. Worked beautifully.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Lovely. I also used whole milk, cut the oil in half, and would cut back on breadcrumbs next time. Delicious. And I would add a third can of beans to make a little larger serving size.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Incredible. Made as is. For following times, I would cut the cheese and breadcrumbs in half to cut the calories.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: One of the best things I've made from NYT Cooking. Really easy but tastes special and complex. I don't typically like lemon but it compliments the beans and parmesan cheese beautifully. I'm sure there are many ways to alter, change, add to this recipe, but it is lovely as is.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Subbed full fat Greek yogurt for the heavy cream and it worked great (suggest adding a little broth or water). Left over this is AMAZING over steamed kale, frozen peas and riced cauliflower with a few pepitas on top and a sprinkle of goat crumbles.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Very yummy and easy. Like others, didn’t want all that cream. Used 1/2 light cream and subbed water for the rest. Decided to roast the garlic and liked the result. Arugula salad nice and crispy, tangy, and a perfect pairing. Used up croutons I had on hand, pulverizing with a hammer, instead of making the panko just cuz. Will be making this again. Thinking a roasted zucchini would be a great side, if still had a little crunch to it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is absolutely delicious and the perfect meat-free meal, satisfying and flavorful. I only added about 1/4 - 1/3 cup half & half instead of 1 full cup of heavy cream, and added a little more water. Used Pecorino as we find it better than Parmesan.
