I had kale, so I used that for the salad and it was incredible! The lemony kale salad was the perfect counterpoint to the unctuous beans. Served it over rotini, and it was the perfect cold weather dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Wow! So so amazing. Instead of 1c of heavy cream, I used 1/2c of soy milk (what I had in the fridge) and 1/2c of Greek yogurt. I made the beans and ate them throughout the week - they reheated so well! I just tossed the arugula in lemon juice fresh every night - I didn't find a need for extra lemon juice.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made the recipe exactly as is except decreased chili pepper flakes to 1/4 tsp and that was enough spice for my family. It was a hit. I would like to try it with maybe half and half cream next time but otherwise it's very good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious. At my first attempt I made it as written. I planned to do the same for the second but as I got started realized I had no tomato paste so used 2 tablespoons of gochujang and it was delish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Don't skip the panko! The textural element adds so much to this dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I thought this recipe was delicious. I used northern white beans instead of cannellini. Next time I will reduce the cream to half and half or half milk and half cream. It is pretty rich as written. I’m thinking this sauce would also be amazing on pasta.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I thought this was a bit lacklustre in flavor. But when I warmed up leftovers the next day with a bit of chili crisp and some more Parmesan, the flavors came together and it tasted amazing. The lemon zest and seasoning on the arugula are essential.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Amazing dish. Had a chicken breast just hanging around, so I diced that up and added it with the onions and garlic in step two. Added a, well, nice meatiness without becoming the star of the show. Also followed someone else’s suggestion of subbing a yogurt/milk mixture for the heavy cream.  Loved the peppery arugula mix. Would’ve been just fine without the chicken. Definitely adding this to the frequent flyer list of recipes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I agree with many other commentators that this recipe seemed unnecessarily heavy in cream and cheese. So I reduced the cream by about half and I skipped the cheese in the salad… After all,  the salad is meant to be a counterpoint to the beans, right? So it hardly seems like they both need Parmesan. I  also reduced the sun-dried tomatoes somewhat since I’m on a budget and I felt like their taste still came through loud t and clear. All in all, they seem like grown-up, baked beans… minus the sugar, but not necessarily healthier because of all the fat.^^ Still, they’re quite tasty.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I just adore this dish! The only changes I made were to use whole milk instead of cream and I used baby greens instead of the arugula since that's what I had on hand. Next time I will skip the bread crumbs, but that's just me. There will be a next time, and soon!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Comfort food at its best.  Vegan variation... swapped cream for soy milk and mustard/nutritional yeast for parm and topped it with a little Kite Hill vegan ricotta.  The arugula salad is a brilliant compliment.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: All the hype around this recipe is correct - it's amazing and different from what I usually cook/eat.   I only used 1/2 cup of heavy cream and it is plenty rich still.  The arugula salad on top is a great contrast to the heavy spicy beans.  YUM

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Really great!  Took suggestions from others.  Used a combo of fresh and sundried tomatoes.  Used 2% greek yogurt and some skim milk.  Parmesan.  Will make this again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used mascarpone (250g) in place of the heavy cream, and chicken stock (2/3 cup) in place of water. Two absolutely fantastic substitutions. Made the dish extra rich, but even more delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was a nice weekend no meat meal but for me it was a bit weird.  The arugula salad on top of the beans seemed too much texture contrast, though the flavors were lovely.  Will make again but perhaps separate the 2 food items.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: My grocery was out of tomato paste and sun dried tomatoes so instead of making the sauce I used 2 cans of Trader Joe’s vegan red pesto and it turned out great!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
It's not often I give a recipe 5 stars. I am an experienced cook and find many recipes don't work as written or are overly fussy. It's rare I don't change something. But other than using half & half instead of cream, I prepared this for lunch today as written. I loved it! My husband, who only likes things with meat in it, also said it was "pretty good." From him that's quite a compliment. 
There is enough for 4 people, or 2 meals for 2 people. If for 2, only make half the arugula salad.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Terrific dish. We don't like arugula at all, so I used baby kale, massaged that with the oil and then proceeded with the panko, lemon, and cheese. Very satisfying and delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious result that was a big hit, but one cup of heavy cream is an insane suggestion. I did a *splash* of heavy cream with a dollop of greek yogurt and a pat of butter. All other liquids I replaced with veggie broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made modifications based on what I had - instead of sun dried tomatoes and tomato paste, used a small jar of sun dried tomato pesto. Added a small chopped Roma tomato. 50/50 whole milk with yogurt. Chopped kale added directly to the beans to soften.  Was good. Will make again but not often since it’s not my favorite

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Substituted baby spinach and 1/2 cup yogurt and 1/2 cup milk instead of heavy cream. 

Added a bit more lemon juice to the beans for flavor. Rated 10/10 by kids and grown ups alike.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was a great dish to accompany some spicy baby back ribs.  The bright lemony dressing for the greens was a nice contrast.  I used 1/2 & 1/2, milk and homemade stock, it’s what I had and countered the richness of the ribs.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as written and everyone loved it.  It was just a little too heavy for me and will try half and half or milk next time.  Made mostly arugula for topping but used baby spinach for my son who doesn't like arugula and he snarfed it up.  Will put in roatation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
The hype is absolutely deserved for this recipe. My husband deemed it 5/5 stars (high praise). 

I would liken the taste to that of penne alla vodka (no alcohol required). It's very creamy and comforting, and the spice level is easily adjusted. I personally don't enjoy arugula, so I used mixed greens instead. Toasted bread is a must for soaking up any extra sauce.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made the recipe as written.  Delicious.  My husband not change would be to add more arugula mixture, as the arugula heavy bites were my favorite.  The crunch of the panko was great, next time I might make my own tiny croutons for a little more crunch than panko can provide.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I knew this would be delicious, but I don't think I was prepared for just how delicious it is! My changes: I found it a little heavy on the cream, so I added a can of fire-roasted tomatoes to make it a touch more tomato-y (didn't reduce the cream), and I used kale (boiled until tender then squeezed to remove some water). Also added about 1/2 of ricotta that I had hanging around. Can't wait to make it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this a week ago and it was really delicious. The arugula gives it a nice compliment. I served it over penne pasta with some crusty bread which made it a very satisfying meal. Very easy to make.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: If you have a person that must have a meat protein then add quick seared smoked sausage. I’ll skip the arugula next time as it’s too stemmy for us.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: For a pantry meal, this was really delicious.  I ended up using a full can of the tomato paste since I didn't want it to go to waste and oat milk instead of the cream.  I don't think we're missing anything my doing that.  I already had a lemon vinaigrette made that was essentially lemon zest, olive oil and a bit of garlic and dijon mustard, so I used that for the arugula.  The amount of olive oil seemed a bit excessive, so I cut it down to 2 TB.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this vegan with cashew cream and nutritional yeast. Loved the dish but would add more liquid.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this and it was sooo good. Even my carnivorous husband liked it. For the liquids, I used approx 1 cup aquafaba, a splash of cream, and 1/4 cup or so of 2% milk.  I’ll add more liquid next time to make it saucier. But it was so tasty and very filling. I think my husband had thirds. No leftovers!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Used what I had and made mine with mixed spring greens and regular breadcrumbs.  Still turned out great.  Huge hit.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We make this recipe at least three times a month. It is a perfect combination of flavors. The raw arugula with lemon and cheese is a bonus salad.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Yeah I can see the hype of this recipe now. I will say that the dish gets dry as it sits, so I would err on slightly more water. I just want to put that arugula salad situation on everything now.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made vegan with a plant-based heavy "cream," a vegan parmesan and a tablespoon of nutritional yeast.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as described. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this as written and it was… decadent. Delicious. Amazing. But… I think I’ll follow some other suggestions and try using half and half or a milk and half and half combo. Watching that heavy cream pour in was a little jaw-dropping. Regardless, it’s a keeper!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Just need to use less cream than the recipe - was delicious with 1/3 cup of half and half per other comments. I also skipped the breadcrumbs. Really good and loved by whole family.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this for a dinner party and absolutely loved--everyone wanted the recipe. Tried to scale up for the number of people and totally worked. Used 2 lemons for zest and juice and the extra citrus was definitely worth it. Highly recommend.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: @Allison. Question Allison:  what did you serve with this for your dinner party?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Fantastic - made as per recipe but I added some crushed pepper flakes to give it more heat. Will make again - for company!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this recipe and my family enjoyed it, but watch the nutrition count!  1/3 oil, 1 cup of heavy cream, and 2/3 cup of cheese for four servings is A LOT OF FAT!  I put this on my "occasional treats" list.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used a plant-based ‘cream’ borrowed from the roasted broccoli and cashew cream recipe from NYT instead of heavy cream.  Blend 14 oz. silken tofu with 1/2 cup cashew butter and add 6 Tbls. water.  Also allowed the tomato paste to caramelize.  So delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was great.  Made as written.  DEFINITELY don't skip the arugula salad on top.  Will make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This recipe was a total hit at a brunch, alongside eggs and toast. I added gochugang for a little depth/heat, which I would recommend trying if you like those flavors. Trying to keep this a little light, I used whole milk instead of cream and used about 1/3 cup cheese instead of 2/3 cup. Adding my five stars to join many others!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: One pot meal. Easy and tasty. Used 1%milk instead of cream and honestly don't think it made a difference -- very creamy and full depth of flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I didn't have arugula so I lightly sauteed up chard instead, and the whole family loved it. Creamy and satisfying, it was a surprisingly filling vegetarian entree.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We used 2% evaporated milk to cut down on the saturated fat the second time we made it, and could not discern the difference when compared to using cream.  Still totally yum!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: My husband only bought one can beans at the store so I subbed one half pound ground turkey breast to the onion garlic red pepper mix, then continued as written except I reduced the cream by half and it was still too creamy. Next time I’ll sub coconut milk as someone else suggested and only 1/2 cup. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is amazing and so easy to make! I used a mix of heavy cream and half and half, only because I had both on hand. Will try milk or yogurt next time. Used chopped tomatoes instead of sun dried, because I had no sun dried tomatoes on hand. The arugula with the seasoned panko crumbs and lemon/olive oil mix was just delicious, and a great foil to the creamy tomato/bean mix. Try it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Couldn’t get sun dried tomatoes in oil, so roasted a drained can of diced tomatoes with a squirt of olive oil and salt at 250 for a couple hours, added a half dozen chopped Kalamata olives with beans for extra umami and used the drained juice of the tomatoes instead of water. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: THA BEANSSSS! again. Delightful every which way.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Excellent .. did use nonfat yogurt and milk and chicken bone broth to thin it out .Served with the spinach lemon zest panko lemon juice and evoo…
Oustsnding!!! I’m super full!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made it exactly as listed except used garlic powder and onion powder instead. Amazing. Will be making again. Whole family loved it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This was delicious. The beans (on their own) could have used a little extra kick, so we might add just a little bit more red pepper next time -- but making sure each bite included the arugula salad made the meal *absolutely outstanding*. 

As others have noted, we'd feel better about having this in the weekly rotation if it had less fat. That amount cream didn't seem absolutely essential, so we are going to experiment with reducing it (we'll also trial lighter dairy and non-dairy options).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Just made and loved this! I will increase the sundried tomatoes next time. While the whole thing was stewing, I cut them in half with a wooden spoon for a better bean to tomato ratio. Also, I added a splash of fish sauce. I think it added extra umami, but this was my first time making, so hard to say. (Almost tasted like I’d cooked mushrooms into it) I already had Trader Joes Vegan Croissant croutons on hand, so I crushed/chopped those up in place of panko. I HIGHLY recommend. The almost sweet, extra savory addition to the whole thing was fantastic. Arugula and croissants are always an excellent combo in my book😁

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I love this recipe, which I cooked for myself to see if my bean-agnostic hub would like before I foisted it upon him. Knowing that he doesn't really consider anything a meal unless it contains meat, I chopped up a single link of chorizo into the onions while they sauteed, but hewed exactly to the recipe otherwise. It was delectable! Given the calorie whallop of the cream, I think I'll try it with something less rich next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Just super delicious!  Amazing!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this recipe twice, with only two changes: non dairy cream and butter beans instead of cannelloni. Other than that I’ve followed the recipe strictly both times. This dish is wonderful, I absolutely love it. I realize it has a lot of cream and oil and maybe that’s why I want to eat the whole pan, but this dish is just so good. This will be a regular recipe for me.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Incredible. Instead of sun-dried tomatoes, I took a can of cherry tomatoes and roasted it in a 375 oven for about 20-30 minutes (didn't have sun-dried tomatoes). And I had a bag of Rancho Gordo cassoulet beans that I wanted to use, so I soaked and cooked those to use instead of using canned. Absolutely delicious! Now that I'm out of dried beans, I'll definitely be doing the canned version next!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Really liked this dish, and my 10 yr old loved it too.  I did find it a bit rich -- next time I'll use half & half instead of cream (or maybe even whole milk) to see if it's a bit less rich.  Otherwise, great.   I used spinach instead of the arugula for my kiddo, tried it this way and it was still really good.  Also, made excellent leftovers!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I found that this recipe called for too much cream. I will use 1/2c next time and compensate with more water or even vegetable broth. 

I did not toast bread crumbs, and instead ate it with a good hunk of bread. 

I also tend to add anchovies to NYT vegetarian recipes, and I recommend adding 5-6 anchovies here.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely Slamming!! cant make again soon enough!  We added frozen broc rabe to add greens but otherwise made it as is...so so good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Lovely recipe, I absolutely enjoyed it and would serve it to guests.  I tried it before the lemon went in and I think I would like a little less next time.  Rosemary might be a nice addition when it's in season.  Shrimp skewers would also be really good with it.  5 stars!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious and simple to make.   We modified the salad slightly by mixing 1 tsp of the bean juice with 3 tsp of lemon juice.  We also finely chopped a jalapeno pepper along with the pepper flakes to give it a little extra spice.    So glad this was shared.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Ridiculously good!   Loved it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Fancy enough to serve others. Slightly more cheese less cream. Can use as baked tuna noodle blueprint.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this today for lunch using another reviewers substitutions to make vegan. Absolutely loved it! When saving the leftovers, I practically licked the pan!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: My husband -- who admittedly hates to cook and avoids it at all costs -- made this for me on a weeknight and not only was it DELICIOUS, he said it was very easy to create. HIGHLY RECOMMEND this recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: One of the best recipes on NYT - rancho gordo beans are the way to go.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I put the panko on top of the beans at the end and popped into the broiler. Very yummy

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Total home run. A new favorite!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This was delicious but the calorie count was just astonishing. I'm active but not THAT active and I even ate this on a day I did a 13-mile run. 

I think instead of cutting the cream down, an easy fix to reducing calories is to instead make the serving size 8. Then each serving size is 425 calories instead an astonishing 851.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made exactly as written and it was incredible, surprisingly elevated. And came together in only 30 min with two cooks :)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I only had one can of beans so I added red lentils. Also used garlic hardwood smoke Pablo that gave it a flavor of having ham despite being meatless. Will be making again! Perhaps with potatoes mixed in…

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Edit to original comment: used garlic hardwood smoked panko

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: OMG was this delicious. I added some of the sundried tomato oil but followed the recipe as written for the first time. We absolutely loved it. Just enough spice too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Fantastic! Used micro greens with fresh parsley and basil mixed in. Make sure you use Panko for the crunch they deliver!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I’m not a great cook so what I found challenging was that after far more than 20 minutes prep I found myself in the bottom of the 9th with the bases loaded - and I’m trying hold a slim lead. A lot going on. 

If there is a next time I’ll use a separate smaller pan for the panko and perhaps use fire-roasted tomatoes. And more liquid.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So delicious! Even better with butter beans and some gnocchi!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I added almond slices to the toasted panko (Just because). This was a keeper addition.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This needed 2x the garlic. I added white wine, cherry tomatoes, thyme, basil and it was incredible. The topping is perfection.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Is there a step missing where you simmer this for 20 minutes? The recipe calls out 20 minutes prep and 20 minutes cook time for a total of 40 minutes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: It mentions a 5 minute simmer in step 4, but it really needs more like 20. Figure a total of 1 hour for the whole process.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: used c. 1/2 cup of Greek yogurt with 1/2 cup of 1% low fat milk and it was a great substitute for heavy cream.. used chopped tomatoes instead of sundried and half and half instead of cream. . ... I cut the cream to 1/4 cup and doubled the amount of sundried tomatoes, and it was *fantastic*...used 2% milk mixed with cornstarch instead of heavy cream...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I haven't made this yet but I would probably use half the cream and I would blot as much oil as possible from the sun-dried tomatoes with paper towels before chopping.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This has crazy nutrition values! The fat and salt are through the roof and the calories are so high. I appreciate the modifications people have posted. I’d try it with far less fat and salt.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I wish you feed columnists would include nutritional listings with your recipes.  It's frustrating trying to figure the nutrients.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this flavorful, bean recipe for my family and everyone enjoyed it. I substituted spinach for the arugula and it was delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I cant eat bitter greens or kale. What would be a good alternative?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Love it! Left out arugula and put in kale. Wonderful!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Any suggestions on cutting down the sodium?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is now a family favorite. It's creamy, tangy, spicy, hearty, not TOO heavy, and has a lovely contrast of textures. I use the food processor to chop up the onion and garlic together so it blends well into the "sauce." I keep the breadcrumb topping separate from the arugula and sprinkle on top. I've used both jarred and dried sundried tomatoes, chopped up. Instead of pepper flakes, I made it last night with a good squeeze of gochujang. My toddler even ate a few bites and said, "yummy!"

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Mine came out VERY spicy - almost intolerable. I followed recipe to the letter.  Might try again after I recover from this try - add less red pepper and pepper...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Please provide measures in weight, not cups! Grated cheese, a cup weighs 3-4 oz, except of course when it doesn't because it isn't packed tightly into the cup. Weights are far easier, being more precise.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We loved this!! Used cream cheese as a sub for the cream. The beans were so rich and silky and at first glance a little too heavy, but after adding the dressed arugula, it all came together perfectly! Definably adding this in the rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So quick, easy and delicious!! Do not skip the sun-dried tomatoes, as they provide a robust base for the dish. Baby spinach works well, along with the arugula.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made exactly as written and wouldn’t change a single thing! It comes together beautifully, one of the best things I’ve made from NYT Cooking. And so easy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Shockingly easy and delicious. I screwed up and didn’t buy the sun-dried tomatoes and it was still a 5/5 stars.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Anything tastes good with heavy cream. Let’s throw bacon and butter in to make in even more fattening. What a waste of beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!  I added a small amount of diced pepperoni, only because my husband feels cheated without some meat.  (I know, it seems like blasphemy in a vegetarian dish).  It was surprisingly good, and it worked with the textures.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Just want to say I love this recipe and I come back to it for quick vegetarian lunches all the time!! I love using garbanzo beans and I usually use regular milk instead of heavy cream to make it a little lighter and it’s just as delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: EZ, just follow the recipe. Wonderful. Would work well as a side to chicken dinner. Can not wait for lunch tomorrow.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! My beans in sauce turned out a lot lighter in color than the ones in the photo here, but were still yummy. I threw in some smoked paprika towards the end to darken the color up some; it helped a little. I followed the bean recipe closely, but for the salad, I didn't add the extra parm and used homemade dressing I'd made the night before that was lime juice + olive oil and a few other things. We served the salad from a second bowl and used a LOT of salad. SO good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used 1 1/5 cups dry great northern beans and I used spinach instead of arugala.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This is basically beans in vodka sauce, so of course it tastes amazing! My left shoe would taste great in vodka sauce. For future experimentation, I may try bringing in some vodka sauce tricks (adding vodka a minute after the tomato, using Calabrian peppers).

The panko breadcrumbs I thought were weird first few bites but they grew on me.

Great on toast as suggested. I had a mild bread on hand, but I think sourdough would be great as the dish could use some more acid.

1/4 tsp of chili flakes is completely undetectable when swimming in a cup of cream. I doubled up front and still didn’t really notice anything. Will double again next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this vegan by using full-fat coconut milk in place of heavy cream, and vegan parm for the pecorino. This was actually so easy to pull together that I was convinced I had more work to do in the kitchen, and mis-judged telling my fam when dinner would be on the table!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: An excellent and easy recipe. I didn't bother to wipe out the pan between toasting the panko and cooking the onions, seemed a silly waste, and I used just 1/4 cup cream and a little extra water, which worked out just fine. I didn't;t find chopping the sun dried tomatoes particularly messy, but I had a big cutting board and a very sharp knife. The finished dish looked just like the picture, and tasted wonderful.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: After reading all of the comments, I just had to make this recipe. Wow, it is absolutely delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made exactly as directed and it was SO good! The contrast of textures and flavors was perfect.  Very rich, I might try substituing something for all or part of the heavy cream next time, but it was so worth it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I really waved to love this, but…eh. It was fine. Not worth the calorie load with all the heavy cream and olive oil, and the cream in particular muted the flavors. I also think I would’ve preferred the salad on the side, which is funny because I usually love mixing all my food together. I just felt like the salad was so bright and delicious on its own, and adding it on top of the beans masked its vibrancy. I’ll consider making it again with the combo of Greek yogurt and low-fat milk another commenter recommended, and probably half the oil. I’d also try it with sun-dried tomatoes rehydrated with hot water instead of oil-packed to cut down on excess fat. I’m not sure I’d add the Parmesan to the beans, either—I couldn’t taste it over the cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made as written and sooooo delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: just delicious!! Can’t wait to make it again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: My dad shared this recipe with me after absolutely raving about it. I made it tonight and I’m raving about it as well! We both used just milk instead of cream, and I don’t think it suffered in any way. My dad used kale and I used a mix of arugula and baby spinach. As a bonus, my house smells amazing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this "as is where is" and it was DELICIOUS !!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
The demon beans, I have had too many. The can I had to myself sit high in my belly, begging to escape. Now we wait to see how they will escape. And I would do it again! 


Add more lemon zest and juice for the delightful zing against the creamy demon beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I have not made it yet, but intend to. I like seeing comments and changes others have made before I make a new recipe. Not sure why that bothers some folks.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We make this all the time. It is so good! Would be good as a warm dip for a party

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this with half and half instead of heavy cream since that’s what I had, otherwise as written, and it was amazing! I think either cream or half and half would work. Incredibly delicious and satisfying. The arugula and lemon cut the richness of the bean mixture and the flavors play together really well. Will definitely make this again and feel it would be a great dish for entertaining. Especially for vegetarian friends.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I loosened the sauce with some champagne I had left over was amazing! Made this three time now, a family fav!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Really delicious! I kept the salad separate from the beans. While the salad was tasty too, and the sharp lemon and arugula flavours cut through the rich heaviness of the beans, I found it a bit fussy - next time I might just do a simpler mix of lamb's lettuce and arugula without all the panko etc.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I made this exactly as the recipe stated and there could never be enough ‘M’s’ in YUMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMMM!!!!!!!
Ps… I did not know that I like Canelli beans or any beans for that matter. 
Creamy but also bright and very slightly spicey.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely amazing! One of the tastiest dishes I’ve ever made-and simple, too!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made it exactly as written. Humble ingredients escalated to perfection. Will add to regular rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. I like a little more spicy heat in mine…added about 2-3 Tsp of crushed Calabrian chili peppers…worked well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: It’s amazing to me all these comments if they are so good why don’t they write cook books!!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I found it delicious

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Amazing!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I don't get it. Followed the recipe exactly as written.  The dish was ok, and might be better as a side to something else.  But as a vegetarian main, its a solid 3 stars for me.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a stand out side dish, not a main.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious! Couple small modifications: cooked the beans from dry (w/Parmesan rinds in a slow cooker), used the bean broth in place of 1/3 cup water, and used half cream/half whole milk. Recipe called for far more bread crumbs than I needed. I will be making this again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Not tasty enough to be worth the calories!  Some fish sauce, shrimp broth, something umami is needed.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We usually make it as directed. Very good. Can be too heavy so as others have mentioned the cream and oil can be cut.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So delicious! Made as written except used leeks instead of onion.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made exactly to the recipe, but would reduce the cream next time. I don't think the dish would suffer and it would hopefully be a little less unhealthy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was a great recipe! I mixed in about 1c roughly chopped Italian parsley and a few tbs of oregano into the beans when they were done cooking, and I used microgreens instead of arugula.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I like the sun-dried tomatoes. Next time I’m adding capers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Used coconut cream, worked great. Cheap and delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this many times as-is and also dairy-free. Both versions are delicious! If you don’t want to do vegan substitutes for dairy (I’m not really a fan of coconut milk in this recipe), you can sub a cup of red lentils for one of cups of beans. Cook down the lentils until they become disintegrated into the sauce before adding in the beans. The cooked down lentils gives the non-dairy version a lot of heft and a sort of light “creaminess”.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Didn’t have any cream so I subbed 1 cup of whole milk and left out the water. Otherwise followed the recipe exactly. Would make sure to make/buy a crusty bread to go with next time, as the recipe suggests. I would never think of the combo of arugula and tomatoey beans but it was a great meatless meal!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: So good, exactly as written.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used half and half and did the recipe exactly..”except” to my beans added Trader Joe’s Crunchy Chili/Onion instead of red pepper flakes as hubby can’t take “spicy.”

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Wait this one is an all-time fave, but we usually make it with gnocchi and sometimes pancetta if we’re feeling crazy

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! And easy and fun to prepare. Cut the cream and oil some like other reviews but that was a weeknight meal for us, I’d add it all if making for a friend or guest. Might try to add more veggies next time, I think bell pepper or mushrooms would work

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used a can of chopped chipotle chilis in adobo sauce as a sub for the sun-dried tomatoes. the result was a smoky, spicy and slightly sweet flavor. did not add the red pepper flakes as the chilis carried enough heat. delish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I add a few glugs of vodka - 10/10 recommend. I’ve made this about 5 times in the 2 months since discovering this recipe. It’s a fave!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
52g fat/serving😳
Definitely would not make as is written.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Thought it was too much work until I tasted it. Delicious. Will make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Add some smoked paprika in the mix and it’s delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Only had chopped red onions on hand and it added a nice texture to the creamy beans. Awesome with a little toast to enjoy this yummy version of beans on toast.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I use half and half and added a little bit of a veggie better than bouillon. I don't think the cheese is necessary, but I always add it.  I tried it once with oven roasted tomatoes from a jar and it wasn't as good; I generally use sun dried tomatoes from a bag, not in oil.  I often use a sprig of rosemary and remove it before serving as opposed to chopping it up.

I have no problem with the fat content but I just don't need another container in my fridge and I always have half and half at home.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious..used lactose free half and half ( what was available lactose free) and it was awesome.. I added some of the lemon juice to the pan after tasting and thinking it needed a bit of acid. Also added chili garlic crunch to add some heat. I really like a Smokey touch so I also added some smoked sea kelp I love to use in many savoury dishes…a bit umami.  I always taste as I go so can tailor to my preferences .  Enjoy :)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is so delicious-I dream about it !!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Good, but not as good, nor as easy, as the Ali Slagle NYT beans and greens recipe. We found this to be more of a hassle for a generally comparable taste, and we were more cranky at the end. If you have the time and energy, I’d recommend, but we wouldn’t make it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I wanted to add protein so I added the meat of two hot italian sausage. Yum!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Excellent!! So easy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made half the recipe last night. Perfect amount for 2 people. Boyfriend doesn't like arugula, so I sprinkled the toasted panko on top for crunch with a squeeze of lemon. We had side salads with lots of crunchy veggies. Was missing something, probably the crusty bread to complete the meal. Would have been tasty over rice too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I sub one can of beans for gnocchi.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: 5 ⭐️ With 10k 5 star reviews I wanted to give this unusual combination a try. It was delicious and weeknight easy.  Such great flavor combinations and different.   Easy to see why it is so highly rated!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used one can of white beans & another can of kidney beans. As we are lactose intolerant, I used lactose free sour cream which gave it a slightly tangier taste. I also added bell peppers & some baby spinach. Hubby absolutely loved it & will definitely make it a regular dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved this recipe. Thank you to the person who suggested replacing the cream with pumpkin seed puree. I toasted half a cup of pumpkin seeds and blended them with 3/4 of a cup of water. I also added chili crisp to the beans and a splash of lime juice at the end to give the dish a little more zing. Healthy and satisfying.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Great flavors - amazing weeknight dinner! That arugula salad on its own is a keeper! Cheers to "beans dinners!"

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Oh.my.goodness.  One more vote for "absolutely delicious!"  We made this tonight and were blown away by the rich flavor.  Easy-peasy creative comfort food that totally surprised us.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was excellent on this winter day in Northern Europe. Per some suggestions, I added about a 1/2 tsp of mustard as well as 1/4 c red lentils. Make this!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This looks delicious, and I have (almost) all the ingredients, so it will be dinner tonight. Thanks to those who have shared their variations; I will be using half and half or cashew cream to replace the heavy cream. Looking forward.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We grew some dry beans this summer and I was looking for a treatment that would do them justice and THIS IS IT.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I will definitely make this but why rinse the beans? I never understand this, especially when it's a creamy dish. There's so much flavor in the liquid

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: These beans taste expensive!  I will make them again and use less cream and maybe a sturdier bean.  My beans kinda melted into the sauce, but still tasty.  We had the oven on already so I put them in to get some maillard browning on the top

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Growing up, my sister and I thought the best dish in the world was "Penne Rosa" from Noodles & Company. This dish is basically that in bean form... incredible. The breadcrumby/parmesany greens are also delicious and I could see reusing them often.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: very forgiving recipe - i didn’t have parmesan or sun dried tomatoes handy so i substituted a bit of grated cheddar and 1/2c rao’s vodka sauce. i also had some preserved lemons that i used in the salad. such an easy & delicious meal!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Very good made as is. Easy to make, and a comforting, but not heavy, lunch or dinner to be sure.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I don’t usually like white beans but I love this recipe! The lemon and arugula are such a nice balance with the creamy sauce. Straightforward and fun to make.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We add a low fat European style yogurt to up the protein and cut down on fat.  You can use Greek yogurt but the results are not as creamy and tasty.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this a bunch now and we love it. We like to add some smoked paprika to it too. Really sends it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This was even better the next day. Oh my!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Lovely dish! I topped some of the left overs with a fried egg for breakfast. Very tasty and filling!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Didn’t have sun dried tomatoes (arg!) but otherwise made as written. Good, interesting, not fabulous. Liked the unusual blend of flavors. Really enjoyed the salad /panko topping. Might try with some nuggets of Italian sausage cooked in.  Would have been better with the dried tomatoes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! When I made it for dinner, I served with some garlic butter shrimp on the side for added protein. Leftovers for breakfast the next morning were great topped with two fried eggs!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made the bean recipe without the salad. It's much too liquid.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Pretty good recipe, but between the cream and olive oil, way too much fat for both nutrition and mouthfeel. I feel like it would do just as well with 1/3 the oil and 1/2 the cream.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this recipe as written for dinner and it was a lovely contrast between the wild arugula salad and creamy beans. I gave it 4 stars though as the heavy cream was just too much (see pretty much all comments below). Next time ill cut back substantially and add milk if it feels dry.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I’m in the minority but this was a big miss for me. I tried to follow the recipe to the letter, except for using pre-cooked Rancho Gordo Marcella beans instead of the canned. In my kitchen, the beans were exactly how I’d have expected from reading the recipe - a fairly one-note and unsatisfying mix of beans in a bland tomato sauce with some cheese on top, like one of your more basic college dorm pasta recipes but substituting beans for the pasta, and the greens mixture on top was a totally different, notably sharp salad that might have been an acceptable side to the dorm room pasta but didn’t add anything to the meal by being layered on top. A rare miss.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: All I can say is wow. So rich and divine, reminiscent of a vodka sauce or tikka masala. When I made this for my family they said they’d order this dish in a restaurant!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Put the breadcrumbs into the sauce, and that was done. Added spinach instead of arugala.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Decreased cream to 1/3C because some didn't want that much cream.  Still excellent results, but add some additional liquid (water/broth/milk...) so beans don't dry out.  Served the salad and panko crumbs in dishes on the side so that they wouldn't become soggy & people could choose amounts.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Added harissa for spice, canned tomatoes (no juice) instead of sun-dried tomatoes, used 1/2 Greek yogurt and 1/2 water in place of cream. Served over rice. YUM!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
First, my edits:
Instead of 1 cup heavy cream, I used 1/4c. heavy cream, 3/4 cup 1% milk. 1/4 tsp red pepper flakes (mistake-would use 1/2 tsp next time). No arugula on hand, so baby spinach it was.
As I was tasting it near the end of cooking, I couldn't understand what the big deal was. But when I started eating it, with the lemony baby spinach on top, and a glass of wine, yum! The lemony salad on top makes it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is easy for a weeknight; The addition of a diced jalapeño along with the onion adds a popular kick at our table.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I just made this tonight for the first time and thoroughly enjoyed every aspect of it as stated. My favorite part was the salad with the Panko and lemon just an explosion of flavors.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
Oh boy is this good! I was worried all that cream would make it too rich, so I scaled back a bit. Otherwise, followed the recipe exactly.

The balance created by the arugula, panko, and the spicy beans is unbelievably delicious- it’s a bit crunchy, then savory smooth, and then the lemon enters and….wow! Loved this.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Use another pan-- don't cook tomatos in cast iron.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This dish is now on permanent rotation. I make a half batch but make extra arugula with all the dressing - that's what makes this dish really shine. I am generous with the onion, garlic, and sun-dried tomatoes (the kind without oil cut some calories). Lowfat milk is just fine, and I go extra there as well to make it a little more soup-like as well as to plump up the tomatoes if I go with the kind without oil - I prefer their vibrant color. Serve with slices of homemade sourdough garlic toast.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this but changed the arugula to chopped spinach and served it over pork tenderloin spice encrusted medallions. Amazing!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is delightful, filling, romantic and simple. It works so well. Will definitely make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is my new favorite vegetarian recipe - wonderful flavors - and although I soaked and cooked my own beans, it was easy to assemble and cook . I used half and half instead of cream (that's what I had on hand) and added a sweet red pepper - it's a forgiving recipe and one that can adjustment to what's in your pantry and the fridge.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made the recipe as is but found the amount of cream suggested spoiled the result. The colour was very washed off and not vibrant red as in the pictures. Would definitely limit cream to 1/3 cup next time. I added paprika (to try and salvage the colour) and half a lemon juice to the beans to add acidity and distract from the fat content. Did not add the cheese in the beans, as they were soooo rich already. I used dried Pinto beans

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This dish is now in my dinner rotation!

I cut back on the cream. I recommend 1/3-1/2 cup. I also sautéed some kale and added that, since it is what I had on hand. Served it with toasted baguette, which was great!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is such a delicious and versatile recipe! I made a few tweaks based on my preferences and what I had on hand. I omitted the garlic and added thyme and basil for some extra flavor. I added mushrooms since I had some to use up. I didn't use breadcrumbs. I used full-fat coconut milk instead of heavy cream. I used kale instead of arugula. I would imagine that the base flavors here are amenable to a wide range of adaptations. I will most definitely be making this again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious! I made two small but impactful substitutions: I used coconut milk instead of cream, which I often do in recipes—it does not change the flavor much and is a great option if you want to avoid dairy.  And for the tomato paste I substituted ketchup. This may sound odd, but it is a great substitute in all manner of dishes (such as chili).  It adds the perfect bit of tangy sweetness.  This recipe will be on repeat!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Good, and even better the next day.  Also squirted extra lemon juiced on the leftovers as they needed more acid than on the first day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Really great. I followed true to the recipe but added some oregano near the end. The crumb topping was a really nice touch.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made this last night, and it was delicious. As I have the time I cooked my own beans (Rancho Gordo "Marcella") which I highly recommend. Also you substitute the bean broth for the 1/3 cup of water that gets added in the final mix for extra richness.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I love this recipe so much!  I agree with many other commenters that it is my favorite NYT recipe….except for maybe the Tomato Basil Chicken Breasts!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Excellent the first time followed exactly except used coconut milk in place of cream, which I’m not crazy about. Excellent the second time going full-on vegan route (as per "Ann"’s comments) even though we’re not vegan, using nutritional yeast in both parts, grainy mustard with beans, and paprika with crumbs. What a great, innovative recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved this recipe, but found the consistency to be a bit thick, even though I used the prescribed quantities of cream, etc., so I added some water, and also added some lemon juice to the beans to brighten the flavor there a bit. All in all an outstanding combination! The lemony arugula does a good job cutting through the richness of the creamy beans. Adding some lardons would be good, if you wanted to opt for some additional flavor and veer from the vegetarian vibe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I halved everything as making for 2
added a full teaspoon of chilli flakes for extra heat and also added a teaspoon of smoked paprika. 

DELICIOUS!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This sounded good, so I mashed it up with one of my favorites, Crispy Haloumi with Tomatoes and White Beans. ZOMG. So good. Added lots of smoked paprika, and some smoked cinnamon. Per other commenters, sprinkled the garlic breadcrumbs on top. Used cherry tomatoes per the Haloumi recipe, one can of white beans, and no sun-dried tomatoes. Cut haloumi into large cubes. Two of us ate the whole thing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I wish I had added the cream a little at a time vs. just dumping it in all at once, because I think I would have liked the dish better if it had been more tomato-y and less decadently rich and gloppy. I will make again, likely with more garlic and less cream. I loved the lemony arugula with the crunchy browned bread crumbs as a foil to the creamy beans. A good trick to use as a side for other rich dishes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Could you use fava beans in this recipe?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Third times a charm for me on this one. I concur with the consensus that the heavy cream is a bit much. Though I’ve only gone as far as to sub half and half. Also, I found it was always a bit much on the lemon side as well. This time I just did the zest and left out the juice and the arugula thanked me for it. Same goes for the panko, which I have consistently lowered. This time I did 1/4 the recommended amount and I could still even do less and it would be healthier. But all in all, one of my favorite vegetarian dishes on NYT.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: crisp, light white wine vs water was a nice alternative.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is a really tasty dish and I enjoyed making it as much as I did eating it. One thing I did was to drain the oil from the tomatoes and used it to saute the onions, garlic. I'm planning on making more arugula salad with more dishes, like left over ravioli

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Easy to make while toddler runs around, even some steps they can easily participate in! The lemony greens just add that lovely acidic zing. Threw in Italian sausage for some added density.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Made recipe as is. It's an incredible dish, the sauce is akin to a vodka sauce. Next time I'll sub in some evaporated milk for the cream. We'll have this weekly!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: My daughter and I made this. Absolutely amazing taste! Not what we expected. We did not use any substitutions to the recipe and have no plans to in the future. This dish will always be in our rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Echoing others, I agree that you can halve the cream, easily, without compromising on flavor. I doubled the red pepper, but it could be tripled if you want a little heat. Doubling it didn’t do much. Arugula mixture is divine.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I really loved this - I am actively looking for vegetarian mains that are actually tasty, and this definitely fit the bill. I especially loved how it doesn’t use any exotic ingredients, but melds commonplace ingredients together in a different kind of way. It’s rare to achieve this! The beans on their own would be lovely, but the addition of the arugula salad added so much freshness and variety of taste and texture. Thank you!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Loved the bean stew, found the arugula salad so-so.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Wouldn’t change a thing. I personally wouldn’t describe it as a weeknight meal due to my work schedule (it’s easy, but there’s enough chopping and dishes to make it too time-consuming for me on a weeknight)… I’d describe it as as an excellent Friday night, end-of-week comfort supper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This dish is yummier than it has any real right to be. I could not quite envision how it would taste, but it seemed worth trying, given the many effusive comments. And so glad I did! I used arugula that I am growing in my garden, and some of last year’s cherry tomatoes that I’d sun-dried (in the oven, but still). Over Rancho Gordo Mayacobq beans, it was restaurant quality. Thank

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made this once the way it’s written & once with coconut cream & both ways were freaking delicious. 11/10 would recommend 🙌🏼

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I love this recipe, and use a fraction of the cream. Recently I used the arugula mixture on a prosciutto and arugula pizza. I highly recommend trying that!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
I significantly cut down the oil on this AND made it vegan, and it's still 5 stars! The brightness of the arugula goes so well with the rich, creamy beans and toasted sourdough.

Here are the change I made:
- Used a vegan cream (coconut / almond base). Next time, I'll try cashew cream or silken tofu since the coconut did come through a bit
- Reduced the oil by half
- Used Violife parmesan - I didn't measure, but it wasn't a ton. I don't think it needs much.

Delicious :)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I don't know why I don't make this more often--we both love it. Next time, though, I'll sub for the heavy cream. I don't often pay attention to calories, but this is a lot as written. I wasn't that engaged when I ordered the heavy cream I guess. I won't wait as long next time to make this again--easy and delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: This is excellent exactly as written and will make a fabulous dish for vegetarian guests.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe! I found I had double the amount of panko I needed for the amount of arugula. Next time I’ll use 1/4 c. I used 1/2 the cream and added in 1 c 4% cottage cheese. Next time I’ll put the full container of cottage cheese in (personal preference for more protein). Sun-dried tomatoes in oil such a flavour game changer vs the kind I usually use.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody:
This has no right to be as good as it is. The contrast between the creamy tomato beans, lemony arugula, and crispy breadcrumbs elevated this so much.

Had leftover bread on hand (and extra time,) so I toasted in the oven to make my own seasoned breadcrumbs. Kept the breadcrumbs separate from the salad to keep their crunch for leftovers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I was looking for the rest of the instructions because it was so easy to prepare! I prepared exactly as shown and it was absolutely delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I had leftover cooked shrimp and put that on the side. Worked! The arugula and lemon zest are key

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I made 1/2 recipe for 2 adults. I thought I had everything on hand but discovered the arugula missing after I had started gathering the ingredients. I used mixed salad greens and it turned out fine. I will make it again with the arugula!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Could I use Swiss Chard for the greens? I have a ton in my garden.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: My, my, my - well, of course this will be delicious w/ 1C of heavy cream & lots of sundried tomatoes!  Used 1.5C dried of Rancho Gordo's cassoulet beans.  Only thing I changed was to put in the entire 6 oz jar of tomatoes & doubled the garlic cuz that's who I am.  Never got around to the arugula last night but will tonight w/ the leftovers.  Yum.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Only I liked it?? Next time use 1.5 tubs beans. And cut all liquid down. Cut cream w hAlf milk. Needs lots of arugula

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: I used 1/2 cup of Greek yogurt and 1/2 cup of half and half, and it was a great substitute for heavy cream, and baby spinach instead of arugula. I cut the amount of sun dried tomatoes in half, and used balsamic vinegar instead of lemon juice on the spinach, along with lemon olive oil.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!!!! Wow!    Umami from the sun-dried tomatoes elevate this dish to be incredibly satisfying from a few simple ingredients.   Drained full fat plain yogurt in place of crreme fraiche - worked beautifully!!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: We have a version of this probably once a week. Arugula tastes great, but you could use kale in a pinch. Also if you don’t have panko, crumbled Ritz crackers are a tasty substitute. No need to toast those.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: The arugula salad is crazy good. Partner immediately requested this to be a weekly occurrence. 10/10 recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Creamy, Spicy Tomato Beans and Greens  Recipe
commentBody: Dank. I added meatballs and mixed in Swiss chard and all the salad dressings at the end, no arugula. The sauce is amazing!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
A note to diabetics (not a doctor and this is only my experience) but wrapping pasta in brocolli sauce and having a salad first has consistently kept my blood sugar low even with a generous serving of carbs.
Worth keeping in mind.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: My favorite instruction in this recipe is “stirring seldomly”.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Added broccoli florets after 5 minutes cooking time of the pasta.  They were perfect.  Also added 2 tbs of nutritional yeast to the toasted bread crumbs.  Delicious recipe I will make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Just want to reiterate what reader "Chuck" said.  I always just peel away the outer woody part of my broccoli stems.  The pith is a solid, sweet, usable vegetable, and that is exactly what I do, and did here.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Rather than discarding the "woody stem" roast it with sweet onion, garlic, carrot, salt, pepper and a bay leaf. Use that as a base for the stock. Time permitting. Roasting brings out the sweetness in the vegetables and you get more bang for your buck.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Halved the oil, broccoli was quite overdone so next time we’ll wait and add the broccoli at the latter half of the pasta step.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Peel the woody stems until you get to the tender layers and then chop. That will allow you to use every bit of the broccoli and also cut down on food waste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: A little too soupy

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Yep, too much cooking on the broccoli, add last next time.  And I admit I used Mini Farfelles instead.  They are cuter.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Cooked according to recipe and came out delicious.  Paired with a nice rosé, perfect for an early summer dinner.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Why on earth would a recipe call for 3.5 c of broth and 12oz pasta when most broth comes in 4c boxes and pasta comes in 1lb boxes! Madness! We just did 4c broth and 1lb pasta and added a bit extra garlic and broccoli.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
I loved this dish but it is not an entree. I added some Italian chicken sausage to make a complete meal. The breadcrumbs absolutely make this dish!
Tip for the broccoli: don't discard. Start a baggie with stems of broccoli, herbs, and other veggies and keep it in the freezer. Use it when you're making broth. Learned that from Lidia Bastianich!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I also would like a more herby flavor.  I will add a lot of fresh basil to the sauce for a pesto flavor next time

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! My veggie broth (Go Bio) may have thrown off the salt balance. I used the prescribed 1 tsp and certainly did not need any more for the final seasoning. Flavours married nicely in the time between my first and second helping; I'm looking forward to lunch tomorrow to see what happens in the fridge overnight! Best of all I was able to use the oregano a dear (and much younger) friend brought back from Sicily in her backpack last summer.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This is really good and easy! I would recommend adding the broccoli florets 5 minutes into cooking (or even later) though, as they got a little overcooked with the full 10 minutes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: It's good, but I wish there was a bit more herb-y flavor for what is essentially making a creamy pesto, but with broccoli. I'd probably do some mint and/or basil into that puree next time. It's good, but also kind of a lot of steps/dishes for a pasta dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: We also used thyme from the garden instead of oregano for the breadcrumbs.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
We followed the recipe closely and it came out beautifully.  We used five medium cloves of garlic (instead of three) and a full half teaspoon of chili flakes. It definitely has some heat so would consider cutting back for to appeal to all palates.  So simple yet extremely flavorful and quite healthy compared to many pasta dishes.   We combined Parmesan and Pecorino for a bit more umami and it was perfect.   

Will definitely add to the rotation!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Had to sub small conchiglie because I couldn't find orecchiette so I just cooked the pasta covered for 6 min instead of 10

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Pretty good. I used bigger pasta pack with fusilli but same amount of stock. Had to add some water at the end. Toasted panko at the end was delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Cut the florets off and leave them full size , some small some large.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made this as directed but used champagne vinegar in place of lemon due to an allergy and loved it. Will def make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: The recipe looks good and l’m going to make it. The intro calls for strict adherence to ingredient amounts. This is just a general comment - why do recipe creators use amounts like 12 oz of pasta and 3.5 cups of broth?  Most pastas is sold in 16 oz containers and broth is sold in 4 cup amounts.  I’m going to use those amounts. I’ll let you know how it works out!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Can this be made with frozen broccoli?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Yes it can.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This had good flavor and I like the crunchiness of the toasted breadcrumbs. I added the broccoli 5 minutes after cooking pasta and it was cooked perfectly. Overall, a good dish and I would make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
commentBody: easy and delicious! As written.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Braised Broccoli Pasta Recipe
