recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Not a weeknight recipe. Took way too long, too many steps. I haven’t tasted yet because I am tired of cooking this!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I did this with quartered Campari tomatoes in a big ol' cast iron skillet in the oven next to a sheet pan with the beans as suggested by Rebecca, and the skillet worked perfectly to hold everything at the end, and looked nice to serve from. Think I will try out the anchovy suggestion from another reviewer to add some more depth to the dish next time, but I was overall very pleased with the meal!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I used 12 oz pasta. Excellent meal

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I too cooked the beans on a separate sheet pan which allowed them to get crispy, I’m not a fan of rosemary so didn’t use it, but other than that I’ve made this 3 times now and it just keeps getting better and better!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I added fresh thyme and will again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was the first time I've roasted tomatoes and beans and I LOVED it!  The beans give a real "meaty" texture to the dish and the garlic/tomato/shallot combo is perfect. I forgot to get rosemary at the store, so went without and don't think it suffered!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Thanks Moira. I don't like rosemary and was wondering if I could leave it out or what I could substitute it with. I'll follow your lead.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Delicious delicious delicious! I put the beans and tomato mixture on the same pan but cooked on convection and found that everything crisped up nicely. I halved the olive oil and did not miss the extra. Only used 1/4 of red pepper flakes for my heat intolerant children. This will become a staple in our house.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Just ok.
I rate recipes based on a work/reward ratio 
This was too much work for the amount of reward.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made this almost exactly as directed (I just added 1/4 cup olive oil at the end instead of 1/2 cup - along with some pasta water).  This was so good- such a hearty and healthy week night dinner, especially with whole wheat pasta and a salad. The beans kind of melt and disappear a bit once you mix it all together but you can definitely taste them in most bites.  Might be a bit garlicky for some but I can handle a lot of garlic.  Even my 3 year old took a few bites (she prefers butter pasta).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Just loved this. Easy cleanup with parchment paper on sheet pans. Loved the way the beans crisped up but then melted into the sauce. Wonderful with orecchiette, but I can imagine it would be great with casarecci, gnocchi, or even a big dollop of the sauce on long pasta. Great last-minute pantry-ish dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This didn't work for us, a family of five, and we are big pasta eaters.  I also question the total time.  I'm not the fastest with a knife but 25 minutes of cooking time leaves 5 minutes for prep.  I envy the people that can meet that timeline.  Deleting from my saved recipes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: "Cooking time" does not include prep.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Add extra beans. Makes it saucier and better.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I doubled the beans — drained well and did some patting dry but didn’t rinse — which let me cut the pasta (whole grain penne, in my case) in half. Also used parchment paper for roasting, much easier cleanup and still left plenty of brown bits. Upped the sugar a little. It is delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Yum yum!! Made with medium sized shells and used a deep 12-inch skillet to bake the tomatoes, which I subsequently used to mix the pasta in the last step. One-and-a-halfed it, and everyone had seconds. There were barely any leftovers!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely dank and easy! The rosemary made the house smell so good, and the crisp white beans are fire. One of the best recipes/pasta dishes I’ve ever made. No notes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Made as another reviewer suggested - I roasted the tomatoes in my Dutch oven and then just used a slotted spoon to add the pasta straight from the water.  

I didn’t have any garlic cloves but I added garlic powder, onion powder, thyme, and oregano.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this a couple times now and definitely recommend doubling the beans and tomatoes (well maybe a pint and a half depending on your tomatoes). I also put it all on one sheet and that was fine. Let it go in the oven for closer to 35 minutes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I used thawed cherry tomatoes from last summer's garden.  They had a lot of moisture, so I doubled the amount of tomato paste.  Next time, and yes, there will be a next time, I will make the tomato mixture on the stove top, bake the beans until crunchy, then assemble on the plate.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made this tonight and it was a hit!  Beans and tomatoes can definitely share a sheet pan.  I used a bit less oil.  My family added shredded Parmesan on top but I didn’t think it needed it.  A keeper!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious. I skipped rosemary because I didn’t have any, and instead of the red pepper flakes I used a tablespoon of ‘nduja for more umami. I also roasted asparagus with olive oil on a third tray, which I mixed into the pasta near the end. Most notably, I forgot to add the final 1/2c of olive oil and it didn’t need it (the oil coating the asparagus must have sufficed). Will make again, especially when late season tomatoes are bountiful.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Don’t forget to save at least a cup of pasta water otherwise your pasta will be dry. I found I did not need the full amount of oil.

I like adding an extra can of beans otherwise it’s just too much noodle. I used the two sheet pans as the recipe states and put the beans on the bottom oven rack - they got nice and crispy that way.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
this has been in rotation in our house since published and we love it, here's my usual edits-
- for (slightly) easier cleanup, i roast the tomatoes in a 9x5 loaf pan and the beans in a 9x13 pan
- grape tomatoes instead of cherry, they're sweeter so I cut the sugar
- sometimes will double the beans &/or add an extra tbsp of tomato paste depending on how I feel that day
- I don't add the final 1/2 cup of oil - i use extra pasta water if needed & drizzle some at the end
otherwise as written !

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I made this mostly as written and we thought it was very good.  What I changed: less pasta and about half the oil specified.  Used one sheet pan with parchment paper and put both tomatoes and beans on the same sheet.  There was no issue with the liquid of the tomatoes preventing the beans from getting crispy.  Next time: Add more rosemary. Instead of putting the beans into the sauce, I will put them on top.  They lost their crispiness and disappeared in the sauce.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Very very bland and disappointing. I made a half recipe and put tomatoes and beans on the same sheet. Would not make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Sounds like your beans and tomatoes didn’t roast because you reduced the pan space. Key to roasting is surface area - free space without stuff! Also tomato juices running into the beans will prevent browning too because acid prevents browning. Simple fix for next time!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Excellent! Use two pans o that the beans crisp. Next time more tomatoes and at the end, I’ll add in a huge handful of chiffonade basil.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Very nice!
I used cut up Roma tomatoes, since that is what I had. Also reduced the oil and pasta water in the final stage

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Really good. Try adding chopped kale or spinach or … to pot

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Outstanding!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: completely tasteless and not worth making.  I have made many of the NYT's vegeterian meals that showed flavor- not this one; bland and uninteresting.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is a winner. I added three anchovy filets to the sauce which added depth. I would double the cannellini bean quantity as their crunchy texture was lost in the finished mixture. Otherwise, make by the book.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I used my immersion blender to really make the sauce nice and creamy after adding all the roasted ingredients, pasta water and olive oil back into the pot and served with some spicy meatballs to get a little protein in there.  'Twas great!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was really tasty despite my forgetting to reserve pasta water. The beans broke down and my kids didn’t find them offensive, but this dirties so many dishes. 2 pans, a pot, a strainer, and a cutting board.  :( I’ll probably make it again though.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Made this and it turned out great, followed up all steps of the recipe except on the olive oil side.

Used about 2 tbsp extra for the tomato marinade/sauce and didn't use the 1/2 cup of olive oil in the final step as there was plenty of liquid already.

Was easy to make and would definitely make again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This is a great concept but the ratio of pasta to bean/tomato mixture is off. It relies on an absurd amount of oil to hydrate the final product which is still dry. I’d double the tomato’s (not beans too) l, add a few anchovies, and cut 1/4 cup of the finishing oil at the end of the recipe. Also recommend reserving more than 1 cup of pasta water, just in case (especially if you don’t double the tomato’s). I’ve also made this dish without the pasta as a bean side dish and everyone loved it. Feel free to play around with the herb choices as well thyme or oregano work here too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Added a couple anchovies and extra garlic and rosemary. Mashed a few white beans for added thickness. Did the tomatoes in a cast iron skillet as someone else suggested. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: It could do with a generous handful of chopped continental parsley

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Delicious, even with little Campari tomatoes quartered. But I can’t wait to make this with cherry tomatoes from our garden.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I may this again when tomatoes are in season. The skins of the roasted tomatoes were rubbery and did not complement the crispy beans.  I think that I will stick to my grandmothers on top of stove recipe for pasta fagioli….

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I made this last night, using 2 pints of tomatoes and 2 cans of beans and some very old dried rosemary, dumping everything back in the pot to mix (and scraping the bits off the cookie sheets with a metal spatula, rather than pouring the pasta water on them, which would've made a huge mess).  I used a bit less olive oil in the final dish.  We thought it was amazing.  No one asked for cheese, which is unusual in my household.  I'll absolutely be making this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Not that this recipe needs one more 5-star rating but holy mackerel is this good! I don't leave a lot of reviews for anything but this one deserves it! Delicious! The crunch from the beans is as welcome texturally as it is nutritiously.  Just aces!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
First of all, it does not take 30 minutes!  More like an hour... and quite a mess.  And it is not easy.  Who are you kidding?  However, crisping the beans was awesome and I recommend doing just that for a snack with cocktails...
My husband said this was very greasy meal...regrettably, will not get cooked again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Everyone in my house enjoyed it, which is high praise indeed. There are five of us, vegetarians and meat eaters, and one confirmed white bean hater, and everyone liked it. Per some of the other comments - I did use about half the olive oil in the final step, no issues with dryness. I did put in some extra rosemary (I just of chopped until it seemed right, but definitely more than half a teaspoon). 

Next time, I might try roasting at 400 instead of 375. My oven can run a little of the cool side and I think the tomatoes could have used a smidge more heat. But, that’s not a criticism of the recipe, just how my oven works.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I forgot to add garlic and it was still delish! This is an easy weeknight pasta meal full of flavor ( even without the garlic!) I added extra red pepper flakes and grated parm cheese to taste and it worked.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: My bean-hating husband even loved it. I made it as written and it exceeded my expectations.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I I didn’t have time to roast the tomatoes, so I sautéed a can of petite, diced tomatoes and onion and garlic, and added the beans it took as long as it took the water to come to a boil soit worked out perfectly. Truly great quick meal would do it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Easy and enjoyable recipe . I concur with the suggestions that it makes for more clean up as written. I understand that the beans were supposed to crisp up like that, but mine didnt' really get that crispy anyway (still tasted great).  If you like your pasta saucy, make as is, but boil less pasta (12 oz instead of 16 oz).  A squeeze of lemon and a bit more salt upon reheating made it a decent lunch the following days.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Very good and would make again, but honestly I would cut the olive oil down in the last step (especially in this economy). I found the pasta to be very oily rather than smooth and silky, so I’d recommend subbing some of the olive oil for pasta water.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: @Brian I can't speak for anyone else. I know that I add sugar to tomato sauce at the recommendation of, wait for it, Sophia Loren. While I think it makes perfect sense for Italians to not use sugar, the tomatoes from Italy that get sent here are, in general, loaded with salt and that offsets some of the natural sweetness of the fruit that is a tomato. The addition of sugar or wine is an attempt to 'restore' the fruits sweetness.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: @ACH. My mother and grandmother frequently added sugar & vinegar both to “American” tomatoes, claiming that they needed to be seasoned heavily to make up for the lack of taste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made this tonight as my snow day dinner and it was V yummy- I sautéed some garlicky kale to throw in and squeezed some lemon on top at the end - really delish! Like others said, I used one sheet pan and roasted the tomatoes on one side and the beans on the other to make for less dishes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was a good, simple-ingredient, comfort food dish for a winter day. In support of my household dishwasher (husband), I cooked the beans and tomatoes on one sheet pan and it was FINE. The tomatoes can take one half of the pan all mushed up, and the beans are perfectly spread out on the other half. I’m not exactly sure how much 16 oz of tomatoes is - clearly not a pint, so I’d use two pints for 8 oz of pasta. I skipped the 1/2 cup of olive oil at the end and while I’m sure the oil would make it even better, it was still yummy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: A bit of a mess to make and well over 30 minutes unless you have a sous chef getting your mise en place. I’d soften the shallot in the skillet then throw the rest of the ingredients in and throw in the oven. I can’t abide finding a crunchy bit of onion in my sauce. Roasted beans are delicious and this is a good recipe but  I’d budget 45 minutes to allow for slicing and dicing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Did anyone else find that there wasn't enough tomato mixture for the 1pound of orecchiette?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Did anyone use parchment paper?...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made this a lot. Double the beans, 1.5 the tomatoes, and double the red pepper flakes. Perfect!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: No flavor for me. Beans added nothing but a poor texture.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I hesitated cooking this for years because I thought it was dumb to make pasta dish using a sheet pan. I made it and it was delicious. But I put parchment paper so there was no deglazing. Forgot the rosemary. Oops. It was still good. I will make this again and try the tomatoes in an ovenproof skillet. That way deglazing will not be awkward. Also, not even sure if the beans need to be roasted. I think this recipe is flexible. No need to overthink it. Relax with it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: made this with chickpeas instead of canelinni beans, didn’t add sugar,  baked the beans and tomatoes on one tray, and used a little bit of purple onion instead of shallot! turned out wonderful!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Any ideas for decreasing the amount of oil (almost a whole cup) in this recipe?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Use less?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This worked beautifully with tarbais beans. I cooked the pasta in a wide skillet, drained the pasta water into a cup measure, and pulled the ingredients together in that same skillet, adding pasta water back in until the texture was right. For my tastes I’ll add more hot pepper next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made this today but didn’t have quite enough cherry tomatoes. I used drained diced tomatoes to make up the difference, roasted as suggested and made the sauce. I only have protein plus pasta so used that, added a bit more crushed red peppers because I like a bit extra spice, and it was delicious. I do also wonder if you can use a but less oil but it did have a delicious sauce!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was tasty…It does get up there in calories and I don’t think (at least for me) it’s tasty enough to be worth the calories.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Didn’t find this to have amazing flavor despite all the steps and the use of so much oil.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I didn't use as much oil as called for.  I set the oven on convection roast. the beans came out super crispy and delicious. the first batch of tomatoes burned. overall,, the dish was very good. but it's too time-consuming to me.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Does anyone think this is missing some acid? It’s good, don’t get me wrong, but might need a bit of tang

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I really liked the cherry tomato sauce. I crisped the beans up (potentially too much) so every once in awhile there was a big crunch while eating. I’m not sure I like the surprise crunch in a soft velvety pasta. Maybe if there was also something in there slightly less crunchy than the beans the texture would make more sense to me.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Very good - be sure to use flavorful tomatoes

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe. I will definitely make is again. Don't think 1/4 cup of olive oil is too much. It makes the dressing saucier. Definitely follow the recipe as written. In the end i even didn't think it needed grated cheese. But that's my taste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: did i miss a step here reading the recipie.  what is on the first pan.   is it the tomato paste??

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This recipe came out well.  Used halved grape tomatoes as they were less expensive.  Still turned out great.  I used dried rosemary rather than fresh (it's what I had on hand) and 1/4 tsp didn't seem like enough in the finished product so I'll ad a bit more next time.  Will definitely make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I loved this recipe!will definitely make it again, perhaps with a little anchovy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I used one large sheet pan for this and it worked beautifully. The beans were toasted and roasty, and I added 3T of tomato paste instead of 2. My meat loving spouse LOVED this, as did my 7yo.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: If you'd prefer to keep the beans crisp add them as a topping rather then folding them in with the sauce.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Very tasty!  We think it could benefit from more tomatoes, maybe 20-24 oz.  I also included some small whole tomatoes (branded as Sprinkles in our grocery store) - added a tasty pop of tomato flavor!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I absolutely love this dish.  A few notes responding to questions and comments:  (1) Use two sheet pans.  The extra sheet pan allows the beans to crisp, and that effect is definitely worth it.  The beans taste much meatier.  (2) If you're worried about the excess oil, still use the amount called for, but serve a smaller portion.  The extra fat (health olive oil) is essential to the dish.  (3) Consider adding more parmesan to the sauce at the end!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Not worth all the trouble of cleaning sheet pans.  Once the beans are mixed with the tomatoes they are no longer crisp..a similar recipe cooked in a skillet would be just as good and a lot easier.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made almost exactly as written on two sheet pans - but the pans seemed to have enough space I want to try on one pan next time. Could’ve roasted a bit longer in retrospect but this is still delicious! Beans did crisp up out of the oven but didn’t retain the texture in the sauce and rather made it creamy. Didn’t use quite 1/2 cup olive oil at the end but it was still delicious! Ended up almost all reserved pasta water.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I was worried about this one, it didn’t look promising at the end as I stirred big time. Once served with liberal grated Parmesan, it was really good and yet different than my many other pasta dishes in a good way. My only tweak would be using both pints of Trader Joe’s organic mixed tomato pints rather than the 16 ounces I cut and measured. My husband loved everything about it and especially the savory quality. A winner for a different pasta dish. Next time I will prep all earlier.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: The sauce is hella delish. Wish I had doubled it for the 16 oz pasta shells I had on hand. Frankly it was hard not to polish off the crunchy baked beans before it was time to throw them into the sauce. Fingers crossed that the family will be convinced to have a meatless meal as this is so good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: It's ok but really I didn't think it was worth the effort. However, I will say that the crispy beans would be a great snack on their own.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
I used great northern beans, which tasted great but didn’t hold up well in the oven. They did crisp up nicely, but looked more like popcorn than beans. I will use cannellini next time!

I also thought the amount of oil sounded excessive, but it was actually perfect. 

I wouldn’t say that this is similar to pasta e fagioli. This dish is much richer and more textured. 

Adding sugar to tomatoes is a southern Italian thing. (Maybe it’s related to the more acidic tomatoes in those regions?)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made this tonight. Assumed Step 7 was intended to direct stirring in the remaining 1/2 oz pasta water rather than more olive oil. It was very good this way. I can't imagine how overly saturated this would taste stirring in a 1/2 cup of additional olive oil at the end.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Yeah, I cut down on the olive oil but suprisingly used more of the pasta water than I thought I would.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was excellent. Used two cans of beans and am glad I did—wouldn’t have been substantial enough otherwise. Might use more herbs next time, too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: The next time I make this I'm going to double the ingredients for the "dressing". With 1 pound of pasta, I think it needs more flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This is very good & filling. Made as written w/ some changes. Had 2x the sauce as only had 1/2 of rotini pasta but will b the way I make it in future. Used 3 TBS of tomato paste.  1st serving didn’t have enuf pasta water. 2nd serving added more & was major improvement. Used only 1/4 tsp of red pepper as don’t like a lot of heat.  Great meatless Monday recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: So I used one sheet pan and it worked fine. Also, what I would change next time is I will double the crushed pepper and I will add sliced green olives to the mix maybe a couple capers. I used grated pecorino Romano and next time I will use some form of shredded Parmesan or pecorino for texture. Very good dish. We ate ate way more than one portion in our first sitting!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Need to add another 8 oz tomatoes. I only added 1/4 cup olive oil at end. Beans pop. Kind of fun!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Just made this. so delicious. Creamy and flavorful. Made exactly as written. May have used less oil as I only measured for the dressing portion.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
I doubled the beans, cut the pasta to 12 oz, and only used about 1/4 cup of olive oil (what was required for the tomato dressing) plus a light addition to the pasta at the end. I used extra pasta water to make up for less oil. 

It was delicious. All three of my kids loved it - definitely one to make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I like this well enough and will make it again but found the heat from the red pepper a bit distracting. I’ll use a little less of that next time. Used about 1/4 cup less oil than called for and was fine.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Nice simple recipe with good flavor easily & quickly thrown together with on-hand ingredients. Made as directed but did beans separately in the air fryer for ~20 minutes at 350 and they were lightly browned & nicely crunchy but they kind of disappeared when blended into the tomato mix. Could have roasted the tomato mixture a bit longer - they were kind of mushy- 25 minutes not enough. 
Cut 1/4 C of oil out of the last step and it was fine - recipe calls for so much oil!
Will make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
YUMMM. We loved this dish.
I used about .75 lbs of pasta because I knew id want it more saucy and Im glad I did. I also added some torn prosciutto to the bean pan and it got really crispy. I definitely recommend doing this or using another salty meat. You have to cook it on two separate baking sheets or the beans wont get crispy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: loved this so much - only changes were .75 lbs of casarecce since it's what we had, & 24 oz of grape tomatoes that I roasted in a dutch oven instead of on a pan, pulled them out and finished the pasta inside the dutch oven. next time I'll use the whole lb of pasta and double the beans - there was plenty of space on the pan. but today's ratio of pasta-tomatoes was fantastic to serve with some foccacia and a green salad. great recipe -- like others have said, incredibly easy & tasty!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I liked it. Added chopped mushrooms and chopped fennel to the tomatoes. Added torn rabe to the  pasta the last 2 minutes of cooking. Even at 25 minutes, the beans were nowhere as crisp as croutons! Next time, I'll roast them longer. Per Brian's comment, I omitted the sugar to no obvious harm.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Lol, why did all my beans explode in the oven?! Guess that’s what I get for trying to use an appliance in a rent-stabilized building… 10 mins at 350, I swear!!Beans were still tasty tho! Used hardcore amounts of red pep flakes, garlic and parm. Loved it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made it, I expected more flavor.  I  added another can of beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Lots of flavor in a can of beans!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
I used a can of drained diced tomatoes, a can of great northern beans, half the amount of pasta called for (I used 8 oz tubetti) and half the amount of olive oil and it was saucy, flavorful and delicious! Loved it! 
(Yes I roasted canned tomatoes, yes it was delicious)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Use half the red pepper flakes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Unless you like red pepper flakes, in which case you should follow the recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious and easy to make on a weeknight! I added a little extra garlic and red paper. I also had some eggplant sitting around and decided to roast and add it, too. Everyone in my house loved it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Used lentil and chickpea fusilli, Marcella Hazan’s tomato sauce and sprinkled the roasted beans over the top of the bowl of pasta and sauce - nice and crunchy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: We substituted the pasta with gnocchi with success, the crunchy beans with the buttery gnocchi are a great combination.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I’d use a second can of beans next time and make sure to time the pasta so it’s done with the tomatoes (otherwise pasta and water will cool too much)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Took the suggestions for an anchovy, use half the pasta, roast the tomatoes in a skillet in the oven, and misread the amount of oil as 1/4 cup. Spectacular. Thanks, folks.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
This very basic recipe was exactly what I was looking for to use up some ingredients. 
I used 12 ounces of pasta instead of 16 ounces because I like extra sauce. Like others commented, I used 1/2 cup of olive oil instead of 3/4. 

It really needs freshly grated cheese for extra flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Yes use an oven-ready skillet for tomatoes for less hassle and fewer pans.  Cut back Past to 3/4 pound?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Great balance of flavor and textures!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Use less pasta 1/2 lb is plenty, use a handful extra tomatoes, this is soooo good

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Recipe is perfect as written. Such deep rich flavor from a few simple ingredients.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Cooked as written, just without the sugar and it was great. I think the sugar would've been too sweet. When I lived in Italy, I noticed how pizza sauce was much less sweet, so I'm biased towards that experience but I stand by my choice with this recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was a huge disappointment, so bland.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: i think this needed some acidity, i added a lil wine when deglazing and added a little balsamic. overall… not worth having to clean two sheet pans. maybe would have been helped by some fresh herb

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Did was just ok for me. May be my tomatoes weren’t the best. Skins were tough. I used non-stick foil so maybe that was why tomatoes didn’t brown.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Use parchment paper next time, you will get the browning, a clean pan, and the suc, allbeit a bit harder to scrape off. Foil will foil you almost every time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Fine. Nothing outstanding. I recommend serving with basil for a bit of lightness.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this several times and have never gotten the beans to be crispy, definitely not like croutons, any suggestions? I think it’s delicious either way.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This is our favorite make ahead dinner when our son has soccer practice at night. It warms up beautifully! After rushing home from work, supervising homework, checking on piano practice, making sure the kid is changed and packed with cleats, ball and water, getting to practice and returning home, just take it out of the fridge and rewarm. Eat with a crisp salad. Yum.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: How do you keep pasta from clumping? Loved the flavors, but when the pasta clumps it doesn’t get cooked. Has anyone tried with a different pasta?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Stir the pasta once or twice while it cooks. I now cook my pasta in minimal water rather than a quart or more. It doesn't clump.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Make sure the water is at a strong boil, and stir right after adding your pasta.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Really loved making this, don't skip on the fresh ingredients - fresh rosemary, garlic, and garden fresh tomatoes come through!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: If one would like to make the above recipe vegan, but still want the consistency of melted cheese on top, try 'mozzarella cheese' from Whole Foods (or others) which is 'cheese' made from soy products. I have enjoyed it for many years.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Yum. And vegans can always top with nutritious yeast for the umami that Parmesan would otherwise offer.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Has anyone made this and frozen for later?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Rebecca, thank you for streamlining this dish. Since I need to divide the recipe in half because I can’t tolerate the pepper flakes and I know my husband would enjoy this dish so much more with them included.  I use the quarter sheet pans often for this reason.

I will add another note once I’ve cooked the dish. No changes needed except for division.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: The tip of roasting in an oven proof pan and transferring that to the stove was great, I wish I had read the one about halving the oil before I cooked — way too much oil for my taste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I made this dish for a large gathering. I’m Italian and have a lot of experience making various sauces. I used the cherry tomatoes from my garden, which are sweet and delicious and my family’s daily snack food. I used about 18 ounces of tomatoes which still didn’t seem like enough, and ended up adding Cheeky Monkey dipping sauce after completely following the recipe as written because it wasn’t spicy enough for me. Everyone complimented it. I will make this recipe again but with several tweaks.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Used 1/4 cup olive oil at end
Used two garlic cloves
Use more tomatoes next time
Maybe add bacon or pancetta.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I made a half recipe and had enough for meal for 2 people and plenty left over for lunch. Made on 2 1/4 sheet pans, which each would have accommodated the full recipe.  I will make again but with less pasta--seemed like too much pasta for the sauce. Maybe 12 ounces for the full recipe. I also cut the salt a bit for our taste. Excellent recipe. I think I just dump sauce, pasta, and some pasta water in a large pasta bowl in the future.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Can I make this without using oil? I have heart disease and cannot have oil.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: All good except for the addition of sugar. No! Don't add sugar! It's awful. Why do so many tomato sauce recipes include this? Totally unnecessary and spoils the flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
So I’d undercook pasta by at least four min because it continues cooking when you combine sauce
I used chickpea pasta and roasted three links of turkey sausage in the oven then cut up and added with some baby spinach. 
It definitely needs some salt adjustment at the end but I’d rather have it need salt then be too salty. I also used whole sprigs of rosemary and removed at end because I like the essence but not the bits especially if over roasted. It’s very hearty and yummy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I also added NO sugar you don’t need it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made as written, except I forgot to halve the tomatoes (didn't matter, though they would probably have caramelized more with the juices running out). Excellent recipe, and I wouldn't rush to reduce the olive oil--the texture was about right. The 3.5-year-old ate it happily.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Great way to use up garden tomatoes with new flavors and textures. The beans are so delicious done this way! I made it as written through step 4, then combined beans and tomato mixture and served it in a dish next to the dish of pasta as an add-your-own amount on top of your pasta. Also fried up hamburger meat separately also as an add your own to taste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: send to Morgan, MB and Taylor

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I loved this great, flavor-filled, saucy recipe and made that much sauce using 3/4 lb. of pasta and at least doubling the amount of fresh rosemary.  Also used Rigatoni and it was perfect.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: We loved this dish and will definitely make it again.  My only negative is that the leftovers were very dry.  Any suggestions?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
This is too many pans for what is essentially spaghetti sauce. Very boring. 
Just open a jar and add red pepper fakes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This recipe made my vegan heart incredibly happy!  Such a lovely dish that actually tasted creamy and smooth.  The roasted white beans added such a special touch. The only change I made was adding an extra helping of red pepper flakes because my family loves spice.  I will definitely be making this on a regular basis.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Just made this tasty dinner. I cleaned/scraped the pan w/a bit of red wine and pasta water to add a taste of the wine and clean the pan a bit more easily/ acid in wine. I used oregano from the garden as well. thanks for this recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Prepared per the recipe, but took the advice of the writer to use less pans.  Worked perfectly using a cast iron skillet!  Used 1/4 cup less olive oil and it was fabulous!!  Would be great as accompaniment to brunch aside scrambled eggs!  Yum.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I wonder how this would turn out cooked on a closed outdoor grill instead. When the AC is on, I prefer to avoid adding heat to the kitchen when possible. Our grill is fueled by pellets so it will be smoke-flavored, which could be nice...or not

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I tried this with fennel. Excellent. Also  microwaved  a slice of prosciutto for 30 seconds and crumbled over the finished dish. used Rebecca's tip about using a frying pan, on less pot to wash. Did not need cheese.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Followed the advice of roasting the tomatoes in a pot. Used a half sheet tray and added 8 oz of halloumi torn into bite sized pieces on one side, beans on the other. Absolutely delicious and sooo super easy. The halloumi toasted up at the same rate as everything else. This is a keeper!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Doubled the garlic, shallot, and pepper flakes. Took a good suggestion & added some anchovy (3) to the tomato mixture.  Also a finely sliced serrano. Used a 9 x 13 baking dish for the tomatoes in which i assembled the dish. Roasting the beans is brilliant.  Used about a cup of pasta water.  Add the sugar and all of the oil!  These flavors are the reason Italy can claim one of our world's great cuisines.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Really wonderful weeknight dinner.  The rosemary and the beans play so well together, and there is a delicious combination of textures and flavors.  As I live with someone who doesn't think its dinner without meat, I added some torn pieces of prosciutto to the pan with the beans.  It crisped up and added fantastic brawniness and saltiness to the dish, so good! I used a little less pasta than was called for, 10 oz instead of 16, so there was more sauce to go around.  Great dish!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I threw them together on the same pan and it was wonderful!! I did have a huge pan though. Great note about the sucs! Added vegan parmesan on top as well

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Superb! So good that after one bite I was thinking, "How soon can I make this again?" It's actually better without any cheese: the best vegan recipe I know.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Delicious recipe!! Enjoy it with my family. Thank you.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Made this when Lucas & Co were here, 5 people, 500g pasta. It would have been too little for 5 very hungry people.
Doubled the beans (2 cans weisse Bohnen vom Spar), they were quite small and needed less than 20 min in the oven (200 C).
750 g of cherry tomatoes.
I was a bit cautious with pasta water and olive oil, it could have soaked up more: give it some time before you de die it’s enough pasta water because it will soak it up.
Definitely not overly sliced, could add more?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: We made this, but without cherry tomatoes. Wild, I know! But it was actually so good and had magnificent depth of flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! Made exactly as written except with maybe a few more tomatoes. I wouldn’t change a thing! Use all the olive oil and sugar. The flavors are complex and satisfying. I have other recipes when I want something light….. this is decadent, as it should be.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Tasty tasty tasy

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: We loved this, used about .5 C olive oil. Needs more tomatoes

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Used 10oz of grape tomatoes instead of 16oz cherry tomatoes due to availability and it was a fine amount. Also, the beans were done about 10 minutes before the tomatoes. Overall satisfied!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This recipe made my house smell amazing :) The beans were overpowering, next time i will use less beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I made this but made a slight change by blending the tomatoes and beans along with the pasta water and olive oil. It was amazing! Also served with a fresh handful of arugula. An amazing addition.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious! I followed a suggestion to use an oven-safe skillet for the tomatoes and that made it easy to add pasta water and scrape. But I then dumped the contents into the pasta pot with the beans for ease of stirring. 
I also didn’t add the last 1/4 c of olive oil—it just didn’t seem necessary. 

The roasted beans gave it such a nice crunch!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I really don’t like white beans but need to include them in my diet. This makes them less yucky. I do agree that the 2 sheets are not necessary. You can always build a separator with aluminium foil. I don’t think the sugar in the sauce is necessary and I would add spicy paprika paste to upgrade the taste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Fantastic as written! Added burrata on top because why not…yum.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I have made this several times and love the recipe. I have cut back on the olive oil in the tomato mix with no ill effects. I do roast everything on one big sheet pan—beans on one side, tomato mix on the other. If hubby were not a vegetarian I would be adding some pancetta for extra joy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I doubled the amount of beans, and put tomatoes and beans on aluminum foil in the same sheet pan, creating a ridge between them -- worked perfectly to keep them separate. Delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Or just add the whole can.  More beans might be nice too

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I did not get any brown bits. Is it because I used San Marzano (oblong) tomatoes? Otherwise this was exceptional with some fresh basil and grated pecorino on top. Next time, I might try this out using a second can of cannellini beans, mashing half and keeping the second half somewhat intact and crunchy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Fully agree with Paul Banas. Beautiful recipe but tweaks could include: Skip the sugar. Have more sauce (or less pasta). And, roast the tomatoes and beans a bit longer to brown...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was great! Used a silpat mat to roast the tomatoes, so no need for deglazing with pasta water. I’d definitely double the tomatoes or halve the pasta next time, personal preference, but the recipe is great as-is.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Meh, not bad, not great.  The crisp beans got too dry in taste and texture for us.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Not worth all the effort roasting for the end product.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was fabulous! Raves from everyone. Easy, flavorful and the texture of the roasted beans was sublime. I love a recipe that delivers so much more than the effort that goes into it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: ….why am I confused. So i’m pouring the pasta water onto the baking dish to scrape it into the pan?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made like Rececca suggested. Used less pasta. Was good. Will make again

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Could add more flavoring / onions to the roasted thing but i really like the idea of a sheet pan becoming your sauce

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: My housemate loved this so much she immediately asked me to make it again! I used one sheet pan since I only own one (college student), but based on these notes next time I’ll try to source another.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: In regard to adding sugar to the tomato sauce my comment is similar to another writer's. I also live in Italy, am married to an Italian.  Most often the tomatoes in the sauce comes from a can of peeled tomatoes (called peloti) as tomatoes from the supermarket hardly ever are fresh enough to contain enough natural sugars, nor do the peeled tomatoes in the can usually, so sugar is added to the sauce at the end if there is too much acid  when it is tasted for salt content and acid content.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This had a lot of potential but tasted bitter. Maybe the garlic was overcooked while roasting. We couldn't eat it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
This was a big hit with our family.  I made as written 
and while it did create a lot of dirty utensils, I read someone’s note about using a cast iron skillet for the tomatoes and putting the beans on a quarter sheet to make tidying easier.  Brilliant!  Only charge I made was I added about 2 tablespoons of chopped Italian parsley before serving. 
Next time I am going to try to reduce the oil and also use less pasta.   The sauce was sufficient but it would be nice to have a bit more of it .

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Yum! I used only half the recommended pasta & it was still plenty for 4 servings. Also, It doesn’t really create enough sauce for a pound of pasta. I made the sauce & roasted the tomatoes in an oven proof skillet as suggested by another reader. That worked great helped with clean up.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
I made this last night with two changes:
—halved the pasta
—instead of red chili flakes, did about 2tsp of Calabrian chile paste.

If halving the pasta, prob can reduce some of the garlic. I like garlic but I can see some would think too garlicky.

I think you need to use a large sheet pan, as stated in recipe, so that some of the juices from the dressing browns. These browned bits really contribute!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Thanks for all your notes, people. I will make this, but I always learn so much from reading what you've discovered.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made this as a NYE veggie pasta addition for a get together. Added veggie broth rather than olive oil and sprinkled with parsley. The tomato mixture is super tasty but all added together with the other ingredients it got lost. Would use it on something else, maybe fish? Would make this again but half the amount of pasta.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Used just over half the red pepper flakes so they didn't overwhelm the other flavors. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: One of the truly one-sheet recipes out there - so quick, easy, and delicious. Also makes a fantastic leftover dish the next day!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: No need for sugar. The tomato paste is sweet enough.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This is indeed easy and tasty. I agree with the other commenter who suggested putting the tomatoes straight into an oven safe skillet and skipping the messy baking sheet step. I also saved a little cleanup by baking the beans on parchment paper. I dressed them in oil in a bowl instead of on the pan.  I like my pasta pretty heavy on the sauce so this did not make anywhere near 4-6 servings for me.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Loved the dish, it’s a great mix of flavors, would definitely cook it again.

On a future occasion I would recommend doubling the quantity of the sauce (If you prefer sauce-heavy pastas) and would also add more red pepper flakes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I am not a (pardon the pun) seasoned cook by any means, but this dish was very very good. The only recommendation that I would make, is to probably double the amount of beans. By the time it is all roasted down they kind of shrink into the whole mix and it's kind of hard to tell they're even there

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I liked the bean flavor with the pastas but the sauce needed a bit more taste according to my teen.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Double the beans! Especially if you’re dirtying two pans. They still crisp up nicely and make for a more hefty dinner.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I strongly agree with the other comment to cook the tomatoes in a large oven safe pan and then combine everything together in that pan on the stove at the end (but remember that hot handle!). This was delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Double the beans!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Cooked as directed, but moved the beans to half of the 2nd pan & threw a couple bunches of broccolini (olive oil & salted) cut into 2 inch pieces. Mixed them in with the tomatoes and beans at the end. Roast time was perfect for them to be cooked but maintain a bit of crunch. Delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Should the beans be drained and/or rinsed?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This is an excellent recipe! Roasting the vegetables with tomato paste was a great touch.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was excellent. I added a splash of balsamic vinegar at the end and topped off with some fresh basil. Easy, delicious, and I look forward to making again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Bland and uninteresting. Not sure why so many people rated it so highly.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This pasta is absolutely fantastic.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Tasty. Used gluten free rotini and finished with a basil olive oil and Asiago. Next time I will use a second can of beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Cooked Aug 28 when Lin and tia visited cape.  Delicious

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I make this recipe often for my family and my teenager in particular loves it. We prefer the beans cooked for about half the time (so not as crisp).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Easy and delicious! Also easy to ramp up the volume of white beans, cherry tomatoes, or red pepper as you like. Will definitely be making this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Scratching my head. Why you would add sugar to cherry tomatoes, which are already extremely sweet?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Wonderful recipe! And great for when vegetarian friends are over. Followed the recipe, and as per comments added more tomatoes, and olive oil up front for the sauce. Used 3/4lb of orecchiette. Added more olive oil at end for the right consistency, but it was just out of the bottle as I liked. Under a quarter worked. 
Used plenty of freshly grated parmigiano-reggiano serving and more for the table. If sensitive to heat, use 1/4 or less of flakes. I used a tad over 1/4 and perfect.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: My daughter turned up her nose when I told her I was making pasta with beans. Joke was on her when she came back for seconds. Made it as written except substituted oregano for rosemary. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: The bean to pasta ratio is off. You need a large can of beans at least 19 ounce for a pound of pasta. And agree with other comments that less olive oil can be used although the flavour of olive comes through beautifully when used in the amounts in the recipe. For those calories conscious, you can get away with less oil.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Definitely either Mae hag the pasta or double the tomatoes and beans. I did add the sugar and was glad I did; we also added shredded chicken on top and if you’re not going to use a sharp cheese, definitely add a tiny squeeze of lemon. Despite the tomatoes, it needed more acid. We used Italian herbs instead of rosemary, and quite glad we did. The color of my dish was far deeper of a red than the photo here - mine was prettier! Overall easy to make, oh and I skipped the last 1/4 cup of oil. Completely unnecessary.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Delicious but steps can be converged. Just mix the tomatoes with the other stuff and those and the beans can be on the same baking sheet. I found the way the instructions were written made it seem like way more work than it really is.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I blended the sauce with pasta water before adding the pasta in order to hide the veggies/protein from my picky eaters at home.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Loved this-- and so did my toddler! I smashed up the beans and tomatoes a bit before adding the pasta back in to get a more saucey consistancy. It was perfect! Will definitely make again...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was DELICIOUS. I scaled this down to half but still used an entire can of cannellinis because I love them, and I was NOT disappointed. I used the extra crisped beans to put on top. You build the flavor by how much salt and pepper you use before you cook anything. Salting generously isn’t a suggestion; you need to HEAVILY salt your pasta water, the tomato mixture, and the beans. That is all of your flavor. I can see how anyone who used it conservatively got nothing out of this. If you do it right, it’s amazing. I also used fresh orecchiette from my local Italian market and I couldn’t recommend that enough!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was really excellent. Simple and delicious. I took the advice of another reviewer and put the tomato’s in an oven proof skillet. I also cut down on the amount of pasta. It didn’t seem like enough sauce for a lb of pasta so I used about 3/4 lb.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: The beans make this deceivingly creamy. Made as written. Really delicious - you would almost think there is dairy in it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was a lovely pasta dish that I made for a Lenten meal! Made as is, except I couldn’t find orecchiette pasta at my local grocery store so I used bow tie instead; and I ended up using only half of the pasta water as I was worried about washing the flavor away. Seasoned liberally with salt and pepper and used slightly less than recommended olive oil for finishing the sauce. I loved the crispy beans and next time will double the amount (and use for other pasta and salad dishes in future!). Thank you so much for this recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is so delicious! I recommend adding kale and/or spinach for some veggies—I mix Dino kale in with the tomato mixture and roast it together

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Followed the advice in the comments and did the tomatoes in my cast iron and the beans on a sheet pan. The tomatoes released so much liquid that they never made sucs but the sauce still turned out great and the beans are a nice addition. Didn't end up using the full 3/4 cup of oil, don't think that much is needed

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: To make this ahead of time I roasted the beans on a baking sheet and roasted the tomato mixture in a Dutch oven. Later boiled the pasta for one minute less than al dente, added with the beans and pasta water and cooked for a few minutes until the sauce thickened up. Seemed it needed flavor so added lemon zest. Next time would double the beans. Big hit with the kids

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: I love this recipe and can't wait to try it when we have tomatoes in the garden.  Does anyone have any hints to keep the orecchietti separated so they cook evenly?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: This was my first time making this dish and I followed the recipe to the letter. I would be very happy if I got this dish in a nice Italian restaurant! I made sure to get good cherry tomatoes. My hubby said he’d be glad to eat this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made as written using full amount of tomatoes and beans, but, per reader comments, only 3/4 lb pasta. Used large, ribbed sheet pan, half tomatoes, half beans. Needs a full 30 min. Did NOT add 1/2 c. oil at the end, just a drizzle of oil in the pasta and pasta water as needed. Easy and Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Piling on to the comments about too much pasta. The end result for me was too dry; adding more pasta water diluted the flavor. I like my pasta saucy and my sauce rich in flavor. I’d use half the pasta called for if making this dish again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Thought this tasted good but created way more dishes than I expected. Probably won’t make again just for that reason!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Made 1/4 the recipe and it was very-good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody:
Followed the recipe as-is, except used less pasta in order to have more sauce per bite. Absolutely DELICIOUS! And a really easy weeknight pasta. Will be on repeat in our house!
Also, the little bit of added sugar in the recipe is definitely helpful when making this with out-of-season tomatoes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Roasted White Bean and Tomato Pasta Recipe
commentBody: Would good canned tomatoes, well drained, work?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Frozen beef (and chicken) slice easily and to a consistent thickness on a mandolin.  (You can adjust the slice thickness on all the mandolins I’ve ever had). This is true even when the meat is frozen in marinade, and whether it is raw or precooked (ie sous vide and then frozen). It’s also good for stir fry.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: In Japan, this dish is served piping hot with a raw egg. Pour the egg on top and stir in before enjoying. The texture gains a nice creaminess, and the flavor is boosted as well (which is hard to do!).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: If you keep a nice, trimmed rib eye in the freezer simply pull it out a half hour to 45 minutes ahead of when you want to make dinner. At my house this is a nice pantry meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody:
Worth mentioning but these are the ingredients in the Hondashi brand:

"Salt, Monosodium Glutamate, Lactose, Sugar, Dried Bonito Tuna Powder, Disodium Inosinate, Bonito Extract, Yeast Extract, Disodium Succinate."

I'd recommend Awase Dashi Powder or Kayanoya Original Dashi Stock Powder which do not add MSG.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Thank you!!! I didn't know there were dashi powders that don't contain MSG.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: This was so good. I had everything except sake, so I used a mix of half Shaoxing wine and half water. I also halved the sugar and it was still plenty sweet. The broth and onions were heavenly.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Ridiculously good and simple to prepare. You can easily make your own real dashi by boiling a piece of kombu (dried kelp) with some bonito flakes, both available at any Asian supermarket. My girlfriend, who grew up eating her Japanese mother’s version of this dish, said that my attempt tasted authentic!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: If you have access to a Korean market, you can find packages of really, really thin sliced beef from all cuts of beef, including rib eye.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Update: I make this all the time now except with salmon. I cut the salmon into big chunks and poach it in the broth. Delish. Heats up well too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: This was so delicious! To the person mentioning that Hondashi has MSG... Duh! That’s what makes it delicious :) Dish turned out great made exactly as written. Freezing the beef made it a lot easier to slice thinly. Very satisfying dish!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: I veganized this and it was easy and delicious!  Instead of dashi, used konbu. Instead of real beef, used fake beef. Made the rice in a rice cooker and everyting else was done in 30 minutes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: To make this vegetarian, you can substitute dashi with shiitake broth (soak dried shiitake in water for a few hours) and beef with tofu.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: We are lucky enough to have an Asian market nearby where we can purchase the beef already thinly sliced. So this recipe is not only outstanding but so easy to prepare. Thank you!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: This was incredibly easy and fast. I followed the recipe to the T except I left out the pickled ginger. Next time I would cut the sugar by a third or by a half as the broth was a little sweet for my taste. I used a 3/4 lb rib eye steak and mostly sliced it on a mandolin as others suggested. The meat was wonderfully tender. I would also slice the onions the other direction next time instead of stem to stem. Served on fresh white rice. Yum.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Lovely recipe. Used only 1 tbs sugar and is plenty sweet. Portions good for 2 adults and 1 toddler.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Trader Joe's sells one pound containers of very thinly sliced beef.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: If you keep a nice, trimmed rib eye in the freezer simply pull it out a half hour to 45 minutes ahead of when you want to make dinner. At my house this is a nice pantry meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Hondashi and many other brands of "instant" dashi have lots of MSG.  Any suggestions for non-MSG brands or alternatives to dashi?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: You can make your own dashi easily. Just buy dried bonito (flakes of fish packed in small envelopes available at most oriental stores) and dried konbu, dried kelp, a type of seaweed (also in same stores). FYI, this is the base for miso soup and many other Japanese recipes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Thank you! I didn't know there were any brands of dashi powder that don't contain MSG. I normally use "teabag" style dashi mix, bags that contain premixed kombu and bonito.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Perfect as is but adding an egg or two at the end can be fun.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Used three teaspoons of sugar instead of 3 tablespoons. The frozen meat sliced paper thin using my great soduku.  Used Nagori un-filtered sake.  This was an excellent dish.  I missed the togarashi which I will be buying tomorrow for future dishes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious, even without the dashi. Leftovers were great the next day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious (I left out the sugar)! Maybe won't feed four hungry people as a main course, though.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody:
I stay away from the dashi granules; hard to say what is in that stuff. I sprinkle in some dried shiitake mushrooms and that seems to do the trick. 

This is an easy lunch or a quick dinner option. If you are not working from home after you make lunch, it is, of course, good form to pour a little extra sake for the chef.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: I bought dashi and togarashi online back in April just to make this dish, and finally got around to trying it tonight. So good, and so easy! The first bite tasted a bit too sweet, but it grew on us - next time I'll try 2 Tbsp sugar instead of 3, otherwise it was perfect. I used a 6-7 oz filet and 12 oz onion for two - next time I'll increase onion to at least 1lb.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: I live in Hiroshima.  Gyudon is essential late-night food. It is so easy to get here that I never thought of trying to make it at home but I will return to New York for a few months and may just have to make it myself.  It is also very cheap and filling.  Although I would not describe it as particularly flavorful, it somehow hits all the right notes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Wonderful, umami flavors. Once I had the ingredients, it was easy to put together--except for the beef. Slicing it was a chore and I was afraid I'd cut my finger off. I'd love to make this again, with rib-eye sliced by the butcher.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Trader Joe's sells a 1 pound package of shaved beef in the meat section that is perfect for this dish! I also throw in some sliced shitakes in the end. TJ's also sells furikake seasoning, which I used instead of dashi - it also has seaweed and bonito flakes, as well as sesame seeds.  Other subs: didn't have sake, but I had soju (Korean style sake); didn't have mirin, so I used rice vinegar (just add a little more sugar). perfect umami bomb!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Our Costco sells thinly sliced New York steak for "shabu shabu." It is perfect for this dish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Can anyone suggest a substitute for Dashi  (Fish sauce perhaps?) It's hard to find in the boonies and I have all the other ingredients on hand.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: You can use Better Than Bouillon with maybe a few dashes of fish sauce or a bit of anchovy paste.  Not all dashi is fish based.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: All the major brands are available through Amazon.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Is there a good substitute to make this vegetarian?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody:
I would think firm tofu might work instead of beef.
Or try a plant based meat substitute?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Gyudon Recipe
commentBody: Think this would work with chicken, salmon  or pork?  We have given up beef

author: Alexa Weibel
