A shortcut if you're both the cook and making this because you're sick - keep it easy, Trader Joe's packaged mirepoix does just fine here (skip the shallot).
Optional for some more veg: Add a cup or so of frozen spinach and peas while it's all boiling.
Also: Ground turkey works just fine if that's easier to find.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: When you say ground chicken do you mean chicken breasts, legs, thighs??

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: You can use either but dark meat has more flavor. I used ground dark turkey meat and it was amazing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
This was delicious! I used really good homemade stock, doubled the carrots and doubled the celery and instead of using dry egg pasta - I used fresh egg papardelle (IMO the best in chicken soup)that I cooked separately and added at the end with the chicken. Added the recommended lemon zest and some maldon salt to finish. 

So good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I wanted to make this vegetarian. I used a full block of extra firm tofu and cooked it in place of the ground chicken, adding 1 tablespoon of butter to get some extra fat in place of the meat. I broke it up while cooking into crumbles but left some larger, almond-sized pieces. I kept all else the same, except for using no-chicken vegetarian chicken stock and throwing in some peas at the end. It turned out great! The herbs and creme fraiche are the perfect finishing touch!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used rotisserie chicken bc the smaller WF didn’t have ground chicken but would do with ground. Noodles were great al dente according to package directions- 6 min. Next time use LOW SODIUM chicken broth, or rather bone broth. It was too salty. Season later when you can, don’t overdo bc you can always add later. Also used too much cracked black pepper. We didn’t try with creme fraiche or lemon zest. H said “I usually need to season my own chicken noodle soup, and this time I didn’t!” Make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made this in the instant pot with turkey drumsticks as I that’s what I had in my freezer and used zucchini noodles and it was still delicious!  Next time I will try the ground chicken.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Really good and easy! I used bow ties and just a regular onion in place of the shallot. As others have noted, it’s a great canvas for other flavors. I finished with fish sauce and lime juice, and added a sour cream and lemon sauce at the table. Definitely recommend having a sour note though could be vinegar just as well as citrus. I think in future additions other veggies would be quite good, like potatoes or kale. I also used the cheapest store bought chicken stock and it was still a success!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I loved this and it was easy to make for lunches for my work week. I made it because I have a cold, so I added 2 teaspoons of ginger and a 1/2 teaspoon of ground tumeric when throwing in the veggies to add a little zing to it. Also used small shells as noodles and they catch a lot of the broth which is a good thing in my book!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Easy, quick, nutritious, cheap, delicious.

Tossed a handful of spinach in prior to serving for color and benefit.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used potato gnocchi. Simple and delicious soup. Keep an eye on the salt, it definitely builds as you’re seasoning at various steps.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made this recipe as written, except for the crime fraise, which my market didn’t have (I thought sour cream was fine). I have these comments: 1) I used ground white meat, but next time will use ground dark meat for more favor, 2) I will use more carrots (double?) and celery, 3) I will use at most six ounces of noodles, 4) eight cups of stocks was too little for soup, though perhaps eight would be enough with six ounces of noodles, 5) I will also use more coriander next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Tried this last week and made matzo balls instead of using pasta.  It was fantastic!  Toppings weren’t needed.  Yummy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I'm going to do that next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This has become my go to chicken noodle soup recipe! I do use an onion and two carrots (instead of just one) for additional flavour and sweetness. A super easy and delicious weeknight meal!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Keeper. 
Do the lemon. I’m not much of a lemon-lover I SO hesitated putting it in, but in the end I swallowed hard, closed my eyes, crossed-fingers and squeezed it in. SO GOOD. 
Also in the mist of social-distancing in the US glad to find this recipe as I actually had all the ingredients without having to go out!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used a pound of boneless skinless chicken thighs instead of ground chicken, but otherwise followed the recipe exactly, using mini farfalle. Do not leave out the herbs and creme fraiche! Recommend a squeeze of lemon juice at the end as well. A keeper for sure.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: SO DELICIOUS! Added the juice of a whole lemon   dill into the stock and it made all the difference. Also used egg pappardelle as the noodles and they worked perfectly. Eating this as leftovers as I type this and it’s still as delicious as the night I made it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Literally the best thing I’ve ever tasted

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was great even with turkey. Used sour cream instead of créme fraiche and it was delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made this to satisfy a sudden craving for a brothy soup— used ground turkey and it came out wonderfully. Added rice noodles at the end, that was a surprisingly good addition and kept the soup gluten-free for me!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Yummy, but a couple of notes. The ground chicken texture is not as nice as shredded or diced chicken meat, so it might be worth the effort to cook whole chicken parts. The soup is very heavy on noodles and low on vegetables—it would be better with heavy vegetables and lower noodles. The lemon zest and herbs really added while the crème fraîche felt unnecessary. Finally, it took me 30 min to prep all the ingredients before turning the stove on!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The ground chicken in my grocery store looked skeevy so I opted for chicken breast.  I chopped them into 1 inch cubes and massaged the coriander, celery salt (I had no celery seed), & pepper before adding them to the frying pan.  Oddly, just happened to have crème fraiche and that made it for me, although spouse said it was perfect without. We both added fresh parsley to finish.  Also added extra carrot and celery per suggestions here. Fast, easy and delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I had frozen turkey stock leftover from Thanksgiving so decided to do this with fresh ground turkey. I was a bit skeptical of the dill, zest and crepe fraiche but found that it complemented the turkey flavors nicely. Next time I’ll use a bit more veg and a bit less noodle.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Good idea about separating the noodles. I usually cook them separately in plain boiling water and then add -- otherwise, they can absorb all that nice broth!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
This was wonderful!  The creme fraiche and herbs are a must.

Used Herbes de Provence instead of celery seed and leeks instead of celery.

Cooked pasta separately from the soup and added when serving.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I gave this 5 stars because it hits these notes:  delicious, easy (even if it takes an hour to prepare), is made in 1 pot and can be made ahead.  Used orzo and doubled the vegetables.  Added a squeeze of lemon juice to the broth.  Works with ground turkey breast. Lemon zest and herbs on bit of creme fraiche is the key for taste and presentation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Try this with ground turkey

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is the best chicken soup ever. I cooked subbed the ground chicken for chicken breast. Cooked it in the instantpot with salt, pepper, smoked paprika, and chicken broth. I used the cooked chicken broth as a portion of the total broth needed for the soup. The smoked paprika really elevated the flavor!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This soup was surprisingly tasty for how quickly it comes together.  I used all the items in the soup list but added a bunch of dill at the end into the soup.  I had leftover cream sauce from some Greek bowls I made and it took it to another level.  1 cup greek yogurt (or sour cream), juice of one lemon, a few chopped garlic cloves and a bunch of dill and voila.  It's a "must" topper!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Use one package of trader Joes dried mini raviolis pasta instead of noodles.  I used 2 packages for the RTC race but I think it was too much pasta relative to the amount of soup stock.  
The creme fraiche, dill and lemon zest is essential for finishing the soup.  You should also provide extra lemon wedges at the table for guests who would like a brighter citrus flavor.
Chose a good chicken stock to insure a flavorful soup.  I use kirkland brand organic chicken stock.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
This is just a great recipe, made it several times, and I've made it for sick friends. All agree.  

The dill and parsley do make a different at the end. I've done couple other things to the recipe, sorta developed from what was in the frig. I add greens, like rapi, spinach or kale ( I love greens), and a bit of preserved lemon, and I use labneh - I usually have it on hand- instead of creme fresh. But it doesn't matter, it's all good, a great recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Of the options in the write-up, I made this with fennel seeds and three sprigs of fresh rosemary and LOTS of pepper. I also simmered the stuff for a half hour before adding the pasta to meld the flavors. Easy, and by FAR the best chicken noodle soup I've ever had. Perfect for a chilly, rainy day!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made this with ground turkey (couldn't find any ground chicken) and it was great! Super fast and easy. I would suggest adding a bit more than 8 cups of water if you want something that's true soup consistency, though.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Not a fan of the ground chicken texture so will use cubed chicken next time. Otherwise I loved the soup. I did use 2 carrots because I love carrots.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The pasta to liquid ratio is out of whack. Way too much pasta. I made this according to directions, adding additional broth as recommended for "soup" consistency. After cooling, it looked like a pasta dish with some chicken. Added 4 additional cups of broth...may need more. Next time I'll use 4 oz of pasta which will be plenty.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was so amazingly good for how simple the recipe is, and I was able to knock it out in 30 min with a toddler hanging from my leg. Three kids under the age of 8 all ate it up. I used Better Than Bullion to make the broth. I though the crème fraîche was a little odd on chicken soup, but went with it, using marscapone because that’s what I had on hand, and it really made the dish. Will definitely be making this twice a month.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Did basically this, but with rotisserie chicken instead! Also used a white onion instead of a shallot. Turned out great, but barely fit in my dutch oven and didn't have enough space for broth, so maybe make less at a time!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I sautée either boneless skinless thighs or Brest tenderloins, set aside to cool and chop, then follow the recipe as written. Be sure to have some chicken stock or broth, or some better than bouillon, if you want leftovers. The noodles will absorb the remaining broth, but it is wonderful reheated with broth added.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious!  I couldn’t find ground chicken here(Germany), so I used what looked like thin chopped chicken strips. I went heavy on the coriander and thyme and used Fusilli pasta. Don’t skip the herbs and lemon at the end. It’s clutch!  I did not add the crème fraiche on purpose to keep it light. Really wonderful. I also boosted the amount of carrots. I can see adding zucchini or fennel the next time. Really tasty!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Substituted fennel seeds for celery seeds, as I didn't have any on hand.  Added some torn chicken from recently roasted chicken.  Delicious soup.  I also doubled the vegetables, and a leek.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Used a chicken breast cut into strips and hand shredded after cooking. Added in some oregano, fennel, lemon juice, and onion in place of shallot. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made this soup for lunch and it was a huge hit with my friends. Adding the fresh dill and lemon at the end made it a flavor sensation. Also, don’t skip the celery seed as it adds a nice savory note. I also added fresh zucchini and a thinly sliced fresh jalapeño. Everyone wanted the recipe! Great soup in no time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I love this soup! I make it now with whatever is in in the pantry/refrig. I keep ground chicken in the freezer. Carrots, onions, greens at the end. Labneh instead of creme fraîche. Different kinds of noodles. But always garlic coriander

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Mixed half of the ground chicken with garlic and onion powder, and Trader Joe's mushroom umami seasoning, then made into little meatballs and sautéed. I cooked the rest of the ground chicken as suggested, with the garlic, coriander and celery seeds. Added a few chopped mushrooms. Per suggestions, doubled the amount of carrots and celery and reduced the pasta to 4 oz, which was plenty. I used orzo.  Don't forget the lemon zest. YUM!  Even better the next day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
This was a great dish. So simple but so delicious and comforting. Will definitely be added to the rotation. The fresh dill at the end really brings it over the top. 
A little tip: I like to use Better Than Bouillon   water if i don't have any good stock on hand, it's really delicious and comes in a small jar that lasts forever in your fridge.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Want to make it in half the time, pulse everything in the food processor! Add ginger too and use chicken w bones full the full curative effect! (Hello old rotisserie chicken?)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Much better than the normal rotisserie chicken soup recipe.  The next time that I make this I might try using Italian sausage.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I subbed chopped boneless chicken thighs in place of ground chicken—really happy with the result.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Added leeks too, yum yum! And a squeeze of lemon juice at the end.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious!
I added 4 more cups of broth, a giant leek, and homemade egg noodles. Probably the best chicken noodle soup ever.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Used roasted chicken bouillon and bone in skin on thighs. Added mushrooms and beet tops ( but would use any hardy leafy greens). Also made with rice. Placed rice separately and added broth mixture to it as I ate it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Reallllllly good. I added 3 tbsp of white miso paste and chicken bouillon instead of stock. In love!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used ground coriander and fennel seeds. Very nice. I really like the simplicity of this recipe. I used fusilli, which is a bit large for soup but still tasty. I'll remember to buy something smaller next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Don’t add the thyme right at the beginning, it burns and imparts a bitter flavor to the soup. I instead added it with the broth and added bay leaves for extra flavor. Otherwise this is a go to recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This soup is amazing. My only deviation was Balkan yoghurt instead of creme fraiche and it worked like a charm.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Have found store bought ground chicken not pleasant. I take boneless, skinless chicken breast and chop in food processor. Continue on to soup, chicken burgers, etc.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Oh my goodness...a hearty enough for a meal soup! I used diced chicken tenders, a cup and a half of mirepoix mix ( onion/carrot/celery) from TJ's to avoid all the chopping, and about a tsp. of TJ's chili crunch for heat and sore throat soothing. The coriander was a tasty touch, and I added more chili crunch and a splash of vinegar to my bowl to add to cold remedy properties. Delish and soothing!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is delicious, but the ground chicken has a really unpleasant texture. I think I’ll try it again with chicken thighs and then shred the meat. Another thought I had is maybe make chicken meatballs, sauté them first and then follow the same steps.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Added dollop of miso to increase umami, salt, and another layer of flavor. Very delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this soup three times. I confirmed today that the magic ingredient is ground chicken sausage instead of just ground chicken. The flavors are just so rich with the chicken sausage, which I accidentally used the first time I made the dish. The second time my husband bought exactly what I had on my shopping list, ground chicken. I was disappointed with the soup I then made. This time I loved it all over again! I also added more carrots. Yum!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I use this recipe quite often and double the veggies as well as add tomatoes. I like the suggestions on adding additional chicken pieces.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Easy, but use way less noodles, and increase the carrots and celery.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Too much pasta. I wish I had used half as much.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Tip:  if you grind chicken thighs, cut them into 1” pieces and freeze for 30 minutes and 1 pound will go thru your grinder (I use the grinder attachment on my Kitchenaid stand mixer) in one minute and yield a perfect grind.  Other wise, it’s impossible!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Really good. I used orzo and it worked great. Gonna try with rice next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Next time, use only 4-6 oz of pasta.  Don’t forget the lemon zest, and the crème fraiche is essential.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Double veggies seasonings chicken use orzo

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I make this every time I’m sick now and it always serves me well! Lots of olive oil, season at every step, and good quality broth (doesn’t need to be for the whole amount though - I buy one quart of nice broth then add bouillon for the rest). Sub Greek yogurt for crème fraiche

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I don't get the flavor difference between using shredded, cooked rotisserie chicken & sauteeing raw ground chicken, putting it aside while you cook some veggies & adding it later. You can add the drippings from the rotisserie chicken container for more flavor. 
Isn't ground chicken just left over bits and pieces from breaking up chicken into parts? I emailed butterball asking the difference between 85% chicken in the tub & 85% chicken in a tube. They said the tube was a different quality - cheap

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Easy, quick and delicious. Used more garlic and leftover rotisserie chicken. Fresh parsley & lemon zest are a must.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Good recipe. Shallots are key. (So is marjoram.) Children really like this soup. There is never any left over, so I don’t worry about mushy noodles.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Care to share your favorite store-bought chicken stock?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I use the unsalted vegan chik'n stock, it's cruelty free and won't increase your blood pressure. LOL. Good luck! An easy recipe to veganize.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I love Better than Bouillon.  The have an organic chicken one.  Just watch the amount you add in, as it can become too salty if you add to much.  There is also a low sodium chicken one.  Love it for so many soups and other dishes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I use Better Than Bouillon *beef* stock for any chicken recipe calling for stock.  It amps up the flavor, IMO.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I poach chicken breasts (not boneless) in a mixture of chicken broth, wine, herbs de provence, a ton of garlic, thyme, fresh cilantro, dill and parsley for, maybe 45 minutes. Then add the veggies (I use a vidalia onion vs a shallot), noodles (strictly egg), spice to taste and yum! 

Best broth is homemade. It's more robust.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The texture of ground chicken was really strange for me in a soup.. otherwise the flavors were good but really couldn’t get through the ground chicken

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: A tablespoon or two of soy sauce and some grated ginger are great additions to think about.  Specially if you are serving to some one down with a cold.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made this but instead of noodles I put about one half cup of reheated  cooked brown rice/farro that I had on hand and ladled the soup over the top it was simply wonderful otherwise as written.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Oh, no, no, no! Creme fraiche on chicken soup? Why ruin good chicken soup? Such a shame to even suggest. The rest of it looks quite good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Since this is recommended as a garnish, I think this is very much a matter of preference.  Sounds like it would add an interesting complexity.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: using ground turkey would add more umame...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Make just as the recipe called for except that I substituted anise seeds for the celery seeds because I didn't have any, and orechetti for the pasta. Used all the broth and cooked the pasta separately as a few people recommended. Delicious and easy! The lemon and fresh dill really bumped it up!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Handful of spinach and some Tzatziki instead of crème fraiche because that’s what I had in the fridge. Called it “Greek chicken noodle soup.” Yummy, fast, no leftovers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is amazing as-is. Literally says “easiest chicken soup” in the title. Thanks to all commenters saying “raise your own chicken, kill it, grind the breasts, sauté the legs and thighs, for better flavor.” Just make it like the recipe says 🤙

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made it exactly as the recipe stated, and it's very good and pretty easy.  However,  next time I make it (and I will make it again) I'll take others' advice and cook the noodles separately (also probably use less).  I'll also use less oil- maybe it was the chicken I used, but it felt a little greasy to me in the end.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Too much olive oil.  Taste is good but very oily and I’m not sure I like texture of the ground chicken.  Plan on freezing this batch and then I can scrap the fat off while thawing

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is a fun recipe for chicken noodle soup! I followed the recipe exactly and will make it again using half the amount of noodles and doubling the carrots and celery.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Appreciate all the suggestions to make it "better", but if you're simply looking for a fast chicken soup as advertised, this is terrific!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I made this with rice and it was great but you should half the weight of rice vs pasta.
I believe it would work equally well with ground beefand beef broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Fantastic. I doubled the vegetables to use them up and added chopped mushrooms. Delicious alone, but the creme fraiche with lemon zest, dill, and parsley really make this a winner.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I added a lot to this recipe, including but not limited to roasting an onion a bulb of garlic and the carrots. I used smoked salt and 4 cups of beef stock to add more depth. We typically like flavor from the deepest deep. End result was totally yummy and I think even worthy of company. So pleased to have a  delicious easy chicken soup in my pocket to build on without a major production. Thank you so much for this recipe. Love it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Lovely pantry soup, these are all things I typically have on hand. It comes together quickly, great flavor payoff for the amount of time it takes. Tried to do it exactly as written, but discovered I didn’t have celery seeds at the last minute. Reduced the kosher salt and used celery salt, turned out great. Next time I make this, I’ll reduce the amount of pasta. It absorbed a lot of liquid, so when I added more broth to keep it soup like, it diluted the flavor quite a bit.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Tasted pretty good but the texture of ground chicken is a real turnoff.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: So good. Used ground turkey from Costco cuz it’s what I had. 1.5 lb cuz that’s what the pack was. Did more carrot. Added lemon juice, zest, parsley, fill and sour cream to finish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: My husband loved this so much, he wanted it for lunch the next day and every day after until it was gone.  And then he asked when I would make it again.  I used orzo, so we topped it up the second day with extra stock since orzo sucks up the broth.  But I have to say, this recipe far exceeded our expectations in flavor and ease.  Definitely in our rotation now!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was good and quick as promised. I like to keep a pot of soup available for a couple days at a time because there are just two of us. I would cook the noodles separately and keep them to the side. Also, I'd add more vegetables and half of ground chicken/turkey next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Really good! On initial taste, the broth felt a little “empty”, so we made some modifications:

- added a bit of white wine when browning the ground chicken
- added ~3 Tbsp of white miso paste
- hot sauce
- apple cider vinegar
- doubles the herbs/spices

The added umami and acid made this perfect!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The thing abut these great noodle soup recipes is that you can substitute for the noodles which gives you a healthier version that doesn't go slimy or require you to cook up a fresh batch of noodles every time you warm some up. I make big batch of soup every weekend and use sweet potatoes for the hearty starch or add a handful of cooked rice (which keeps better in the fridge than noodles, which dry out and stick together), or just bypass that part altogether.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Some variations that I thought made my soup superb: 

- deglazed chicken with white wine (as suggested by some of the other comments)
- subbed miso for chicken broth (forgot broth at store)
- used orzo for the pasta
- in addition to herbs, topped with Greek yogurt, chili crunch sauce, and home made pickled red onions

So good, especially when sick!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This recipe has made me a rock star.  I live alone, so I share.  Best endorsement came from the 18-month old daughter of a friend:  "The soup was a huge hit in our house.  Zoe kept asking for more and every time she would eat a bit she would say, 'mmmmmmmm.'"

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Tried this soup today. Subbed in leftover rice for the pasta. Will make again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
This is fantastic! My family loves it. 
My husband is pescatarian so I make it with cod. 
I follow the recipe adding the spices to the veggies and then drop cubed cod in as the broth and pasta finish up at the end. Cooks in just a few minutes. The dill is a remarkable herby finish!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: As others suggested, cook egg noodles in a separate pot. When you’re ready to serve, add the noodles and top with soup so you have the desired ratio of noodle and soup. I didn’t do this and the egg noodles absorbed way more broth than I would have liked. Still delicious though.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I found that the ground chicken and store-bought chicken broth left the soup without much flavor.,  If I make this again, I will definitely use my own stock, but I think the ground chicken is the problem.  It make this "the easiest," but using "real" chicken legs and/or thighs make a world of difference without much additional effort.  And more both is needed for 8 oz. of pasta. I needed 3 quarts.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The recipe doesn't specify store-bought chicken stock, so if you got your broth out of a carton, it's your fault. Maybe the ground chicken you used wasn't top qualiity, but ground chicken is "real" chicken. Get a mix that's heavier on the dark meat for more flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Per notes: use 2x veggies & 1/2 noodles

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Added ginger, garlic and chilli flakes. Used chicken thighs (just thrown in whole and then shredded). Added a can of corn that I wanted to use up. Added a squeeze of lemon at the end. Simple and delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Love how easy this is — I’ve always used rotisserie chicken instead of ground. I mix in the spices that are supposed to go with the ground chicken when cooking the veggies.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is one of my favorite make-again-and-again recipes from NYT Cooking.  It's endlessly adaptable and surprisingly flavorful for how simple it is. Going to go make another batch now!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Did not like this recipe…can’t do ground chicken for chicken soup! Much better recipes out there that are quick and easy

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Sooo good! Used chicken thighs instead and it worked great. Don’t skip on the crème fraiche- it takes it over the top.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Shoulda added the spice from Dune :(

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Nice soup,easily made, lack a signature

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Such a great recipe! There are two soups that I tend to keep ingredients around to make quickly and satisfying. Bittman's Garlic soup for one and this one. The lemon and herbs are very good, I'll put in whatever greens are in the frig, rapi, kale.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
As a shortcut chicken soup recipe this works far better than your standard rotisserie chicken. As others have mentioned I did cook the pasta separately (in chicken broth) to add separately. This will work better for us when it comes to leftovers. 
I think I’m going to try to “meal kit “ this for my college students. Cooked broth/veg in individual containers along with  separate containers of prepared/portioned chicken. They can freeze/refrigerate those for a meal at a later date with freshly cooked pasta.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Try using a “soup chicken”.  A mature bird that weighs 6-8 lbs

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I thought this was OK--flavors were pretty good.  It certainly is quick, though, if you need to pull something together fast on a weeknight.  Followed directions as written but should have held off on adding the egg noodles until ready to serve, as others suggested.  It required at least 8+ cups of broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is delicious and so pretty but mine always turn out a dark and undesirable color. Any tips?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Loved the flavors in this soup but didn't love the ground chicken.  I see where someone made small meatballs instead and that sounds like a wonderful idea.  When I make again, I will do the meatballs or shred some chicken thighs.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Add a Parmesan rind if you have one to the broth while waiting for it to boil! Much richer outcome. And don’t skip the crème fraiche. Tastebuds deserve cheese :)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious! I substituted the ground chicken for ground turkey and the creme fraiche for sour cream and it was still amazing!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Half the noodles and at least double the veggies. Flavor is great especially with the finishing garnishes!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: It’s definitely the easiest chicken noodle soup to make but not the tastiest. Still, it’s very quick and easy when you want the comfort of soup but don’t want the hard work of making it. Adding a swirl of miso at the end elevates this too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is what it is: simple and refreshing….if you want a wintery Chicken noodle soup with that root vegetable base , look elsewhere. The ground meat was a great idea…and with the fresh herbs and zest, wow!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used rotini for the pasta because that's all I could find to use. It seemed it wasn't a small enough pasta because it absorbed too much of the liquid! Still really tasty though. I'll try again next time with a different pasta.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Substituted chickpeas (2 cans) for the pasta and made it a stew. Easy and flavorful.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Needed soup in a hurry but this was bland and kind of wierd. Way too many noodles even with adding less and adding more liquid. Not a keeper

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Definitely fennel!  Great recipe and easy enough for my 87 year old self!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is a great and very flexible soup recipe. For protein, I’ve used ground chicken, rotisserie chicken, & diced boneless/skinless chicken thighs. I’ve flexed the veggies, adding parsnips, fennel, celery root, mushrooms, and probably other stuff I’ve had sitting around. I’ve also used chicken broth and veggie broth, both homemade and from the better than bouillon jars. The one thing I do consistently is prepare fresh noodles to have with each serving. Even with my tinkering, it always turns out great. And I typically make a batch every week or so during fall/winter. I love the flavor the coriander & celery seed adds to the broth and the lemon zest, dill, and lemon juice garnishes elevate it even more. Highly recommend this recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: this soup has changed my entire life trajectory. my mom made it once a few years ago and i’ve been hooked ever since. it’s delicious and expensive-tasting without being too difficult! if there’s one recipe i absolutely recommend to everyone, it’s this one. yay NYT!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: One of my family's favorite soups. I know it seems a silly detail, but we only had whole coriander - grinding it myself made it ten times more flavorful then when I'd used the reground version of the spice!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Love this recipe. I make it all the time. Delicious when prepared as written, but also: very flexible and forgiving with substitutions. No ground chicken? Use chicken parts or rotisserie chicken. I use soups as a vehicle for liquidating whatever vegetable odds and ends are left in the fridge. Tonight that was parsnips, mushrooms, and a leek. The essentials here are the coriander and celery seed + garlic at the start & the finishes of something creamy (yogurt can sub in for crème fraiche), lemon zest, & dill. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I live on this soup all winter. I have started adding some sliced white mushrooms to it and use Israeli couscous for the noodles. Before serving, I throw in a handful of fresh spinach.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Could you use diced chicken breast instead of ground chicken?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Where do you get ground chicken?  I just don't recall seeing it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Poultry section at the market. There are two styles: one has a higher fat content (85% lean) the other lower (93% lean). I think the higher fat has better flavor. If you use the 85% lean, cut back on the amount of olive oil in the recipe by 1T.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Quick and easy.  Use less black pepper and go easy on noodles next time!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Tasty and easy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: No celery on hand, so I added one bunch of rainbow chard instead.  Diced the ribs and threw in with the carrots; added the rough chopped leaves in for the last 10 minutes.  Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and even for a novice guy cook

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is some of the best chicken noodle I’ve had. The creme fraiche really adds another layer to it.  I added one more carrot, and I still felt like it needed a little more so I might do 3 next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Terrific! Popular with my family, not too involved for a relatively easy dinner - & best of all it is warmly filling & delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Definitely easy, especially if you buy mirepoix mix at the store! Wished for more depth of flavor but the fresh herbs, zest, and creme fraiche help a lot here. Next time I might season the ground chicken and the final soup more aggressively, but overall it hit the spot.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made many variations of chicken soup with homemade broth many times, but this is a great “cheat” recipe for a busy night. I added a teaspoon of fennel seed to the chicken while breaking down and followed the recipe as is otherwise- Delish!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I substituted fat-free plain Greek yogurt for the creme fraiche and it was delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was quick and easy, but too bland for me.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was a great recipe! Came together quickly for a warm and filling weeknight meal. The herbs and lemon zest really make it something a little more special for a quick soup. I cooked the noodles separately as it's my preference for soups, but otherwise followed the recipe as written.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I substituted the noodles for Stellinne pasta, put a good handful of mushrooms in and added a good splash of lemon juice. Very tasty, easy to make and thumbs up from everyone.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Meh. Not great but not awful.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Has anyone tried it with ground turkey?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I did, and it was great!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Yes I used dark turkey meat. I tried it both ways and ground dark turkey meat was the best. Use fresh pappardelle pasta and twice as much celery and carrots...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: How would the end result change if I used bone broth instead of stock?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Since the base is very important - the best thing you can do is use the most flavorful stock/bone broth. If you think the bone broth has enough flavor to carry this -then use it. You can also add sauteed carrots/celery/onion to your bone broth - and cook for a while - to give it more flavor. You can also add this to your broth: https://www.betterthanbouillon.com/products/roasted-chicken-base/ It adds amazing flavor to chicken stock!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used 1/2 stock and 1/2 bone broth the third time I made it. It was much blander and needed more salt and pepper. I added more carrot at the beginning, at the end cilantro and a few drops of lemon juice, but it still wasn’t as tasty as with stock.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Great flavor with minimum effort!  I liked the ground chicken much better than rotisserie chicken I’ve used in other recipes. Suggested garnishes were nice, although I did use whole milk yogurt instead of creme fraiche.  Flavor even better next day although pasta getting a bit soft.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!  I threw in two parsnips I had in the fridge along with extra celery and carrots.  Quick, easy and very tasty!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Excellent. Tastes like it cooked all day. I added the lemon zest to the broth and a bag of fresh spinach at the end(copied that from  Judy's Chicken's Aunt Bridgett Soup recipe). I cooked my ditalini on the side so it did not absorb all the broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delicious.  Reduced olive oil -- sauteed chicken in 2 tbs olive oil.  Sauteed veggies and spices in 1 tbs olive oil.  Used cavatelli.  My husband hates dill, so he didn't add the fresh herbs at the end, only the creme fraiche and lemon zest and loved it. I put the parsley and dill in a separate container for me to add to the soup.  The leftovers in the fridge soaked up most of the liquid, so when reheating, I added about a cup of chicken broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I’m Jamaican and gluten free so subbed pasta for yellow yam. Threw in a silver of Scotch bonnet pepper because Jamaican. So good and very versatile!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Love this recipe! Need to avoid dairy so I opted for full fat coconut milk. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: My son and I loved this - I was worried it might be a bit boring but the flavors were great.  I wouldn’t skip the dill, lemon or creme fraiche but I didn’t have parsley and didn’t miss it.  Will definitely make it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I've made this twice in the past week because the whole family loves it! It's incredibly flavorful. I didn't have dill, lemon, or creme fraiche and still really enjoyed it. This will definitely become a regular family recipe. Thanks NYT!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Reality prep and done = 1.5 hours.
Never used ground chicken , this is a game changer for a quick and tasty bite. Did add sliced jalapeños at the end, the right bite for us. Kept the noodles separate, even now for left overs. Def will add to rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is the ultimate comfort food! Made as written. Savory wonderfulness.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Use only 5 cups broth

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
This dish came out great:
- cut up 2 chicken cutlets  because we had them. 
- used chik bone broth 
- used spaetzle because we ...
- 2 carrots

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The cooking time is optimistic. But the result is really good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is a very good, simple recipe. I couldn't help but feel that something was missing however... I added some cayenne when cooking the chicken but that was the only change I made. Next time I think I'd add some spinach. In addition: the flavor was much better the next day after the pasta absorbed more of the flavors! In hindsight, maybe that's what this recipe was missing. It might benefit from some time simmering on low heat, even though that defeats the purpose of a "quick" soup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Subbing ground turkey adds more depth, especially 85-93% lean. Chicken breasts diminishes depth... white meat has little flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Amazing depth to this soup.  Another person commented on using chicken thighs instead of ground chicken, so I went that route.  Seasoned the thighs and then sautéed them in a little olive oil, added the carrots, etc. and left the chicken in to finish cooking after I added the stock.  Simply the richest chicken soup ever - and so easy and quick to make.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This dish is amazing. I used ground turkey instead of chicken and it still turned out excellent! The finishing toppings made it exceptional. A+ will cook again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: It’s such a fresh tasting chicken noodle soup!  The herbs and zest really brighten it up.  I licked the bowl!! :)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I think it's a good idea but proportions were off for my tastes. Would probably double carrots/celery and halve noodles/chicken (and that was after using the higher range of broth in recipe). Recipe seems aimed at fixing a problem with other chicken noodle soup recipes that I'm not sure exists.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made it for myself and it provided much relief from my cold.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I loved this soup! I definitely continued to add more broth as I heated it up from the big pot stored in the fridge, but it was great each time. Used the already shopped mirepoix mix from Trader Joe's to cut down on prep time and topped with lots of fresh dill and crème fraiche. Was a delicious, nourishing soup perfect for battling cold outside temperatures!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made this last week and it is delicious. I did add an extra carrot and celery and will do that again. Since I only had coriander seeds, I ground them with a motor and pestle, grinding double the amount so that I can make this soup again soon!  Great flavour!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I forgot to say in my previous note, that while I didn’t add creme fraiche as a topping, the chopped fresh parsley and dill to top the soup is a must. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: As a nice Jewish boy from New York, I can't get behind the addition of creme fraiche in chicken noodle soup. Sacrilege! Otherwise it looks delicious and I will be trying this. I, too, agree that leaving the chicken in the soup while it's cooking is undesirable. Nothing appetizing about chicken that's fallen apart in the broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is a great recipe, indeed. In addition to the ground chicken, I add roasted chicken bites.  I also increase the amount of carrots for flavor and texture.  As such, this really is a nice meal that is sustaining for hours!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
More complicated for me. Ground chicken, turkey and lamb are not readily available in France. My butcher will do it for large amounts, but my small freezer is full of stews for my 92 year old mother-in-law. Had to grind my meat and it seems to be to dry (just breast meat, because no bone thighs are not available).
Can't even get small pasta, except for elbow macaroni.
Have this issue with many recipes here that sound wonderful, but I have to substitute.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made this with ground chicken from Trader Joe's. It was tasteless and definitely not as delicious as almost every other poster says it was for them.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Extremely tasty with egg noodle!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I cook NYTimes recipes all the time but this is the first that’s motivated me to comment. I’m sorry to say this Mom, Grandma Sylvia, and Rhoda’s deli, but this is the best chicken noodle soup I’ve had. 

I used thin soup noodles, which thickened it up a bit. Delicious! Only change I’d make is less or no salt when seasoning the chicken and vegetables if your broth has salt.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Deliciosa receta! Added all herbs and the crème fraîche, they are key to keep all the flavor. Will certainly add to our usual menu

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I just made this and I love it! I always cook the pasta a bit first, so the soup liquid isn't too starchy. I had some green beans, so I cut them up and put those in there too. And, I only had orzo which was just perfect for this soup. This is going to be a 'go to' recipe for me this winter.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I did not remove the ground chicken while cooking the vegetables, but the soup did not become greasy. I included green beans and used a yellow onion instead of a shallot. Skip the creme fraiche and use Parmesan instead! Very good on a cold winter night.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made a couple of changes due to family preferences. I added potato and green beans and made the ditalini pasta on the side so it didn’t get overcooked in the soup since my husband wasn’t going to be home to eat it until several hours later. It was really delicious! I didn’t add creme fraiche at the end, but the lemon zest and FRESH dill were essential! It turned a good soup into an amazing one.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Very good, just be careful with the salt.  Will definitely make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Even better with ground turkey. This soup is awesome. And so easy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Wonderful! Would add more carrots and celery, and thanks to K for the suggestion to cook the noodles separately.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made this, and it was both quick and tasty (perfect for a flu-ridden household). That said, step 1 says to add the garlic to the ground chicken and then to remove the chicken from the pan. This made it impossible to keep the garlic in the pan, so the garlic didn't simmer/boil with the rest of the soup ingredients. As a garlic lover, I think the soup would've benefited from the garlic cooking in the pot throughout, so next time I'll wait to add the garlic until after the chicken's been removed.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delicious! I added in some ginger when cooking the veggies and it gave it a little something extra. I used orzo since I had some on hand. Also added kale at the end to up the nutrients and try to kick this dang cold! I liked the added texture.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: In the minority here, but we found this one a bit boring...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This broth is delicious. Instead of noodles, I dumped in some leftover rice at the end, and along with the rind, the juice of half a lemon. Quick, easy, and so satisfying.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious! Perfect while hiding out from COVID. 
Used chicken thighs instead of ground, so cooked some at first then removed, put back in to finish cooking at same time as the noodles; took out when nearly done and cut meat off the bone, returning to pot.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Will definitely keep this one in mind when I’m looking for something super comforting that’s also quick/easy. Didn’t think I’d like the ground chicken, but the meal ended up coming together with nice flavor, homemade stock really makes a difference here but boxed probably good too

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious, my new go-to chicken soup recipe. So flavorful and bright. I also used orchiette.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: YUM. I used ground thigh meat and upped the quantity of meat for a heartier dish. Used coriander + cumin as I love that flavor combination. Tripled the amount of carrots and celery because I love a veggie-full soup. And did a sprinkle of parsley and chives with the lemon zest instead of dil. Found the end result filling, flavor, and so comforting - I'm enjoying my leftovers!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Super fresh and bright and weeknight fast for a soup.  I added the cooked chicken to the pot with the liquids to cook with the noodles.  Don't skip the finale topping of herbs, zest and creme fraiche.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Great overall base, can add lemon juice for extra punch. Added Greek yogurt on top for serving. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Also, i cooked the noodles separately.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Added fennel to the veggies and red pepper flakes when sautéing the chicken. Hearty and warming meal. This will be my go to chicken soup recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: DO NOT add the noodles into the soup, directly. It turned into a giant mush... urgh. Cook noodles separately.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Does anyone know if there is a vegan alternative  to crime fraiche?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used Orzo pasta (1 cup) and added it at the end. Also added 1/2 - 3/4 cup of fresh lemon juice...game changer.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I’m so glad I found this recipe; absolutely delicious soup. I used chicken breast and it was still under 30 mins, including chopping.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used 7 1/2 cups of chicken stock with < 8 oz of extra wide egg noodles. I definitely could have used the whole 8 cups, maybe even 9 or 10! The egg noodles absorbed so much of the stock that it was much less of a stew by the second day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Great simple recipe that is family-friendly.  We used 250g (8oz) dried egg noodles which consumes most of the broth, so if you prefer more broth I would recommend 10 cups.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe.  I used some left over chicken thigh, diced into 1cm or so pieces.  Also risoni instead of noodles and added the kernels from one cob of corn towards the end. Really fabulous result- will make it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Loved this recipe! Next time with more noodles. The broth provided sufficient salt. Additional salt not needed.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is an excellent dish.  I did add chicken broth for flavor. I love the ground chicken substitute for regular chicken.  Quick dish also.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Good, but not quick or easy, at least not if you start with ingredients other than ground chicken and creme fraiche. A good base to learn to riff of after a few tries.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Used fresh chicken basil sausage filling. Delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Needs more broth! I love this recipe but usually make 12 cups of broth, keeping everything else the same. The noodles absorb a lot of liquid, and broth is one of the best parts. Works better to plan for it than add it later and risk overcooking your noodles.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: 8 oz is too many noodles! I think 4-6 would have been a better call

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I couldn't find chicken mince anywhere but had all the other ingredients, so subbed in pork mince instead. With the juice of half a lemon added at the end, and poured over udon noodles, this was so delicious and perfect for these suddenly autumn nights.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I love this soup so much. It’s light and vibrant with the addition of fresh herbs at the end. I was skeptical about ground chicken in a soup but it’s delicious and flavorful. This is a new favorite!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The most wonderful and comforting recipe!  I used quinoa shell pasta instead of egg noodles, so had to season a bit more.  I now have my lunch for the next couple of days during the work week.  My kids really enjoyed it as well -  and they hardly like anything.  This is definitely going to be a go-to recipe as the weather cools off!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious! We liked that you get chicken in every bite, the lemon zest adds a nice touch and the creme makes it nice and creamy. Definitely recommend this soup!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
A hit at home with two kids - will be making for a second time this week! 
I used rotisserie chicken instead and it was amazing and quick. The lemon and creme fraiche added flavor and depth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used leftover cooked rice instead of pasta  (because my husband had eaten the pasta ) Not as good on day 1 , but it held up well for day 2

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used this recipe a few times last winter and it did not disappoint! As fall is already upon us, I pulled it up again. I’m looking forward to making it again in the coming days!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This has become my go to chicken soup recipe. Always delicious and very easy to make. I substitute or leave out some of the spices if I don’t have them on hand.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was so so good.  Classic chicken noodle soup.  I used orchiette pasta which worked really well.  Loved and will make again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: If I made this again, I would saute shallots, garlic & spices, then add chicken. It needs quite a bit of salt. Would be good to specify how much. I used 4 pinches of Kosher. Maybe 2 Tbs? 4 cups low sodium Chicken broth & 4 cups reg sodium.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Definitely at least double the vegetables. They are meager. Also recommend adding juice of one lemon rather than just zest.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Very good!  I didn't have coriander, so used cumin which worked quite nicely.  I thought this soup had much more flavor than the usual chicken noodle soup.  I made it with a blend of both ground turkey and leftovers from Thanksgiving and elbow macaroni.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Very easy and excellent. I used chicken bits pulled off the bone from a roasted chicken which was already seasoned with salt and pepper. The chicken bits were frozen so I added that to the pot first with olive oil and let it defrost a bit while I cut up the vegetables. Added dry bill and thyme with veggies, stock and let it simmer. Cooked pasta separate and added it to the bowls and then soup on top. Lemon zest and freaks have parsley. Perfect fast lunch.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was good but the seasonings are not what I associate with chicken noodle soup. More veggies would be welcome. The use of ground chicken worked well though.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Seriously good! I used fennel seed instead of celery seed and Pipette pasta cuz that's what I found. Highly recommend this for an easy winter night recipe. I wish I had used the lemon zest but the dill was essential!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used two boneless skinless chicken breasts and German spaetzle farmer-style egg noodles. A perfect perfect toothsome soup!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: My (local) nephew just tested positive for Covid so Auntie wanted to bring by something easy for him to warm, that was healthy (and delicious- even if he has no sense of taste). Whipped this up in no time! Thanks yet again NYT Cooking- you are truly carrying us through a pandemic!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made this soup the other day following many suggestions that people left- most helpful was to cook the noodles separately- must say that it was good, not great, and lacked flavor and was thin. And I had leftovers! So,...I decided to add more veggies and thicken broth so now my chicken soup (without noodles) was been remade into chicken pot pie!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Actually impressed my family by cooking this one. Used fennel seeds instead of celery seeds. Added baby spinach and chopped leak for increased fiber. Made the chicken stock from scratch by boiling a roast chicken carcass with thyme, one large yellow onion, and a piece of kombucha.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: the second time I made this with ground turkey and turkey stock from thanksgiving, it was excellent.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I added kale cause it was sitting in refrig and used labneh instead of creme fraiche,  cause I had it. Very good and easy dinner

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Great idea - if only ground chicken was sold in Germany. But you can only get ground beef or pork over here. And if you ask them to ground it for you, they can‘t because of health regulations, since they would have to clean the machine before they ground another sort of meat. I guess I could buy breast and chop it, but it probably would be weak in flavour, and deboning legs takes the quick and easy right out of the recipe. (Very few deboned legs on sale as well.)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Everyone raves about this one. Excellent flavor for something so easy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Ground meat in general sounded strange to me for soup, but I made a version of this yesterday with ground turkey breast, and whole wheat broken up spaghetti noodles. (We don't eat white flour products etc. and rarely, meat.)   I used Oregano instead of thyme.  Simmered the veggies in the broth. Sautéed the turkey with sliced mushrooms in olive oil, and seasoned well.   Cooked the noodles separately. Put it all together, and somehow, it turned out to be super flavorful.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Very light and fresh. The broth needs a little more flavor

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: My husband and I thought the soup had way too much pasta...I used ditalini, and too few vegetables.  I added another carrot and a parsnip, and when we have leftovers I’ll add more celery and carrots too.  I do have to confess I forgot to add all the garnishes, and I’d bought the creme fraiche especially for the soup!  And had taken the lemon out of the fridge!  I’m sure the garnishes will add some zip.  Oh yes, I used poached thighs, cut up, for the chicken.  It worked well.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
As some suggested,  will double the carrots,  and maybe the celery next time. Did cut the oil to about 4T.  Was surprised--pleased--when  the carrots were perfectly done when the noodles were. Ran out of broth and added water with double measure of vegan bouillon, which worked. 
Makes a LOT! And with gathering ingredients and peeling and chopping, took at least 45 minutes start to table.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made this earlier in the week.  We didn't find it very flavorful.  I will try adding  onion instead of shallot, fennel instead of celery, and maybe chicken sausage instead of chicken next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This recipe takes an hour or slightly more from start to finish

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This turned out quite good. One change was I used diced chicken breast instead of  ground chicken, but other than that... it has great flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This, as well as being quick and easy, was seriously GOOD.  Better than I expected.  The creme fraiche and fresh dill put it over the top.  I used papardelle, which made it more like a traditional noodle soup.  A keeper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was surprising good. I used ground turkey, and increased the shallots carrots and celery. Used all of the spices and herbs suggested with boxed stock. Flavorful soup and will make again in a pinch!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Just one note: I always leave the noodles on the side and plop them in bowl first and pour soup over. I love leftovers and typically enjoy soup the next day but i hate when the noodles are left in there and breakdown. This method keeps it all still kinda 'fresh'.

Our family loved this. We couldn't find cavatelli so used orchiette instead. Sauteeing the chicken added a really good depth of flavor, as did the coriander and celery seed. We added dill and lemon zest--just the right amount of savory

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The garnishes take this chicken soup to a new level.  Don't omit them!  And,  if you want gluten-free soup, Barilla gluten-free elbows work great although they absorb more liquid than regular pasta.  You might need an extra 2 +/- cups more broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: You can use plant-based crumbles in place of the ground chicken. Can Add later in the recipe, since they are frozen and just need to be heated. I thinned next day with chicken bouillon concentrate from a jar (Better Than Bouillon), and added dried thyme. Delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Used Herbes de Provence and leeks instead of celery seed and celery.

Cooked the pasta separately and added just before serving.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: don't half carrot and celery

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Easy and delicious, if not earth shattering.  Made as directed.  Get my going while I had Covid.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This soup is delicious. Next time I make it I will experiment with adding extra *cold* broth to the pot after serving, so that the noodles perhaps stay a bit more al dente and don’t keep cooking. They do soak up quite a bit of the broth sitting in the pot, and get softer.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I have cooked this recipe 6 times in past 2 months. It's so quick and easy, and really delicious. When I don't have ground chicken, I just chop up 2 uncooked boneless skinless chicken breasts and use them instead. The whole recipe comes together in less than 30 minutes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Regarding the question about anyone else who left the chicken in while noodles cooked—I accidentally did the 5th time I made this and it was not nearly as good as the previous times. Lacking some depth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Loved this. Followed recipe except used whole coriander crushed and loved the pop of the taste with the meat. Did not have celery seed. Like a little more veggie in my soups so might add a bit of spinach next time. Kept noodles separate- some of us are on a low carb diet.  If you have the broth, it is pretty quick.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: It is shocking how great this soup truly is. Even my kids love it. Everyone I passed the recipe along to also loves it. No substitutions necessary. But I do sometimes make it with ground turkey instead. Just as good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Half the carrot..too sweet
Add worschesyer sauce or fish sauce or lemon

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made mostly per the recipe instructions and it was very good. I used fresh cilantro instead of thyme. I agree that higher fat content ground chicken is better for the recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Really prefer dark meat ground chicken. Loved this recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: A great soup that doesn't taste like water! Followed the recipe exactly and it turned out great, very taste and filling!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Delicious! Was looking to make a Jewish-style soup, so I included two parsnips and a turnip, omitted the thyme, and tied up some dill and parsley, which I added with the broth and removed before serving.

Used one container of chicken bone broth (Pacific brand, unsalted) and one of stock. I credit the bone breath with giving this soup a wonderful richness.

I was dubious about using ground chicken but it worked well in the finished soup and was so easy. This recipe is a winner.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious! I threw in some leftover chicken breast chunks from a previous meal in addition to the ground chicken. Made a vert hearty and satisfying soup. Used 8 cups of broth and it was perfect.  Did not use the creme fraiche but used just fresh herbs and lemon zest.  Leftovers for lunch today! Will definitely make this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Needs a lot more seasoning and additional salt. Maybe double the veggies and half the noodles.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I added spinach and parsnips to  this soup and subbed gemelli pasta. Such a simple dish. Loved it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: instead of coriander and celery seed you can use fennel seed and rosemary or herbes de provence

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I've now made this soup a dozen times. It's YUMMY.
My changes: 
Homemade chicken stock
