Dark turkey meat instead of chicken
Tons more carrots and celery 
Fresh papardrelle noodles
Squeeze of lemon juice in your bowl before you eat.
Soooooo good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Trying this tonight. Will be back with my verdict.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely loved this recipe - this will be our go to for chicken noodle soup! We followed the recipe, but made some adjustments to the ingredient list. We used tortiglioni pasta instead of small pasta (totally worked as a sub), sour cream instead of Creme fraiche (this does not exist where we live and worked just fine) and I highly suggest not skipping out on the dill, I think it brought the entire soup together. Delicious and this will certainly be added into our rotation!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Never cooked with ground chicken before - turned out great!! Even with cheaper substitutes. I used bouillon for the stock, Greek yogurt instead of creme fraiche, dried thyme, and skipped the celery seeds but added extra dried spices to compensate

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Use Amish noodles to make to take this to another level.  They absorb a lot of broth so have more for reheating.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made stock from the carcass of a Samin Nosrat buttermilk roasted chicken. I had a significant amount of the meat leftover, too. So, I sauteed the garlic, veggies etc as directed, and then put the chunks of chicken in the soup once it was off the heat at the end. It remained moist and did not overcook. The addition of dill and lemon zest for serving is fantastic and a game-changer. This is great chicken noodle soup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Good recipe. (I used chicken breast.) I also found that a healthy pinch of marjoram was really good and enhanced the chicken-y taste. The (visiting) children pronounced it excellent. The biscuits were a hit, too. No leftovers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made a half batch for myself and really enjoyed this! Easy enough that my post-booster-dose, cooking-novice self could execute well! I didn’t have coriander or celery seeds and it was still flavorful, though I’m sure it would be even better with all the spices. Next time I will use slightly less noodle, but noodle to other stuff ratios are highly personal and there are no bad choices.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I had ground turkey - no ground chicken - and still great.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I love the flavors in this recipe. It's lovely. 

I wondered if anyone had any issues with the chicken drying out upon reheating? I cooked this -- then took the whole pot to a dinner party and reheated it on the stove.... and the chicken was a bit dry. Wondering if I should have waited to add it in? Thoughts?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I don’t consider myself a soup person, but this soup makes me want to be one. I may make it every week for lunch as long as I continue to work from home. I use fennel seeds instead of celery seeds, farro instead of noodles (cooked and kept separately), always more veggies than called for, and add greens like bok choy, kale, or spinach. Never skip the lemon zest and crème fraiche. Chef’s kiss.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made this tonight and everyone loved it. My notes would be to use homemade chicken stock (I used two boxes of Campbell's no salt added chicken stock. Next time will try salted, or have a batch of homemade on hand). I also did not have celery seed and did not compensate with something else, and wish I had. At the end of cooking I really had to play with depth of flavour by adjusting salt, pepper and lemon, which is always fun, but I wish I had got more right from the beginning.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: More veggies. Can add potato, green beans.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This soup is delicious as written--a recipe for quick chicken soup. Changing/adding ingredients is not needed.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I make this every single time my kid has a cold. Instead of ground chicken, I chop up boneless, skinless chicken thighs & Sub 2 tsp of Herbes de Provence for the required dried herbs. Cook the noodles separately. Cures whatever ails her!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is great--easy, comes together quickly. I didn't see any reason not to add the chicken back in after sauteeing the aromatics, so I did that. My kids loved it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: UH-MAE-ZING! Wow. When knew? The trick, I think, is the generous salt and pepper when sautéing the chicken. Def can get salty so use low salt stock/bone broth… one one handful egg noodles is enough or you will have too many. I didn’t miss the garnishes. Though had I tried them maybe i would change my mind about that! My kids wouldn’t have touched the garnishes, so it didn’t feel worth the trouble.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Whipped this up in 40 minutes for ailing neighbors. Used boneless thighs, chopped into small (1/2") pieces and tiny bowtie pasta. Cooked the pasta separately to preserve the broth.  It was a big hit, especially with the fresh dill, parsley and lemon zest. A keeper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Best chicken soup ever. We used chopped thigh meat instead of ground chicken.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Turned out good. Next time I would reduce the amount of pasta and increase veggies. I ended up adding some kale and a parmesan rind.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Recommend using more broth than called for

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is so good. A winter staple! Had to pause eating to come and thank the instructor. Made per the recipe with all the optional bits and added a very negligible dash of umami powder as well. It was a rough day and this soup has healed me.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Loved this, but found it somewhat bland. Next time I fix it, I will add some chili flakes and add more of the named spices. I agree with the previous note author about adding the cooked noodles separately. I purchased ground thigh meat at my butcher shop and mixed in salt, pepper, a tbsp of Worchestershire sauce (L & P) prior to cooking it up. Definitely a recipe to keep. I will try it with home made stock too!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I was thinking of trying it again with home made stock too! Have you done it yet?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was really good, and easy as the title suggests. I added the chicken back in with the broth so it would all come together. I did require closer to 8-9 cups of broth, even though I prefer a “stew like” consistency. Next time I’d also add more carrot and celery - they disappeared into the soup with all the noodles!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Super yummy soup! Lemon zest really does add a little tang that is so perfect and unexpected. We did a bit of soy sauce to add a bit of umami. 


Ended up needing a lot of extra broth. Ultimately ended up using an extra 4 cups of broth (so about 12 cups total) to get a good, soupy consistency. 


Perhaps our noodles absorbed more water than what was expected.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
this is not chicken noodle soup.
chicken noodle soup is made with meat on bones
and noodles are cooked separately and added to dish

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Tell us what you would call a soup made with chicken and noodles.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Yes, it is chicken noodle soup. It is one of the many ways you can make chicken noodle soup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I add a Parm rind and sub barley for the noodles (freezes better).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Substitute shredded cooked chicken for ground chicken. Add parsnip to array of vegetables. For added flavor blend the vegetables as a puree and add back to stock.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This recipe is perfect as it is, and I encourage you to make it that way. However, it is also a perfect recipe to play with a little. I've made this with bbq grilled chicken thighs, and it is very yummy. I've also replaced the ground chicken with chicken thighs as others have done, and it is also great. It is quite easy to try some different spices or other flavors and still have a great soup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Loved cooking this recipe for the cold weather. Instead of using chicken I chose ground turkey, added butter when cooking the veggies, sub creme fraiche for sour cream since I couldn't find it, doubled on the spices, and included my favorite all purpose greek seasoning. 

Noodle in broth note: I thought the same thing about noodles becoming soggy but the starch actually thickened the broth which made it even heartier & cozier. lol

Can't wait to try it with lemon & garlic herb chicken next time.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is far better than it has any right to be. I ground chicken thighs in a processor to a rough but not sticky consistency and used red onion (no shallot in the house). Dry thyme and fregola cooked separately and added to individual bowls to keep leftovers non-mushy worked great. Lemon zest makes it. No creme fraiche, no problem. Next time I’ll double the carrot and celery. Otherwise stellar.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used leftover brown rice instead of noodles. Eliminates soggy noodles in any leftover soup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made as written but doubled carrot and celery. Used unsalted broth so I aggressively salted but even then didn’t seem properly seasoned. Even though I always keep ground chicken on hand the idea of using it in soup seemed odd but it works! We enjoyed a simple meal of the soup with some crusty bread. Don’t skip the zest,dill or parsley!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was a really nice meal! I used lean ground chicken, so the 5T of oil was absolutely necessary. I doubled the shallots, carrots, and celery, and added an extra garlic clove. I also added slightly less cavatelli, and used sour cream at the end rather than creme fraiche - was really nice with the dill and lemon.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I’ve held off on making this because I was skeptical of the ground chicken. It was actually really tasty here! To my surprise. I always use better than bouillon and water rather than stock, but otherwise followed recipe exactly. Very good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Add more liquid.  This is the BEST soup ever!!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious and the absolute comfort food we needed last night. A couple of hardy ciabatta rolls and it was a whole meal, a great recipe! (Had thin chicken breasts on hand, not ground, but poached them in broth and shredded, perfection!)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Ground chicken seemed like a bad idea to me, but since I hadn’t made chicken noodle soup before, I decided to go along with it, which was a terrible decision. The rest of the recipe is pretty straightforward and reasonable, but the decision to go with ground chicken makes absolutely no sense. Please don’t follow that direction; use chicken breast instead.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Did anyone else find 8oz of pasta a bit much.  I used 4oz and still had to add more stock.  Soup was delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Be well, fam.

Be generous with salt, pepper, and garlic.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Great recipe!  BUT I didn’t eat it right away and the pasta (I used Orecchiette) soaked up a lot of the broth overnight!  When I went to reheat it, it had lost its soup consistency and was more of a “wet pasta” dish.  Still great flavour though… but I like my soup to be “soupy”.  Know what I mean?
The next time I make this I will: a) use way less oil when browning the chicken (1.5 TBSP) to encourage a deeper flavour, and b) cut pasta in half.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: My daughter-in-law made this at Christmas and everyone loved it.  I just made it, and it was great on a cold night!  One question--did anyone add the herbs to the broth and veggies instead of the meat?  I would think that would make the herb flavor come out more.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Use a leek cut into small quarters instead of the shallot.  Used 1/4 lb more of the chicken and used some locally made pappardelle egg noodles which were just right with the rest of the soup.  This was a real crowd pleaser.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Really good! I was skeptical of the ground chicken but it worked out. Did everything mostly the same but added some curry powder and turmeric to bump of the flavor! DELISH!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I've made this many times and it's always a hit, including with my toddler. I generally follow it as written but add extra vegetables and often forget about the toppings, although they really do add something to the flavor. Homemade broth really elevates it, but I don't have as much time for that as I used to (see: aforementioned toddler) so boxed or better than bouillon works just fine.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Didn't have celery seed or coriander so used fennel seeds instead- was tasty!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Added some harissa powder with the coriander and celery seed. Really added  some additional depth. This is such a good recipe you can add almost anything and make it your own.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I love this soup and also enjoy giving it to friends in need since easy peasy. I use no-yolk noodles which do not break down like egg noodles do.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: My family and I love this soup recipe -- it has so much flavour and the dill and lemon really elevate it. It's really one of the most delicious chicken soups! As I have made this soup many times now, I use boneless, skinless chicken thighs cut into small pieces.  It cooks just as fast as the ground chicken you don't get that strange texture of ground meat in a soup.  As another person mentioned below, to save some of the calories, we also use low fat sour cream instead of crème fraîche.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Used fennel seeds instead of celery.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: They didn't have ground chicken at my market (or any bone-in chicken other than wings), so I just used chicken breasts and cooked them in the broth after browning in the oil. I cooked the noodles as I was shredding the cooked chicken breasts then tossed it back in. Turned out great. Don't skip the crème fraîche on top. It added a delicious richness to the broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: My daughter stopped eating chicken once we bought three laying hens for our backyard coop... so we'll give this a try with ground turkey. :/ Has anyone tried this substitution?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I make a vegetarian version with chicken tvp (textured vegetable protein), and some yellow cooked split peas for color and heft. It is delicious. Would work good I think with lean ground turkey but I have not specifically tried that.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Yes, lives up to the hype! In order to cut down on calories, I used cooking spray to sauté the vegetables instead of the additional 2 tablespoons of olive oil. And I used good quality fat free Greek yogurt instead of creme fraiche as garnish.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: okay to use bone-in skin-on thighs

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Shoulda known better, but: Way. Too. Much. Pasta. Way. It hovered up all the broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Found it too light on carrots, used three. Otherwise delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I thought that white blob was a poached egg, which also might be good but a little macabre. We make this recipe all the time. Two other additions are some yellow split peas with more broth for cooking, and a pinch of saffron. Can use turmeric too. Adds to the warm yellow color and health benefits.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delish!  The only change I made was chicken breasts cubed instead of ground.  The broth is divine (the coriander???).  My husband must have told me a dozen times what a home run this soup was!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: V good. Made with egg noodles and used sour cream and lemon zest as garnishes. Only used 500g of chicken but added 4 celery stalks. Yield was only enough for 1 lunch for Rugs and I plus an Asha dinner. Next time double the recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Excellent, but agree with the note to half the pasta and double the veg.  I used 2C dry macaroni and it really overwhelmed the dish. Also the salt level of the stock will obviously shape the overall taste a lot so choose carefully!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is a delicious soup, v hearty and filling. I used chicken breast fillets instead of ground chicken and it was great, especially with Eric Kim’s stock. That is the best stock, bar none. Nice to have an excuse to roast a chicken every week.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: great.  I used fennel seeds instead of celery.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I enjoyed making this recipe.  It was tasty and nutritious to eat as well!.  Ingredients easy enough to source at my green grocer.  I used egg Angel hair pasta.  Only tip would be maybe 1/2 teaspoon of dried thyme instead of 1 teaspoon.  Thankyou!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Help! I’m not a coriander fan. Any suggestions for another substitute?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was lovely! I used smoked paprika instead of coriander and celery salt. It tasted wonderful with the fresh lemon zest added at the end. I used a leftover chicken from a carcass I made that is Ina Garten’s engagement chicken, so I added leftover roasted fennel.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This might be the easiest but it’s also the dullest soup ever.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Quite good! I needed to amp up seasonings a bit: I didn't have celery seed so subbed a combo of fennel seed and dried dill, S&P generously, and incremental pinches of paprika, turmeric, and onion salt & onion powder. Much improved and very yummy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was delicious and quick!! Partner was nursing a cold and it was just the antidote! Next time, I’ll add more carrots and celery. Other than that, it was  great…and did I say quick?l?!?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Love this recipe! I have made it trying the chicken in a few different ways (shredded, chopped, etc) and the ground chicken is my favorite. The blend of herbs/seasoning really holds up with the ground. I use half the amount of EVOO and its plenty. And add lemon juice at the end to brighten up and pair perfect with the herb broth. Agree with cooking noodles separate and adding them in with each bowl you make. Easy to store and reheat that way too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. I use two teaspoons of Costco dried garlic powder instead of fresh garlic cloves. Can’t tell the difference.  I find one garlic clove equals 1/2 tsp dried.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Definitely use small shells for the pasta. They trap the broth/chicken:)

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Leave the noodles separate if you plan on saving any of the soup. They will soak up all of the liquid.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This easy, delicious soup was just the ticket when my partner and I were recovering from a recent cold.  I added extra carrots and celery, as well as chopped curly-leaf kale and subbed whole milk greek yogurt for creme fraiche. Kept the egg noodles separate until serving.  Very handy as a quick, make ahead dinner.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made it with about 1 1/2 lb boneless skinless thighs, 8 c chicken stock, 8 oz ditallini ... and when I just dipped what was left of my morning bagel in the broth, it was excellent!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Soup was delicious! Used breasts which I browned first then cooked through with the noodles and shredded. Used eight cups of broth and it reduced down to nothing. Maybe next time I will use more or keep the noodles separate. Did not have crème fraiche  but used parsley and a squeeze of lime juice. (This is what is used in Cuban style chicken noodle soup.) Recipe is a keeper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is so good and easy.  Made it first time with ground chicken.  Second time I boiled the chicken and shredded it.  Then I sautéed shredded chicken in 2T oil, coriander and garlic per directions.  Turned out even better!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely lovely. Made it with skinless, boneless chicken thighs. Don't skip the garnishes -they really elevate it another full step. Used sour cream since we didn't have creme fraiche. Definitely making again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Why not just cook the noodles separately and drain them? That way, you don't soak up your broth? That's what I do.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Really good and easy. Lemon zest, sour cream, and herbs for garnish add a lot.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: 4 cups chicken stock   4 cups chicken bone broth

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Easy to make and delivers big time on flavor - absolutely perfect

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Subbed rosemary for coriander, but otherwise made as written. It really was quick and tasted as if it had been simmering a long time. The herbs and lemon zest at the finish were key.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I put in 5 cups stock (home made) for less soupy consistency.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
a recipe that lends itself to improvisation, indeed. 
thanks you, richelle, for the detour toward fennel. : ) 
bone-in chicken does enrich the broth. & how. (should i skim the foam?)
lucky for us, we have access to STOGLINI organic pasta: saffron malloreddus, for a journey to sardinia 
lana, dear, thank you for the inspiration. hadn't considered straining the broth to keep the contents from soaking it up. brilliant. who the heck needs to waste energy & water to cook pasta? so smart.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used chicken thighs instead of ground chicken but pretty much followed the recipe otherwise. Turned out great!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Hmmm...I made it exactly as written, but it came out with little flavor. I'm not sure the ground chicken was a better approach than non-ground chicken.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was really really good. Just followed the recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Fudged a little bit, but the base recipe is successful and allows you to tailor to what you have in fridge/pantry and your tastes!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I make the soup often. I love it! I always boil the noodles separate so that they don’t soak up all of the broth. I love the combination of the crème fraîche and some dill on top. It’s delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Followed the recipe, except: used 1 lb fresh chicken breast. I also added a couple sprigs of thyme and rosemary (because Kate next door gave me a bunch of rosemary) to the chicken while cooking. Also chopped up about a teaspoon of rosemary with the rest of the thyme for the main recipe. Also, didn’t have chicken broth so I used 8c of a mix of BtB Carmalized onioin, mushroom, and no beef stock. Added 2T spoons butter when i put the chicken back in. Noodles absorbed most of the broth, prob add mor

author: Alexa Weibel

commentBody:

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Not ground!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I tried this today and it was SUPER bland. Had to add a bunch of other spices to make it taste like something. Won’t make again and don’t recommend if you’re looking for something flavorful.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made this with Quorn meatless diced “chiqen” (added it in with the noodles). The best chicken noodle soup I’ve had/made, meatless or otherwise. The lemon and crème freche make it extra special. This will be on our winter rotation.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is a tasty, easy soup! Made as written even though I was skeptical of ground chicken. Turned out great and was a family hit on a cold day. Such a simple weeknight soup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Great recipe, really love the flavor. As others noted I would also double the veggies in this recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Great quick recipe for cold and flu season.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used ground turkey since we didn't have ground chicken available at our grocery store, and it was great.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is the best-ever chicken soup recipe!  The flavors are amazing.  I sometimes throw in some Penzey's mixed spices, but you don't have to.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: How do I print the recipe without the picture, which uses tons of ink? There's no option to not print the photo that I can find. Thank you!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Try to copy and paste the recipe itself to a word document and then print and name that ?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This really was the easiest chicken soup I've made. I can't have a lot of salt, so I was happy it still had a lot of flavor without having to add too much. I didn't have the celery seed, but I did add extra dill to the soup, in addition to the garnish. I agree with the other reviewer who suggested to keep the pasta separate if you aren't eating all of it right away. The pasta soaked up most of the broth after a couple of hours (I left it out for latecomers).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This soup is a time saver yet packed with flavor, simply put it is delicious. I used fresh herbs, lemon zest and added celery seed. The only other change I made, for dietary reason, was substituting fennel for shallot. I did, on advice of others, double the vegetables and I will add more next time. I loved the combination of herbs, wow, I will be making this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: We really liked this soup and it’s super easy to make. Made great leftovers too. I didn’t have coriander, so I used garam masala and it was tasty. I’ll definitely be making this one on chilly weeknights.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I've made this twice now and it will  go into the winter meal rotation.  So easy, so flavorful!  Cavatelli is hard to find in my area so I used orchiette.  Family loves it and so quick to throw together during the week.  Yum!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was pretty good though made some changes. No ground chicken so poached and shredded chicken breast. Halved the recipe but kept herbs and veggies the same as full recipe. Made it pretty garlicky but I liked it. Also added some Lee and Perrins as well as a couple of anchovies in the sautéing stage to give more umami. Used orecchiette for pasta.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delicious and more savory than traditional chicken soup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: pretty bland, and I can't say I like the ground chicken

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: It was ok.  Ground chicken not great and surprisingly blad without adding a ton of salt.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Pretty good, but not amazing.  Next time, I will try using thin chicken breasts and brown them, in lieu of the chopped chicken.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made this and loved it. Extremely easy to make and hit the spot. Left overs were perfect. Crème fresh, lemon zest, and dill is a must.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made this as written, with egg noodles and ground chicken breast. It was fantastic. This is going into regular rotation. So easy and so flavorful. The only thing I'll change next time is to use 4 or 5 ounces of egg noodles; 8 ounces was a bit much.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is a winner! Delicious, and could not be easier. That said, I added the 8th C of broth since the 7.5 oz egg noodles made it even to “stew-y” for us. So I would cut back the noodles to maybe 6 oz and add more carrot.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Hi all, making this tonight and flavors are wonderful. Just wondering if your veggies are soft enough by following this recipe “to the t.” Doesn’t seem like enough cook time to get the veggies tender. I’m letting mine simmer in the broth longer to soften up more.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Just cooked this. While it tastes fine in general the ground chicken turns into dry lumps which are neither attractive nor flavorful. If you don’t want to bother using a whole chicken, or parts with bones, I would use diced chicken thighs.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made this tonight using ground turkey, turkey stock, and gemelli pasta. I added a little fresh spinach and hot sauce to kick up the flavor. We also used sour cream instead of crème and my family really enjoyed it! Super easy and quick. Great week night dinner for our busy family.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
When I read the recipe I wondered if the ground chicken would be flavorful enough and it was!
Really easy to make, I use Better Than Bullion chicken stock, rather than canned or boxed stock. I did dollop some sour cream on a few helpings but it stands up with or without it. It was a steaming bowl of deliciousness, perfect for these cold days.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Next time I will use chicken sausage instead of ground chicken. I think that would have a better flavor and texture and wouldn’t add any difficulty to the recipe. Also used and onion instead of a shallot and added some white wine in with the broth! Really great easy quick recipe

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I added a package of shelf stable gnocchi instead of pasta to make this a meal and it was delicious. Also added some hot pepper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This recipe can become overly sweet with the carrots and shallots. Next time I will put half the amount of carrots and also be careful not to overcook the ground chicken, because it gets crumbly and not the most pleasant texture in the soup. I added lemon juice to brighten it up, otherwise a great recipe.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
This was a big hit with my family.  I made a couple of changes:
Instead of adding the spices to the chicken in step 1, I added them in step 2 to the vegetables. This enabled the spices to flavor the broth mixture before adding the chicken back in.
I used fennel because I didn't have celery seed
I added about a cup of frozen corn.  I will definitely make this again!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Excellent! The only reason I didn’t give it 5 stars is because I followed the advice of other reviewers and decreased the noodles by 1/3 and doubled the veggies. The soup needed still more broth. 
Flavor was superb though!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Underwhelming.  Ground chicken might absorb more flavors but it is definitely less appealing than torn pieces of chicken, rotisserie or otherwise, in the soup. also 8 ounces of noodles is too much for 8 cups of broth, I would start with half that. My soup was excessively noodle-y

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was truly delish, used all of the variations mentioned by other reviewers, the ground chicken really makes a difference!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I think the idea of ground chicken is to make this soup quick and easy. Lots of other recipes for using chicken pieces instead. I have made this many times just as written although sometimes add more carrots and celery. I use yolk free noodles so there is less chance of them becoming soggy in leftovers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Shockingly delicious!  So easy and still flavorful!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I thought the ground chicken was easy and pretty flavorful! I made dumplings instead of noodles bc I was in the mood and it was a really nice meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Yum!  It's terrific as is, though I used onion b/c I didn't have shallots.  Also added asparagus, b/c I have it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is so easy when we're craving chicken soup but I don't have the time or energy to make the full blown scratch version.  We've made this a bunch of times this year.  Creme fraiche really takes it up a notch.  I like to add extra veggies.  Making it again tonight!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Made exactly as is except:
1.5 pounds chicken
3 very large carrots
Extra celery and 2 large shallots
7.5 cups fluid (better than bullion)
Extra thyme
Plain yogurt instead of crème fraiche 

Fantastic!  Everyone loved it! I will definitely make again.  The lemon was a lovely addition that I wouldn’t skip

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Rather than ground chicken, I boil about a 1/2 pound of chicken breasts and shred.  So yummy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: A real hit! Used ground turkey and homemade chicken stock from an earlier roast chicken. This is a keeper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This creates a really delicious stock, but I don’t like the texture of the ground chicken. I’m going to try it with sliced up chicken thighs instead.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is a keeper. I added ground fennel & a small diced jalapeño for punch. I used black pepper & lemon pepper mix.  Used mirepoix from Trader Joe’s with added carrots just to spend less time chopping & so I don’t waste celery. I also added chicken bullion to enrich the chicken taste like you get when you make chicken soup from bone-in parts. I used ditalini instead of noodles—personal preference.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Loved this!!! The crème fraiche and herb garnish is a must. We also added a hearty squeeze of lemon and a pinch of coarse salt on top. I forgot to buy noodles so I doubled up on the veggies and added a couple big handfuls of spinach. It was still perfect!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Add can tomatoes

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made this recipe a few days ago and it was a success, i.e., my wife liked it. Used 3 each of carrots and celery stalks to increase the veggie content. Used 1 quart each of chicken bone broth and chicken stock. Best to all.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Loved the soup as written and like it brothy! Quick and flavorful!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious, every single time. The 'remove meat and cover it' is brilliant, why it works I don't know. I'm not a follow-the-recipe person but I do with this one. But this last time I made it, I was leaving for town and used what I had, it worked.  Added mushrooms and rappi at the end, used fennel seed instead of celery (it's what I had) and used labneh, perfect.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Absolutely delicious! I used medium-sized macaroni elbows and added a few fennel seeds in place of celery seeds. Filling, flavorful and generally awesome.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Really good! Doubled the vegetables and added an extra 1 1/2 c broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Cooked Chicken tenderloins instead of using a rotisserie chicken and a little bit of mexican table cream instead of crème fraiche, turned out great!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Definitely one of my favorite soup recipes especially in winter or when I’m sick! I find this recipe works super well in an instant pot. Instead of ground chicken I use chicken breast or thighs and just throw it in the instant pot - 15 min on high with a bit of the broth and the coriander. The chicken shreds super easy and i personally find the texture more satisfying. After that I sautée the veggies in the pot, add everything else in and pressure cook on high for 5-10 minutes.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I don't know if this is the easiest chicken soup but it certainly is a different and delicious one.  I made a few substitutions with herbs I had on hand  (parsley not coriander and Herbes de P not thyme) but otherwise made as described (well no garnishes at the end).  

I think it's a keeper.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: 1 tsp of salt

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is my favorite chicken soup recipe.  I make it for every holiday and it’s perfect with light and fluffy matzo balls.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: More carrots (6), more celery (3 stalks with leaves), use bite sized pieces of chicken breasts, no garlic, use a small sweet onion instead, use fresh herbs like parsley, basil, oregano, add a finely diced potato for a thicker soup. Cook all at the same time for 30 minutes, partially cover and stir a few times from the bottom. 2 cartons of chicken stock is all you need.  Add salt and pepper to taste. Stir in baby spinach to plate, top with grated Parmigiano if you like. Delicious and healing.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Good recipe!  Can sub red onion for shallot in a pinch and doubled the celery and carrot. I usually just buy rotisserie chicken and throw it in while the pasta is cooking.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: lemon zest, poach a chicken breast in wine

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Not bad. My butcher was out of ground chicken (!?) so I used ground turkey, which was fine. The recipe was, indeed, easy. It went a little heavier on the garlic than I would have instinctively done, but I'll try this again with the right meat, less garlic, and maybe a spice swap-out or two.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made it last night I used egg noodles and shredded white meat chicken from the grocery. Wife liked it. Will make it again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Unbelievably delicious flavors!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Underwhelmed.  No matter what you do to boxed chicken broth it's always basically going to taste like boxed chicken broth.  And if you want his to be more stew like ... try 5 cups of broth instead of 7...

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Something about the ground chicken tasted odd to me, so I will try with either thighs or breasts next time. Maybe it was the coriander too? I will play around with it bc it was quick and easy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Homemade stock, approx. 9 cups
4 teaspoons salt 

Cooked noodles in soup as directed - might benefit from another couple cups of stock

But great otherwise

-Sam

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: If you have homemade stock in the freezer, sure, but making it takes away the "quick and easy" nature of the recipe. I make stock from scratch frequently but if I want soup on a work night, I use packaged broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: There is too much meat in my opinion. Use a quarter of suggested and triple veggies. Very nice broth

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Does using less chicken affect the flavor?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Elevates basic chicken noodle and easy to make!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The soup, vegetable and chicken blend together to make the perfect, tasty chicken noodle soup! Highly recommended!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Easy and so flavorful!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Very easy.  Next time, however, I will poach some boneless chicken breasts in the broth for a richer soup.  I also used almost twice as many noodles and only 7 cups of broth.  Finally, if I were to use ground meat again, I would brown the meat first, leave it in while the rest of the soup cooks, and use ground Turkey instead of chicken for more flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Anyone make this with ground turkey instead?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used  TVP and  white beans instead of chicken, omitted the oil, and used unsalted vegan chik'n stock. I added the mini-rigatoni, so cute!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Ground turkey also works well with this recipe, don’t spare the herbs either!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I am garlic allergic. Will it compromise the taste if I omit the garlic? 
I also must go very easy on onions. Thank you.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The best chicken noodle soup noodles are the reames brand in the freezer section. Goodbye egg noodles, these are our favorite.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Whenever I make chicken soup I always make a large batch and only add the pasta to the portion that will be eaten as soon as the pasta is done. Reheated stock and certain veggies are fine but pasta is certainly not.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: My husband hates celery. I was thinking of substituting zucchini but wasn't sure if it would get too mushy. Any other suggestions?

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Try fennel.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Best way to avoid mushy noodles - try gluten free corn pasta. Total game changer

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The idea of using ground chicken rather than starting from scratch with thighs etc. is to make this a fast, relatively easy dish to prepare.  There are lots of recipes for chicken soup from scratch but these are more of a production.  This can be prepared quickly for a week night meal.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I agree.  Keep the noodles separate.  It seems fresher.  I also remove the "soggy" veggies and replace them with fresh ones (toss them in when reheating the soup). Too me, it taste better.  Plus lots of pepper!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used 2 lbs ground pork and didn’t have celery seed so I used raki anise seeds. I doubled spices and aromatics. I cooked fine egg noodles separately and added chopped basil and lemon zest at the end. Didn’t use the crème fresh.  Delicious and family loved

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: A yummy, easy soup! I upped the amount of veggies and spices. I used marjoram and added carrot stem leaves and fresh parsley at the end. I cooked the noodles for a minute less than the al dente time and it was cooked the right amount once the chicken was reheated.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is very good, and it tastes like it took all day to cook. Note: I use marjoram with chicken. It’s great with the shallots.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Wow.  I think a lot is owed to the coriander.  It creates such a warm flavor. I used orecchiette, it's pretty and yummy.  Glad I didn't skip the fresh dill (which I don't always gravitate toward). The crème fraîche and lemon zest really leveled it up. Huge heap of fresh parsley went into each bowl for extra color. I used a rotisserie chicken so I had the bones for a home made broth as liquid.  If you have a pressure cooker, this is done in 2 hours in the middle of the afternoon with zero effort.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I had to make a quick meal for unexpected company last night, and this was suggested in my daily NYT Cooking email, so I didn't even have to think about what to make.  I made it almost exactly as stated, except I also added a leek, and I kept the chicken in the pot while cooking the noodles.  Perfect!  Everyone loved it.  It made 7 hearty servings although not enough for seconds.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I considered this a ‘riffable’ recipe. So, I used ground turkey (a little more flavor?). I lacked celery and celery seeds (boo). I had a leftover leek, so I substituted it for the shallot. Added some frozen peas and used Trader Joe’s pork potstickers. The same recipe? No, but close—and delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I made this soup exactly to the recipe with garlic and Herbes de Provence in first section. We enjoyed the result greatly.
I don’t understand when people critique a recipe without trying said recipe as written. I understand omitting an ingredient if it isn’t in someone’s wheelhouse but that in fact changes the taste.
Oops, guess I did make a change - I cooked pasta in separate pot for easier use for leftovers

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Even after increasing the intensity of bouillon, the soup just didn't taste very rich to me.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: We made this tonight. Delicious, but it needed a lot of love for me. More body and veg etc. i love the seasoning, though!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: We make this all the time! It's our family's favorite chicken soup. We use homemade bone broth. Don't forget the sour cream and a few dashes of Tabasco!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Superb, boy's loved it! Served with sourdough garlic bread. Total keeper.

Watch the celery salt dude, this was SO salty I had to water down. Have just ordered celery seeds!

1 brown onion
Lots of celery and carrots and garlic, rosemary instead of thyme
Used about 2L chicken stock/broth
Used entire bag of pasta (also known as cicatelli from Tony and Mark's)
Topped with dill only

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made it. Loved it. You should do it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This tastes like a 30min soup. No depth of flavor really. I recommend an instant pot or a pressure cooker approach with a real chicken.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I omitted of the coriander (I didn't have) and replaced the shallot with 1/2 cup chopped onion.  I added an extra tsp. of thyme and 2 tsp. Indian curry powder. 
I added egg noodles from my pantry and a big bunch of fresh spinach and fresh cilantro tossed in towards the end.  On the side I served leftover pita which I buttered and toasted until crispy. 
Absolutely delicious. Rave reviews from my family. I will be making this again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This soup is really simple and delicious. I typically don’t make substitutions but the grocery I went to was out of chicken so I ended up with turkey. This soup had a wonderful fattiness and spice. I put in a ton of dill at the end but it’s a favorite herb of mine.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Modifications:
Chicken better than bouillon instead of canned/box stock or broth
Double batch - served our home of three nicely with some lunches and dinners after the first night
Because we wanted leftovers, kept the noodles separate from the rest of the soup. 

Could probably sub in ground turkey if that’s on sale, maybe add a little more oil if it’s super lean?

Will repeat again this winter!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made it exactly as instructed with mini farfalle, 7 cups bouillon,dill and lemon zest and it was easy and superb.  Great for a singleton like me who works from home.  This is my weekday lunch!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
For a cold:
Also add ginger with herbs
and 1-2 T Soy sauce - only Low sodium!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made as directed. Didn’t care for the texture of the ground chicken in the soup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
This recipe is amenable to substitutions. (Ground turkey, chicken fat, this time) I had less meat so bone broth and chicken fat really added flavor.
I am planning to make this again, probably different next time, but happy to have this recipe as a guide

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I did not like this at all. The ground chicken had a mealy quality that was off putting and the taste of it was what I call “dirty”. It did not have a bright, clear taste of chicken breast or even chicken thighs, it was more like the inside parts of a chicken carcass, but maybe that was a reflection of the spices used. For my time and money, I would rather use chicken breast and a good broth, if you cut the breast’s into smaller pieces it will cook very quickly.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I made this and it was a big hit.  My husband had three servings.  Instead of using a carrot, I used mixed vegetables which made less work.  For the pasta, I used ditalini which cooked quickly and were evenly distributed throughout the soup.

I am looking forward to the leftovers.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
This was the best chicken noodle soup I’ve ever had.

I roughly hand shredded chicken breasts instead of using ground, and enjoyed the toothiness of the larger pieces. I’m sure it’s great either way but will use shredded next time I make this since it was so superb this way :) Thank you for this recipe!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Quick tasty recipe    I found the ground chicken good I had my doubts   Now I’m a believer

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is tremendously good  soup* ... the zip from the lemon zest at the finish is genius !

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Best soup ever!  Especially after it sits overnight in the fridge.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Just made this as written and loved it. Easy and delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delicious. A winner. I doubled the veg and halved the noodles. Tried to keep use of olive oil down (ended up using 2.5 TBL. Added fresh lemon juice at the end of cooking.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Less turmeric
Less salt
Brown meatballs first
Part dark part breast
Make them smaller, soup size

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Instead of egg noodles, i added drained and rinsed chick pea Orzo. It was delicious. Love using ground chicken - a wonderful change!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Used baby shells because that was what was available.  They soaked up too much broth.  Next time, cook them separately .  I had to add one full cup of broth to each frozen portion.  However, it is really good and something that will always be in the freezer

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Yes!!!! Season cold be gone! Love this soup, hits the spot.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Wow this turned out great. Definitely doubled the veggies, added some Kale, and made homemade egg noodles, which is a family requirement (super easy). The flavor of the broth was just was exactly what was missing from the store bought roasted chicken!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I would increase the vegies. I added spinach

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Never cooking another chicken noodle recipe. So good.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Very good, very quick and quite savory.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I added the juice of one lemon to the recipe and it was delicious.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delicious, easy, better than grandma’s chicken soup. After making several times, I cook pasta separately and add more carrots and celery. Also, used onion instead of shallot. A keeper!!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was easy and quick to make and hits the spot when you want the comfort of chicken noodle soup but don't have a lot of time. Because it's a quick cook, it doesn't have deep rich flavor like a more traditional chicken noodle soup, but the heavy garlic in the ground chicken adds an interesting flavor note and the ground chicken ensures you get the full combo of flavors in every bite. I used small-size egg noodles and the full 8 cups of broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This soup gets RAVE reviews every time I make and from everyone I share it.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Simply delicious. Great for a soup exchange during this freezing winter in the island.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Phenomenal recipe. My grocery was out of ground chicken, so I bought boneless skinless thighs and ground them in a food processor -easy and great depth of flavor. The creme fraiche and lemon at the end are absolute musts.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was easy and good.  I used Mark Bittman's roasted chicken stock, which added a lot of depth.  I added parmesan rind, and cooked the egg noodles separately so I could freeze the leftover soup.  Didn't have a lemon and I don't like dill, but a blob of creme fraiche added a nice tang.  Next time I'll add some white wine to the vegetables and let most of it evaporate for a bit more flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Tried this as written, first time using ground chicken. Learned that I do not like ground chicken. I think the flavors are lovely but I will stick to using whole shredded chicken.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Bone broth too strong. Added 2 tablespoon lemon juice and quite a bit of alpine touch and nutritional yeast

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Bone broth too strong. Added 2 tablespoons lemon juice and nurtional yeast and lots of salt. Used Italian seasoned ground chicken

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: So easy, filling and delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I love this recipe.  It is on the table for every holiday.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: EXCELLENT. But double the broth.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Just because I had some, added sautéed shiitakes and a generous handful of chopped parsley.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is really good comfort food - and tastier than most chicken noodle soup recipes. I reduced the chicken, noodles and stock by half but upped the spices, garlic and shallot. Used fennel seeds instead of celery. Added broccoli halfway through the pasta cooking stage. Yum.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Worst soup recipe ever, should boil a chicken no ground chicken

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Chose my adventure from other comments: I used anellini o’s for noodles and cooked separate. I pulsed 1.5 lbs chicken thighs instead of pre-ground. Added fennel bulb chopped, 1/2 tsp turmeric, and a pinch of cinnamon. Don’t skip the magic garnish: sour cream, dill, parsley, and lemon zest   juice. Thank you!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made with skinless chicken thighs, browned in pan first, removed to cook the veg, and added back in with the liquid. Great flavour! Will return to this recipe again, for sure.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I love this recipe! I don’t love celery so I sub leeks (1 large or 2 small) and omit the celery seeds. I also use 3-5 carrots and keep the thyme on the spring and fish out the stems. I use orzo for the pasta which holds up well for leftovers! It does take me a bit longer than 30 minutes, closer to 45-60, from start to finish. Enjoy!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Has a great flavor and easy, using the ground chicken works really well. Could use more veggies for amount of broth and noodles, at least another carrot and celery stalk. Like all soups cooked with pasta, the pasta will continue absorbing the liquid after cooking. I would suggest keeping the pasta or noodles quite al dente for the first cooking or eat it all at once since inevitably you will need to continue adding broth or water when reheating.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: On day 5 of a cold and still craving chicken soup, so I decided to try this since I had all the ingredients. Left the noodles in a little too long and they soaked up most of the broth which made it more like a “stoup” which was fine by me! Served over leftover mashed potatoes for a variation on the midwestern favorite “mashed potatoes with noodles.” Will definitely make again since I often have ground chicken in the freezer.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Substituted Barley for pasta.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: What an easy, lovely soup for a quiet Sunday afternoon.  I simmered it longer because I was not in a rush, added a sprig of rosemary, and floated plain dumplings with parsley to cook on top instead of noodles. No cream just because I didn’t have it.  Very nice.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Did this with bone-in chicken thighs, 15 minutes sautéed on different sides, then pulled apart and cooked for another 5.  Probably overcooked, could have pulled it apart earlier and cooked for less than 15 mins.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: 3/23/24 this was delicious.  Used about 1/2 cup rice.  Added ginger, but should have grated it.  Used fennel seeds as we didn’t have celery.  Will definitely make again. The lemon juice was clutch.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I had to use ground turkey as the store didn't have any ground chicken. Still came out great!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Will be making this again! Easy to make, simple ingredients, and lovely taste.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: An easy peasy hit with all 6 of us after a busy day on the farm and sunset swim.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I don't know what went wrong but this was horrible. Absolutely flavorless with no depth of flavor. Followed the recipe to a T.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I was intrigued by the concept but, in the end, I really didn’t like the texture of the ground chicken in this recipe. My quick chicken soup starts with a roasted chicken from the supermarket.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Made it "as is".  It was fantastic.  This recipe is easy so easy and so good!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I used ground coriander, fennel seed, and fresh rosemary. Then subbed pasta for 1 cup white rice and 1 can cannellini beans. Added kale, lemon zest and juice at the end and it was a huge hit. Will definitely be making again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: You forgot the parsnips!  Cook the noodles separately!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Whole family enjoyed.  I used bacon grease (I keep in the fridge) as well as olive oil to give more flavor.  I also find the ground chicken at our store so lean there is rarely 'fat' lying around in the pan.  Deglazed pan with dry white wine under the same thought that I wanted a bit more depth in flavor.
Important steps IMO keeping ground chicken in covered bowl to keep in moisture & adding/cooking the noodles just before serving.  Egg noodles love to consume broth.  
Will make again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: It got me through a pinch on a weeknight.  It did not have the rich unctuous broth I was so craving.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delicious as written,loved the combo of celery seed & cardamon. I did however add lemon zest & the juice of a lemon directly to the soup. Used capellini (.99) at TJ’s.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Without a doubt, one of the very best chicken soup recipes I have ever made. I did not have a shallot, but I had some Cipollini onions so I used those. Also no celery salt because I don’t like it. And I skipped the cream fraiche. Such umami in this soup! I throw you a kiss!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is perfect as is.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I always save set aside some chicken stock for cooking my noodles separately. That way the noodles stay intact and they don't thicken the soup with starch. Cooking them in stock imparts flavour, too.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Try adding cheese tortellini (frozen, cooked to al-dente). I do that with several broth-type veggie soups I make.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Excellent. Had 99% lead chicken, so kept all OO and was fine. 
Fresh ground coriander and 1 tsp fennel seed - this made this dish. 
Per cmts, doubled all vegetables. Added about half a fennel bulb.
Doubled cook time at almost every step, other than chicken, esp. to get vegetables soft.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
I’m obsessed with this recipe. I use boneless skinless chicken thighs which I mince in the food processor (good to cut them into smaller pieces first), with a tbsp of low sodium soy sauce added for umami. I double carrot for an equal amount as the celery roughly. 

Otherwise I cook as is and hubby and I are very happy.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made just a few modifications. One, I added turmeric because my husband was sick, and I wanted to pack as much get-well spirit into the soup as possible. Two, I added some yellow squash because I had it on hand, and why let it go to waste? Three, I used orzo. Four, I omitted the creme fraiche because it seemed unnecessary. There was so much flavor and such good texture. I’ll make it again and again.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: We found this to be a bit bland. With added red pepper flakes at the table it was improved, but I doubt I would make this again because I have so many other excellent soup recipes. I made as directed but didn’t have the celery seeds so used a small amount of fennel seeds and rosemary which had been a suggested alternative. I used homemade chicken stock which was excellent but it still needed something more to really pick it up.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I made this the first time when my whole household was sick and I didn’t have the bandwidth for something fussy.  Chicken soup for when you can’t be bothered. It is perfect in its simplicity. I didn’t change a thing. Served it with dill, parsley, lemon zest and crème fraiche.  It’s now in heavy rotation on our dinner menu. No modifications needed.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Instead of the dill and crème fraiche I added cilantro, lime and Cholula for a nice tang that cleared the sinuses. Also used a bone broth for richness instead of two stocks.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This was great! Next time I would double the amount of carrots and celery. The whole recipe could be doubled on top of that easily.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The noodles are really good in this but I often sub them out for a chopped head of cauliflower, 1 cup or so of peas, 1 red pepper and 1 cubed sweet potato. Delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Next time try 6 oz noodles. And minimum 8 cups broth. Definitely 3 or four carrots. Used fennel seed for lack of celery seed

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: The lemon zest really tops this off

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I’ve made this recipe for my family at least 50 times. Last night I added parsnips just to live a little. This is by far the easiest, tastiest chicken soup recipe out there. Soup, soup a tasty soup… a spicy carrot and coriander! Chili chowder. Crouton crouton - crunchy friends in a liquid broth! Perfection. Don’t skip the celery seeds with the coriander. It’s heaven.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Wow, this was really good for chicken noodle soup!  I did not have coriander, so I had to use cumin.  I did use celery seed.  My husband had seconds!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
Dear Alexa Weibel,  Thank you for the 'easiest chicken noodle soup' recipe. 
 One thing I need to point out is: 
 The recipe takes far longer to prep and cook than 30 minutes.The recipe needs to be prepped, everything ready to go in the pot at the right time and it will be cooked in 30 minutes.
 I adjusted the ground chicken to half a pound ground chicken and half a pound ground mild pork sausage. Used orecchiette no. 91 pasta. I needed to add' Better Than Bouillon' for more flavor.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
This is a winner!! Asked for regularly and enjoyed by my pickiest eater! I make exactly as written, and my husband and I think the creme fraiche, dill and squeeze of lemon really make the dish, while my picky eater prefers to finish with parmesan. 

On occasion, I've added mushrooms, which works well, but they do absorb lots of broth.

The rare moment when this falls short is when I've failed to salt and pepper the chicken enough (be liberal -- this is a simple recipe).

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Used rotisserie chicken instead of ground and turned out great. Lemon at the end is a must. Great for a cold day, or a sick day.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: this is indeed super easy and very delicious! we used some chow mein noodles that we had left over and we cooked them separately and just spooned the soup over them in each bowl.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I doubled the veg, halved the pasta and used plant based chicken and this was delish! Loved that seasoning in this recipe, each bite had a different flavor and they all blended tog th

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Several years on, and I still can't get over how easy and delicious this soup is. I add grated ginger and red pepper flakes for some warmth, and then throw in about a cup of frozen peas at the end. Lemon and dill to serve are non-negotiables, and creme fraiche really takes it over the edge, if you have it!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Basic and good.  Should have listened to the advice of K and kept noodles to the side.  Next time.  Lots of room to improvise.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This soup is so delicious and so easy. I have always been a traditional-cook-the-whole-chicken chicken soup maker, but I'm a convert now that I have found this one. The garlic and coriander give it so much flavor. And the fresh herbs make it so special. We use pastina or acini pasta...the tiny pasta is really fun. I've made this maybe four times in 2 months.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This is the easiest chicken soup ever.  I made it for my flu-ridden wife and she said it made her ears tingle.  I added a tin of Mexican flavoured beans and a tin of Cannelini for extra fibre, including their waters, and used yoghurt instead of crème fraiche.  Plus a drizzle of olive oil on the herbs at the end.  The herbs not to be missed out as they speak up the soup.  Came together in the 30m, as described.  So easy and souper delicious!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Oh, my supermarket sells a veggie bolognaise mix of carrots and celery and turnips (soffrito) and I used that instead of the veggies.  I got my butcher to do a 50/50 mix of breast and thigh for the mince so there was some fat.  And I only had 2/3 of the noodles left in my store cupboard so cut back on that, and it was fine.  Very fine!

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: This recipe wasn't successful. The texture of Whole Foods ground chicken was thick and dry. Used fennel instead of celery. Used pasta shells instead of egg noodles. The recipe calls for 8 oz, which is too much. Broth lacked flavor. Also should have reduced ingredients because this recipe makes many servings, too much for two.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: Soooo rich in flavor and so easy. Make sure you really leave the ground chicken as clumpy as possible (like sausage) to really give you bites of it with the finished soup.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I added fresh shredded ginger to the broth! Came out wonderful

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody:
So, I first wanted to say, I loved this. It was as easy to make as suggested, and it was delicious. 

But.

The accoutrements: the parsley, the creme fraiche, the lemon zest -- they all turn this into something that isn't your traditional chicken noodle. I was shocked enough at the flavor change that I had to try it without to see if something was weird about the soup. No, it tastes like chicken noodle without the additions. With the additions, it DOES NOT taste like chicken noodle soup to me.

author: Alexa Weibel
recipeTitle: Easiest Chicken Noodle Soup Recipe
commentBody: I've used this recipe dozens of times and have found it to be so adaptable.  I definitely recommend doubling the veggies for a hardier soup.
